# Thread: Impending Cataract Surgery?..How about a blu-blocker lens!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impending-cataract-surgery-how-about-a-blu-blocker-lens.19886/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dennis Durost — 2017-07-25T05:11:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impending-cataract-surgery-how-about-a-blu-blocker-lens.19886/post-219997>

I was kind of bummed out when I learned that cataract surgery would block the beneficial morning light from my eyes. I checked with my eye surgeon and he informed me that my lenses were ALCON Acrysof IQ IOL with UA and proprietary blue-blocking technology. They sent me the following information:

**BLU BLOCKER CATARACT REPLACEMENT LENS**
Recently, there have been great advances in artificial lens technology. The Acrysof IQ Intraocular Lens (IOL) is an example. All currently available artificial lenses filter ultraviolet (UV) light, which is harmful to the retina in the eye. The Acrysof IQ IOL filters both UV light and high-energy blue light. Clinical studies have shown that high-energy blue light causes oxidative damage to the retina and may be a risk factor for developing macular degeneration. The Acysof IQ lens incorporates a patented chromophore that blocks harmful blue light and UV light in much the same way as our natural lens does prior to developing cataracts. The FDA clinical studies showed no adverse side effects on color vision or quality of vision. The Acrysof IQ IOL inserts through a micro-incision that requires no stitches, and it is designed to conform to the natural shape of the lens capsule. This helps it stay stable and centered in the eye. But what makes the Acrysof IQ IOL unique is its ability to filter blue light. The Acrysof IQ (Blue-Blocker) IOL enhances the benefits of advanced cataract surgery by filtering out harmful blue light and, thus providing a replacement lens that protects the retina more like your natural lens. Additionally, this lens is designed with wavefront-guided, aspheric optics that help improve contrast sensitivity and overall visual quality.

Downside: $900 an eye (out of pocket)

This helps it stay stable and centered in the eye. But what makes the Acrysof IQ IOL unique is its ability to filter blue light. Additionally, this lens is designed with wavefront-guided, aspheric optics that help improve contrast sensitivity and overall visual quality.
>
> Downside: $900 an eye (out of pocket)

Hi Dennis, did anyone come back to you on this post? My mother is about to undergo cataract surgery and I am also wondering if this is a good option compared to the classic UV blocking lenses. From what I understand, the one you mentioned above blocks both UV (not ideal) and blue light (better than just UV block one would assume?). Many thanks.

# Thread: Grounding on Ceramic Tile and Brick
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-on-ceramic-tile-and-brick.19888/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dennis Durost — 2017-07-25T05:28:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-on-ceramic-tile-and-brick.19888/post-219999>

I have read numerous articles on grounding and no one even mentions grounding indoors on ceramic tile. My gut was telling me it should work if the tile was installed on a concrete slab. So I did a hack on it by purchasing a meter and putting it to a test. I found that the meter reading drop was exactly the same when I stood barefoot on indoor tile and barefoot on my front sidewalk. I also tested on brick pavers installed on ground and again had similar results.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-25T08:25:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-on-ceramic-tile-and-brick.19888/post-220007>

I'm wondering that too. My entire ground floor is ceramic tile laid on top of concrete I guess. Straight on the ground. I don't have a meter to test it.

### Greenie — 2018-01-31T00:25:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-on-ceramic-tile-and-brick.19888/post-233841>

> [Dennis Durost said:](/goto/post?id=219999)
>
> I have read numerous articles on grounding and no one even mentions grounding indoors on ceramic tile. My gut was telling me it should work if the tile was installed on a concrete slab. So I did a hack on it by purchasing a meter and putting it to a test. I found that the meter reading drop was exactly the same when I stood barefoot on indoor tile and barefoot on my front sidewalk. I also tested on brick pavers installed on ground and again had similar results.

I observed the same phenomenon in the place I currently live. The body voltage meter read .20 volts inside (without insulating shoes) as outside on the concrete patio. (Body voltage goes up to .65+ with rubber-soled shoes on).

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-31T04:53:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-on-ceramic-tile-and-brick.19888/post-233858>

I've found not all tiles are similar and they need to be tested......and believe it or not how they are set has an effect. It is not as easy as one thinks to figure out.

### brandie — 2018-01-31T06:24:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-on-ceramic-tile-and-brick.19888/post-233861>

I'm a huge home rehab/remodel show addict. In the last ten years or so, contractors are laying tile over a moisture barrier more and more. The barrier is made of plastic. If the tile goes directly onto concrete there could be a membrane that helps prevent cracking of the tile when the foundation shifts.

# Thread: My journal - Allergies/Eczema/Retinal issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-allergies-eczema-retinal-issues.19891/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BPS — 2017-07-25T13:19:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-allergies-eczema-retinal-issues.19891/post-220020>

Hi all

So I started looking at health hacks a few years back, starting with Paleo eating, and have geeked up and extended my knowledge from there. I've seen lots of practitioners and read lots of journals, etc to get where I am now:

**Quick history:**

\* 1st Born. Emergency C Section birth. Breastfed for 6 months
\* Live in the NW of United Kingdom
\* Male, 5"10'. 72kg
\* Had eczema since age of 2, after being in hospital for hip operation (stress, antibiotics, etc)
\* Office worker (company director)
\* Worked in city, Lived in semi-rural.
\* Since 2015, moved office to rural, stone built building, 10 miles from nearest city. Moved house to same area.
\* About to buy new 1850s stone built house in 1 acre of land. Right at foot of Fell in a national park. No powerlines, no wifi signals, very little phone signal.

\* Maternal health: 65. Excellent, no known health issues.
\* Maternal grandparent health: Died at 75. Chron's.

\* Paternal health: 63. Excellent, no known health issues.
\* Paternal grandparent health: Died at 55. Heart failure.

**2014-2016**

\* Started with a personal trainer, big focus on 'bulking', eating lots of protein and carbs. Bad food, bad advice. Overtrained (5x/week).
\* Eczema flared up, lots of yeast issues
\* Also had muscle strain in groin that was misdiagnosed as UTI, had 3x Antibiotics back-to-back
\* Long term hamstring and groin strain lasted around 2 years
\* Prescribed strong topical steroids to deal with eczema
\* Surprise surprise, adrenals burnt out. Yeast issues and skin got worse. Thanks conventional medicine

\* Started with a functional medicine practitioner
\* Saliva tests showed reverse cortisol curve (very low in AM, high in PM)
\* Organic Acids test showed lots of yeast metabolites
\* Found I had big magnesium deficiency
\* Stool test showed no parasites, but gut disbiosis (didnt test for yeast)
\* Mould exposure due to renevating new office
\* Heart palpitations, very 'stressy' feeling, even when on holiday, lots of aches and pains
\* Suffered from 3 retinal tears, no known reason, also showing signs of peripheral retinal degeneration
\* Taking a trace minerals supplement (CMD drops) reduced visual effects of retinal tear (flashing lights).

\* Studied to learn about nutrition, biology, etc
\* Found out what foods worked for me, removed massive triggers (brassica/mustard family)
\* 2 month gut clearance using herbals
\* Skin issues solved 90-95%
\* Balanced adrenals using Pregnanalone/DHEA
\* Big digestive issues, massive GERD and Gallbladder pain. Resolved with Zinc Carnosine, Betain HCL, Digestive Enzymes
\* Homeopathic practitioner diagnosed and treated Coxsackie virus. Palpitations gone.

\* Later in year, Yeast issues cropped up again

**Current issues**

-   Skin is around 90% resolved, but I still suffer from eczema triggered by foods, specifically around my eyes and cheeks, small amount on fingers.
-   More recent stool test (2016) shows high yeast
-   Slight tinnitus (right ear, same side as eye issues)
-   Diet is **_very_** limited. Can't eat any brassica veg (most green veg), very sensitive to rice, wheat, barley. Meat is fine. Fats are fine.

-   Seem to be able to tolerate small amounts of some foods but not larger amounts
-   If I am not very careful, yeast issues crop up.
-   Re-tested adrenals, now high in morning and OK in evening. Probably normalised now as test was 1 yr ago.
-   This year I had to revisit eye hospital for epiretinal membrane affecting vision. Retinal tears had been repaired using laser, and are now stable (no progression)

Recently I've been focusing heavily on lifestyle modifications after realising that food got me 90% of the way, but no further.

**Current Routine (for last 6 months)**

6am Wake up, go for 1 hour, 2-3 mile walk. It's often cloudy here. Either go to local stream and paddle, or walk up nearby fell (300m altitude). Probiotics and 1.5L of water with Concentrace minerals

7am Breakfast - Usually eggs with tumeric.

8: 30am Work

12:30pm Lunch - Walk into local village and back for noon sun. Eat outside when possible. Usually eat salmon and salad, with olive oil, feta, trying to get other fish/shellfish into diet.

4:30pm Home. Try to get outside for an hour, weather permitting but this isn't often.

5:30pm Dinner. Usually meat, with steamed veg. Butternut squash as only carb source.

7:00pm Blueblocking glasses, Salt lamp, trying to reduce laptop/iphone usage but this is a problem.

9:30pm Bed, blacked out bedroom. Electronics & Wifi off.

**Big wins**

-   Adrenals seem much better. I re-tested early 2016 and morning had reversed to high, with PM ok. I am _much_ more chilled now and don't have any energy issues so haven't retested but from obvervation seem fine
-   I sleep very well now, I fall asleep quickly, don't wake up in night then wake up at 6am without any issues. I don't often dream however.
-   I now know food triggers to avoid
-   Feel much calmer and balanced
-   Work/life balance is very good
-   This year I spent 4 weeks on holiday near equator so far (2 weeks in Jan, 2 weeks in May)
-   In a routine to get an outdoor walk, 2x/day.

**Current challenges:**

-   I use my laptop and phone too much. I get a 'fix' from using them (low dopamine?) and find myself craving information. My workaround is to listen to a podcast which engages my brain and I don't then need to browse on my phone. I've already got RetinaGuard screens to block blue, plus Iris software on macbook.
-   I need to eat more shellfish, etc. I go to the supermarket every lunchtime and get salmon, but it's Scottish farmed, no other choice. I do get crab meat, mackeral/sardines (tinned), prawns, etc.

-   I really need more food flexibility, I flare up easily if I go out for a meal, etc.
-   I know the autumn/winter makes me go backwards considerably. All of my eye issues have occured between October and April, and there's been one every year for the past 3 years so determined to not have another this year!

Loving Jack's work, especially as it seems to have given me a possible reason for the current health issues. I'm an uber geek on health so it's great knowledge.

Looking forward to hearing people's input on my current progress!

### BPS — 2017-07-26T09:06:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-allergies-eczema-retinal-issues.19891/post-220107>

This week I’d love to focus on reducing my iPhone usage.

It’s like crack to me. I don’t use social media much but I gave a thirst for knowledge so as soon as a question pops into my head I’m on my phone looking for answers.

I don’t use it at work but as soon as I’m away from my desk it’s in my hand.

Any tips to reduce usage, in not sure going cold turkey is going to work...?

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-26T09:47:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-allergies-eczema-retinal-issues.19891/post-220109>

> [BPS said:](/goto/post?id=220107)
>
> This week I’d love to focus on reducing my iPhone usage.
>
> It’s like crack to me. I don’t use social media much but I gave a thirst for knowledge so as soon as a question pops into my head I’m on my phone looking for answers.
>
> I don’t use it at work but as soon as I’m away from my desk it’s in my hand.
>
> Any tips to reduce usage, in not sure going cold turkey is going to work...?

My god, yes!

I had this exact thing recently as I too have been dropping my iphone use. I'm now attempting to use it twice a day for messaging over whatsapp - came off social media, that was my biggest use of it!

What I found was that for the first few days - and still a little bit now - random thing would come in my head to 'check' or look up. You know, like, what happened to that random 80s band. Stupid things that we've grown accustomed to be able to get our answer straight away.

So I decided I would make a note of all the things during the day I wanted to look up and then do it in the evening whilst on the computer. In truth, most of it I realised was utter bullshit - I can go on living without knowing what happened to that 80s band and a lot of the other random things.

Anything that was truly important could wait until my evening computer time to look up if it was important. Because, that's what we used to do 10 years ago before smart phones. The internet existed so you had the knowledge but you'd just use it more productively rather than instantly any time you thought of something.

I don't know if that helps but the reality is that over the last few weeks the number or random urges and desires to look shit up has diminished. It's like, the more you don't go there by instantly gratifying that impulse or need, the more it subsides.

The only thing I’ve done differently has been increasing my sun exposure to mostly high noon (I live at 5k ft), and because my digestion was rejecting the BAB I started drinking an organic grass fed bone broth protein powder with amino acids. It contains 720mg of histidine among many others and all of the essential amino acids. I know it’s not necessarily the food, but it could be contributing??
I can’t sleep like this. I gave in last night and took Benadryl and gabapentine. I know I shouldn’t but I was desperate to sleep.
I’ve also been consuming French Cornichinos and suspect that’s also a contributing factor. Maybe they’ve turned 5G on. There’s little to no talk about us getting it, but I have been seeing small cells on the power grid in some places since I’ve become aware.

### Sean Waters — 2019-08-19T18:45:21+0300

[@Janette Layne](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22918/) Do you need the iPhone?........ I had a severely burnt nose/ forehead when I owned a smartphone and travelled Sri Lanka/ Mexico (hot places).

It wasn't until I switched to a Nokia 105 "old school 2G" phone that my forehead recovered. The Redox is still not great in it... it burns quicker than elsewhere, but I still work in an Office @ a screen, under BL... but it is reduced.

The proximity and intensity of the BL & RF from your smartphone is worse than a screen. Also, my screen has F.lux/ IRIS... and the overhead lights are further away than a phone. So that would explain the "gains"..... the other thing that has gone, was a stye/ eye twitch, I hated that fucker.

My nose still goes red with too much UV. But it isn't scabbing.

One hack that I did, when I refused to give in to not having a smartphone... was Face Dunks for 30 mins, into a bucket of half ice/ half water.

The temp was below 50 F, freezing... but it gave me EPIC sleep as it depleted deuterium @ the brain stem. And, it really healed my face.

Instead of buying Ice... fill up a Plastic/ Paper Cup.. one of those one's you'd get at a "Frat party". Fill it with water, put it in your freezer for 24 hours... Free Ice maker.... I make 12 x 1 Litre cups, everyday, and it gets my Cold Tub Water from 68°F to 55.8°F in the UK.

A certain amount of Pain and Sacrifice is going to be needed for you to get anywhere with anything on this forum.

None of this is going to be comfortable......

I was out in direct light almost all day yesterday and I must say, I am very pleased with the results. The face dunking really helped to alleviate the pain!
It has cleared up almost completely and no scarring!!!

# Thread: Sheddie's Optimizing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sheddie — 2017-07-25T14:17:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/post-220027>

Greetings all. Sheddie begins her 'Optimize -- or Bust!'

**Age:** 67 in 2017 **Sex:** F **Haplogoup:** H4a1 **Joined JackKruse.com:** July 2017
**Location:Lat.~33N
Dx:** Osteoarthritis (OA), Degenerative Disk Disease (DDD), Fibromyalgia, CFS, severe Dust & Mold Allergies, Asthma & Chemical Sensitivity Syndrome; Hashimoto's (1999); post-antibiotic SIBO, COPD (2017), MTHFR issues.
**Surgeries:** Cataracts (2017), Melanoma stage 1 (2017), Lumbar disk repair (1995), Arthroscopic (knee) repair of a 24 yo torn medial meniscus (1987).
**Current Rx:** Albuterol PRN; SIBO-related supplements; Lugol's...// 2018 Rx: 25mg compounded dessicated T3/T4
**Current Therapies / Protocols:** Leptin Rx; Cold Thermogenesis (face); Magnetico sleeping; regular sunlight exposure
**Extra Protocols: (\*=discontinued)** Continue Pain & AI management modalities: including; Neuro-muscular massage, Chiropractic, FIR/NIR Sauna, Lumen Photon (IR+red LEDs), \*Alpha-Stim CES; \*LCHFHP diet with an eye to FODMAPs; Sperti Lamps (winters); Ellis Water; \*Sporebiotics; DDW-25, 1 pint/day, 30 days on 30 days off (began 12/2018)
**Health WINS:** Tolerating cold showers; 12/2017-Weight down 11.6 lb; inflammation improved; D3 (2018) from 36 to 45 from skin Sun mainly, and what my IOLs will allow through; Summer 2018, no Fibro symptoms
**Health Goals:** Pain & Fatigue to zero; Light-Not Food lifestyle; 30-lb weight loss; reduce obscene number of supplements; 'medically stable' since ~2000 BUT hoping to bust that status for actual (true blue) recovery, in a race against actual aging!

Related Reading: Kruse website--Podcasts, YouTube clips, Forums; Hof-The Iceman; Epi-Paleo Rx; Pollack's Fourth Phase of Water, DDW publications.

My Haplogroup H4a1, while 100% European, broke that down to 10.5% Scandinavian (0 Finnish) with French & German beating that at 14.4%. The rest is Broadly Northwestern European.

**Journey Start-up:**

LIGHT: Managing 3-5 sun exposures daily; _still unclear if the 'pre-dawn UV blast,' or at the moment of dawn, or sometime past dawn should be the first UV exposure of the day?_; Have IOLs after cataracts so intent on skin as next best route of sunlight absorption; if sitting up sometimes allow sclera more exposure by sitting sideways (?); use a tan-thru' tube dress; wearing UVEX blockers when the house lights go on & f.lux all the time for wired desktop computer; unable to have a 'smart' or cell phone due to arthritic hands (not interested in 'hands-free' or wireless non-necessities!); planning more frequent _IR-sauna sessions in evenings._ Turn on a UV 'black light' bulb first thing on waking to take my time to safely get out of bed, and then (~5 minutes) make my way to grab some water and go outside. Every day is a sun-day. 12/2017 not cold adapted enough, so trying Sperti Lamps. 8/2018: after months of facing daily sunrise & sunset along with minimum 40 minutes full skin UVA & UVB, often more, Fibro brain fog & fatigue are gone, 2018. Installed a privacy fence and quit the kiniki when no one's visiting.

WATER: Intent on "a glass of water every hour" (Thanx to the recent video tipster!); Experimenting with spring waters and intent on moving away from distilled with trace minerals or Sole salt added, sometimes vortexed (magnetic funnel) or sun-exposed for 'structure;' structuring water with glass container set on a Lumen red-LED & IR mat as carrying glass out to the sunny patio is risky with thumbs that don't grip... 12/2017 decided on Icelandic Glacial bottled water, and, an Ellis distilling machine for tap water purification.

MAGNETISM: Grounding on un-treated cement patio when sunning; stopped use of earthing products hooked into the house electric socket ground until I have an outdoor spike set up; Have Orgonite and Q-link items in the car, house, and around the property perimeter outside; use a metal-free bed frame; use an Alan Maher mat for feet under the desk; sleep on a Magnetico.

Diet: Never was fond of sweets/sugar (mad for crunchy salty snacks); Low carb and 'as clean as possible' foods for decades (chemically sensitive after several years of workplace n-hexane exposure in the 1980s); currently on FODMAPs food choices for SIBO (post-antibiotics 4 yrs ago), with some temporary food allergy restrictions; Told No Wheat, forever; Paleo mostly the last few years; New action; advance to Epi-Paleo/pescetarian, mostly seafood for protein. _Getting the hang of that first meal/smoothie of the day! Thanks for the recipe ideas!
_

### Sheddie — 2017-07-26T05:11:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/post-220095>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=220094)
>
> sardines!

You bet, and I like 'em, too. Up to a point. Every so often in the past, my dog and I would share a can of sardines. I thought it would be easy.

However, a whole can is only 16g of protein and I think I need at least 25g of protein for breakfast then and there? Two cans might make my stomach rebel... What else on the plate? Not enough time to cook a fresh fish, and I wouldn't dare use a nuke-unit, if I want to eat 30 minutes after rising... Leftovers?

I just know many before me have conquered this bottom rung detail! I'm not sure but I doubt I can get out of, or find some hack around Leptin Reset Breakfast (new thread idea (?) for contributions??) Okay, just tell me, get it over with, if TWO cans of sardines are 'usual & customary?'

And Thanks, Caroline!! (Occam's razor rules!)

### caroline — 2017-07-26T05:43:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/post-220100>

There is someone here - darned if I can remember who ....they used to mix sardines with eggs and a little cheese and stick it in the oven. I think they broke the sardines up in an ovenproof bowl topped with two eggs and grated cheese. I would probably throw in celery and onions and bacon to mask the flavour of the sardines - lol

BTW - think outside the box ........dinner for breakfast. Before you go to bed stick a leg of lamb in your slow cooker and then wake up to breakfast - plus some nice broth.

When I cook I make a big batch of whatever so we have leftovers. We will lightly grill 4 salmon fillets and then, the next morning, mix it with greens and cucumbers and onions etc and homemade mayo and top with poached eggs. This is definitely our favourite.

Do you know what a western omelet is? chopped bacon and eggs and onions and mushrooms sometimes. I add in minced garlic and minced tumeric too.

There is an epi-paleo recipe section - have you found this yet?

### Sheddie — 2017-07-26T14:48:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/post-220121>

Found it! Will let its name have another curtain call: Moqueca – Brazilian Fish Stew Recipe.

For sure, 25+ grams of seafood protein would go down better with flavorful liquids. Can taste it already! Of course. It's all coming back to me now.

After being ovo-lacto Vegetarian for many years I took up with meat stews and soups to learn how to use meat. I was raised on primarily Scandinavian and French grandmothers' cooking, but, went vegetarian right when I moved out on my own. Went on a paella kick some years ago which I bet could stand the substitution of paleo cauliflower rice. Thai cuisine does wonderful seafood soup using coconut milk... I'm on a roll now!! And, ever cognizant that I'm in the age cohort of those who need to switch from Food First to LIGHT First! Being retired, I confess to being a Foodie for something to do. (More than 'enough to do' now with sun-seeking and CT, etc.) Strangely, I didn't gravitate to seafood recipes before. (Those darn beef-meat 'n potatoes guys in the house.) That's changed! It'll be a lot easier when my chicken & eggs restriction is lifted. I just got the OK from my functional medicine doc for dairy again...

Thanks for the wake-up! Now, where did I put those crock-pots?

### Sheddie — 2018-11-27T03:55:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sheddies-optimizing.19893/post-256673>

Update: Went all summer with no Fibromyalgia symptoms or flares -- first time in two decades! Also, thyroid test values came in at normal range for the first time in 20+ years. Kept off 10 of 13 lbs lost; still would like to drop 20 lbs. Still have The BAB to conquer, and while I apparently don't have SIBO anymore, I would like to see the belly shrink. All in all, energy stays up and I even went on two long trips this year. One place, I met a Black Swan! He didn't look at me but one day perhaps he will? { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### shiran — 2017-07-26T14:12:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ir-a-near-infrared-bulb.19895/post-220116>

I have a regular dry sauna , I want to add IR-A Near Infrared Bulb inside the sauna .
[https://www.rubyluxlights.com/product-page/pre-order-rubylux-nir-a-individual-infrared-bulb](https://www.rubyluxlights.com/product-page/pre-order-rubylux-nir-a-individual-infrared-bulb)
Do you think it is effective? If so
When I'm the day should I use it?
What else do I need to know about a IR-A Near Infrared Bulb and how exactly should I use it in a sauna and inside my house ?
Thanks for the help

### JanSz — 2017-07-26T14:53:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ir-a-near-infrared-bulb.19895/post-220122>

This is really nice question to discuss.

The lamp that you posted, while it is called IR-A it creates lots of heat.
Lots of different frequencies.
So in sauna application it is likely ok.
Saunas work minutes/day.

With discussion on Quantlet and beneficial wavelengths, 850nm stands out.
850nm can also be called IR-A but it is just narrow frequency range (just the one you want).
Its benefit is mostly due to this narrow range.

If lamp creates only 850nm it does not create much heat and uses most of power just for 850nm creation.
It is easier to use that kind of lamp for long hours indoor, it does not raise room temperature, can use DC to power it, which is a plus from pov --->(help pls).

DC power is healthier than AC.

.....

Hi everyone!

I found out about JK about a month ago when listening to tge podcast he did for Ancestral Health Radio. At first it all seemed a little nutty to me, but the ideas kept popping up in my mind. Another reason that fruits and vegetables are becoming less and less important in our diet, and why seafood should be our primary food source. It is no wonder we need to import most of our food from the equator, and consuming high photonic foods in a low photonic environment is the most efficient way to pack on fat.

Perhaps the obesity epidemic is tied to a premature preparatory program in place for humans to adapt to the massive energy losses on earth to combat the famine and cold we will soon face.

# Thread: Peri menopause insomnia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peri-menopause-insomnia.19902/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Doyourcycle — 2017-07-26T23:01:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peri-menopause-insomnia.19902/post-220151>

Any hacks to help balance hormones that impact sleep? My cycles are all over the place at 52 and my sleep is pure shit.

Sun, yes--morning too; great diet, yes, blu blockers, yes, not overweight...

Thoughts--suggestions please!

### drezy — 2017-07-26T23:12:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peri-menopause-insomnia.19902/post-220154>

My wife is near your age and through the other side of menopause.

Morning light seemed to help, but 54-58 degree sessions for 40 minutes 3-5x / week seemed to help her way more. She hated the cold at first, but if I stand in her way to the cold tub these evenings she'll flatten me.

For the 14 years I knew her prior she ALWAYS had shit sleep.

### Doyourcycle — 2017-07-26T23:32:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peri-menopause-insomnia.19902/post-220155>

I've been using cold showers and one full cold bath so far. I'll put one on tonight's menu.

On another note, wondering if low insulin is an issue? No raised glucose level.

### drezy — 2017-07-26T23:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peri-menopause-insomnia.19902/post-220156>

About 1.5 to two weeks into regular cold(54-58) baths my wife started to notice a difference. Not on one cold bath.

### Doyourcycle — 2017-07-27T14:15:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peri-menopause-insomnia.19902/post-220197>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=220156)
>
> About 1.5 to two weeks into regular cold(54-58) baths my wife started to notice a difference. Not on one cold bath.

Well baby steps, ya know.

[https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-8-women-gain-time-menopause/](https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-8-women-gain-time-menopause/)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-27T17:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peri-menopause-insomnia.19902/post-220215>

[https://www.jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-7/](https://www.jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-7/)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-27T17:15:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peri-menopause-insomnia.19902/post-220216>

LADIES: No AM sunlight (UVA/IRA) or TOO MUCH PM blue light = no sulfated vitamin D3 = no estradiol = lowered testosterone = no progesterone = dehydration due to poor mitochondrial flux = no sex drive = no sex = no oxytocin =poor sleep = poor regeneration. You want to regain it? Get some AM sun and block all PM blue light to lower your heteroplasmy rate. So how does sunlight help blood vessels? Well many ways as I have discussed here before (also in Vermont 2017 video) but one way you might not know is via the sex steroid hormone pathways of progesterone. In males, luteinizing hormone (LH) causes the production of testosterone, whereas in females it affects estrogen and progesterone levels........ In females, luteinizing hormone works on the ovaries--where it makes its effects by changing the levels of estradiol. The levels made are tied to the amount or lack of sun one gets. Melatonin lowers estrogen naturally. The more blue light one gets the more estrogen one makes centrally and peripherally and the less progesterone one makes. Sunlight increases both sulfated Vitamin D3 and progesterone in case you did not know this. People should be more aware that progesterone has many important functions: it looks after the smoothness of blood vessels; The link below shows you how these pathways affect are arterial health. Progesterone also affects the myelin sheaths of nerves in men, women and children and it links why MS is tied to a lack of solar exposure in AM and too much blue in the PM; and it prevents water retention in the brain of men, women and children and this affects the amount of CSF that is made and recycled in the brain.

Progesterone receptors are found, among other sites, in the endothelial lining of blood vessels. It’s those receptors that keep endothelial lining nice and smooth. With excess estrogen, the exact opposite happens: the endothelium becomes lumpy and bumpy, therefore excessive estrogen (unbalanced by progesterone) makes us prone to clotting diseases, strokes and angina. Also, the ability of the blood vessels to relax is related to the presence of progesterone receptors and sufficient progesterone level to stimulate them. Insufficient progesterone may affect one way or another the many systems of the body, including blood vessels. Men who do not have sufficient amount of progesterone tend to get cardiovascular diseases in their 40s, whereas women are protected until after their 50s. In other words, progesterone is necessary to maintain cardiovascular health.
Progesterone facilitates healing process after an injury. If you look at my pre operative considerations blog see what I say there about progesterone. Trauma, including that resulting from cerebral accidents, heals quicker in pre-menopausal women than in men because they have more progesterone. Also, they have far less water retention in the brain. For that reason some neurosurgeons (ME) use progesterone injections prior to surgery to prevent swelling due to water retention.
It is made in the brain and stimulates BDNF with appropriate levels of cortisol to get the proper physiologic effects. In women, it is made the corpus luteum of the ovary and in smaller amounts by the adrenal glands. In men, is produced in adrenal and testicular tissue as well. Progesterone is produced in the body from cholesterol and is a precursor to most of the other steroid hormones, including cortisol and testosterone. Look at Hormone 101 blog post or BG 12 as well to explain why prolonged stress further depletes progesterone because it increases the demand for cortisol. I think doctors should consider progesterone supplementation for men in their late forties and older, when they experience low energy or fatigue, decreased libido, have increased body fat, enlarged prostate or to want to help prevent prostate enlargement.Progesterone is needed to counter-balance the effects of excess estrogens. Also, by blocking the enzyme 5a-reductase, progesterone inhibits the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which is also a stimulant of prostate cell growth. This is why sunlight stimulates LH and testosterone as we talked about on this page yesterday. Benefits for men are as follows:

Protects against prostate cancer

\- Helps normalize blood sugar levels

\- Helps use fat for energy

\- Prevents water accumulation (acts as mild diuretic)

\- Helps (normalizes) thyroid hormone function

\- Stimulates new bone formation (osteoporosis protection and even reversal)

\- Improves brain function, has antidepressant properties

\- Improves skin problems including acne, seborrhea, rosacea, psoriasis

\- Diminishes muscular aches and pains, has anti-inflammatory properties

\- Improves sleep pattern

\- Improves libido.

In women and men is also makes hair growth big time. When I hear a lady is losing her hair I know she is deficient in sunlight and thyroid hormone and progesterone due to her blue light toxic world and her burying AM light. I see most often after performing a craniotomy when I use it after we open a head on the incision line.
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26469335](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpubmed%2F26469335&h=ATNY-5AhoNHfxIRSJxFrRhANl2A7TteNrIc4AFU-8FGQzUGFHBg5PXdX_yP0yVZp6j8gsYxr9UhwE_A_gNEBK2N3lIPjqJ8K58CQS1cVPz0YktCOrWIZwfYLj4zIyf6lQo5EipJMeUHRezK1FC2EIdl4cyTQ&enc=AZMU1CjruocSqhyM0slm3GhRuqgpxez50MjYSRUoUwBzG8-Ily5C1QPU6_M4RJDJrDp4t9VnJdh2Cbn9FWJRF9ESh4aT1IpCteYyMg4tpXXnwAmOKou-QcXQjDIJurDn8a7rvYcOc1wZvPoiZMo4P_Im-QxUxSiiainhXfet-2fS9Un5te78XGY_6knfK_EGhqse_0lkM4RwpyylbonlyIgz&s=1)

# Thread: 1950s : Pituitary removal to "cure" certain cancers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1950s-pituitary-removal-to-cure-certain-cancers.19906/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2017-07-27T16:53:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1950s-pituitary-removal-to-cure-certain-cancers.19906/post-220211>

I was only looking for a translation of "Turkish saddle" (sella turcica)for some Hollwich papers I am translating and came across this interesting article. If anyone is interested a rough translation below .

[http://www.spiegel.de/spiegel/print/d-28957339.html](http://www.spiegel.de/spiegel/print/d-28957339.html)

09/01/1954
**CANCER / MEDICINE Operation in the Turkish saddle**

Gently, Professor Herbert Olivecrona lifted a piece of brain-shell from the patient's forehead, pushed back the frontal lobe of the brain, felt with a probe on the underside of the brain-shell, pushed the right optic nerve aside, and cut the fibers of the soft brain where the pituitary gland is embedded.
After the surgeon had approached the organ, which was only a cherry-pip size, he would penetrate the tissue of the hard scalp, which seals the gland in a bony cavity of the cranial base. With a spoon-like instrument and surgical forceps, he then removed piece by piece the tiny chemical miracle lab, from which most of the crucial life processes are controlled and supervised from birth to death.

This surgical expedition into the caves of the human brain took place three years ago in the Stockholm Serafimer-Lasarettet. It was the first time a surgeon had dared to remove the vital pituitary gland (pituitary gland).

The operation, called pituitary sect, was at the same time one of the most daring attacks which a surgeon used to fight against the cancer. For the Swedish physician is convinced that a link between the hypophysis and the proliferating cancer cells must exist

Normally, the pituitary gland works as a reliable control organ that fine-tunes the many complicated life functions via hormones. The hormones, body-borne active substances, which are poured directly into the blood or lymph from the pituitary or other hormonal glands in the body, control the processes of metabolism, growth and reproduction. The self-hormone-producing pituitary gland not only directs the other hormone-producing glands. It is also closely linked to the cerebrum, which is regarded as the central nervous system of the entire vegetative nervous system. It is thus the important bridge between the hormone and the nervous system.

The endocrinologists, specialists in hormonal research, have discovered as many as nine different hormones produced by the busy brain pituitary gland. In the case of a damage or disease of the tiny cells in the pituitary gland, the strangest effects occur. An overproduction of the growth hormone, for example, can produce two-and-a-half-meter giants and an underproduction dwarves. Disorders of the hormones of the germ-glands can aggravate a man with female forms or make him grow a 400-pound fat pad.

**The more the physicians studied** the phrenic influence, the **more** urgently the question arose: Is there no connection between the hypophysis and the cancer?
In the thirties, the pathologist Dr. Leo Loeb at the Washington University in St. Louis experimented with test animals, in which he artificially provoked breast cancer by overdosing pituitary hormone. Further experiments by other researchers have shown that in animals the cancer can be increased or inhibited by increasing or diminishing the intake of pituitary hormone.
Cancer is a proliferating growth of body cells: Suddenly some body cells become wild, divide without law and break out of the sensible order of the organism. They destroy the surrounding tissue, continue to spread over the blood and lymph system and form new rebel colonies of malignant cannibal cells.
To date, no one knows _how_ healthy cells become cancer cells. But many researchers believe that a chemical disorder within cells destroys the finely balanced equilibrium. Prof. Olivecrona and his co-worker Dr. Luft: As the pituitary is the most important regulator of the chemical balance in the organism, it would be only logical,that it is involved in the cancer development process.
Recent investigations also reject the assumption that the cancer cells are completely autonomous structures in the body. In order to proliferate, they need certain chemicals and hormones, which are supplied to them through the blood stream. We must therefore be able to starve the rebellious cancer cells, the two Swedes said when the pituitary was removed and the hormone was blocked.

This theoretical consideration presented a difficult problem: How to keep the patient alive while the cancer is famished? Sections of experimental animals and deaths proved that destruction of the pituitary gland leads to collapse and degenerative symptoms of body organs.
Now Dr. Luft: The failure of the gonad glands after removal of the hypophysis is safe. The missing thyroid hormone can be replaced by thyroid extract. Only one remained problematic. Without pituitary gland the adrenal cortex could not produce its vital hormone. There was not yet a replacement for this hormone.
The dilemma was not solved until spring 1949. At that time, the Mayo Clinic in Rochester (USA) reported that it succeeded in synthesizing one of the most important adrenal cortical hormones (the cortisone).

The discovery encouraged Dr. Luft and Dr. Olivecrona early 1951 to dare the surgery to disengage the pituitary gland. Although an internationally recognized brain surgeon, Dr Olivecrona was not new to his pituitary gland. His scalpel had already often cut off the morbid parts of the gland. The pituitary gland itself is, however, largely protected from accesses in a bone cavity, the so-called "Turk's saddle". In addition, a thin strand of 50,000 nerve fibers connects the gland to the brain. In order to be able to separate this cord, the surgeon must work through a tangle of important nerve and blood vessels. He must do millimeter work to avoid accidentally cutting the optic nerve (which would cause blindness) or piercing a main vein (with the operation field being flooded by blood).

Extremely difficult is the last stage, the advance into the "Türkensattel". Without sight the surgeon must also remove the last traces of the gland from the walls of her protective cage. Even if only a few scraps of active pituitary tissue remained, the success of the operation would be questioned.
To this day the two Swedish doctors have removed the brain pituitary glands in 43 patients. Eighteen of their pituitary-less patients are still alive.

One of the patients, a farmer's wife in her mid forties, suffered from a malignant tumor which covered her whole chest from the neck to the navel as a raw wound. Almost 72 hours after hypophysis removal, the pain had almost disappeared. Within a month, Dr. Olivecrona reported, small islands of healthy skin tissue formed in the destroyed area, and reliable signs indicated that the cancer was stopped.

Thereupon, in New York, Baltimore, Paris, and Havana, brain surgeons ventured to the surgical removal of the pituitary gland. Every intervention seemed to prove again that it actually plays a role in cancer growth. But in what way it is doing this, still remains a mystery. Somatotropin, the growth hormone of the pituitary gland, however, was under intense suspicion.

**They then experimented** with Stanford University and the University of California with pure growth hormone. Results of animal experiments: Large doses of pure growth hormone, as produced by an overactive pituitary gland, produced numerous malignant tumors in rats.
This was a step further, but it had not yet been determined whether cancerous growth is attributable solely to the somatotropin of the hypophysis, or whether the other hormonal glands, which are in a constant interplay with it, are also involved.

This question could only be answered by the operation of cancer victims who had already been removed from the adrenal cortex and gonad glands.
Recently, the pituitary gland has now been removed in eight patients, who no longer had gonad glands and adrenal glands at the New York Sloan-Kettering Institute for Cancer Researc. The doctors had tried everything they could to stop the cancer: a radiation therapy, hormonal treatment, a decrease in the breast, removal of the gonad glands and the adrenal glands, in the case of a female patient whose breast cancer had jumped to the vertebral column. All measures, however, only brought about a temporary improvement. Last August, the woman lay in agony, unable to get up from the bed. She quickly lost weight, her blood was permeated with crusty bone marrow.
Then the hypophysise was performed. The patient gained 22 pounds in the shortest possible time, the pain almost completely ceased, the blood anemia disappeared.

Thus, the doctors of the Sloan-Kettering Institute seem to have proved that the pituitary gland has a direct influence on the cancer through its growth hormone. The German doctors want to discuss the new research results this week at the Therapeutic Congress in Karlsruhe.
So far, however, it is still unknown why the small cherry nucleus-sized organ affects only certain types of cancer - such as cancer on the breast, prostate, uterus - but not cancerous tissues. The chemical relationships between growth hormone and cancer are still unclear.
Dr. Luft and Dr. Olivecrona warned again and again about an overestimation of the successes so far. To this day, there is as yet no proof that the removal of the hypophysis will bring _definite_ healing.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-27T17:19:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-and-pregnancy.19907/post-220218>

Dr. Kruse was asked: "Aside from the obvious (no sex drive) could Vitamin D deficiency make it harder to get pregnant?" I've been Vitamin D deficient and I cannot get pregnant even on clomid. My husband also is vitamin D deficient.

Answer: Very much so. No sex drive is nature trying to tell you not to have a kid. Why? Your germ cell line has a high heteroplasmy rate. The European Journal of Endocrinology had an article in 2102:

Among women, vitamin D appears to impact in vitro fertilization (IVF) outcomes, endometriosis, polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), the most common female endocrine disorder, as well as boost levels of progesterone and estrogen, which regulate menstrual cycles and improve the likelihood of successful conception
In men, vitamin D is essential for the healthy development of the nucleus of the sperm cell, and helps maintain semen quality and sperm count. Vitamin D also increases levels of testosterone, which may boost libido
The researchers state:

“Given the high prevalence of infertility as well as vitamin D insufficiency in otherwise healthy young women and men and the possible role of vitamin D in human reproduction, research might lead to new therapeutic approaches such as vitamin D supplementation in the treatment of female and male reproductive disorders.”

In 2008, Australian fertility specialist Dr. Anne Clark found almost one-third of the 800 infertile men included in her study had lower than normal levels of vitamin D, (bear in mind here that "normal" does not equal "optimal" -- so by optimal standards, the rate of vitamin D deficiency was likely far higher than one-third) stating that:

"Vitamin D and folate deficiency are known to be associated with infertility in women, but the outcomes of the screening among men in our study group came as a complete surprise. Men in the study group who agreed to make lifestyle changes and take dietary supplements had surprisingly good fertility outcomes."

In fact, of the 100 men who agreed to make and maintain certain lifestyle changes (quitting smoking, minimizing intake of caffeine and alcohol, weight reduction, along with a course of vitamins and antioxidants) for three months prior to fertility treatment, 11 of them went on to achieve pregnancy naturally, without IVF treatment.

Previous studies, such as one published in The Journal of Nutrition, also found that although vitamin-D-deficient female rats were capable of reproduction, it reduced fertility by an astounding 75 percent, diminished litter sizes by 30 percent, and impaired neonatal growth.

Interestingly, another study published in November 2009 confirmed that human sperm does in fact have a vitamin D receptor. Analysis indicated that vitamin D is produced locally in the sperm, which suggests that vitamin D may be involved in the signaling between cells in the reproductive system. According to the authors, the study revealed "an unexpected significance of this hormone \[vitamin D\] in the acquisition of fertilizing ability," and the results imply that vitamin D is involved in a variety of sperm signaling pathways. CITES: [http://jn.nutrition.org/content/110/8/1573.full.pdf](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fjn.nutrition.org%2Fcontent%2F110%2F8%2F1573.full.pdf&h=ATPlchXbakKFA7wAE1qm8ITpvqD1svixshJWPVKe6Tc6jlfoewqHcqfkVp2JqonO6ygwS6GJH-gOOa8gnABdJO10KVYhWesWGr1dcbFtV8IgepEK9aPr6CdD3fQQ6-LMtRXDEDBM8S-NdCVt)

When you first start on a ‘wellness journey’ you chasing down health issues one by one with this supplement - that protocol etc. With wisdom you learned that if you change the conditions of existence, this releases the doctor within and most of the individual problems or symptoms vanish.

Last point:
When man began to internalize and ultimately subject the evolutionary process to human control his demise began 700,000 years ago when he invented clothing and blocked the morning sun from his skin. It steepened when we began to cover his eyes with glass and plastic for fashion and vision.

Clothes block AM sunlight (UV AND IR) to the skin and send the signal of winter year around. While carbs out of season, indoor heating, blue light, nnEMF send the signal of perpetual summer & daytime. Our ability to manufacture a comfortable environment is driving neolithic biological chaos. The mind can't distinguish comfort/convenience from safety and has created a hell for the body.
Clothing mandates that we begin to use seafood to cover our losses of the sun's power because of DHA's unique pi electron cloud. Food guru's often forget that solar light only interacts with electrons to excite them. Who's law dictates this fact? Einstein's photoelectric effect. In fact, that is the only reason the paleo diet works for some. Grass fed meat contain DHA from the grass. The marine seafood chain has exponetially more DHA. The USDA data base shows oysters are a better source per unit weight thangrass fed liver. That is how powerful seafood is in reversing disease to a mitochondriac.

Humans are believed to have left Africa using water ways, so to stroll away from them now is a grave error. The Paleo food fallacy: The supply of plant based and animal based food sources vanish in the winter months but our ANCESTORS COULD FISH OR HARVEST shellfish year round. In the skin and eye balls of these creates lies hidden the sunlight we are missing from blocking the solar panel of the sun with clothing. We seem to forget this still happens in the Arctic even today. Ice fishing is way more successful than finding a caribou in January- March in the Arctic. Now you know why, because it is a mariner's way of sun harvesting, when the sun is hiding its UV-A on excited electrons that make up the flesh of seafood!!!!!!** DR KRUSE MAY 28TH 2017

Hello, there. Believe it or not, I'm stronger than I was a couple of years ago. I took my first "post-COVID" trip to Costa Rica a couple of years ago--it was horrible. The plane ride wiped me out, and I ended up having something similar to a panic attack at the Costa Rica airport. Costa Rica got me closer to the equator, but I was in no shape to travel--I had been on lockdown for so long, I didn't realize how bad I was until I stepped outside the comfort zone of my home. I wear blue light blockers. I take walks and work outside every day. I'm trying the nicotine patches, and I also have methylene blue troches. All I can say is I'm trying to get better. I have a TBI history, then on top of that Long COVID is a TBI, in and of itself. My body has already been through something similar to Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (CFS). I'd rather feel bad physically than to be without a healthy brain, which I believe is what's causing all of my problems. I've been accused of doing too much, but I'm just desperate to get better. I've been on the wrong diagnosis path for so long, so my condition has festered and become chronic. I don't know what to do at this point. I'll never give up on myself, but I'm starting to think about the possibility of my brain deteriorating to the point that I can't work. A year ago, I asked for reasonable accommodations from my job since I was having a tough time from mental health medication withdrawals. Suffice it to say that my job showed me no love, and now I believe I know why. Everyone is working remotely these days. I don't have any direct contact with my co-workers, but I believe I'm probably not the only one suffering mentally and/or physically from Long COVID. I want to work. I've been doing nothing but pushing myself for the past four years. My battery isn't dead--not yet anyways.

### drezy — 2017-07-27T19:29:40+0300

### Samy Colddigger — 2018-11-09T22:46:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/babys-vaginas-and-the-microbiome.19909/post-255357>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=220234)
>
> An infant gets its innoculation my mum's vagina and bottom as they enter the world.........so what happens if they don't?
>
> A lot and it is not good.
>
> What happens if Mom puts wired toys around that baby and gives it a cell phone at 6 or an ipad to learn???
>
> ENVIRONMENT REALITY: This paper just proves my very controversial point. Environments drive mitochondrial energy flux and lead to disease. You cannot get well in the same environment you got ill within. It’s a stark and unpleasant message that most people don't want to ponder. In fact, as soon as Verizon/ATT/GOOGLE/MSFT upgrades their network in your area they do not need to notify anyone. As soon as the TV station install 5 G doppler radar you don’t know, but your mitochondria will. Soon you’ll be sick with a mitochondrial disease that showed up out of know where. The light they put around you is slowed down in you in different ways than sunlight is and creates a cornucopia of new free radicals that turn molecules in you into something new that cannot work in your cells. That is why bent proteins are see in brain diseases. As soon as your new neighbor moves in and is an electrical engineer or DJ and adds 12 routers from Comcast to your living area he does not tell you. But guess what..........the alien light is there affecting your mitochondria, which create that cadre of free radicals, whether you realize it or not. Quantum reality of the modern world 101 because changes really are an N =1 game. A RCT is really of little value in a mitochondrial driven world and few have woken up to that very counterintuitive reality. [https://medicalxpress.com/news/2017-07-gut-bacteria-trigger-immune-responses.html](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2017-07-gut-bacteria-trigger-immune-responses.html)

To what extent is this intergenerational? I mean the effects of non vaginal birth? I am asking because my partner was born via C-section......but in last 5 years (shes 28) we have really fixed things up.......last March she gave birth to a healthy girl in hospital via Vaginal birth. Baby popped out after a full day of sunning the belly in the spring sun. This was also proceeded by trips to the equator/Yucatan pre conception. So mums light environment was likely better than Granny's. An perhaps her microbiome changed somewhat with all the trips south/more selective eating. BUT is there anything obvious we could do for baby's gut other than watching what mum eats, avoid nnEMF and circadian mismatch? And getting plenty of sun for mum? Ps were are in Yucatan and baby loves it here.....shes naked all day and started elimination cummmincation  Seems her bowels dont have regular schedule though.....she just kinda poos whenever. Is this perhaps some sort of mismatch or is it normal for babies bowels to lack any sort of obvious sched?

### Rela — 2017-07-27T22:24:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brushing-teeth-toddler.19910/post-220243>

When should we start brushing baby's teeth. My daughter is 19 months and has 11 teeth.... She had her first tooth at 13 months... that was little late.

Instinctively I don't feel like doing it as of now( might be wrong).

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-27T23:30:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brushing-teeth-toddler.19910/post-220249>

### Rela — 2017-07-28T22:09:25+0300

### drezy — 2017-07-28T23:22:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brushing-teeth-toddler.19910/post-220332>

1 c baking soda
2 T fine sea or himalayan salt
14-20 drops of peppermint essential oil
2T dry xylitol

The xylitol is optional but balances out the saltiness and we do not really ingest it
We've also made orange, clove, and a manly black spruce version that made me feel like I was breathing forest all day.

Some of the folks from India that I've worked with were fans of neem oil I think for this purpose BTW.

### Penny — 2017-07-28T23:42:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brushing-teeth-toddler.19910/post-220336>

Peppermint oil will kill anything... neem smells disgusting - but I do spray the neem on my plants/trees for aphids -

### Rela — 2017-07-29T00:09:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brushing-teeth-toddler.19910/post-220338>

The xylitol is optional but balances out the saltiness and we do not really ingest it
We've also made orange, clove, and a manly black spruce version that made me feel like I was breathing forest all day.

Some of the folks from India that I've worked with were fans of neem oil I think for this purpose BTW.\[/quote\]

My grandparents used to clean their teeth with small neem twigs.... I have done that too.... definitely feels refreshing afterwards

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-29T02:12:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brushing-teeth-toddler.19910/post-220342>

You finger wont damage gingiva or hurt teeth......a brush does.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-29T13:12:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brushing-teeth-toddler.19910/post-220357>

I'm just back from the dentist. I need two fillings  They tried to get me to fill in my patient detail forms on a tablet ------ refused!

She told me she'd like to give me a couple of x-rays to see what was going on in my back teeth ------ refused!

I love being so damn assertive

I'm interested in brushing with my finger!! I think I've sensitized my teeth by over-brushing.

# Thread: Post Menopausal Woman

**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 44
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kvond — 2017-07-28T02:26:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220255>

Hi Folks,
Jack says in CT#7 that women who are post menopause need progesterone to keep their weight down and a host of other benefits such as Prolactin-autophagy cycle. Anyone try this?

I've recently lost 30lbs by adding CT via cold showers, and the Wim Hof breathing method. My thinking is I'll use progesterone cream and continue this additive process.

Thoughts?

### drezy — 2017-07-28T03:00:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220256>

Perhaps, I'm not the main audience but 30 lbs is a big deal. Bravo.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-28T08:25:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220269>

WOW! That's incredible! What's your CT protocol? Out of interest,

### caroline — 2017-07-28T12:31:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220283>

> [kvond said:](/goto/post?id=220255)
>
> Hi Folks,
> Jack says in CT#7 that women who are post menopause need progesterone to keep their weight down and a host of other benefits such as Prolactin-autophagy cycle. Anyone try this?
>
> I've recently lost 30lbs by adding CT via cold showers, and the Wim Hof breathing method. My thinking is I'll use progesterone cream and continue this additive process.
>
> Thoughts?

you have made me re think what I am doing - which is pretty much nothing right now. I had stopped my progesterone thinking I really didn't need it as I am getting so much outdoor time.

Lately - I really can't shift some weight I have gained and my sleep can be iffy at times. Obviously cold showers and lots of sun and grounding may not be quite enuf for a post menopausal woman.

I am also finding that too much fat in my diet is going directly to my tummy and my weight is going up.

I struggle with what to eat these days .... I don't want too much protein either. I definitely ate too much fat this summer - huge mistake.

what are you eating Kvond?

I have been thinking of trying a collagen shake with a bunch of good stuff in it ...like some greens and eggs and numeric etc.
I would have some protein around 10:00am and then dinner around 4:00pm which is when I naturally want to eat anyway.

I do think that IF is the way to go - for me.

It would be great to hear others thoughts....... Lots of ladies here have been struggling to get this right.

and the biggie ...... reduce nnEMF!!!!

### caroline — 2017-07-28T12:32:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220284>

I gotta re read C/T#7 .... it's been too long...

### kvond — 2017-07-28T16:08:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220297>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=220269)
>
> WOW! That's incredible! What's your CT protocol? Out of interest,

My CT is fairly lame - I step into a cold shower in the morning for about 2 min. It's warm here right now, so I hope to do better in the winter. I think what made the difference is adding the Wim How breathing while I'm out in the sunrise.

I do need to up my CT, I'm having significant cervical degeneration and facing another fusion - don't know what gains can make but I want to try.

### kvond — 2017-07-28T16:14:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220298>

As to food - if I get the sunlight - I notice I'm not as hungry and can get used to less pretty comfortably. Right now I have a bone broth protein shake with ketones around 8:30 in the morning, a small cup of coffee mid day to keep me alert - then try to hold off 'til dinner which is meat, greens and some fats. I want to IF but my adrenals are fairly shot still and I notice this begins to really tax me.

Recently I gained a few pounds back because I was comforting myself with carbs over the neck issue. Time to get back to mostly keto.

I have been able to hack my migraines - this is a huge jump for me - I attribute this to all of the above and Byronia 30c every day for about a week took care of my morning headaches that used to flair - plus the limited diet. I know a coupdl good homeopaths if you want to add that route for any reason.

I don't imagine any medicine of any kind can be as powerful as the whole body effects of CT, Sun and exercise. I do walk each day while at work and sometimes take work outside and sit in the sun.

Finally got my goggles for the work computer to combat the nEMF - working on that piece too.

### fitness@home — 2017-07-29T15:08:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220362>

" I have been thinking of trying a collagen shake with a bunch of good stuff in it ...like some greens and eggs and numeric etc. "

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) - I have been incorporating collagen daily and drinking a salad every morning. I find it easier to get down some veggies that way. Avocado, romaine lettuce, juice of a lime, mint, and a handful of spinach (ala Gundry). Sometimes I even add a TBL of olive oil.

Menopause sometimes sucks. No longer have flashes but the weight thing is harder to fight these days. Deal with the same 12-15 lbs over and over. Time for the annual Genova Complete Hormone test and visit to my BHRT doc soon. All things considered though, menopause is just another natural stage of life. I won't give up my BHRT anytime soon though

### fitness@home — 2017-07-29T18:28:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220379>

I have used Haidut's progesterone in the past, but don't see it on the website anymore. Currently using a compounded version by local pharmacy.

I am not eating 100% Gundry style, but have made changes. That breakfast salad is a favorite. Are you in any of the Plant Paradox Facebook groups? I do know some women that are really liking the lectin avoidance. @[endless](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/160/)

Picture is Haidut's progesterone

### Sheddie — 2017-07-29T20:39:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220384>

The minute I saw the drinkable salad ingredients I knew I could do that in my blender. Because of reducing lectins I'm limited in what to pick for my SLO-food deliveries (Fresh Harvest). Lots of lettuces for me right now. (I'm loving Inger's raw fish dish, here on Forum. Lots of Ceviche recipes online if I want to change up the idea but she'll always be remembered as my Initiation! You, too, for the idea of 'drinking' salad!!)

Everything I'm learning right now is new, really new, after 5 years of going blind from cataracts with cornea-scar complications, the last 2 being more than 'legally blind.' Hardly any computer use for learning during that period. It's like I've 'been away' for an extended time but no, I just dropped out! So glad to be back, despite UV-blocking IOLs. I picked up on Gundry and Dr. Jack Kruse only recently. Was grateful to Ray Peat's work for a long time, probably since the early 90s. Missed Haidut so will bone up -- if there's time over & above the present Kruse course of learning!

At first, any change seems to take enormous amounts of time, and then, it's on automatic pilot and I don't even think about Time... Later, too, for adding any more forums! (This is only my second online forum and I don't know a lot of how they can be used with sophistication... )

Thanx for the picture -- I like DMSO a lot for skin apps; short term, though, for the body odor side effect! Hope for you a compounding pharmacy isn't using synthetic 'compounds' or carriers in the formula? Like so many drug delivery forms, the additives/adjuvants are sometimes the culprits in unsatisfactory individual results.

Awhile I go I was having 2 BPC over a morning with 1 tbsp collagen, 1 tbsp MCT oil and cacao and 1 tbsp hemp seeds. I had one about 10am and another at 12:00pm ....and then dinner of seafood around 4:30. I wound up gaining 3K - and was pretty horrified. I was probably reacting to the hemp seeds. ### fitness@home — 2017-07-30T06:33:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-220408>

I use a scoop of Great Lakes collagen in my morning cup of coffee. It dissolves well and I don't even know it is there.

My protein intake is low to moderate right now. The olive oil adds some good fat to my diet plus I love the taste. I found some lemon infused OO & orange infused OO that is amazing. My local store was clearing this item out of their stock at $2 a bottle (I bought ten).

P.s. I bet the farmed salmon was fed grains, etc. Gundry recommends wild caught seafood, pasture raised poultry and eggs, grass fed beef, etc. Hard to find meats and eggs that grains werent part of the process unless you raise them yourself or have access to a good farmers market.

> I would have some protein around 10:00am and then dinner around 4:00pm which is when I naturally want to eat anyway.
>
> I do think that IF is the way to go - for me.
>
> It would be great to hear others thoughts....... Lots of ladies here have been struggling to get this right.
>
> and the biggie ...... reduce nnEMF!!!!

Try Penny's morning coffee shake... this will last you 8 hours - wrong time of year for it - obviously - but if you want to intermittent fast, this is the way to go:
8-16 oz coffee
1/2 can coconut cream
5 Brazil nuts
10 almonds
10 walnuts
2 oz pumpkin seeds
1 oz sunflower seeds
1 tsp turmeric
some black pepper (to make the turmeric systemic - gut issues, you might not want to do this)
3 eggs (omega 3 flax fed ones are great)
cardamom (helps your P450 enzymes clear the coffee)
cinnamon (blood sugar)
1 tsp cocoa powder (polyphenols)
ginger powder
1 tsp glycine (helps your gut lining, helps make glutathione, helps if you've ingested lots of glyphosate and it tastes just like sugar)
some methylene blue - whatever dose you are currently doing
1 tray of ice cubes
Blast it all in a blender and down the hatch -
Then, start drinking ice water and doing face dunks and getting some morning sunshine - truly, you will have energy and not be hungry -

If you're close to the beach, jump in - best energizer there is - I've found though it is better to eat prior to jumping in - and drinking a lot of water - in fact, whenever I'm tired, I think "I'm dehydrated" it is amazing what water does for you - you can also try putting some baking soda/potassium bicarbonate (don't go nuts with this - 2 grams tops at one time) in the water - also, if you're doing fat, adding salt will help your mitochondria burn the fat as will the cold (even a face dunk)

### drezy — 2017-09-03T18:11:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-222802>

> [Debntx said:](/goto/post?id=222797)
>
> wow, this kind of explains something for me - the bag of chips things - a couple months ago i went all out on chips and noticed hip pains - i thought wthey- hard to go down stairs smoothly, get up from work chair etc... then stopped chips - miraculously pain went away not immediately but pretty dang quick - i know each person is individual but definitely chip (lays in particular ) have this affect on me

I agree with doc on the following two statements:

> It's not about food

> When you've got good redox you can eat shit on a shingle

The conclusion that I draw from this is that if I eat junk and subsequently inflame then my redox is faltering and it's like a test.

My current understanding is that when your redox is low the stomach lining (those cells that are typically most rapidly replaced) runs on senescent cells. This is what some call leaky gut.

Wild Part: When those lectins and bacteria and viruses slip on through your "leaky gut" all of your mitochondrial colonies assume that the environment is kicking your butt and that maybe they will borrow bits of exogenous viruses and bacteria (horizontal gene transfer) since they "evolve" to the environment faster. You mitochondria want to get the "answer" to the environment and will cheat so the you can pass these "answers" to the next generation. Not all "answers" passed on are considered desirable.

Related threads:
Huck and Jan are digging into Doc Gundry's work over here you might want to jump in: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-and-aortic-stenosis.7685/page-20#post-222245](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/huck-my-journey-bp-and-aortic-stenosis.7685/page-20#post-222245)

I started way back on a similar-ish blood type into Paleo into kept diet 12+ years ago. For the last 8 I've been almost eli-paleo since a mid to mid-high amount of my calories came from gulf seafood. I think that patched my gut and by dumb luck put me pretty close to epic-paleo mode.

Caveat:
I've only seen his interviews and have not read his book, but I suspect Gundry might have saved me some time on the way toward more optimal.

My N=1:
Potatoes,lentils,gluten = ouch when I'm not redoxing really well.

Bottom line:
It's not about food. All the above may help you frame it but if you stay fixated on food you run the risk of not realizing that your gut is a redox/environment indicator. If I hadn't made that realization I'd have been all \*almost\* eli-paleo and had maybe 35 extra lbs clinging to my saddlebag area. Even at 6'4" that starts to show up and I need to keep beach body going. That's my priority #1. A far-far second is making a difference in other's lives.

Jan,the point is though that you cannot eat anything to reverse the problem with very few exceptions. Only Dr Kruse's detractors maintain that he is advocating that you can eat a "Skittles and marshmallow diet". Dietary interventions are a way to freeze time for a duration, depending on your heteroplasmy that time can be short indeed. He said this from the beginning, has reiterated it in the Vermont 2017 talk. You can maybe arrest an illness but reversing the haplotype dependent progression as laid out by Dr Wallace needs to come from outside of this circle. As the workings of nutrients are hormone/enzyme etc dependent,what hope can you have that you can rebalance the system when circadian control is lost. Our modern bodies in essence look like the picture below if you imagine every clock showing the time for a physiological process to occur. Woe betide the nutrient/mineral or whatnot that needs to find its way around that maze and encounter the right binding site at the correct time within the correct sequence of events...And by the time you have reached menopause which is the subject of this post you have a lot of clocks that show the time but on different planets ....

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-03T20:08:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-222811>

Why are women from Venus and men from Mars with respect to light? Day and night offers changes in thickness and transparency for women. Your skin, tissues, blood plasma, collagen and cell water are all biologic semiconductors When light travels from a denser, higher refractive index medium to a less dense one, it similarly will be reflected if the the angle relative to vertical is greater than a critical angle. This phenomenon, known as total internal reflection, imposes significant restriction on the efficiency with which emitted LED's can transmit light into the air. This is why lady semiconductors are from Venus and not Mars.

### Sandra Barton — 2019-04-07T18:01:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-menopausal-woman.19911/post-267545>

Excellent article! [https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fnagi.2018.00124/full](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fnagi.2018.00124/full) but most of you know all this already! Having sex and orgasim is the best way to boost your hormones naturally. Best naked on the beach in the sun. IF and increased mitophagy is essential for pretty much everything. I find methylene blue and nicotine help me for all the reasons they are known for. I block blue light, get cold exposure, exercise regularly. I’m 61, 5’5’ 112 lbs. I consume at least 1500 to 3000 calories a day and have difficulty keeping weight on. Seasonal eating loads of shellfish and fish, I chop and haul wood for fun, am a mountain bike fanatic, Kayaking and never miss an opportunity to jump in the water no matter what. I am a firm believer in if you don’t use it you loose it. You can’t stop aging but you can slow it down. I brought myself back from a traumatic brain injury after being struck from behind while riding my road bike the van was traveling 70 miles an hour. I do believe I reversed the aging process this accident took from me. The big turn around moment was when I started fasting because of Autophagy/mitophagy. I find a 48 hour fast once a month works for me and cycling on other days.

### Sheddie — 2017-07-31T14:28:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minerals-electromagnetism-light-the-genetic-code-and-its-associated-minerals-and-polarities.19912/post-220505>

>

>
