[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6#post-220124g](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6#post-220124g)
\=========================================

Living in NY and some other states have a drawbacks (or possibly it is helpful).
I have similar situation in NJ.
"Normally" tests done by Spectracell laboratory can be purchased over internet, cash only.
But if script is written, those tests are paid (partially) by my insurance (Medicare).
Often, the problem is on how to find doctor who deals with this.
I solved that for my self (and posted) here:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6#post-220066](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6#post-220066)
\---------------------
If you want to follow my track:

#1, find a place near you that draws blood for this tests (and have necessary kits).
here:
[https://www.spectracell.com/patients/find-a-drawsite/](https://www.spectracell.com/patients/find-a-drawsite/)

#2, go there, find names of doctors who write scrips for those tests

#3, from that list pick a name that accepts your insurance

............

### caroline — 2017-08-12T03:16:42+0300

Karen - please remember that everyone's n=1 is different. The best Doctor is the one in your head. Trust your instincts!

### karenr — 2017-08-12T03:55:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-221495>

Thanks Caroline! This work is so exciting for me..partly because the quantum mindset is so right up my alley, and the learning curve is so challenging!.. But I've always had very different views from almost everyone I've known personally, and have always done my n=1 even when I had little support and just felt utterly lonely in following my own instincts.

I'm sure I never would have survived if I'd followed my docs' prescriptions all these years.. but then I wouldn't have been who I am. For all my health problems, somehow I managed to make it through life with some critical thinking intact! The learning process has to be an organic cycle.. or maybe spiral.. my instincts lead me to investigate principles that resonate and develop my own instincts. And in the process, I feel a real connection with others doing the same thing, so it's never really lonely work anymore.

In this community, I'm absorbing so much that sparks my own insight - it feels like the information has to be metabolized, embodied... sounds awkward the way I'm describing it, but that's sort of my process that is very different from just absorbing information. I don't subscribe to any groups based on fixed belief systems - the reason I'm here is because there is no dogma to swallow whole, but real transformative work being done here, and it's nothing short of exhilarating to me.

I guess I needed to articulate that .

### Sheddie — 2017-08-12T18:14:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-221524>

> [karenr said:](/goto/post?id=220466)
>
> Hi folks,
>
> I'm Karen from upstate NY, excited to be in the company of such terrific critical thinkers, feeling very inspired.
> ^^^^^
> Some basic info:
> Age 64. Grew up on Long Island, NY, now way upstate, went to school for classical piano, my lifelong love. Taught for many years, haven't physically been able to play for many years but **music** is such an inner experience that I access all the time, my saving grace.
>
> Eastern European ancestry, **Ashkenazy** Jewish, mother's side from Austria, father's from Russia.
> Family health history is below.
> (Interestingly, I seem to take after my father much more than my mother)
> Currently I'm in an apartment bldg in outskirts of town, 2nd floor, NW exposure, in visual distance of a cell tower, yikes! My devices pick up a dozen wifi networks .
> ^^^^^
> Also I'm of the quantum mindset, having been a homeopathic practitioner, having read Robert Becker back in the 80s, etc., and am very plugged in to nonduality spiritual teachings that see consciousness as primary...
> ^^^^^
> So I'm chronically short of breath and can't walk more than 10 yards without getting winded, and the general fatigue compounds the distress.
> ^^^^^
> Eyes: astigmatism. Very photosensitive, and can't tolerate much sun generally due to fatigue.
>
> My goal is to get to a remote environment, soak up the peaceful silence and healing energy fields. I would first need my energy switch turned back on, in order to make that happen. Seems impossible but I don't believe anything is impossible.
> **FIRST goal is to take a walk** without feeling horribly exhausted. To enjoy the outdoors, to enjoy life.
> ^^^^^
> Medical diagnoses:
> Failure to thrive in adult, cachexia (currently 90 lbs, 5 ft tall)
> **Osteoporosis**, thoracic kyphosis (waiting for MRI results)
> Chronic migraine
> Silent gallstones
> Dysautonomia
> Cardiomyopathy with lowered LV function
> **CFS/fibromyalgia**
> Insomnia
>
> Meds:
> Small doses of Ambien, Tylenol, microdoses of medical cannabis. **Can't tolerate most meds (blessing and curse both!)**
>
> Current treatments:
> Weekly acupuncture and LLLT with this device he uses: [http://www.multiradiance.com/products/mr4activcordless](http://www.multiradiance.com/products/mr4activcordless)
> IR lamp from Redlightman, 5-10 mins/day
> EMF hacks as described above
> Supplements: Standard Process, Transdermal Mg, Krill oil, some Chinese herbs from acupuncturist
>
> Other practices: nothing else to do but meditate. My apartment is my meditation "cave," a built-in retreat center. Now I need the retreat center to be in a beautiful natural environment!
> Buteyko breathing.
>
> Recent labs:
> Spinal X-ray: anterior wedging of T7 and T8
> Thoracic kyphosis
> Bones diffusely demineralized
> BUN:creatinine ratio - 25
> ANA titer - 80, speckled
> CRP = 20
> **Vit D = 33**

Hello Karen,

I must say, your Journal bio inspires me greatly. And, your thoughtful, careful processing steps as you digest all this new material with old conditions and take action. Thank you so much for coming here. Today, I am trying to respond to aspects of your history. (These aspects I bolded and lifted from your text. Hope it comes out in the above when I push the button!?)

A few things we share in details: I'm 49.7% Ashkenazy (paternal) with, to the best of my skimpy knowledge, lineage arrival in the US from Belarus in the late 1880s or 1890s; I'm long-term auto-immune disabilities since age 44, which developed after a lumbar surgery that was excessively delayed by an incompetent (and suspected psychopathic) orthopedist; and, I'm in my 60s.

Basically, I had no pre-existing medical ailments so I have been SSD & insurance-covered all this time with a "catastrophic" designation. (Not even close to what the lost half of a work-career would have provided as retirement at this age!) Like you, I interact with the medical industry from a position of extreme caution and eventually got it in my file that I cannot tolerate drug regimens (Multiple Chemical Sensitivity Syndrome). I'm also wary of cancer on my maternal side and a recent Dx & surgical excision of a melanoma in Stage 1a was a very lucky call since I can't see chemo- or immuno-therapy as options for me. After escaping the pressures to accept drug therapies, I could move into 'alternative' and the more fringe areas to seek medical help and a supportive 'team' of therapists. They are all amazed at my "medical stability" which is no more than my adaptation to life like this... but, I have never lost hope for reversal of my conditions.

As I considered your Journal input I kept coming back to your... residence and osteoporosis. There's much more there, and it's wonderful what forum members are suggesting and, your spirited aim to try anything reasonable. I'm cheering for you out here.

Here's my two cents: I wish you could arrange for a better housing situation although I expect you've created a beautiful sanctuary of peace and serenity in your home interior. I'm wondering if you've been able to see what services are available, say, in your county office of the New York State Office of the Aging? They serve the 60+ population. I'm not in your state so have zero intel about what is where you are. (If you have a long & 'tired' history of reaching out to social services and maybe don't feel that experience applies or is appropriate for this forum, do PM/converse with me if you prefer.) My experience was generally with insurance-generated rehabilitation & disability professionals but some had federal-level training & national credentials; my "rehabilitation supplier's" job description included doing home evaluations and recommending 'custom' housing needs assessments. Even so, it takes a talented and upper-level professional service provider in this department to be more thoughtful than just thinking in terms of entrance ramps and grab bars in the shower. My contacts were not trained, two decades ago, in my issues with mold and chemicals but may be more competent in these today? It would definitely take some 'educating' to materialize your Light-Water-Magnetism needs but if it was just even a possibility you could move with agency help I'm sure you could incorporate that additional 'bio-physics' dimension to some degree.

Another information resource may be in what community resources are in place for the aging disabled citizen trying to Live Independently, on their own and not in 'facilities.' For myself, when my husband died, there was some discussion about my living in a facility for special disability needs and planning for increased needs with aging but it was not a good avenue for me, in my area, what with new construction materials out-gassing, a different diet than central kitchen food services could handle, and so on. All I know is, government policy-makers write lovely encouraging 'words on paper' about Independent Living; and it takes research and phone work to get a sense of what's actionable out there.

Osteoporosis. I spent six months totally bed-ridden and another six months able to sit up more than being in bed. Once I could get to a D.O. specialist in women's health (as well as make it to the neurosurgeon's appointments) I learned I was in the severe osteoporosis category and had very low endocrine function. I asked for pituitary tests but insurance would not pay for that and back then I didn't go beyond first requests, and fight for myself like I eventually learned to do. We fiddled with thyroid, adrenals, hormone levels... and I got some energy coming up and a few sparks behind the brain fog, again. No grand ignition and steady flames to anything to reverse Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, or what today I'd discuss as the medical care necessary for a "mitochondrial" co-morbidity! I intuitively knew I had ATP problems but before this year did not know a functional medicine practitioner, nor, a couple months later, the expanded illumination from viewing the human design in "bio-physics" terms... At the time, I took ionic calcium-magnesium and got better bone scans, down to top-tier osteopenia. Perhaps being bed-ridden my bone density just started seeping away but 'came back' as it was not years & years of slow deterioration. But, my body was not properly utilizing the ionic Ca in the formula and my MRIs were showing bone spur development. I dropped all Ca supplementation and the bone spurs actually disappeared in future MRIs! Another time, in this past decade, my D3 test showed a 300% 'toxic' overload score; it seems too many of my supplements had D3 in them. Off those supplements, my D3 was down below 35 in the next year's test, and I re-started supplementing, results unknown until my next D3 blood test.. (Now, it's the high noon supplement!)

Here's the mysterious part. My last bone scan showed me in "young normal" range! I wonder if really high D3 (over)dosing was a factor? Also, I wonder if bone scans can give an equivalent to a 'false positive' reading? I will say my ever thinner, dry, fragile once curly hair might have been from too much D3 and that seems improved now about 3 years later. I would never recommend excessive D3 unless the research on that is there. It was a mistake on my part. I'm supposed to have another scan this year and I'm eager to follow-up on that vector. I'd prefer to be sharing this after, not before, another bone scan. But those of us with Medicare know how by-the-book these tests are, especially if you now don't, apparently, have osteoporosis anymore... In my case, I'd say my bone situation was not yet a 'chronic' settled-in thing, and sometimes the acute vs. chronic status of what's going on in a body makes a difference.

Lastly, I love music, too! Early in my new life with illness chapters, I gravitated to work with sound & light frequency self-treatment. There was some decent pain management from my explorations there, as well with integrating the 'vestibular stimulation' chair. I could get some quality rest effects when I slept in a slow-moving rhythm, above and below and around (almost) zero gravity with select lower frequencies transmitted through transducers into the chair padding to my whole body. In the day-times, TENS had a mild effect on pain, and later, Alpha-Stim CES (cranial-electro-stim) was more effective for me. I also recovered hearing loss in the middle ranges from following a program of classical music using full ear-covering headphones (Patricia Joudry). This was the audio-TAPE format and I'd have some questions about the CD format today after learning there was a 'problem' (related to hearing physiology?) in the early days of CDs with the carrier wave used. (I'd beware buying old CDs in used music shops from that time period, which I've forgotten but might be searchable. Digital tech today is probably hugely different...) I also have a lot of PEMF equipment which, at present, I've stopped using on understanding there are some contraindications. Following up on why that is is just too beyond the "simplexing" I need! (For example, I 'get it' about my curatron and earth-pulse being pemf, but what about what's not referred to as specifically "pemf" in the literature; is cranial CES and the Vielight in that category for 'pulsing?')

Another personal 'win' was discovering music using traditional Chinese instruments had amazing effects not just on my hearing but also on my mood (cosmically depressed!) and I fiercely craved the high, squeaky registers in that flute & Pipa music. No explanation, except the Joudry material worked best (for me) with classical compositions, strings. Anything in those days that had a positive effect on Chronic Pain was a go -- and my IEEE-member spouse was my stalwart set-up engineer! When I moved I could not replicate the light & sound tech environment as it had been, neither dedicate space to a whole 'therapy' room. Too bad neither of us brought the Sun into it. Just wondering if he would have lived longer and I'd still be in that charming house...

But, the forum is a great place to "Simplex" and my improved DHA & sunlight status feels promising with an eye to 'trigeminal' face-dunking CT and nnEMF considerations on the horizon. It was overwhelming at first. Better & better every day! I hope that for you, too!

### karenr — 2017-08-12T18:24:24+0300

@[Sheddie](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20386/) . I am speechless. But will say a few things , although I need some time to digest what you've shared. I am so humbled and grateful for this connection with such like-minded folks here.

It's uncanny how much we have in common. Will get back with you soon.

### karenr — 2017-08-13T20:29:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-221603>

@[Sheddie](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20386/) , not sure where to begin, so just a few thoughts that popped up..

> They are all amazed at my "medical stability" which is no more than my adaptation to life like this... but, I have never lost hope for reversal of my conditions.

My experience to a T. An old friend of mine insists that I have 9 lives. There is some kind of strengthening that the adversity itself promotes, as a built-in self-healing mechanism which I think we have been able to tap into. For me, this gets into a spiritual discourse which might derail my journal thread a bit! But has to be acknowledged as an important aspect of this journey.

Re. moving, crazy, but that idea won't go away, even though it seems absolutely impossible from a physical and financial and logistic point of view. Even the thought of going somewhere for a month to really disconnect from all the tech. The kind of thing that would require a miracle, which is just something we don't understand the mechanism of, but not necessarily rare..

I feel the presence of the miraculous in my daily, ordinary life - hey, the way I've received an outpouring of support here in such a short time, is one example - so I'm open to this kind of unexpected way that life can reorganize itself in optimal ways, playing out on other fronts and in some very concrete ways too.

That's how I think. If something rings true, it's true across the board, and not just in the one narrow context or under the specific conditions where I first saw it. So I'm always looking to make these connections that tie things together beyond conditions. When the conditions suck, you start looking at things differently, as you know.

So, about the nuts and bolts of looking into moving, I will PM you. I've worked for the past year with social services and legal counsel on this issue, but if there's something way outside the box that I haven't explored, I'd love to go further with this.

I could see getting VERY serious about how a new environment could impact my health, and seeing where that mindset takes me..

In any case, I seriously have a lot more to do to work out my lifestyle choices. Being alone almost all the time, not being able to do a lot of reading, means I'm on the iPad a lot (easier to read shorter bits than pick up a book). And for social interaction. So although it's hard wired and blue blocked, it's still a lit screen I'm looking at. Spending long winter evenings alone with little activity is challenging even for a seasoned meditator.

Re. Osteoporosis. Quite a journey you've had. Is your D3 normalized now? I just started the Sperti lamp, we shall see. Re. bone scan, I'm having my first one next month. There are so many tests I've never done and specialists I've never seen, because of the profound fatigue preventing me from going out except for highest priority stuff. But..

My understanding is that bone density doesn't equal bone strength - like a piece of chalk, if that's a fair analogy, something can be very dense but still easily breakable. So I take all that with a grain of salt (whole salt . And will do the bone scan anyway to add to the picture.

Re. Music! I'm sure we could talk for hours.. besides my own playing (not able to do in recent years but continues in my consciousness of course!) I've explored sound therapies, did the Tomatis training (on CD), tuning forks, solfeggio fequencies, all kinds of sacred music of different traditions, weird homemade instruments, and then there's Tom Kenyon, oh my. But very often I just don't want any outside input and need to sit in the silence of my own body and experience the inner music.

Lately I'm into ASMR, helps me sleep, but requires headphones, have to work out a safe way to do that now! Listening to bubble wrap popping and plastic wrap crinkling, it's not the sublime tones of a Beethoven sonata, but it's amazingly powerful.

Have you explored Rudolf Steiner on music? Books I love:
The Harmony of the Human Body: Musical Principles in Human Physiology, by Armin Husemann
The Inner Nature of Music and the Experience of Tone, Rudolf Steiner.

Thanks for the mention of face-dunking CT. I love when principles can be applied in creative ways - I figure even just starting with splashing cold water on my face could trigger a little dive reflex, maybe? I'm usually so chilly that that doesn't occur to me, but I'm sure it's doable.

Brings me to Buteyko breathing (book: The Oxygen Advantage by Patrick McKeown), another hack I've been using to get to that disturbed ANS. Another one of those counterintuitive things, where everything we thought we knew about deep breathing was wrong! Love it.

And someday hope to see the bigger picture of how all these hacks fit together in a simplex pattern that is eminently clear.

I'm so heartened to hear of your improvements and the great spirit with which you approach this challenge of life!
Cheering for you too,
Deep bows of gratitude for this connection.

Karen

### karenr — 2017-08-13T22:59:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-221606>

Here's the thing.
Direct sun comes to my balcony after 2:30 PM now, not ideal, but I'll take it! Today was a perfect day, blue sky, low humidity. So I put aside all my concerns about overdoing it, given my sensitive condition, and just allowed myself to take in the glorious solar nourishment for about 15 minutes.

It has been a very long time since I experienced direct sun for more than a minute. But not just the longer exposure, it was also my feeling so receptive to the nourishment that was so healing. I knew I would be okay, better than okay. Then I went inside when my gut feeling told me to.

While in the sun, I had some visceral feelings of actually feeling good in my body. This is downright monumental. Sure the exhaustion was there, all the usual stuff was there. But this other experience of wellness somehow superseded all that.

I don't think that we are just physical bodies putting ourselves through protocols to make the body run better. How everything fits together to form this magnificent thing called life is a breathtaking mystery, and we can choose to live awe-inspired.

### drezy — 2017-08-13T23:58:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-221609>

Hey, I like the new pic. Is that a "pineapple" ukulele? I have a theory that it's impossible to feel anything but happy around ukulele music and laughing babies.

I'm glad to hear about your adventure in the sun and good feel. I'm very much into subjective data like that especially in the beginning. I think we get a little too hung up on objectivity (if humans are even capable of that).

> Then I went inside when my gut feeling told me to.

Love it. If you have this kind of intuitive sense and tight coupling between your conciseness and your body then it's a matter of coupling that body slowly and sensibly with more of what it needs.

Based this website you took on quite a little UV index spike today(Just choosing Syracuse to split the difference of "upstate NY"):

From [http://uv.willyweather.com/ny/onondaga-county/syracuse.html](http://uv.willyweather.com/ny/onondaga-county/syracuse.html)

I've used AM light to build up my solar callous and then did my own crazy experiment. Central Texas sun for about 4.5 hours through the middle of UV index 13 as it's peak.

If the kind of light you need to build your solar tolerance is not reaching your balcony in the AM then we can make it a PM game.

Look at the "green" sections of the daily curve. For your location it's roughly 7AM-10AM and about 4PM - 7PM. Oh well, we can't target 7-10 AM, but it sounds like we've maybe got 4-7PM light. Based on your tolerance A 6:15-7PM balcony session or even earlier to 7PM session might be like your equivalent to my AM callous maintenance I do now. Maybe slowly increase the amount of time also. It's still sorta close to summer solstice so you're locations is still getting as generous amounts of light as it's going to be getting.

> I don't think that we are just physical bodies putting ourselves through protocols to make the body run better.

I'm with you there.

### karenr — 2017-08-14T00:56:25+0300

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=221609)
>
> Hey, I like the new pic. Is that a "pineapple" ukulele? I have a theory that it's impossible to feel anything but happy around ukulele music and laughing babies.

For sure! Even such a cheap pineapple! I'm more of a guitar player, in that category of instruments, but the uke is easier on my body, and I need my fix of some live acoustic music even on the couch.

> I'm glad to hear about your adventure in the sun and good feel. I'm very much into subjective data like that especially in the beginning. I think we get a little too hung up on objectivity (if humans are even capable of that).

Yup. My feeling is that what's real is always more dynamic than any either/or dichotomy..

> Love it. If you have this kind of intuitive sense and tight coupling between your conciseness and your body then it's a matter of coupling that body slowly and sensibly with more of what it needs.

This is so encouraging to hear!!

> Based this website you took on quite a little UV index spike today(Just choosing Syracuse to split the difference of "upstate NY"):

Wow, thank you so much for looking that up! I'm in Monroe county, pretty close. (Many people think upstate NY means the Catskills, but there's a whole lot further up!)

So this is a new concept, building my solar tolerance by being out later in the afternoon or early evening. I could definitely be out in the area of 6-7 PM. But I go to bed early, and have been starting my blue blocking routine by 7 at the latest. Does it work to go from a strong sun exposure to more nighttime darkness in a short time, in other words without a more gradual darkening?

I'm also using the Sperti Fiji lamp around noon, just a few minutes at a time, my skin not getting any color yet from that. But I've been feeling very itchy the past few days, not sure if it's from that, but I have a feeling I should be careful with it - I trust the sun more than I do the lamp . But the thing will be a lifesaver when the weather changes.

Thanks so much for your input here!!

### karenr — 2017-08-14T01:49:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-221617>

Another consideration is that being in the sun is overstimulating my nervous system, and I don't wind down for many hours after.. but my guess is I can't avoid that at this point - considering how out of whack my system is, things are not gonna just straighten out in a linear progression, and there will be bumps, and I may overshoot at times and have to make adjustments..

The thing is to trust the process and approach things with mindfulness, balancing the structure of a given protocol with the kind of anarchy of life such as it is..What I'm bringing to this endeavor is a full participation, not just passively following a regimen.

I guess that's what the n=1 is about, and we're honing our instincts about how to keep growing the ongoing experiment in an organic way. Just thinking out loud now, so grateful for this space.

I think this picture may be useful to you. Ignore the Kelvin scale along the bottom for now.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I circled the morning session that I prefer. Note UVA+VIS+IR. Note that UVB shows up after it, but leaves at some point in the PM when you go back to UVA+VIS+IR. Given the constraints that you have expressed I'm not going to pick knits. Get the UVA & IR in the session that best works for you, but try to make the first light that hits your eyes in the morning NOT be artificial light.

The gradual darkening that we are constructed for is built into every evening. If I were badly decoupled and had redox that really needed building the , aside from DHA, I'd chop that image right in half along that "UVB+UVA+VIS+IR" line. That would be my someday goal, maybe even my 15 minute and gradually increasing goal like you braved today. I'd focus on both ends and try to be out for a while to witness the transition where UVA shows up and disappears.

### karenr — 2017-08-14T02:50:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-221621>

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) , this really hits the spot, thanks! Will be fun too to start really paying attention to these cycles. To think of myself as actually living in the natural world, what a concept!

### karenr — 2017-08-21T19:54:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-222005>

Progress report.

I shouldn't be surprised, but I am.. I have been falling asleep at night without sleep meds for the first time in many years. Four nights in a row so far, record setting. Feeling sleepy at night, what a concept! What a relief! Those meds were undermining my progress.

Sleep is still interrupted and I wake up exhausted as usual, but this is a huge first step.

The main things I've been able to do so far are:
Early morning light exposure,
Late afternoon direct sun exposure or whatever is out there
Aggressive blue blocking all evening
Greatly reduced wifi exposure

Basically, I'm outside more than I have been in many years.
And my eyesight has improved already!!
I think it's due to the combination of light exposure and focusing on distant objects (trees across the street) which relaxes the eyes.

Really breaking the iPad habit, not easy for me being so isolated and not many options for evening activities that energy allows. But when you start getting results, you get more motivated to keep going and do more things that seemed impractical before.

Still ironing out wrinkles in projects to install lizard light and RubyLux bulbs, and put up blackout curtains. I need a personal assistant!!

### Sheddie — 2017-08-21T22:24:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-222010>

Outstanding progress!! Whoooo-Eeeee!

I was wondering if you had to back off some for nervous system over-stimulation, but you're forging ahead. Waking up -- is it physical or mental 'exhaustion' that lingers?

I've been playing with an old light pole that holds 2 bulbs. One I was going to install red and the other UV. I found a UV LED bulb at the grocery store but hadn't yet found a red bulb. Well, while I sleep like I'm in a coma these last 20 years, having to 'fight' to get up and get moving around safely after the night, I found that a couple minutes' of the UV in my face as soon as I sit up (and I have UV-blocking post-cataract lenses) I feel alert and wide awake. Most unexpected and it has been consistent now for more than a week... Perhaps, the UV 'signals' my pituitary to do something, or stop doing something? I lost the reference here that would explain why I ended up buying a bulb ostensibly for evenings... As a chronic fatigue sufferer I am sure something occurs (for me) with this morning UV exposure... (I'm still slow to 'wake up' muscles, etc., but it helps I'm more alert in general that time of the day!)

Best luck carrying on such thrilling changes -- maybe after sleep improves so many other physical bio-physics events will follow.

### karenr — 2017-08-21T22:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-222011>

Yup, forging ahead even when I'm a bit quiet with posting. Lots of good stuff percolating beneath the surface, and Yes, I think the sleep improvement will have a vast ripple effect that remains to be seen!

And that's so interesting the energizing effect the UV is having for you! In the beginning stages of my CFS I was also sleeping a lot and unable to get started.. after a while that shifted to sympathetic overdrive, and now I bet I have cortisol in the morning making me jump out of bed and move around, feels like "fake" energy .

Speaking of UV, I forgot to mention in my last post that I'm doing the Sperti Fiji lamp every day, up to 5 minutes now on front and 5 on back, so that's quite some UV right there! I can't determine exactly what effects that's having - no tanning yet, but I trust it's a good thing to keep up, and increasing the time.

About my exhaustion.. it's mostly physical, I'd say, although taking in new info can be exhausting too, but it's mainly an overwhelming feeling of not having any "real" energy and having to constantly struggle to push through.

My creative energy is through the roof!.. which is such a frustrating mismatch because I feel like there is an energy dynamo there that doesn't have the physical vehicle for expression. So the life force gets backed up, and I can feel myself running on stress hormones. Hence being so "tired but wired" all the time.

It's one of the worst human experiences, profound exhaustion and not being able to rest, a kind of indescribable pain. But I'm sure glad that I'm mentally active and as highly functional as I am in some ways, given that I can't even walk around the block. I'm always able to find ways to stay inspired and engaged in life. Wherever that impulse comes from, I'm grateful.

OH! Another update - my thoracic vertebral fractures are apparently halfway healed, no surgery recommended, whew!
So the ball is completely in my court now, where I like it.
Light, light, light!

### Sheddie — 2017-08-22T02:08:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-222019>

Totally off the wall? -- my Intuition kept thrusting a notion at me... Can you use your musicality to integrate some of these energies? I 'answered' that I thought you couldn't play instruments presently because of stiffness. I got a word back -- Humming.

I looked it up... saw a factoid (no reference to studies) that 'humming increased nitric oxide 15%.' Then, stuff about sinus type infections which didn't seem to match up. However, the frequencies would also vibrate in the whole head, maybe even to bone below the neck...

But, I'm wondering if humming might focus some energies and get them to flow in your body. As per an active meditation... Or, if you have applied this in the past, perhaps it's wanting another try?

Seems there's energy there for you but not moving/flowing through the right places, maybe a bit out of order for you right now, this early in... a marvelous re-patterning (for lack of a word) from seeking and receiving light, and reversing chronic light deficiency...

I'm just passing on the word, Humming. Maybe it's meant for someone else. Or even me -- think I'll try humming for myself tomorrow. Sorry if it's not a connection. I'm really ecstatic to hear such good news.

### karenr — 2017-08-22T02:24:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-222020>

Totally ON the wall! I can't play instruments because of upper body nerve pain, but humming is an idea that is very familiar to me, especially recently in studying vagus nerve stimulation hacks, that's apparently a great one! And more fun than gagging .

I just haven't done it much because of shortness of breath. But again, like so many things it's just a case of applying something in a different way and making it possible.

And I have a friend who's quite proficient in sacred chanting and such.. will see if he has some prescriptive ideas!
I enjoy exploring the lowest tones I'm able to sing - they seem to resonate in my body.

Now this is reminding me that I wanted to post about Buteyko breathing.. another one of those counterintuitive principles that hits the spot for me! Basically, reduced breathing, more CO2 to to trigger parasympathetic response and utilize O2 better. And another thing I want to incorporate more into daily routine.

Humming and breathing. Hmmmm.

### karenr — 2017-08-27T20:41:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-222366>

So while I'm establishing so many new routines, I decided that posting here at least once a week would be one of those good things.. a lot of juggling going on, so many balls up in the air, no idea how I'm managing any of it, but I suppose I am. Lists.

And meditation time for letting go of all the to-do's, is essential.

So how I'm doing...I'm in that phase where the new regimen feels discombobulating. Healing is never a linear progression, and things seem crazy chaotic now, but **the key is that there's an overall feeling of progress and being on the right path. Really important to notice that, rather than fixate on all the individual things that are out of whack.**

Orthopedist visit - surgery is not recommended, yay! Looks like I'm about halfway healed, so maybe another couple months. He said that with compression fractures, the normal height of the bones can never be restored. I just don't buy that. Sure I can see all the limiting factors, but the innate healing potential is always there. I'm up for it.

Oh! I'm doing 23andme, very excited!

Thoughts and questions coming up now:

1.  I unearthed an old SAD desk lamp from Northern Technologies, 10,000 lux at a foot away, no flicker, doesn't say what wavelengths, but looks pretty white. [http://northernlighttechnologies.com/sad-light-store/nl-desk-lamp](http://northernlighttechnologies.com/sad-light-store/nl-desk-lamp) How does this compare, therapeutically, with the Exo Terra Solar Glo lizard light, 125 or 160w (I ended up getting the 125 because I'm very sensitive at this point). For getting that one dose of intense light early in the morning, when I can't get it naturally, would the SAD lamp work too?
2.  Planning to do a phone consult with Michael Neuert of EMF Center or his technician! And rent his test meters and all. Huge project, a little overwhelming.
3.  Found out that smart meters haven't come to my area yet, but are planned to roll out over the next 3 years, ugh.
4.  Measured the distance to that cell tower I can see from my window - 1,000 feet, another ugh.
5.  Sperti lamp - I'm up to 6 minutes per area, yay!
6.  Getting outside is getting challenging already, with mornings being chilly.. in a month or two I won't be able to sit outside at all many days, even bundled up. Not sure that can change much until I can change the diet, just doing the best I can for now..
7.  As for some cold to warm me up, the most I can do now is splashing cold water on my face. I'm generally so chilly that I almost never go without socks even in summer, unless my feet are in direct sun. I have to keep the indoor temp at least 74, or I have to bundle up inside! And for sleeping too, or my nose is like ice and I can't sleep.
8.  Decided to get some sashimi or sushi rolls once a week, even if I can only eat a tiny amount, but to get my body used to seeing some dense nutrition. Love it! Also some sardines packed in water, trying to overcome my aversion to sardines. Next project - making ceviche (marinated raw fish). I did it years ago, so simple to make the simplest version, with lemon or lime juice, some salt and chopped tomato, onion, etc. On the fence about whether to avoid nightshades - my diet is so limited, and a little tomato helps a lot of things taste better! Hoping it'll be okay to have some small amounts of nightshades.

On another note, I'm noticing how most of the people I come in contact with here have zero interest in this whole subject of study. Even the ones who are seemingly health conscious.

To question mainstream thought on even just one point, is a huge challenge to people's sense of security and even identity. It goes that deep.

I've already taken the red pill probably at the moment of birth or earlier, no going back now! Even all the fluoride didn't seem to affect my ability to see that the emperor has no clothes. That's fascinating to me, that so many people are sleepwalking through life and others aren't as subject to the levels of manipulation and control going on.

And that I always have my own blind spots to wake up to.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Beautiful update! Going strong.

I'm so missing my 'nightshades' but a test result put them on an allergy list. Even so, I hope someone will confirm my sketchy sense that I think Jack (Vermont 2016?) mentioned nightshades in connection with ROS. Not generally recommended, although that may be limited to the Leptin Reset stage? I was so unhappy about 'lettuce with lettuce on lettuce' salad that I took a tip from a forum woman here who mentioned 'drinking her salads!' Just by blenderizing with added water, not from a juicer. Surprisingly mild and refreshing.

Good luck with trying some ceviche -- Inger on the Forum has a delightful YouTube (Ingerliese) demo of her Salmon Tartare recipe! For you, the carb sushi rice carrying the protein (DHA) ingredients could be a very positive beginning. Great idea. I'm struggling with 'morning anorexia' and forcing food too early was not working; I have backed up food acceptance a good two hours earlier, though. In several weeks of trying to avoid a most unappetizing queasiness. (Shhh, don't mention that I'm getting up a few hours earlier anyway so the 'net success' is a little suspect at this time.)

You're so right on -- there's "no going back," only forward! Keep mentioning light, water, nnEMFs... to folks; you're just ahead of the curve and most people are used to thousands of message repetitions these days thanks to the 'authority' of media and the dearth of learning from 3D people all around as credible sources for useful, maybe even life-saving, information. Many times I've been the first to say something I didn't feel was trivial, and got the blank looks. Generally, about 5 years later (in my case) the very same persons would be rushing around promoting what I told them about so long before. It doesn't matter if you told them first as much as the fact you see them catch on to something you know is important. I can't wait to revel in a mass awakening to turn back to the sun and turn off the lights... I have a lot of faith in the mothers of the world, like their campaign against endocrine disrupting chemicals in baby bottle plastics. I'll be energetically supporting them when they realize daycare, schools, and ultimately the workplace, have some accommodations to make. Light Deficiency -- it's not a rare cause of disease anymore, but it surely IS the elephant in the room. And, I think the child who said the emperor has no clothes must be the first ever 'whistle-blower.' Don't be disheartened! It's all good. ...Cheers!

I'll put this maybe-dumb idea out. The other day I was wondering if someone had very bad metered nnEMFs coming through one wall, might there possibly be some sort of dry wall with (?) metal wire (chicken wire?) embedded in it? I haven't metered and plan some day to get that done. More like I'm imagining the worst and moving would be (for me) too hard in the near future, maybe ever. I could, more easily, think of replacing a wall, and doing something up in the attic... Also, I can't recall the source but there was a 'hack' for EMFs through windows where it was suggested to put blue painter's (masking?) tape on the inside window sills... I think it came from some orgonite site but can't find it.

Hey Sheddie!

Kinda depends on the frequency of the emf. Higher frequencies generally get absorbed by materials & thinner metals (think catching minnows) lower frequencies often require thicker materials (think catching orcas) simple sheetrock won't cut it.

> Also, I can't recall the source but there was a 'hack' for EMFs through windows where it was suggested to put blue painter's (masking?) tape on the inside window sills

one resource pointed to there:
[http://www.slt.co/products/RFShieldingWindowFilm/](http://www.slt.co/products/RFShieldingWindowFilm/)

I used to sit 5' from a window with this type of film on and had NO cell signal.

### karenr — 2017-09-04T17:23:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-222857>

Shorter entry this weeend, been going through intense migraines and upper body nerve pain.. not fun at all, but some of this pain is actually a result of moving around more, which IS a very good thing, just that the rest of the system hasn't caught up yet.

Weather patterns all screwy, some days too chilly to sit outside much, yikes, and it's only September! Gonna have to do what I can with light hacking.. I love the idea that the true remedy is simple and all the hacks aren't necessary, ideally.. and even while I do need to work with my bag of magic tricks it still helps to be grounded in that bedrock of understanding that nature is what heals.

So.. I also want to mention the emotional side of things, which often gets short shrift.. As so much change is happening at the cellular level, old emotional patterns come up, which I recognize as a positive thing.. All the fear and sadness and loneliness that was locked in the body is released as energy, just that the process can be painful, but it helps to understand it as a healing process.. not to suppress feeling with a false form of positive thinking (been there done that), nor wallow in it, but to accept everything that comes. It's taken me decades to come to understand this for real in my actual experience and not just as a nice concept to aspire to.

\--> Comes down to this.. finally starting to get that struggling to get well doesn't work when it's an internal fight against suffering.. but there is a way of standing up for the good and claiming the win without exhausting myself fighting the demons anymore. Then, with a little guidance, the actions I need to take seem to follow easily from there.

It's like pulling the plug on the old limited power source and plugging into the inexhaustible source that we were meant to run on, and I'm beginning to get a glimpse of that incredible potential. Really inspiring to ponder this.

### Sheddie — 2017-09-04T21:24:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-222866>

That upgrade to lots More Light than Before will want good hydration to make the EZ water; and let the process underway weave and flow fully to the right rhythms... Have you tried hydrolyzed gelatin, easily mixes in any liquid? Skin feeding with coconut oil, olive oil? (Perchance for better tolerance of protein and good fat along with lonely carbs via the alimentary canal?) I know food is a supreme challenge... just enough food is needed to balance with Light's role?

Don't you think you could be some Gladiator (or any other super-hero/heroine) doing what has to be done? Movie hype aside, some Gladiators did, I think, win their well-fought Freedom. I'm feeling 'rich' and more 'alive' with so many new things to do, in spite of being a long term 'slave' to this state of being with disabilities. An epic adventure has much space for mythic characters.

Thought precedes form: This came along just when I'd run out of thought material! (Nothing left to do is the worst case scenario.) Look at us! I don't envy the emotional detox but it does pass -- and doesn't return forever and ever. I know this well, and with confidence. (Ten years of it but always better and better.) Three-legged stool? Feels more like a three-ring circus broken out in mayhem at the moment!

### karenr — 2017-09-04T21:57:44+0300

@[Sheddie](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20386/) what an awesome reply! Gladiator, sure, why not? I'm obsessed with the old TV series Fringe right now, an infinite number of parallel universes that aren't really so hard to move in and out of, once you know what needs to be restored and what you need to let go of!

The emotional detox I would say is no less intense than before but there is a huge difference - It's just energy moving through, and that's aliveness. I spent a lot of time in the past trying to disidentify with suffering, trying to "surrender," never worked, and just made me feel more disconnected and despairing.

I think what we want is just to be real. That intimacy of experience. I hate the pain and don't have to love it or solve the ultimate mystery of suffering! Just bring myself to whatever I experience with all my heart.

So bring on the 3-ring circus, celebration of misfits, we're in good company!
Mayhem is apparently where it's at. Isn't that what the Greeks called Kaos? The fertile womb of creation? Like the dark night of the soul appears dark but is so full of light.. hmm.. the upgrade to Lots More Light Than Before, is in full swing!

And skin feeding, yes. My Ayurvedic/Chinese herbalist/acupuncturist recommends topical sesame oil with warming herbs, for chilly Vata types, as coconut oil is more cooling. And it's more seasonal now too. But I'd love to hear more thoughts on that.

### karenr — 2017-09-08T15:35:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-223159>

STARS

You are tired, friend.
Your body aches to rest.

Give in.
You have wanted to fall apart for so long.
To let go of your defenses.
To be transparent and authentic.

Your cynicism has protected you.
Your fear has served you well.
Your dreams of enlightenment were beautiful dreams.
But there is no need to hold your 'self' together any longer.

Surrender.
Or simply stop pretending that you don't know 'how'.

Fail.
Fall.
The vastness will hold you.
Only illusions can disappear.

The deeper the heart breaks
The more love it can hold.

Don't tell me you are not worthy.
Don't tell me you are not made of stars.

\- Jeff Foster

### karenr — 2017-09-15T20:08:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-223742>

So the expedition to track nnEMF is underway, and it's quite an eye opener!

First of all, Michael Neuert and his technician have been an immensely helpful resource. [http://emfcenter.com](http://emfcenter.com). I rented the 3 meter package for a week, during which time I had a very enlightening phone consult with Michael, then decided to extend the rental another week.

1.  magnetic field meter (milligauss)
2.  RF/microwave meter (volts per meter)
3.  Electrical fields - Body voltage meter

For electrical fields, Michael recommends the body voltage meter that measures voltage on your skin, vs. other meters that measure electrical fields coming off the source. This way you get a better idea of your actual exposure.

I initially was focusing on RF (router in the bedroom and can't get far enough away from it, not good).. so I didn't do the body voltage testing and planned to just use Michael's recommendations for mitigating electrical fields. But he said that first, it's not possible to guess what your actual exposures are without testing... and second, testing would be very important for someone with a lot of symptoms, even as important as RF. In his experience, many people get very quick symptom relief after lowering body voltage to very stringent levels.

With the meters, he provides very detailed printed instructions on how to use the meters (besides his free YouTube videos), what levels are optimal according to various sources including his own experience, (for general safety and then more stringent levels for sick people), and some ways to mitigate exposures.

I had a lot of questions, and decided to do a consult even though the info given was very thorough.

The upshot of this - First the good news, my magnetic field exposure isn't very significant, and that's easy to control by not standing in front of the fridge for long periods of time! Nor the stove, very high levels there. The fields drop off very quickly once you move a foot or so away, so that was interesting to see.

The RF/wifi issue is a bit of a nightmare. My ambient levels are very high, as far away from the router as I can get. Plus the bed is cooking in fields off the chart. I do keep the router on a mechanical timer so it's off all night, but I'm home all day, and it's a big dilemma as to how to be online in the living room if I disabled wifi completely.

Even rigging the iPad for Ethernet, it has to be plugged into power all the time, so I'm trading RF for electrical fields. The only solution would be to use a laptop on battery, Ethernet, and USB keyboard and mouse. Which would be very difficult to manage physically with all the pain I have (unless it would reduce the pain!), and I'd have to consider buying a reliable laptop that doesn't overheat all the time.

So, lots of wrinkles still to iron out. But the body voltage was really the shocker. Optimally, highest voltage for me being something like 0.1, my reading on the couch where I spend most time is 5. Pulling out all the appliances nearby, brings it down to 1. Couldn't get anything near 0.1. So that might be due to wiring in the walls, which I can't do anything about. But moving stuff around, and deciding to sacrifice some conveniences, just a matter of how far I can reasonably go with that.

At this point, I'm longing to get myself to a cabin in the woods with food and a few good books, for a month. Seems impossible in the condition I'm currently in, but I gotta get closer to some version of that and really do a whole lifestyle overhaul. Seriously questioning and rethinking every convenience that I believe I need. Whew.

Although I see the bottom line is reducing the total load of nnEMF - so even if it's not possible to get to optimal levels on every parameter, it might not be necessary.

I may revisit the body voltage measurements after doing a bunch of moving things around, because that can be quite a project- the ground rod has to go in the earth, and then the cord runs inside the house where you hold the meter and turn off circuit breakers and put them back on one at a time and test things individually. I didn't get to do that thoroughly, so it might be worth another session when I can get the help.

### karenr — 2017-09-23T19:15:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-224194>

Weekend update! Topic for the week- **Energy Drain** and how to just say no!

Moving at a snail's pace with all the hacks, which is a good thing to accommodate my sensitivities and not blow me out of the water.. Even just the afternoon balcony sitting for 10 minutes in full sun, wow, that's a whole ‘nother experience I’m just getting used to, just in time for cold weather when it will be a challenge to bundle up and catch some rays!

So basically it’s all going pretty well, despite the bouts of overwhelm with so many projects - after a little bit of the EMF hacking, that has to go on the back burner. I still haven’t gotten to getting rid of WiFi, due to all the rearranging (energy) that would require. But mainly, I need to focus on what matters most and NOT STRESS about it. Even Michael Neuert's very nice technician reminded me of that! Chronic mental/emotional energy drain can be even worse than exposure to nnEMF's. It’s s tricky balance to strike, when energy is so limited.

The genetic testing interpretation also has to go on the back burner, as that will be a project just to get a handle on so that I can present the relevant data and ask for some feedback. I fed the raw data through Promethease.. even that is pretty overwhelming, even the haplotype info is a little confusing. I haven’t even formulated intelligent questions yet! In time.

But I have made a decision to NOT pursue working with a FM practitioner as I thought I needed to. As I'm getting healthier even just a tiny step at a time, I'm trusting my heart-centered judgment more.. Sure, I’d love to get data on what's biochemically out of whack and how to fix it. But that avenue and all the energy and money it requires just feels like an energy drain. And where does the belief come from, that “that's the only way to get well?”

To assume that an energy drain is the only path to wellness, there's something wrong with that picture! Seems important to look more deeply at the beliefs and choices and make clear decisions to cut away the energy drains from my life.

### karenr — 2017-09-23T19:20:50+0300

Then the corollary.. whatever choices are really supported by the heart, WILL be in line with my best healing, whether they are the ones that the voices in my head are pressuring me with, or not.

And maybe a consult with an FM doc will appear at some point in this process, who knows? But it’s the trust in letting go and allowing the decision making process to unfold from the heart, that’s the thing.

### karenr — 2017-10-11T20:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-225601>

@[shiran](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20240/) , I had that same question, as the Grade A bulbs are out of stock, and this is the response I got from the RubyLux rep:

Thank you for your interest in our bulbs. Please accept our apologies for the lack of stock on Amazon of our Grade A infrared bulbs. The response for this bulb has been enormous. Demand consistently outstrips supply despite our best efforts to order larger quantities with every shipment. I know this is very frustrating and disappointing for our customers. Amazon does have a supply of our Grade B infrared bulbs if you would like to check those out. If you are only interested in our Grade A infrared bulbs we do have some available through our website. We are expecting another shipment of our Grade A infrared bulbs to arrive within six to eight weeks, at which time they will be available on Amazon again.

The difference between the Grade A and the Grade B bulbs is purely cosmetic. The Grade B have minor irregularities in the red tint that is baked into the glass of the bulb. The performance, specs, CE approved status, safety, source and everything else about the two types is identical aside from those irregularities in the bulb’s cosmetic glass coloring. We tint the glass because most people have said they like the glowing red light and find it pleasant. However, the visible color of the bulbs is not usually the reason our customers purchase the bulbs. They are usually concerned about the heat and near infrared light output.

Debi Morris
RubyLux

### karenr — 2017-11-13T19:07:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-227914>

Haven’t kept up with the journal mostly because I haven’t been seeing any major shifts yet.. but that’s exactly why I'm writing now, because a change of consciousness is really where it’s at!

So this is all about mindset now.

Been reading and hearing a lot about the “**drama triangle,**” which came out of Transactional Analysis in the 1970's, and is a really good model for identifying dysfunctional ways of relating to everything in life - being either a victim, rescuer, or persecuter - and those roles are like musical chairs that once you’re in the game, you can’t help but keep switching places as you’re triggered in different ways.

And you keep getting triggered because the underlying belief is that external things control your health and happiness, so you’re always dependent in unhealthy ways on external circumstances giving you what you need. Hence the need to control everything and everyone. The opposite of self-responsibility and freedom. You shift roles but always still in the game, playing a role that’s keeping you from living from your deep potential. The thing is to get off the drama triangle, opt out of the game.

Reference for this is Lynn Forrest's work, and her book **Beyond Victim Consciousness.**

So I had to reevaluate what am I looking for, for healing. Looking at various light devices and tools, what’s behind all this? What beliefs are driving my need to keep searching for more tools which I can’t even afford?

First I got clear that I need the general light nourishment my body is starving for. I can improve on that even when I literally can’t go outside. Which has to help everything (the autoimmune thing, fatigue, migraine, etc.). I’ve been working with light the past few months but really haven't been clear on what I'm trying to do.

So exploring the idea that light trumps food, that has taken me out of the eating issue despair. When I believe that I can’t get anywhere until food improves first, and everything rests on that, that’s a miserable thought going nowhere, keeping me stuck on the drama triangle, victim role. That’s why I’m so motivated to do the light.

So what’s the goal?

For ages I’ve put the whole notion of goals aside, because it was too painful to look at. I couldn’t seem to make any kind of incremental improvement, so the best I could do was struggle to survive and try to manage pain and discomfort as well as possible. Bear up under bad circumstances. Victim/martyr mindset. And meditate and soul search every day, which aren’t bad things, but there was no active yang-type of energy for moving forward.

This is what’s shifting. And that's worth more than all the biohacks in the world, IMO, because it’s driving the whole process that’s leading me to all those things.

So. My goal is to get back on my feet, even to be at the starting point of incremental improvement is a goal that's feeling realistic now.

There are days when I can do a little walking around the house and down to the mailbox, and move around to do some household chores. But doing a little more doesn’t help, and doesn’t add to some conditioning to allow for doing more next time. There are many days when its a major push to just take case of very basic human needs, so no way yet to work incrementally.

To just be a good sport and endure as I have, isn’t a mindset that promotes healing. It needs to be more focused. But also not to set unrealistic goals that just amount to wishful thinking and don’t have a lot of actual creative power. Been there, done that.

I’m thinking that the light therapy could be the thing to focus on to get to where I can really see progress and be actively building on it. That would include improving my lung capacity, because shortness of breath is the main aspect of the exhaustion and the main obstacle to activity.

Then I came across Leanne Venier's REDjuvenator recently, which I'm feeling very drawn to intuitively, more than other kinds of infrared lamps. I can’t afford it now, but the fact that I'm drooling over it seems important. Where is this new impulse coming from - it's out of the new energy being freed up as I get my butt off the drama triangle merry-go-round and into the flow of life!

The victim mindset with no way to even start getting out of the hole, that’s the thing holding me back. Feeling desperate has been a motivator, but that comes from fear and just reinforces victim consciousness.

This is different - a kind of will rising up that’s not a reactionary kind, but just coming from the heart and the life force that isn’t ready to give up on life.

### karenr — 2017-12-29T19:23:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-231236>

Some thoughts on mental diet, which I think is so underrated. As I’ve said before, writing helps me articulate this stuff for myself, and this time I thought I'd share..

Funky thoughts: Things to consider when in a funk. Read to the end for my best trick, called “Minnie Mouse voice.”

#1 - Awareness. Cultivate awareness of your thought patterns. Your judgments, your criticisms, your anxious thoughts, the whirlwind in your head, your beliefs about the way things are. Don’t try to suppress them. Slow down from the habit of running to the next thing to get away from them. Look at them without judgment. Just allow yourself to notice.

Positive thinking can be helpful, but it’s never to suppress feelings or instill “better” beliefs, because then you’re draining your energy being hypervigilant to keep “bad” stuff at bay. This doesn’t solve anything.

I used to think that seeing the negative side of things was being realistic. I was rebelling against my Pollyanna upbringing where “negative” emotions got shoved under the rug. What I didn’t realize was that taking the opposite stance was just as unrealistic, just as distorted a view as the one I was rebelling against!

When in a funk, Stop fighting it. Slow down. Get quiet. Breathe. Just allow the real feelings that are there, beneath the storyline you're telling yourself. There is no funk in honest feelings. The funk is in the storyline.
We've never been taught that it’s okay to be quiet and alone with ourselves.

Also take some action, even if you don’t know why it would be helpful. Just do something you wouldn’t be inclined to do, which can help loosen a stuck pattern. Go for a walk, clean out a closet, talk to a neighbor. Make up a silly song. This is not to keep yourself in constant distraction so you don’t feel anything, but to shift out of an unhelpful rut. Learn to discern the difference.

Have a moratorium on complaining for an entire day. Just as an experiment. Not even any moaning about the weather. You will see what goes on in your head!

If it’s someone else's behavior that’s triggering me, I say to myself, “Me Too.” For example, if I'm criticizing the other for being selfish, I realize that I have been selfish too at times. Every nasty thing about another person, I have at some point expressed too in some way. This fosters compassion for myself and others and a sense of connectedness.

Realize that when the other person is behaving in a way that triggers me, it’s because of two things: They are in pain, and I have a trigger point. I realize I have compassion for a person in pain, because I know what that's like. I realize that I can take responsibility for my own trigger point.

If I can’t feel forgiveness for the other person, I can wish for them the healing that forgiveness would bring. Forgiveness isn’t exonerating the person from wrongdoing. It’s compassion for the human condition, for the pain that causes unhealthy behaviors.

Every morning have some routine of reading a page from some uplifting book, or whatever floats your boat. Be consistent with some routine, not rigid but flexible enough.

Manage social media so that you’re only seeing articles that are supportive of your healthy lifestyle. You don’t have to isolate yourself from bad stuff happening in the world, but be mindful of the thoughts that you’re ingesting, just like the foods you’re eating.

Every day practice gratitude. Gratitude is more than feeling lucky when things are going great. Even when you’re in pain, you can reach for inner resources and feel your strength in that. Dig deep and there is always gratitude. We can’t depend on circumstances to be great in order to feel okay.

There are many tricks from Neurolinguistic Programming (NLP) that can help to reframe your view when you're stuck in a thought pattern. Thoughts aren’t good or bad, but thought patterns we get stuck in that cast a shadow over everything aren’t useful or health promoting. Much of that is just habit patterns that can be broken, even long held ones that go back to how your parents treated you.

Recognize your victim consciousness and learn to disengage from that drama. Read about the **Drama Triangle,** a great model for understanding dysfunctional patterns.

Disengaging from dysfunctional relationships doesn’t mean shutting your heart to others. It just means refusing to engage in the drama. You can choose your friends, but aversion to others is the flip side of the same dysfunctional coin. Cultivate like-minded friendship but watch out for an “us vs them” mentality.

Cultivating healthy lifestyle is not the norm. Celebrate this. Respect yourself for this great commitment, no matter what your life looks like on the outside. Know that YOU are not your thoughts, your judgments, your work, your finances, your relationships, your illness, or your body. You are vastly more creative and resourceful and resilient than you know. No need to have a formal meditation practice or follow a particular spiritual tradition, but ponder this beautiful mystery every day.

Fun tip: When you notice a stuck thought pattern, for example, “Why is he always such a pain in the ass!” Say it loudly in the best “Minnie Mouse” voice you can. Practice it. You will crack yourself up and forget what was so serious!

### karenr — 2018-01-06T01:33:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-231733>

Ordered my DDW! I chose the least expensive option, the 85 ppm instead of the 25 ppm, about $24 per 1.5 liter bottle, plus $60+ shipping to NY, yikes, well I expected such. This is not exactly Amazon Prime! And I'm not shopping around for bargains in this case, but just going with Cignature Health's protocols seems right for me.

Can’t really figure how long that amount will last me, diluted to ~105 ppm, so I'll just wait and see.

Got some more info on testing - the Breathy test measures the condensation from your lungs which indicates what’s in the tissues, whereas the other ones that test saliva and urine are only looking at the D that you’re excreting, which is useful but not needed for now.

The first Breathy test is a baseline and doesn’t really have any bearing on how you use the water for the first 4-6 weeks. After that time on the water, I do another Breathy test and then they can advise me on how I need to adjust the schedule for the water, to get to target level and maintain.

Woohoo!

### karenr — 2018-01-14T03:05:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-232492>

Filling in some details on my new DDW protocol that started today! I'm using Preventa 85 ppm. As I posted in the Deuterium testing thread, my baseline Breathy test result from Cignature Health was 148.7 ppm deuterium in the tissues. Should be < 130.

The second number, the theoretical correlation of D to ATP is 14.0 and should be >50.

Usually the Breathy test is repeated 4-6 weeks after starting the water, but I'll wait longer, because I'm taking it slower..
My plan is to use the DDW undiluted as Anne Cooper suggested, but only 16 oz per day, to make it barely affordable. My understanding is that a small amount is better than nothing, so it seems that using about half my water intake as DDW instead of all of it, I can still expect to see progress even if more slowly.

So starting the water today, I noticed I was peeing about every hour for four hours, that’s unusual. Nothing else to report yet.

Excellent interview below with Dr Que Collins on ketogenic diet.. most interesting to me were the points about deuterium, and in relatively simple terms. I really appreciated his comment about a patient who had swallowing difficulty and was resorting to drinking Ensure - basically saying that the DDW could give him more energy so that he could eat. Makes me feel hopeful that the water can help and my poor diet won't sabotage that!

_Suddenly it starts to make sense that the eating issue doesn’t have to be this huge roadblock._

After so many years I'd given up hope on some level, and kind of forgot the truth of the matter, that it shouldn’t remain such an enigma, but that getting to the bottom of it is very possible.

Something I should start to expect. Not to just hope wistfully. And not the victim stance that has to keep fighting for lost power. All that inner kicking and screaming, no wonder I'm exhausted! Finding out that activating the will that has the real power to heal, comes from a whole 'nother place.

Why it’s taken me decades of inner and outer work to develop this, I don’t ask anymore, but I know that there is a power of decision that’s quick like lightning and doesn’t require a long hard slog to work through.
Do I keep saying this same thing in different ways?! Feels like a new hit of inspiration each time.

### karenr — 2018-01-14T04:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-232494>

Hey @[Fausty](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20679/) thanks for chiming in. My situation involves two thoracic vertebral fractures, and I have painful costochondritis to boot, so motion has to be very limited. But I’ll check out the “functional patterns”.. pretty impressive that it helped you so much!

My favorite movement system has been Hanna Somatics Education, and I wonder if there are similarities.. have you come across that? Somatics helps establish reconnection between the brain and the muscles, so that unhealthy habits of contraction can be released. The feedback that the slow movements are giving the nervous system is teaching it how to regain control of the muscles without all that useless stress.

I love the concept that Thomas Hanna described as “sensory motor amnesia” which is when the connection is broken, and in that condition, any kind of training even yoga isn’t teaching the nervous system anything and you may just be reinforcing the unhealthy pattern.

And the concept of “pandiculation” as an active training as an alternative to passive stretching. Maybe I mentioned this before in my journal, sheesh, I don’t remember! But this practice of very subtle, small movements similar to Feldenkrais method, has been the only kind of movement I can do without pain. And sometimes the movements even have to be so small and subtle that I’m barely moving at all, but the sensory-motor re-education is happening even then, quite an elegant thing!

Well, thanks for the reminder, and I'll go check out the video..

### karenr — 2018-01-14T04:45:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-232497>

Ok.. another favorite method, Buteyko breathing. Been doing my own version of it for quite a while..
So just now I was rewatching the video of the panel discussion with the Cignature Health folks on deuterium, and Dr Que Collins was talking about the mask that they sell for depleting deuterium, which is based on the Buteyko principle.. he said that the air we exhale is lower deuterium than the air we're breathing, so that’s why we want to recycle that and breathe that back in. I understood that mechanism as the Bohr effect, the presence of CO2 allowing hemoglobin to release O2, but I never heard that bit about deuterium being the reason!

Now the question is, why would the exhaled air contain less deuterium, given that the body is trying to release deuterium?

Anyway, the mask is just supposed to be easier, but there are lots of variations of Buteyko breathing techniques that aren’t difficult at all, and which personally I'd rather do than wear a mask, so I'll pass on that one. Was just wondering about the deuterium connection.

### karenr — 2018-01-18T15:45:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-232835>

Day 6 on DDW (Preventa 85 diluted to 105 ppm).

I'm not sure what to make of this, but after a few days I started feeling very wired, and after 5 days, full blown exhaustion with physical agitation that I can’t associate with anything else. Chronic fatigue, dysautonomia and chronic fight-or-flight is usual for me (tired but wired is the pits) and being extremely sensitive in all kinds of ways.. but this was way more intense than usual, requiring sedative drugs to get any sleep, even herbs and all the relaxation techniques couldn’t touch it.

Seems odd, because DDW is not some kind of adrenal stimulant, as far as I know! But I decided to test it by going off the water for a few days and see how that goes. Then start up again with less, or more diluted.

I'm thinking it would be good to have a forum thread dedicated to DDW experiences and test results.

### karenr — 2018-01-18T15:53:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-232838>

Just to fix the discrepancy in my reporting.. initially I started the Preventa 85 at full strength, for the first 2 days, and a smaller amount. Then I received a personalized protocol from Cignature Health with recommendations for diluting to 105 ppm, so I switched to doing that.

If anyone is buying the water from CH and didn’t automatically receive the protocol PDF, ask for it specifically. I thought the instructions would be included with the water, but they email that separately.

1\. GENERAL WEAKNESS, DESPONDENCY, DROWSINESS
"Weakenss and drowsiness are attributed to the necrosis of tumour mass and the reactions connected with it."
2\. FLUSH, OCCASIONAL HIGH TEMPERATURE
"Flushing of the face and local skin blush were frequently observed"

4\. ALLEVIATION AND CESSATION OF PAIN

6\. WARMING OF THE TUMOUR AREA

"attributed to necrosis of the tumour, it is advisable to consult a doctor and test blood levels for uric acid"
