### karenr — 2018-01-29T17:04:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-233739>

Two part entry this week. What’s been going on under the hood, then my DDW diary.

The usual jumble of symptoms (although I suspect a lot of detox is going on, and I view all this in a larger context that will make more sense over time)..
Constant upper body inflammatory pain. Crushing fatigue, insomnia. Migraines of various degrees of bearablity. Passed kidney stones the other day. itching all over flaring up at night. Physical jitters that prevent any feeling of rest. Whew.

What’s new, for one thing, is an inner declaration that the nights of sleep meds are numbered. I take miniscule amounts, but over the years it’s taken its toll. So I had 3 nights in a row of falling asleep naturally, which was a huge deal for me. My body remembers how to be human, unbelievable. Tears of joy, what a profound relief.

Not easy to tough out the awful sensations and jumping out of my skin until that happens, but I'm staying the course more often now, and often I wake up with a smile for the win. Used to be that taking the damned pill seemed the compassionate thing to do. Now it’s not so black or white.

I know of loads of tricks for natural sleep inducing, which don’t make a dent when I'm a jangled mess of stress hormones that won’t quit. But some nights I just find that the will to get through it is stronger. Like it’s already a fait accompli, “This drug business is on its way out,” comes to mind.

Then the image of Yul Brynner in The Ten Commandments decreeing, “So let it be written, so let it be done!” (except he was the bad guy, of course  But it's the kind of proclamation that has the power of truth behind it to move mountains. Knowing they’re already moved, not meekly hoping that they might.

Fearful desperation never led to progress before, and I don’t get caught up in that mindset for very long anymore. What is productive is just being with whatever I'm experiencing, in a simple way, as experiences come and go like clouds passing.

It’s not the false dichotomy of passivity vs action. But the best interventions arise out of that peaceful mind. Reminiscent of the I Ching perhaps, in the sense of stopping the whirlwind of habitual action and letting action flow from the deeper springs of wisdom.

But I never learned more from any spiritual teachings than I have from my own intimate experience of listening to the body. Even when all I want to do is make the symptoms shut up, I can open my heart to that. No resistance ever healed, only love that understands that sickness dissolves in the face of it. The strength of will to heal that’s grounded in love, that’s the ticket.

There's no prescription to follow for how to be with pain or adversity, but in the past month I find myself completely changed, in the face of the same old shit. And watching the same old shit change, from that place, is a whole nother ballgame than the fearful controlling game I was stuck in, addicted to, my whole life.

A new level of self-reliance now. The search and the focus on getting well is great, of course, but there comes a time to let go the dependence on external teachers and teachings to rescue you. The belief in the all-powerful rescuing guru is gone. That was always an inherently disempowering setup. I'm finding that I'm learning a lot now from “ordinary” friends on the path.

Ironically, I think it’s this kind of settling back into myself, that is really allowing for all the new helpful information to hit the spot.. Jack's teaching and all the research being done on the forum, using the DDW, etc.. but my taking responsibility for my healing in a new way is what allows all these things to come into my life in a real tangible way that moves things forward. Without the fearful fixation on “I gotta find the solution!” a more peaceful quality of will emerges, driving in a lower gear, more powerful than noisy bravado or tantrums of a victim. And how subtle those tantrums can be, in someone with a reserved, nicey-nice persona, ha!

So next up, the actual DDW diary

### karenr — 2018-01-29T18:12:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-233740>

Now for DDW diary, first two weeks!

Jan 15, 2018.
Began Preventa 85, 16 oz diluted with 8 oz spring water = 24 oz/day at 105 ppm
(I'm very small and it’s a stretch to drink even that much)

(For my own reference, 16 oz of 85 ppm = $9/day, buying one case at a time at $280 + $60 ship)

1/18 - felt extremely wired, stopped DDW for a few days.
1/20 - resumed slowly, felt ok
1/24 - Passed kidney stones, severe abdominal pain in waves and blood in urine.
1/26 - evening burst of “energy” - still feeling like stress hormones not real energy, but was able to do a lot of movement I haven’t done in years. Just that one evening.
1/28 - feeling flu-like, wall of exhaustion
Very wired, little sleep

Hard to pinpoint causes, so I'm just watching this. Not sure if the water could be overstimulating in some way.. I do have the issue of weaning off sleep meds, and the ongoing dysautonomia and wired-but-tired feeling is chronic for me, so it’s hard to tell if the water is bringing this out more..

I stopped the Sperti Fiji lamp for one week to test whether I was reacting to that, doesn’t seem to be a correlation.

In any case, I get a sense that pieces are being thrown up in the air to be reorganized, so I'm not concerned that things are out of whack.. I was pretty whacked to begin with!

Also.. diet.. it's the first time I can even consider making changes! The resolve came over me to get glyphosate out of my life. So this is a step beyond the helpless position I’ve been stuck in. Two years ago I couldn’t get anything down but bread and crackers and juice. Then I had to try to sneak in anything that I could tolerate, no real choice. That slate is cleared. I still need to do some grains, but I have some real choice now.

(Yes, I will increase the time!)

If there's any other way to test the power of those bulbs, that would be interesting, but for now, this lamp seems the best game in town for someone with very limited sun exposure!

### karenr — 2018-02-03T23:35:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-234054>

Super excited about a new diet direction.. not only getting rid of glyphosate and the awful gluten-free products (gluten-free-free at last!) but also - getting raw dairy from pastured Jersey cows!! Now that I have a good source, which I had forgotten about for years!

It came up in a conversation about The Milk Diet as a remedy for chronic disease, based on the work of Dr Charles Porter,
[https://www.amazon.com/Milk-Diet-Re...\_title\_0?\_encoding=UTF8&qid=1517692509&sr=8-1](https://www.amazon.com/Milk-Diet-Remedy-Chronic-Disease/dp/1375965352/ref=tmm_pap_title_0?_encoding=UTF8&qid=1517692509&sr=8-1)

You basically stay in bed for a few weeks drinking only highest quality raw milk, a mono diet, and taking specific therapeutic baths. Requires an assistant, as you’re supposed to rest and not work. Ahh, I can envision being waited on hand and foot!

But not exactly doable for me, besides the fact that I can’t stand drinking milk, and never did... although it’s said that the taste is acquired, as you do the mono diet and get used to the purity of raw milk from pastured A2 cows. Still, I don’t think I’ll be doing this protocol, but it got me thinking about pastured dairy as a deuterium depleted food given that “what you eat eats” is its natural diet. Thoughts on that?

So this opens up an area of my diet that has a lot more options and more doable for me than meats and seafood at this point. I was getting organic, raw cheese, but not A2, and that might make a big difference. And what’s considered raw is often heated to a certain extent, which may still damage the protein.

So I'll see how financially feasible it will be to order such pure stuff on an ongoing basis, but I'm pretty excited about the whole deal. Price per pound is actually low, but shipping, not so much.

### JanSz — 2018-02-04T15:22:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-234106>

> [karenr said:](/goto/post?id=234054)
>
> You basically stay in bed for a few weeks drinking **only highest quality raw milk**, a mono diet, and taking specific therapeutic baths. Requires an assistant, as you’re supposed to rest and not work. Ahh, I can envision being waited on hand and foot!

You are practically describing my life from 5 to 13yo (1945-1953)

I had two highly attentive waiters, my Mom and my Dad, dirt poor farmers on tiny farm.

5AM, (pre-breakfast) my Mom comes to my bed with a kiss and large beer stain (25oz =~ 0.75 liter) full of warm eggs and milk that she just got from cow and chicken still sitting on their eggs. My Dad would watch her to make sure Mom did all as she should.
After drinking all to the last drop, I would went back to sleep.

7AM, wake-up time and then breakfast.

....
March 1953 Stalin died.
Confusion in school. Teachers do not know what to teach.
I tear apart (beautifully made) book teaching about Stalin's virtues.
My parents are scared, sure that we will be taken all to jail or straight to Siberia.

....

### karenr — 2018-02-04T15:39:41+0200

I bet it wasn’t easy for them, but I bet they didn’t have to study nutrition and unlearn propaganda to know how to do right the things that mattered. Thanks for sharing that.
When did you come to the US? Maybe you've posted this before and I missed it.

### JanSz — 2018-02-04T17:14:30+0200

I bet it wasn’t easy for them, but I bet they didn’t have to study nutrition and unlearn propaganda to know how to do right the things that mattered. Thanks for sharing that.
> When did you come to the US? Maybe you've posted this before and I missed it.

Came to US (legally) 1972 (worked on being legal, for over three years).
After my Dad died my Mom came to live with us 1980.
I enjoyed my Mom's company for 25 years, till March 2005. She was 95 yo.

Had I knew Jack's preaching's then, she could have been still around.
She basically died of cold.

Be careful of March time.

-   _Kiedy marzec przeżył starzec – będzie zdrów,_
-   _Kiedy baba w marcu słaba – pacierz mów._

### karenr — 2018-02-04T18:18:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-234125>

My mom is about the same age now as yours was, 96, and is declining fast now. But was basically healthy most of her life, grew up on traditional foods, lots of chicken fat! And bone marrow - she said they spread it on bread like jam!!

Interesting traditional wisdom about March, well noted. Maybe it’s the “darkest hour is just before the dawn” of spring? And “April is the cruelest month” (or end of March, change of season). I did have my worst medical crises at that time of year. And it can be very cold up here then, and windy. This year will be better, I'm sure, whatever the weather brings.

Just reminds me.. my grandmother who lived with us for many years, spent a lot of her time in later years sitting outside. Even in winter, she would sit under a pile of blankets and gaze at the trees. She didn’t know anything intellectually about the benefit of sunlight, but her instinct was intact! She also walked everywhere. Good to remember these good habits in our families.

### karenr — 2018-02-09T16:39:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-234416>

DDW update.. this installment - DDW on a tight budget!

Anne from Cignature Health suggested the best option economically would be the 65 ppm next time, instead of the 85 ppm I'm using now. (65 ppm gets diluted 1:1 to get to 105 ppm). The 25 ppm even though the most cost effective over time, is just too much stress on my cash flow and my nerves! So the 65 seems a good compromise. Shipping is $60 each time, so I want a supply to last longer.

Anne also suggested that the DDW be my highest priority, and to skip the expensive supplements and spending on other therapies. That helped straighten out my priorities. I was going in that direction anyway, and only using certain supplements to fill in for my dietary deficiencies, like MitoLife omega-3 oil, Standard Process Catalyn (containing organ meats), sometimes some Vit K2 (Life Extension Super K), sometimes some chlorella. Plus some Chinese herbal formulas from my acupuncturist. And microdosing medical cannabis. Very micro.

Meantime, I got my raw dairy products from all grass-fed Jersey cows - just cheese and butter this time, but what a treat! A trusted source for highest quality. I'm thinking now that with the DDW, and having these wholesome foods as staples, will allow me to be a little less strict with the rest of my food quality. Buying everything so-called organic to avoid glyphosate as much as possible, is just too expensive. And who knows what we're actually getting, but it helps to use some guidelines even if imperfect..

This is the list I'm using, from
[http://janeshealthykitchen.com/avoid-these-foods-with-monsantos-glyphosate/](http://janeshealthykitchen.com/avoid-these-foods-with-monsantos-glyphosate/)
Avoid these Common Sources of Glyphosate:

1.  Avoid the primary GMO foods including all soy, corn, canola.
2.  Avoid all non-organic cereals and grains, which are harvested with glyphosate, including wheat, barley, buckwheat, millet, rice, oats, rye, sorghum, wild rice, popcorn, and teff.
3.  Avoid additives such as corn syrup, maltodextrin, fructose, baking powder, natural flavors, and cornstarch.
4.  Avoid all non-organic canola oil. (including non-GMO canola!)
5.  Skip prepared foods high in glyphosate include bread, muffins, and crackers, snacks.
6.  Avoid sweets such as cookies, pastries, bars, sugar, cane sugar, beet sugar, corn sugar, chewing gum, high fructose corn syrup.
7.  **Fruits and veggies to avoid include non-organic varieties of papaya, zucchini, summer squash, potatoes, apples, oranges, lentils, beans, peppermint, spearmint, peas, instant tea, and spices.**
8.  All animal foods (alfalfa, corn, hay, fodder, and soy) as well as the resulting meats, poultry, dairy and eggs, contain glyphosate.
9.  Prepared foods with glyphosate include bread, potato chips, corn chips, multi-grain chips, popcorn, soy sauce, soy milk, rice milk, almond milk, corn tortillas, olive oil, and canned fish in soy oil.
10.  Nuts such as almonds and sunflower seeds may contain glyphosate.
11.  Avoid prepared foods labeled just “organic”, as they may legally contain up to 5% non-organic ingredients. Instead look for prepared foods marked “100% organic”. Better yet, skip prepared foods altogether and cook from scratch.

There are only a few veggies on that list that I often use, so those are the only ones I'll buy organic. My diet is still pretty compromised in any case.. that's why I'm going with the idea that DDW will cover the important bases and everything else will come out in the wash. Maybe even the dreaded mixed metaphor syndrome.

This is all subject to change.. priority juggling with great flexibility is an important skill to practice! But it really helps to have a very specific focus to avoid overwhelm. Or bounce back from inevitable bouts of overwhelm.

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) , thanks, I'm feeling better about white rice! I never liked whole grains even when they were obligatory back in the tofu and brown rice days. ### karenr — 2018-02-09T18:41:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-234433>

So to summarize how I'm feeling on the DDW.. so encouraged. And miserably exhausted like my usual flare ups that seem viral to me. Shaking exhaustion, like my energy source is stress hormones. Although that’s nothing new. It’s hard to make clear correlations (but what a relief that correlation trumps causation!).

Something a former teacher of mine once said about treatment, when I asked how do we know that what we're experiencing is really due to the treatment and not just a post hoc fallacy? He said, when you use a powerful treatment, it's like there was a room full of people talking quietly, and now someone with a lot of charisma enters the room. Everyone takes notice, and that person affects the energy of the whole room after that. So DDW has entered my room in a big way.

### karenr — 2018-02-17T17:02:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-234981>

One month on DDW!
Some observations.
First the nasty part.. the flareup of chronic fatiguey flu-like symptoms, especially exhausted but wired and jittery, awful sleep, nightmares, etc..
Not sugar coating it, but all this really means nothing in the larger scheme of things!

What does mean something is the improvement in the migraine pattern.. I've had none of the soul crushing episodes in about 5 weeks. I used to have them every 1 or 2 weeks, since I stopped taking the horrid triptan drugs in Dec 2015 and began going through these unspeakable experiences without pain meds. Now they’re just nasty but in the tolerable range, and less frequent. This is new.

A bit of a tangent, but in the next post, I’ll write up a brief summary of the saga that I feel should be told.. about how I got rid of my cardiac arrhythmias that were escalating dangerously in 2015. And what the triptan drugs did to me, how my docs were leading me down a path of escalating pain and dangerous interventions until I pulled the plug on all their advice, identified the offending drugs that were causing the problem in the first place, got off them on my own, and got rid of the arrhythmias!

So..Sleep. Getting off the sleep meds still feels like a long slog, not a straight uphill climb, yet I think I'm ahead compared to where I was a few months ago. To have at least a few nights a week of falling asleep naturally, feels like an immense relief. I wake up exhausted as usual, but there’s a strong sense that something is beginning to right itself.

This is how my attunement to natural cycles is happening VERY gradually but taking every opportunity to nudge it along seems to be working.

Along those lines, I'm resurrecting my old photo blog, posting daily pics of the wooded area from my balcony, a lot to see when I'm looking for detail. Got the Helio app that reminds me at the Golden Hour, “Grab your camera and get outside!” This is one notification beep that I actually enjoy!

Even when it’s necessary for me to stay inside, being aware of sunrise and sunset and looking through the window when necessary - even that is a life changing routine. Noticing the shadows and light and how the colors change, the position of the sun each day, all that focuses my attention where it should be, and I'm getting the experience of natural light even when I can’t do it perfectly optimally. But this is the way toward that goal.

Ideas are percolating along the lines of @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) 's mention of Ellen Langer's fascinating work. (Thanks for that.) I ordered her book Counterclockwise. Will see how I can apply the idea in various ways.. currently I’ve been bringing more things into my life that remind me of times when I was strong, healthy and happy. That would be some times in early childhood, around playing piano, and also first year of college, also intense piano study.

Not just the visualization of it as if viewing it on a screen, but the full sensory experience of it - that’s the difference. How the exhilaration of working with my teacher and penetrating the depths of Beethoven felt, in my body. Sights, sounds, smells, visceral feelings. Maybe the practice rooms did have fluorescent lights. But there was lots of outdoor time in between practice time, and I can tune in to how good that felt in my body, even now.

So I figured out, this is why I enjoy looking at old pictures of myself! Narcissist? Maybe a tad! But more so, it’s about the urge to recapture that essential feeling of wellbeing. It’s not just nostalgia for some idealized memory and wanting to deny present reality to live in the past.. but realizing an essence of myself that never changes.

I used to be immersed in spiritual search, that’s a whole 'nother topic, but these days it’s not any kind of rarefied experience to seek, just ordinary everyday experience that's right under my nose. And some very simple routines that help me realign with natural rhythms and experience something more fundamental to who I am than misery and pain.

Ordering my next case of DDW 65 ppm next week!

### karenr — 2018-02-17T18:12:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-234988>

Here's a description of Ellen Langer's study in 1981, having a group of men in their 80's experience life as it was for them in 1959, immersing themselves in those experiences..
[https://www.nytimes.com/2014/10/26/magazine/what-if-age-is-nothing-but-a-mind-set.html](https://www.nytimes.com/2014/10/26/magazine/what-if-age-is-nothing-but-a-mind-set.html)

Most fascinating to me was the control group who only _reminisced_ about the past, but didn’t actually embody the whole experience as the study group did. Sheds light on why a lot of the positive thinking approaches don’t work.. I spent a lot of years with the New Thought movement, mindfulness meditation and positive affirmations.. and just now am having this light bulb moment about the distinction between thinking _about_ an experience vs a full embodiment of it.

Ted talk by Langer, specifically on the study, at 16:10

### JanSz — 2018-02-17T18:17:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-234989>

> [karenr said:](/goto/post?id=234981)
>
> Ordering my next case of DDW 65 ppm next week!

**Yes, DDW is where future is.**
Today is a day #69 while on Preventa-25 one part, and two parts of waters.
First Coconut warer(bad), CostcoRO (neutral), Aquafine (who knows).
I am sure you are interested in my maximum volume that I pee getting off the bed.
It varies, but from time to time I am reaching new heights.
Today is my new max since my prostate surgery 8 years ago.
385cc at one shot.
Since I have more and more ability to control urges,
I am beginning to think that I could probably gain some by sleeping deeper.
Pressure on my bladder wakes me up.
When I do not sleep it is much harder to not concentrate on bladder pressure.
I have a good sleep.
Go to bed 10PM fall asleep right away.
It is different story around 4AM, when I have 6 hours of good sleep behind me.
**Even if I go to bathroom, it is harder to fall again asleep.
That looks like low melatonin.**
Actually on my DUTCH test my melatonin is not that great.

**Not really sure what I can do about it.**
Overall I think I have pretty good light exposure.
Possibly somewhat exaggerated by artificial light,
but hopefully good,
specially knowing that I was starting from the low line.

### karenr — 2018-02-17T18:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-234991>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) , haha, sure, of course pee volume is always of interest. Since you already have indications of improvement, doesn’t it make sense to expect more improvement on the DUTCH test? When was the last one done?

I find that I have to be very mindful when I get up during the night, and it's a challenge to be mindful at that time, but when I try to keep my eyes half shut and **very carefully** make my way to the bathroom, my chances of getting back to sleep are better. Even my small amber flashlight can be too much light, and I feel more awake from the brightness of the light than the color.

Have to use some light for safety, as little as possible.. but to focus on the fact that improvement with more uninterrupted sleep is already in the works.

Nice.
\-----------------------------------------------------------------

This tables below, should give you some idea of how much DDW and what kind is need per pound of mammal, and for how long (at the minimum).

All those discomforts that you are describing
make me think that it is working for you (in the good way).

Describe what you mean by:
**I can only drink about 24 oz/day currently**.

That 24oz what is it? How many ppm's water that is?
24 oz=709.765cc=0.7 liter

is that 0.7liter of 65ppm per day?

I can post it larger (like the first table), but it is going to be in pieces.

Thanks, I actually have the book by Dr Somlyai, very impressive.

> Describe what you mean by:
> **I can only drink about 24 oz/day currently**.

I meant 24 oz of the diluted water, (85 ppm diluted to 105 ppm). I also pour some of the 85 ppm undiluted water into foods that could stand to be more watery, so I get a little more there. But the reason why I'm drinking less than a liter is because of my basic appetite issue. I just can’t take much food or water without feeling too full.

I’m thinking now that I should start with the water before meals, so I don’t fill up too much from food. It’s tricky, since I need to maximize my food and water intake both! I'm so used to being so focused on eating as much as possible, now I might be able to relax that and focus on water more.

I'm only 93 lbs now, and probably don’t need to drink more than a liter/day anyway, so maybe I'm not that far off.

I'm still not able to digest dense foods like meat, fish and eggs, but I have to trust that will come in time as it all shakes out! The great quality cheese and butter I'm getting now will help with some fats.

Oh, and thanks for supporting my feeling that the Breathy test might be postponed!

### JanSz — 2018-02-17T23:38:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-235017>

93lb=43kg

So you are good if you drink 0.75 liter of water

to get there if you want to drink 105ppm and have
85ppm
(2\*85+1\*150)/3=106.7ppm
(Two volumes of 85ppm mixed with one volume of ordinary water)

or 65ppm
\=(65+1\*150)/2=107.5ppm
(One volume of 65ppm mixed with one volume of ordinary water)

/

### karenr — 2018-03-02T22:47:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-236162>

Update! Phone consult with Dr Que Collins!
I’ve been in an overwhelming symptom flareup lately, especially eating/digestion and the constant crushing fatigue is so hard to live with even at minimal functioning. So I wanted to check in with him and see if I'm on track with the DDW.
Really glad I did.

The take-home from this chat- the DDW is doing what it’s supposed to - now my nanomotors are spinning and the ATP is being used but there is so much repair work that needs to be done, that ATP is being used for those purposes first. So it makes sense that I'm feeling like a very special sort of crap right now.

He will be sending me additional recommendations, but we talked about “The Mask” - I was under the impression that it was just like doing Buteyko breathing, which I already am well versed in, but haven’t seen much results with it. My control pause or BOLT score is still under 10 seconds. .

Apparently the mask can be used 20 minutes twice a day for the same reduced breathing you’d get with Buteyko methods, but it’s more efficient. AND, it has the added benefit of having you breathe the moist exhalaed air which is deuterium depleted, and if you can use it during the night, it’s like you’re getting the benefit of drinking DDW all night! That was an interesting tidbit!

Also I was reminded that migraine is a metabolic issue, and fixing the metabolism as we're doing should help that.. increasing insulin sensitivity in the brain. But we have to deal more aggressively with the breathing. The cardiac insufficiency I have doesn’t have to hold me back - fix the metabolism and all these things improve.

Oh, and he asked me if I'm out in the sun every day! I reminded him that I'm in upstate NY - we had a snowstorm this morning! I told him I'm using the Sperti lamp in the meantime, but he may have some other recommendations on that front.. more soon.

### karenr — 2018-03-10T21:46:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-236878>

Quick one for now.. starting to surface from a kind of flu, sparing the gory details.. I say “kind of,” even though I had high fever which is not my usual “flu-like” experience, it becomes harder and harder to pin these things down to a simple diagnostic category that tells you where you stand. No, it has to be more enigmatic than that!

So after about 6 weeks on DDW, symptoms all began to flare up to where I could barely manage them all at once, a whole new level of out of control... and then after about a week of that, the acute flu symptoms hit.

More correspondence with Dr Q, he suggested “keto flu,” which seemed curious, since I'm not on a keto diet by any stretch. (Although I have at times in my life been in ketoacidosis from starvation, not the same thing!) Anyway, I learned that being on the DDW can cause “keto flu” because keto diets are really DD diets. Light bulb moment!!

So the term “deuterium depletion flu” was coined, which seems more precise. And the general recommendation being to eat more food, fats, salt, 4 Brazil nuts a day (selenium) and potassium citrate supplement. (I thought the soil was selenium depleted?) but I can manage a Brazil nut, yum.

I won’t be doing The Mask, because of my very low control pause and hence need to adjust air flow and not restrict too much..but am practicing my Buteyko methods more diligently now. I learned from Patrick McKeown a bunch of ways to make it very easy, and it doesn’t have to be as complicated as the earlier writing on Buteyko made it out to be! His book _The Oxygen Advantage_ and especially _Close Your Mouth_ for practical instructions.

>
> More correspondence with Dr Q, he suggested “keto flu,” which seemed curious, since I'm not on a keto diet by any stretch. (Although I have at times in my life been in ketoacidosis from starvation, not the same thing!) Anyway, I learned that being on the DDW can cause “keto flu” because keto diets are really DD diets. Light bulb moment!!
>
> So the term “deuterium depletion flu” was coined, which seems more precise. And the general recommendation being to eat more food, fats, salt, 4 Brazil nuts a day (selenium) and potassium citrate supplement. (I thought the soil was selenium depleted?) but I can manage a Brazil nut, yum.
>
> I won’t be doing The Mask, because of my very low control pause and hence need to adjust air flow and not restrict too much..but am practicing my Buteyko methods more diligently now. I learned from Patrick McKeown a bunch of ways to make it very easy, and it doesn’t have to be as complicated as the earlier writing on Buteyko made it out to be! His book _The Oxygen Advantage_ and especially _Close Your Mouth_ for practical instructions.

Yes, brazil nuts are quite yummy, but be careful not to have too many of them. Just one of them might have close to 100mcg. of selenium, so it is easy to go over the limit.
You can get this Selenium product on Amazon to get an idea about your selenium situation.

About the product

-   TASTE N’ SCORE: Use your natural built-in navigation system to guide your health. Take the minerals your body is asking for by a simple taste test. Add a few drops to a cup of water. Taste good to mild? Take it. Not so good? Don't take it. Re-assess often. Your needs change due to diet, activity and stress.
-   100% PURE: No Preservatives, No Additives, and No Sweeteners. Contains pure, highly absorbable liquid minerals essential to human health. Minerals support all metabolic processes including heart function, enzyme production, and glucose management.
-   PERSONALIZED: 41 Mg per 10 drops - over 700 drops per bottle! Use more or less as needed or wanted. Create your own mix of minerals based on your taste score. Once you've found which nutrients your body is asking for, simply add recommended dose to a drink of your choice!
-   HEALTHIER LIFESTYLE: SENSEable Supplements provides the tools needed to create a healthier lifestyle based on the minerals your body requires. Learn how to use taste to guide your health, with a simple taste. Taste N’ Score often to maintain a healthy and balanced lifestyle.
-   1 OF 9 TASTE N' SCORE LIQUID MINERAL SUPPLEMENTS: Potassium, Zinc, Magnesium, Copper, Chromium, Manganese, Molybdenum, Selenium, and Iodine.

Thanks, Tanya! I was looking for a liquid individual minerals since I recently started avoiding excipients in supplements -- who wants more (nano) silica, titanium, microcrystallines, etc., in supplements for 'health' when we have similar/related forms floating down from the skies? They say product manufacturers are moving to all vegetarian (cellulose) capsules but I don't consider anything 'micro/nano' that's touted as not metabolized is quite ideal anymore. I'm not so sure the ability to make true nano size constituents can be assumed to be used by the body the same or they may NOT act like larger 'tiny-size' ingredients for ingestion? For example, there's controversy about "nano silver" not working like earlier forms, and not leaving the body so it may become just another (oxidized) metal lodging inside us? A long time ago I got liquid minerals from Life Balances but that name seems to be for a different business now... maybe the old one is defunct, or changed the name? Or, since the Life Balances protocol back then was based on smell & taste, these could be a new generation from back then.

Yeah, I probably can’t digest more than 1 Brazil nut at this point anyway, but was hoping to blenderize them for a nut drink that even guests might like. Maybe best to use other nuts in combination, then.

### Sheddie — 2018-03-11T19:20:30+0200

> ETA: aha! The whole test kit..[https://www.senseablesupplements.com/store/p44/Taste\_n'\_Score\_Mineral\_Kit.html](https://www.senseablesupplements.com/store/p44/Taste_n%27_Score_Mineral_Kit.html)

Yup, I did that program for a few years -- had a very useful (clinical) nutritional analysis workbook to apply to regular blood test results from the doc office.

I still had some of their mineral bottles lying around and, get this, they used the same ordering of 1-6 minerals as this supplier our Tanya linked! I think there's a connection? My bottles were made by Electrolyte LLC out of Spokane WA. The Selenium seems to be a new generation offering. Their B-vitamin 'smell & self-dose' trick was great. I regularly had one of the Bs that always stank, which meant I didn't need it. If I'd been using B-complex formula I'd be unbalancing...

Just a wild thought -- if people who from overuse of synthetic fragrances shut down their olfactory sense at the brain level, they might not be enabled to guide food and supplement information from using smell and taste??

### karenr — 2018-03-11T19:35:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-236988>

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=236987)
>
> Just a wild thought -- if people who from overuse of synthetic fragrances shut down their olfactory sense at the brain level, they might not be enabled to guide food and supplement information from using smell and taste??

That’s a wild thought I have about once a year. Because of my father. He had a concussion when he was in his 40s, and for the rest of his life (age 89) he had no sense of taste or smell! Luckily it was only those senses!

But we had interesting discussions about how he perceived foods, how his food preferences didn’t seem to change much, and how he knew that he liked or didn’t like something.. funniest part was that he always raved about my mother's cooking, when my sis and I always knew it left much to be desired. So maybe his disability was helpful to their marriage. Worse sacrifices have been made.

But taste and smell are funny things.. once in a while I'll want to throw out some food that I think tastes rotten, but I'll keep it around another day, taste it again, and it's fine.

### karenr — 2018-03-14T00:23:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-237136>

Thinking out loud. Formulating a plan for getting my EMF hygiene to the next level, and hopefully feeling a lot better. Funny, this feels more personal than sharing the details of my medical issues. It’s not so much about how to reduce EMFs.. I pretty much know what I would have to do.. but can I switch off the “it’s not possible” program, and make it possible.

With so many priorities, I didn’t get as far as I'd hoped before, when I did a consult last year with Michael Neuert and rented the meters. The info was eye opening. But with my physical limitations, I can’t sit at a desk to use a desktop computer, and the iPad just seemed necessary. I tried to hardwire it, but with the next OS update that didn’t work anymore.

So I knew this day would come.. I need to be offline most of the day, maybe just a half hour online in the morning and half hour early evening. With a hardwired laptop with wired keyboard and mouse..

This would actually be my preferred lifestyle if I could be active! IF I could be outside much of the day, take photos, and inside do music and any other sort of low tech creative activity - reading, writing, cooking, knitting, drawing, even closet organizing - I actually look forward to that! Then a half hour online for research, shopping and email. Maybe longer for certain important videos like member webinars. That balance of mostly low tech activities with very limited online time would feel so natural.

So.. the challenge is how to get with that sort of program before I'm physically able to do those activities for balance. Right now, if I'm not online whether researching or shopping or some social email, my other options for activities are very limited (not counting basic everyday stuff like domestic chores and biohacks like IR lamp, etc).. so..

I can read for maybe a half hour.
I can write longhand for short periods of time.
I always do some form of meditation that comes naturally while resting.
If that’s mainly it, that sounds harshly monastic.

Then there are other things that I can do for very short periods of time before it becomes too painful or tiring. That becomes too frustrating to pick up a project for 5 minutes and barely have time to get the juices flowing with it.
I will never give up doing that with the piano, though, even when I can only walk over to it and play standing up for 2 minutes (easier on my shoulders to stand up).

But it presents a challenge to create an offline life, being alone almost 24\\7 without going out except on the balcony, and so few possible activities.

My intention to create this is so strong that I even think I could make my existing hermetic cave even more cavelike and be ok - how about I try this for a month and see what possibilities emerge from the experience?

Speaking of facing your fears.. being even more isolated than I already have been for decades. My self-pitying dramatic self would whine, this is just crazy masochism. Even my existing level of isolation has been one of the hardest challenges of my life, and no one should have to live this way.

My drama-free self would say, Karen, you can discern the difference between drama-ridden self-torture and a truly necessary sacrifice. Do what you need to do, and you'll find your way through.

What would that look like? I don’t think I can even imagine from my limited vantage point right now. My sense is that doing it will open up the resources I'll need for really thriving in this space. Then it won’t be a sacrifice but a door.

### karenr — 2018-03-15T22:08:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-237262>

Confessions of an Internet junkie.

I'm writing this as someone who hasn’t watched TV in 20 years. No mainstream “news” all that time, only independent sources. No social media except for biohacking research on a few FB groups, checking Jack's FB posts, and a favorite meditation group of good friends.. Even I could be an internet junkie. No political rabbit holes. Well, sometimes a YouTube vid rabbit hole, and one incredibly stunning puzzle game for iOS, (the Quell series if you must know) but oh this all seemed so benign!

And my screen is totally blue blocked! And all the information I'm consuming is GOOD stuff! But the pull to consume even good information, to google everything that comes to mind just because I can, to follow up every good info lead, is really something to take a hard look at.

As I'm in the process of lowering the tech quotient of everything I do, it’s dawning on me - I have been hooked! I didn’t realize it had gone this far. But I was doing all these things -

Tracking Amazon shipments and other transactions
Checking finances more than necessary
Looking at email immediately
Checking Facebook for the latest from Jack, EMF Warriors, Biology Reboot, etc..
Checking apps like D Minder and Helios (gotta know exactly when Golden Hour is!)
Lots of maintenance and optimizing and configuring stuff to make things easier for my tired self
Lots more stuff.
“There's an app for that!”

Now this may not sound excessive for a shut-in who can’t do a lot of reading. But only as I'm withdrawing from this immersion in a world of tech am I realizing how immersed I've been. Even the convenience of setting the iPad next to me in front of the Sperti lamp to listen to a podcast.. the habit pattern and the way it's altered the natural rhythm of life is really astounding.

I worked on CRT terminals at Harvard starting 1975, one flight above the massive mainframes. Owned my first PC in 1992. So I have a history. Got my first iPad in 2011, and my health took a new dive the following year. The iPad felt like a lifesaver to help me feel connected given a lifestyle on the couch.. But the tech-immersed lifestyle seems to have been tied to the debilitated lifestyle in ways I never realized -
**The tech was making my debilitated lifestyle more livable.**
Something's wrong with that picture!

Sure, a crutch can be helpful, until it becomes an obstacle. And the transition to a higher level of functioning isn’t all steady and smooth but requires quantum leaps of faith and pulling rugs out before there's much else under there. That’s where I'm at right now. Pulling out rugs, extricating myself, and it's exhausting and discombobulating with plenty of “what was I thinking?” moments.. and so it goes.

But it takes so much energy to keep those old habit patterns under control all the time, in all the subtle ways that we're not even aware of.. so as stressful as this phase is right now, I'm already feeling a sense of relief, how freeing it’s gonna feel to simplify my life and be rid of all the “conveniences” that bit me in the butt.

### karenr — 2018-03-29T23:59:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-238276>

Still working on my nnEMF-reduction diet, just got my Acousticom-2, awesome little device! My ambient levels even when the WiFi router is off, are alarmingly high. That changes my focus and will require some more investigation.

I had rented the Acoustimer from Michael Neuert last year, but didn’t have much time to be thorough with my testing then.. now owning the meter will help to see what’s going on under changing conditions. The Acousticom-2 is a cheaper version and seems very adequate. The audio feature is great to pinpoint exactly what's being picked up, although it doesn’t go higher than 8Ghz.

### karenr — 2018-03-30T00:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-238278>

Taking a break, watching a bit of The Prisoner for around the tenth time! Classic Brit TV series from the 60s, most intelligent thing on TV ever, IMO. A gvt agent quits, is then kidnapped to “The Village”, a place where people are numbers and are controlled by various methods - radio programming, trauma-based mind control, etc., to dumb them down, erase individualism and extract critical information from them. In-Formation. A brilliant allegory, so relevant today, although the methods of control are more advanced now, but so amazing that this show existed in the ‘60s!

Of course people thought it was just a rather quirky spy thriller about how our hero keeps trying to escape but always ends up right back in The Village every time. (No spoiler there, it’s obvious!)
Quirky because it was more real than anything else on TV!

Ooh going lower, DDW at 65 ppm undiluted now! I thought that might be about right given that my daily water intake is pretty low. Asked Dr Que and he agreed with that plan.

### drezy — 2018-04-01T19:39:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-238459>

Karen I was thinking about you this morning.

Many public libraries participate in the Overdrive program. I think it allows you to checkout e-books and audiobooks from home. Depending on your screen you probably don't want e-books, but there are some good audio books out there.

I'm not sure if it breaks your internet fast, but I like to play an audio book at night because it allows me to get the house darker.

### karenr — 2018-04-02T04:08:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-238494>

Thanks! I’ve used Overdrive in the past, usually don’t find the obscure books I'm into, but maybe worth another look, and if you have any recs!

Had an Audible subscription a while back and have a lot downloaded there..even with my own picks I'm so picky about what I'll read/listen to and when!

Now I'm finding that even with the router off, I'm never seeing acceptable RF levels anywhere in the apartment. So that’s kind of putting a dent in the expectation that that would be a significant fix. Will report on my current findings with the Acousticom 2 soon.

### drezy — 2018-04-02T04:17:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-238495>

Have you digested A Short History of Nearly Everything by Bill Bryson. His thought/tangent pattern matches yours pretty well.

Less tangent-dense but also good is The Whole Shebang by Timothy Ferris

I'm not sure if they are available in audio format.

Jan, I haven’t done any further testing since my first baseline Breathy test Dec '17. Just a matter of finances, putting everything toward the DDW. My water intake is low, and I was diluting the water to ~105 ppm, so I wasn’t expecting a dramatic change in a short time. I'll probably do the test again in another month or two.

Buying the 65 ppm was suggested as more cost-effective than the 85 ppm bottles I started with, and not as big an investment as the 25 ppm. So that seemed reasonable. I started by diluting that 1:1 with spring water to reach the approximate concentration as I had before.

But recently, my appetite/eating issue has flared up, and I'm eating less and even drinking less. I described that to Dr Que and asked if it would make sense for me to drink the DDW undiluted now, since my water intake is even lower than before. He agreed, with the reminder to drink throughout the day rather than a large amount in one sitting.

I don’t know if it’s possible to have too much D in one area and not enough in another.. but I'm going on the assumption right now that continuing to deplete D is the way to go.. given that I'm always up for throwing out assumptions as understanding improves!

How many Breathy tests have you done so far, and have you seen steady improvement in the results?

### karenr — 2018-04-02T17:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-238516>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=238508)
>
> karen
> I would not mid if you started dedicated thread to the topic of using Artemisinin.
> [View attachment 7243](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/7243/)

Jan, I'm really not qualified to say a whole lot about moxa (aka mugwort or artemisia) except that I’ve been watching my acupuncturist treat me with it weekly for about 6 weeks now.

I can speak to the method, not so much on the therapeutics, just that it introduces the element of fire and energetically warms areas that are considered cold/deficient in Chinese medicine terms.

The Stomach 36 acupuncture point on the upper calf is a point that’s commonly used for moxa, and that’s exactly the one I need to warm my digestion, “kindle the digestive fire”.

This brings up an issue I’d love to hear about, how Chinese medicine fits into what we're doing on the light-water-magnetism front.

My acupuncturist uses a bamboo snuffer rather than fingers, but the direct moxibuston method basically looks like this.. The tiny piece of moxa is placed on the point, then lit with an incense stick and removed. It usually doesn’t hurt, although sometimes does a bit. There's also an indirect method.

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) , yeah, that could muddy the waters, so to speak! That’s like how I sometimes (not as often now) put a spoonful of honey in my mouth while waiting for tea water to boil, but no, I don’t put honey in my tea!

The Cignature Health folks don’t say it’s necessary to drink the DDW away from food or other drinks in order to keep the D concentration consistent, but just to drink it throughout the day, so I'm thinking that’s the key to balancing it all out.

We're naturally drinking DDW close to other drinks/food at times, and sometimes separately, probably more often separately. They say you can cook with it or make tea or coffee with it (the issue of evaporation aside), so they’re not concerned about diluting it at times with other substances of questionable D content.

I guess it all comes out in the wash. They've seen what works, including the fact that the plastic bottles don’t seem to interfere with the success of the DDW.

It is easier to see concerns via looking at what is suggested for animals.

You can get suggestions of amounts of DDW as a function of time per pound of animal.
Also, often animals are fed dry food, so those water volumes are likely close to the total daily water intake.

What is your weight?

I weigh 150"=68kg drink 2 liters/day water (averaged over 90 days) also small amounts of soups.

### karenr — 2018-04-09T17:53:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-239060>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=238545)
>
> what is your weight

I'm at 41 kg, so according to that chart I need 21 ounces. Some days I can manage that, but mostly I'm drinking less.

Now I'm mulling over an idea to get some kind of gut biome testing, was looking at Viome a while back, [http://viome.com](http://viome.com), although it’s expensive.. It's pretty obvious where my diet needs improvement, a complete overhaul, and for a long time I didn’t see the point in testing what I already know is out of whack.

Although my gut hasn’t been allowing me to make significant diet changes, which is why I've been focusing on the DDW instead. But now I'm thinking there might be some benefit to knowing some specifics about what’s going on in my gut..

The Viome website is infuriatingly lacking in critical info, like how the testing is done and what it actually tells you! That kind of dumbed down marketing really turns me off. Focusing on describing the problem, to pull you in, then jumping to the conclusion that you need this service, without explaining exactly how the service works.

But I did manage to extract details from the customer service rep like pulling teeth.. and also found a video that shows a sample test result, which does look interesting..

(Other types of testing requiring physically going to a lab isn’t doable. No independent labs near me.)

### karenr — 2018-04-09T22:17:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-239081>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=239080)
>
> And those details are????
>
> ..

There are several components -
glucose challenge drink
blood glucose test kit
stool sample kit
metabolism questionnaire.

From emails from Viome, when I asked for specifics of the testing procedure..

The challenge pack contains glucose and cream of tartar.
At a minimum, everyone should do a fasting glucose and fill in how much of the drink they drank, even if it was none. Better than that would be to drink ⅓ or ½ if they can and complete the test. Just so you know, the challenge drink has about the equivalent \[amount of sugar\] of 1 can of regular Coke, but is safer than coke. We don’t want you to substitute it.
Please let us know if you have any other questions.

1\. The ingredients: Pure Dextrose (non-GMO). Mix the Challenge Powder with 8-12 oz. of water. Stir well and drink.
I have attached the instructions for the Metabolic Intelligence test and the Gut Intelligence test for you to view.

2\. After enrolling in our service, you will receive two test kits: the Gut Intelligence kit and the Metabolic Intelligence kit. Gut Intelligence is a stool collection kit that you can complete at home and mail to Viome for analysis. Metabolic Intelligence is a series of measurements that you'll complete at home, and then enter your results into your Viome app.

Your annual membership for $399, includes one set of Gut Intelligence and Metabolic Intelligence testing, but you have the option to order additional kits throughout the year for $199 each.

We created a more flexible plan to better suit your individual needs. Maybe you'd like to test more frequently to monitor your progress, or maybe you need more time in between tests - whether you're on vacation, still working on making changes to your diet, or you just can't find the time.
We look forward to serving you!
Regards,
Ashley
TheViomeTeam

Instructions for taking measurements for the metabolic intelligence test:
[https://p14.zdusercontent.com/attac...TFH03OtPqtucljExfl-NYM.InvuJkqaoaLcBNAWCdbrxg](https://p14.zdusercontent.com/attachment/1253404/spCTEJfHobQ3Eon8GKX4dMNko?token=eyJhbGciOiJkaXIiLCJlbmMiOiJBMTI4Q0JDLUhTMjU2In0..S6XxsvwUgoXH8Xus0s6vTQ.vcTRxHSCgUwq3pOv9f_fOGu0xSXRWLeLbJhXzZ6lwIhZfs8n-yJRXt7W1vUoiXVJj54FqZWwUFHrHoLDI7G4nJ-lCNwiHzYKiT6SFS32k-MP7eG4xXRbld9K-iYa1L8OhS_Iq-MlT2sCNeRbuRoy7A0CxRDIAO4DLoc6xJk3sR_4zI0aBnAgTEWmOLXGVmSloHQCDxzYW0HDpQdB4G5UybWqXHXUDHXqHPJwbbaSLbbpgfSDPBP3OxgDoihnFAD1kefdOj68PWTltNHF2KO0dTFH03OtPqtucljExfl-NYM.InvuJkqaoaLcBNAWCdbrxg)

**Resistant Starch
**
If you are acting unusually to resistant starch, that would suggest to me that you do not have or have only little bit of bugs that feed on it.
It is good to have those bugs.
If that was happening to me,
I would try to eat resistant starches more often, expecting that after a while all that excessive gas would stop.

Green banana...

...........

Interesting, thanks, will try it. Right now I'm in a phase where I'm so uncomfortable I can barely stomach anything at all for the whole rest of the day, after eating a very small amount of easy to digest foods like bone broth with cooked carrots and a few rice crackers. Sometimes a little of the raw cheese, on a good day.

This is nothing new, a flare up of the old pattern, but I'm not going down that same old road this time.

### karenr — 2018-04-10T18:57:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-239157>

More thoughts on Viome..

1\. Based on the analysis, you get dietary advice, what to eat and not to eat. I haven’t found any info about how exactly they determine that.

2\. Maybe the microbial profile data alone could be useful, I suppose, but how can they get from there to an understanding of exactly how those microbes are expressing or not expressing in your particular case? How they're interacting in biological functions and the implications of that?
So, IS it worth getting the microbial profile data even if the limits of this kind of testing don't go further than that?

3\. I hate the marketing model and lack of discerning info they expect people to be okay with for shelling out $400. But I'm giving it the benefit of the doubt that the product could possibly have some value anyway..

I’m sure sounding like a lot of maybes and sort ofs.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-05-01T03:06:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-240267>

I'm going to put there here for your reading later.

Why avoiding functional medicine pseudoscience linked to gut health is WISE: Read this post if you’re interested in gut health, leaky gut, autoimmune disease, and how to fix it:

This paper is so full of gold nuggets that I recommend you read it carefully from beginning to end. But let me tell you the big takeaways as I interpreted it:

1) our gut epithelium (single layer of cells) is what protects us from toxins we ingest and what allows nutrients from what we ingest into our bloodstream.

2) in order for the epithelial lining in your gut to work properly, ALL of the cells must communicate with each other at all times.

3) the communication between the cells in your epithelial lining happen through electrical (LIGHT) signaling.

4) gap junctions are what lie between each cell that facilitates this electrical signal communication between the cells.

5) dysfunctional gap junctions = dysfunctional epithelial lining = leaky gut, poor nutrient absorption, toxicity in the body.

6) one of the major events that can shut down gap junction signaling is an influx of too much calcium into the cells.

7) in 2013, Dr. Martin Pall discovered that WIRELESS RADIATION (FROM CELL PHONES, LAPTOPS, BLUETOOTH, WI-FI ROUTERS, SMART METERS, MICROWAVES, etc.) directly affects the voltage-gated calcium channels sitting on the cell membrane of every cell. It “swings the gate open” and allows CALCIUM TO FLOOD INTO THE CELL. Don’t believe me? Here is the study. Read: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3780531/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3780531/)

let’s recap:

Leaky gut is real because nnEMF causes it.

Leaky gut is an epidemic of massive proportions and is possibly a root cause of all gut dysfunction, poor nutrition, toxicity, autoimmunity, autism, cancer, Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, and every other disease known to humans.

Gut permeability is regulated by the ability of cells in the epithelium to be able to speak to each other.

electrical impulses in the gap junctions between cells regulate the ability of cells to communicate to each other using electrons protons, and light.

Wireless radiation SHUTS DOWN GAP JUNCTIONS and causes breakdown of cell communication, this CAUSING LEAKY GUT.

If you cut your finger, and then subsequently reopen the cut on your finger 3 times per day, will the cut heal if you put a band-aid on it in between reopening it? That’s exactly what changing your diet and taking supplements for leaky gut is when you continue to hold a cell phone near your gut and bathe in wireless radiation 24/7.

Think about this the next time you are taking a supplement that is purported to heal leaky gut.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK26857/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK26857/)

### karenr — 2018-05-18T18:42:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-241520>

From yesterday's FB post.. **“Getting tired from the sun is a reaction to what the sun can do to a trashed hormone panel of a lifetime of mismatches cause by calcium efflux...”**

What about getting the jitters/insomnia and migraine? After a winter of using the red Sperti lamp, I was in the sun the other day for about 10 minutes, feeling very uncomfortable but figured I'm gonna need to push a bit past my comfort zone. Then spent more time with body in the shade and legs in the sun and felt better. Feels like the full brunt of solar power is just a bit much for me yet..

Then a severe migraine all day yesterday, calling for an emergency acupuncture session, which always brings the energy down from my head and resolves it. He said it was a “liver heat” condition, with excess heat getting into the blood. Treatment for that did help. Well, I do tend to have these migraines other times when I'm not in the sun, but it did seem like in this case the sun exposure could have triggered this one..

So my guess is I just have to find my sweet spot with the sun exposure and increase very slowly and carefully. And I shouldn’t be surprised that my tolerance is so low now, while I used to soak up the rays and revel in the glorious healing warmth when I was young.

Then when a friend sent me the article about the new migraine drug.. I just laughed. All they can do is keep targeting one thing or another to block, until our life energy is all blocked. I already had years of my life ruined from triptan drugs until I figured it out, and I wouldn’t touch this one with a ten ft pole..
My current mantra being “I Do Not Consent...”

### karenr — 2018-05-26T20:13:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-242090>

Sleep update. Haven’t touched the sleep meds in 10 days. Haven’t gone that long in many years! What a relief to fall asleep naturally and just trust that every time I wake I'll eventually get back to sleep again. No matter how long it takes, that’s how it goes until morning. What a revelation for my body to rediscover this natural rhythm.

Getting more natural light, I'm sure made a difference. Probably everything I've been doing helped to muster whatever kind of energy allowed me to dump the old pattern. Really feels like I got freed up from some old barnacle.

How it happened.. it wasn’t like I’d finally won the battle after such a long struggle. But I had an evening of calm, quiet knowing that this dysfunctional relationship with sleep meds is history. Knowing that for sure, in a very matter-of-fact way, very different than the perpetual struggle toward it.

Hey Phosphene! Nice to hear from you. I'm keeping on keeping on. Seem to be always busy with all the vicissitudes of life (way too complicated, if you ask me!) And I just remembered that when I turned 60, it occurred to me, NOW I certainly have the right to live life on my own terms!

And then of course I laughed at how I had made up this arbitrary timeframe for “permission” to be myself. Well, what “being oneself” turns out to be in actual experience, can be a lifetime exploration, but why I had to wait 60 years to be on board with it? Well, I guess the answer is blowing in the wind.

Happy independence on your own terms!

### karenr — 2018-07-16T20:50:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-245999>

Have become very interested in Lyme and the history of how it developed (the modern weaponized form, created at the Plum Island research lab..good YouTube vids that go into all that covert stuff)..

I grew up 75 miles from Plum Island, and my eating problem kicked into high gear in 1974, just one year before the big outbreak in Lyme, CT.

An article on Lyme that describes the severe anorexia-like symptoms that could be part of this syndrome, really struck me.
[https://www.practicalgastro.com/pdf/April06/SherrArticle.pdf](https://www.practicalgastro.com/pdf/April06/SherrArticle.pdf)
This article comes closer than anything I've seen in all these years, to acknowledge the kind of early satiety and digestive incapacity that I have, linking it to something meaningful. Anorexia nervosa is such a misleading and sometimes dangerous catch-all label that’s been used even when it doesn’t fit. And of course doesn’t lead to anything helpful.

So. In view of all this, I'm thinking about what direction to take with treatment options, given that I don’t have the energy or money to pursue a diagnosis.

Reading the forum threads on Lyme, I came across some of Dr K's comments.. the focus on nnEMF,s of course, and that's always a work in progress.. It makes sense to focus most on what's upstream (mitochondrial health), but since I'm so limited in what I can physically do, it also makes sense for me to just do all that I CAN do that seems relevant.

I’m on my last case of DDW, can’t possibly afford it anymore by any stretch. It’s a very rough time financially. Interesting that my will to heal takes me in all these directions that could be very costly, and I get to figure out the cheap DIY way.

I figure that all this time I’ve been on the DDW, it must have been helping with a lot of deep level repair work even though I haven’t felt any positive results. I'd continue if I could, but will be tapering off over the next month or two. Now, a new chapter.

### karenr — 2018-09-11T16:37:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-250736>

Always a lot going on on the inner front, not always a lot of tangibles to share..
but I wanted to share this newsletter from Jon Rappoport, one of my fave journalists (who had nomorefakenews.com decades before the term became popular)..

Usually I'd yawn when I’d see a title like this - 25 quotes on the power of your imagination.
But I gave Jon the benefit of the doubt and found some real gems here..

[https://jonrappoport.wordpress.com/2018/09/11/25-quotes-on-the-power-of-imagination/](https://jonrappoport.wordpress.com/2018/09/11/25-quotes-on-the-power-of-imagination/)

A few goodies -

“There are those who believe life is a museum. You walk through the rooms, find one painting, stroll into it and take up permanent residence. But the museum is endless. And if you were a painter, you'd never decide to live inside one of your canvases forever. You'd keep on painting."

"Imagination can be used to invent a better shade of nail polish or a universe. In a society devoted to nail polish, imagination is not to blame."

"Imagination has extraordinary equanimity. It is just as happy to entertain and embody two conflicting realities as it is to spool out one uniform reality."

"We re-learn to live through and by imagination, and then we enter and invent new space and time. But space and time aren't the superior forces. They operate and come into being at the tap of imagination."

"I'm not breaking a system into parts. I'm not trying to teach a person how to tie his shoes. I'm talking about the proliferation of endless new worlds, not seen through a porthole, but imagined and invented."

"A metaphor for imagination might be warp drive. You skip ahead in space by huge leaps. It's not 1,2,3; it's 1,2, and then suddenly four thousand. You're not working by serial cause and effect."

### Phosphene — 2018-09-11T21:03:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/karens-journal.19930/post-250754>

Nice to hear from you Karen. The first quote above about the museum struck me since I was just JUST wandering somewhat aimlessly around the house I’ve been in/out of the past 17 years, wondering (yet again) What Now?

Going through a weird stretch. Will talk more in my journal.

Was just in New England for some family stuff but can see myself taking a little solo trip before the weather degrades. Or maybe even after—I do love driving in snow. Forces me to focus.

Will keep you in the loop.

### drezy — 2017-07-31T17:41:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wonder-woman.19931/post-220533>

The beginnings of rumination on unbroken and living mitochondrial bonds crossed my mind. Then I started to think about the wonders of the possibilities and implications of Yarkofsky signaling/coupling between mother and child during feeding. In some ways, especially from a mito view, it's like watching a macro version of mitosis with frequent reconnection so as to ease the new mitochondrial holder's transition. I think I even started to connect much of what I am learning here into a beautiful framework until the fact that I was staring at boobies set in.

My thoughts ended on:
Eat up baby and take a long nap. Papa wants access to those next.

### shiran — 2017-07-31T20:02:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wonder-woman.19931/post-220542>

my thoughts at first:
me inside my grandmother inside my mother
my little girl who grew up in my womb
And the girls who grew up in her womb inside me...
Made me feel empowered
Wondrous of nature and wonderfully magical

### drezy — 2017-08-01T06:31:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wonder-woman.19931/post-220586>

Thanks for getting my humor @[shiran](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20240/) and @[Allin](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/11212/). I'm under fire elsewhere. Just know that I've come to the realization that every human is an amazing being. I am honored to be able to interact with you.

@[shiran](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20240/) us males watch in on the process of child bearing and we are mystified and we are shocked and we are without words to explain what occurs.

The second that I saw my own son emerge seemed like a timieless moment. Causality itself, at least in my mind, seemed to bend. We were all there because he was GOING TO EXIST. Damn, that's tough to explain to people. Maybe I jumped the shark...

### caroline — 2017-08-02T12:41:11+0300

sharing is so important - we are all connected ......I love that.

Tony and I stayed at an airb&b last Nite and I have been in overdrive ever since. It was a simple little cabin add on to a double garage.
It is set in this beautiful little garden. I have been in awe ever since.

This place is owned by Linda. She is on her own and she looks and sounds slightly butch.....but.....

This woman overwhelms me for so many reasons. She has many skills that I totally envy and admire. Everything she does has this amazing sensitivity.
She bought a kit home and had it erected in her back garden behind her main house. Her small cottage is so simple but completely captivated me.
Her use of colours and different materials all in the same tones was so lovely. She has built a trailer to carry her quad bike and installed a kitchen it it. She has just bought a van and will convert it to a camper.

I have been ruminating on this since last nite - and driving Tony crazyI might add.

I have come to the conclusion- I _think .....she is an avid gardener and prefers to be outside all day -planting fruit trees, flowers, veg, painted the garden gate red etc.etc. I think this beautiful sensitivity comes from being connected to mother nature and translates to everything she touches._

IF STARS LIKE THE SUN IS TOXIC WHAT ABOUT MITOCHONDRIA? How can the sun be toxic when both stars and mitochondrion make water? Now you know why I tell my members stars and mitochondrion have a lot in common..........both create water. What are those implications for our understanding? [http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2011/06/110613-space-science-star-water-bullets-kristensen/](http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2011/06/110613-space-science-star-water-bullets-kristensen/)

### JanSz — 2017-07-31T19:03:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/as-it-is-above-so-it-is-below.19932/post-220536>

Water is no question important to life.

Two things come to my mind when thinking of star shooting water:

1---Thanks to Velikowsky. Not that long ago, when Earth was within Proto-Saturn, Sun was nowhere near to be even seen. Then sun appeared, created a mess in previous order, messed up Proto-Saturn into much smaller entity, and captured all that including Earth and Mars. So if our water originated from the star it might have been a different "sun" then the present one.

2---For life, there is no need for a lots of water. Currently, including oceans, rivers, lakes and all else, relatively speaking, there is a minimal amount of water on Earth comparing to its size, just one not very big droplet.
I have a picture somewhere presenting those proportions.

....

### Ccra79 — 2020-01-13T11:49:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-saber-uvb-light.19935/post-284507>

I have found a supplier in China that can provide T8 tubes in UVB (310nm) range.
Product page: [https://www.alibaba.com/product-det...04.html?spm=a2700.12243863.0.0.2ce83e5f7UWNrn](https://www.alibaba.com/product-detail/uvb-led-311nm-light-Ultraviolet-for_62404560104.html?spm=a2700.12243863.0.0.2ce83e5f7UWNrn)
The sizes varies from 60 cm to 1.5 m.
The price is from 10 to 25 times more than the UVA versions 365 nm and 395 nm this due to the higher production cost of the chips.
For example the 1.2m:
365nm 42 USD
395nm 15 USD
310nm 415 USD

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/alibaba-prices-png.14503/) [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/alibaba-prices-led-chip-png.14504/)

365nm UV LED test report
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/365nm-uv-led-test-report-png.14529/)

# Thread: A curious mind
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-curious-mind.19937/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimal Health is EZ — 2017-08-01T18:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-curious-mind.19937/post-220636>

“In the early fifties I suffered temporarily from a disease of middle age: I used to give philosophical talks about science-how science satisfies curiosity, how it gives you a new world view, how it gives man the ability to do things, how it gives him power-and the question is, in view of the recent development of the atomic bomb, is it a good idea to give man that much power?“

\-Richard Feynman

Dr. Jack Kruse is dooming humanity. He has seen the writing on the wall and is working us towards extinction. If you listen to and follow what he says we will perish.

Richard Feynman in the 50s was one of many curious, genius minds. Inquisitive. Pressing the boundaries of known knowledge. This has been rectified.

The main signaling molecule identified with this disease was treated in an insidious fashion: using invisible energy beamed in an ambient, omnipresent manner around the world. You can’t escape it. Without this human creativity would be thoroughly unleashed.

Human minds created The Bomb. Quantum mechanics is a blind alley - ultimately it is inaccurate. Feynman in the right context with the right understanding could end the world. The world didn’t give him or his mental counterpart this option.

Whether the decision to saturate the ionosphere with ungodly amounts of electromagnetic radiation and thus destroy humanity’s dopamine was purposeful or not the end result is the same: we won’t have time to advance science enough to the point we kill ourselves. The fields of science have stagnated. Dr. Pollack has mused on this subject and now we know the answer: human beings are not what they once were. Technology is killing our brains. Somehow this is accepted perhaps fear of nuclear annihilation is so great we’d rather be dumb and stymied than creative and riding waves of new understanding into future unknown.

Listening to Dr. Jack Kruse will liberate your brain. Are you willing to be this dangerous?

# Thread: In France with insane nerve tooth pain (rooth canal??)(help)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-france-with-insane-nerve-tooth-pain-rooth-canal-help.19938/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Remo — 2017-08-02T05:20:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-france-with-insane-nerve-tooth-pain-rooth-canal-help.19938/post-220681>

friends, im in a situation i dont know how to deal with.
The last two days i have been struck by a major tooth ache which is unlike any common ache; the situation is not a throbbing/spiking pain but more of a consistent dull 8/10 pain.

I have had a similar ache in the past where my dentist could not see ANYTHING on the scan yet the pain was insane. She suspected teeth grinding or inflammation of the nerve end.

Personally i suspect it could be my lyme disease but try to explain that to people and doctors... especially in a foreign country liks france.

// if the pain doesnt subside should i get a scan rooth canal here in france or is there an alternative ?
// what if i do a scan and nothing shows up again ?
// what if it does show a root canal/ inflammation ?

\- i noticed i got more nautious/pain when i was in the car, close to 4g radio and phones.
\- I have been on my phones 3g more then usual the last days due to a lack of wifi.
\- have not been sleeping well the past days
\- i have been getting ALLOT of sun and cold water the last days, which makes it so darn frustrating.
\- my diet has consisted of implementing more carbs/breads then usual. No rich DHA food available except farmed salmon etc.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-02T05:24:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-france-with-insane-nerve-tooth-pain-rooth-canal-help.19938/post-220682>

If the tooth is not bad you got to consider trigeminal neuralgia. This is how it starts.

### Remo — 2017-08-02T05:40:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-france-with-insane-nerve-tooth-pain-rooth-canal-help.19938/post-220683>

i

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=220682)
>
> If the tooth is not bad you got to consider trigeminal neuralgia. This is how it starts.

i see, it could indeed be the case seeing i have had weird neurological symtpomes come and go in the past. Yet... why would this display now in France of all times ? I have been exercising, getting plenty of sunlight and relaxation, cold water... only thing i can think of is a big increase in omega 6.

Also, what IF there is something with the tooth, should i get a root canal here ? Please i need to know.

### Rubicon — 2017-08-02T07:12:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-france-with-insane-nerve-tooth-pain-rooth-canal-help.19938/post-220685>

Remo, this could be a long-shot, but have you considered that your bite may be the issue? I had a similar tooth pain years ago. Here's a brief history of my experience:

In 2009 when I was 31 I developed a dull pain in my bottom right second molar (#30). I had it checked at the dentist but the x-rays showed nothing. My dentist adjusted my bite several times to try to calm it down, but it did not work. It seemed to be a phantom pain, but I was advised to get a root canal which I did. The pain went away briefly but quickly returned...eventually becoming worse than ever.
In 2011 I got the tooth extracted. The pain finally went away.
In 2013 I had a titanium implant installed at the extraction site.
In 2014 I had a crown placed on the implant. At that point, the pain returned!

After a lot of research, I decided to undergo a jaw repositioning procedure using a dental orthopedic appliance that will move my teeth so that they will bite tip to tip. [http://dentalorthopedics.com/what-is-dental-orthopedics](http://dentalorthopedics.com/what-is-dental-orthopedics)
Previously I had a bit of an overbite.
I've been diagnosed with cavitations at a previous wisdom tooth extraction site, and at the implant site.
I've had issues with a chronic lyme type infection.
I suspect all of that is related.

Have you ever had any teeth extracted?
Do your front teeth bite tip to tip as seen in the skulls of ancient humans, or do you have an underbite or overbite?

Rubicon, thank you so much for your
story as it could actually makes sense!
I had a HUGE overbite as a kid which i got braces for. I had my permenant bracewire removed over a year ago, it made my bottom teeth a bit uneven again but i rather have that then have a metal rod in my mouth.
