this is also what was said which seem to support your story rubi;
If your bite is off, if your occlusion is off, if your bite is collapsed, it causes problems. Your brain has trouble detoxing. It is the relationship of the upper jaw and the lower jaw that determins the entire structure of the cranium, the jaw, the neck, the shoulders, the hands. Every joint relationship in the body is programmed by the teeth. If you have perfect occlusion in the jaw, everything in the body will fall into place

### Rubicon — 2017-08-03T05:31:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-france-with-insane-nerve-tooth-pain-rooth-canal-help.19938/post-220787>

Remo, it sounds like you could potentially benefit from a procedure to align your jaw & bite. My understanding is that the procedure was first developed in Europe, and I would expect you may have a relatively easy time finding someone there to diagnose and potentially treat you.

Regarding the root canal, I would be concerned that there is now a possibility of developing a long-term chronic infection in that dead tooth that can lead to cavitations in the jaw and other health complications. It does not happen to everyone with a root canal, but given your lyme issue that could put you at greater risk. There's lots of info online about the dangers of root canals. Personally, I would not ever get a root canal again...straight to extraction for me if I ever need it.

### Rubicon — 2017-08-03T06:14:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-france-with-insane-nerve-tooth-pain-rooth-canal-help.19938/post-220791>

OptimalSkr, yes I am currently a patient of Dr Jennings. I couldn't really find anyone that specializes in his type of treatment in Western Canada, so have been opting to travel to Oakland to visit him every few months for the last 2-years.

I've been meaning to write up a post about my experience in my journal, but here are some random notes I've taken from conversations with Dr Jennings. These were recorded from memory and I may be confusing various details.

**Background and my jaw & bite**
\- Back molars are supposed to hang out half a tooth out over the bottom tooth
\- The jaw should open and close on a smooth arc. If you track the jaw and it goes forward it's an indication of dysfunction
\- In my case, before starting treatment my back molars were off by more than 1 mm. A significant amount. 1 mm is too far to grind teeth in. At that distance they need to be orthodontically moved to correct the bite
\- Procedure involves using appliances to stabilize bite and prevent mis-aligned teeth from contacting. After a couple of months, the jaw will relax and adjust itself to where it wants to be opening on a fair arc. The back teeth will be allowed to erupt so that they hold the jaw permanently forward, front teeth biting tip to tip, and the jaw always moving on a fair arc. The individual teeth that are out of place are then moved into place with wires on the appliance. In my case it will be a ~2-year procedure.
\- Dr Jennings has seen many cases with a history similar to mine. A tooth gets sensitive, gets root canal, then next tooth gets sensitive, gets root canal, etc. If you get the jaw lined up you can reverse that sensitivity and calm them down
\- My crown/implant won't erupt with the other molars, so it will have to be re-done
\- I will have a night appliance when the procedure is complete, but eventually I won't need it.

**General Notes**
\- Treatment should help with general tooth sensitivity (it has)
\- All pain fibers from trigeminal goes to the same nucleus as the gut. When the jaw is off, often the pain can cross over to abdominal pain. Jennings has seen patients that have had exploratory surgery only to find nothing. Fixing the bite resolved the abdominal pain.
\- Hearing loss, grinding teeth, constipation, tinnitus could be related to bite
\- 100x more dense pain fibers are in the trigeminal than in the rest of the body
\- pain fibers sends signals via Substance P
\- Substance P affects gut motility, mood, hypersensitized sensory neurons (more sensitive to noise, light, drugs, stress, etc) and affects brain stem central sensory processing disorders (balance, Tourettes, scoliosis, T2D, etc)
\- It's hard to find a dentist who understands this stuff and also has the precision to finely adjust the jaw and bite. Braces don't work.
\- Sleep apnea - bringing the jaw forward brings the tongue...85% of the time fixes sleep apnea
\- Once the jaw is lined up, cavities should no longer occur ... eliminates a lot of stress on teeth demineralizing them. Jennings suggested I've been getting cavities because my bite has been off
\- Substance P controls calcium metabolism, could contribute/lead to heart attacks
\- Jaw mis-alignment can lead to anxiety issues, depression, anger

**Cavitations**
\- Biological dentists treating cavitations don't know about bite dysfunction.
\- Cavitation is considered to be a localized avascular osteonecrosis. Blood supply is controlled by the trigeminal nerve, so when the bite is off it can't supply enough blood to get things healing.
\- Some relief from cavitation associated pain can be expected when the bite is stabilized with appliances. However, full circulation will not be restored until down on real teeth.
\- Substance P will also fall completely once on real teeth

**Japanese Research, Mitochondrial Notes**
\- The Japanese have done extensive research for 40 years looking at bite destruction in animals and the effect on the body. When the bite goes off, they develop severe autonomic dysfunction.
\- Part of the reason is that the functional center in the brain that controls jaw movement is paired side by side with your sympathetic ganglion on the right. You go into sympathetic dominance when the bite goes out
\- The main purpose of the central nervous system is to maintain cell membrane integrity. With bite disturbances, elevated Substance P and sympathetic disturbance causes a major disruption in cell membranes, and when the cell membrane becomes porous calcium rushes in which messes up the mitochondria -- mitochondria primarily communicates with calcium. Book reference: Cancer as a Metabolic Disorder. The implication is that long-term bite dysfunction could be a substantial source of cancer
\- Few know about Substance P and the link to bad bites.
\- Researchers think that chronic fatigue and fibromyalgia are the same diseases with a slightly different manisfestation. In FM they have 3-5X normal levels of substance P in the spinal fluid, so they hurt all over. CF manifests with cell membrane disturbances causing a decrease in energy production by mitochondrial disturbance.
\- When you open up cell membranes, research shows that's what allows viral infiltrators. A researcher at the children's hospital in Philadelphia thinks if you could control substance P, you could control viruses like HIV

### Remo — 2017-08-03T17:23:37+0300

my sinus clears or another tooth will be infected.

### OptimalSkr — 2017-08-03T18:38:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-france-with-insane-nerve-tooth-pain-rooth-canal-help.19938/post-220843>

Rubicon. Excellent post! I have strongly considered going to Jennings because it is impossible to find someone who treats like he does. My only concern is the money issue. When I called them they said it wS about 250$ a visit and appliances in the 700$ USD. Not bad but with a flighty and hotel to Oakland it gets pricey. How have you managed all that travel every 2 months? I am unsure if the value would help my issues vs the cost. But I guess without the assessment it would hard to say. Have you seen improvements in overall health as well as the teeth? How is your timeline been going? Did it take long to see changes and how much longer do you plan on going? Thanks again.

### Rubicon — 2017-08-04T05:33:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-france-with-insane-nerve-tooth-pain-rooth-canal-help.19938/post-220879>

OptimalSkr, those cost estimates sound roughly correct. I believe my initial appliances were around $2500 USD.

I've traveled every 2-4 months. I typically try to time trips for when they're most convenient and when I'm on the way to/from the tropics in the winter. Lately, I've been using travel rewards points to cover most of the cost of the flight/hotel. Other times I've stayed at cheap AirBNB's. The bay area is expensive!

I've seen improvements in overall health, but I've been doing a lot of different things in the last couple of years so it's hard to say exactly what the contribution has been from the jaw alignment. The most directly noticeable effect has been my cavitation symptoms subsiding whenever my bite has been stable. The cavitations cause a dull pain in my right jaw and down my neck into my shoulder. My bite destabilized a few times over the course of the treatment as I went long periods of time between visits. On several occasions I consistently observed symptoms slowly return over a period of weeks/months and then disappear after getting the appliances adjusted. It took a few rounds for me to realize just how much of an effect the appliances were having on that.

Timeline:

-   June 2015: Initial consult, appliances created & adjusted over 3-days
-   Sept 2015: Jaw evaluation, appliance adjusted
-   Feb 2016: Wires were added to start pushing teeth
-   June 2016: More wires & adjustments. During this time I was adjusting the wires myself every few weeks to keep them pushing my teeth. This cut down on office visits.
-   Sept 2016: More wires & adjustments
-   Jan 2017: Changed from a twin block to single lower block appliance, upper ALF appliance
-   March 2017: Adjustments
-   July 2017: Adjustments

I experienced changes as soon as the first appliances were in use and the bite was stabilized. Things got a bit worse a few times as individual teeth were moved and the bite became temporarily destabilized.

I found Oasis Dental in Milton Ontario offers functional jaw orthopedics. They might be able to provide treatment. Or, to minimize travel perhaps go see Jennings to get diagnosed & get some appliances, and then have Oasis do the bulk of the ortho adjustments.
[http://www.oasisdentalmilton.com/bl...sful-reshaping-of-the-jaws-and-better-health/](http://www.oasisdentalmilton.com/blog/functional-jaw-orthopedic-appliances-fjo-alf-expander-facemask-bionator-twin-block-bioblock-orthotropics-homeoblock-dna-appliance-successful-reshaping-of-the-jaws-and-better-health/)

### OptimalSkr — 2017-08-04T19:32:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-france-with-insane-nerve-tooth-pain-rooth-canal-help.19938/post-220916>

Thanks for the in depth reply.  I will look into it some more and see if I can give it a go.

### Remo — 2017-08-05T16:55:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-france-with-insane-nerve-tooth-pain-rooth-canal-help.19938/post-220974>

back in holland now and my sinus/nerve problems have became much less severe. My lymph nodes are still a bit swollen but i am not worried about this. Thank you all for the support. The jaw adjustment treatment could maybe help other people so thanks for the suggestion.

My thoughts about the eclipse coming in 21-22 of august

The word eclipse - eclipse, comes from the Greek "eucalypse"
Which means leaving, liberating or abandoning.
And that is precisely what is experienced in the eclipse, the desire to surrender, to release and to abandon
The parts that limit us from expressing our greatest potential.
Eclipse generally invites us to change / release in our lives,
So we have the choice to surrender and act consciously in this internal process,
Or was chosen to rebel and resist the energy that comes. No matter what is chosen, the change will be made.

Wondering what are your thoughts?

### Vervaina — 2017-08-22T01:20:01+0300

Was near the red zone in TN for the eclipse today. Still have a headache. About 15 before the sun was at full eclipse and it got dark, the contrast around us changed. It was really weird and the light was muted white light, it gave me a terrible headache and eye strain then went into a migraine and felt sick to my stomach. I wasn't even looking at the sun, it was just the ambient light. Went over to Dale Hallow Lake and got in and grounded. Felt better. Wished I had been in the lake for the event

Got home and saw lots of people reporting headaches from the eclipse.

Some "experts" are blaming it on being outside and getting more sun in our eyes than usual today.  My guess was it was the white light causing the headaches.

# Thread: Becoming Mitochondriac
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-mitochondriac.19946/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WildForWesties — 2017-08-03T02:54:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-mitochondriac.19946/post-220778>

I am excited and grateful to have been led to Dr. Kruse's work and this group of smart, courageous, leading-edge thinkers. I've spent time in a lot of health forums and this one is unique. Reconnecting in a fundamental way with nature and myself as a "being of light" resonates deeply. It feels right and vital and utterly transformative. What a gift.

I have been on a downward health spiral since contracting either EBV or HHV6 (I have both and don't know which started this FGO) in 2001, Lyme in 2005, autoimmune thyroid issues in 2007 and mold exposure in 2009. Prior to 2008 I rarely went to the doctor except for gyn exams - didn't trust them and thought they were worthless since all they ever did was prescribe drugs or say "your labs are fine there's nothing wrong with you". I could have never imagined the depths of the chronic illness rabbit hole I've been living in since. I've tried many things, been to 50 doctors and practitioners, and spent MANY thousands of dollars trying to get well. I \*have\* made significant progress however light, water and magnetism are foundational pieces I've been missing. Through implementing these strategies it feels like my life is about to change in ways I can't imagine. Bring it on!

I'm reading, listening and absorbing as fast as possible, which is probably slower than most since I have absolutely no science background. Doesn't matter! I am 100% committed to fully recovering my health, I love learning and especially love Dr. Kruse's no-nonsense approach and his direct and practical applications of science. I look forward to many fruitful interactions and celebrations of success.

### Robert Marking — 2017-08-03T05:43:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-mitochondriac.19946/post-220788>

Welcome aboard!!! Like you, I have battled chronic fatigue due to Lyme & coinfections, EBV, and Mold Illness unknowingly for over 45 years and have finally won the battle. I am glad to hear that you have started the Shoemaker Protocol I just completed the protocol recently myself. That's going to be huge for you.

I truly feel that what Dr Kruse says is true, "you can't get well in the same enviroment that you got sick". It's all about minding your mitochondria and doing everything in your power maximize mitochondrial energy while lowering oxidative stress.

### Robert Marking — 2017-08-09T07:27:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-mitochondriac.19946/post-221251>

> [WildForWesties said:](/goto/post?id=220789)

My doc adds that in and it's helping although very expensive. I also completely agree about the role of environment. I'm looking at moving back to Texas where I spent the first 30 years of my life. Until then there is much I can do to improve where I am now.

Hi WildForWesties, Sorry that it has taken me a few days to get back to you. I hope that you are doing well.

So let me share with you a few things that I am currently doing to combat Biotoxin Mold Illness, Lyme and Co infections and it's symptoms.

First off, I am an Holistic Health Practitioner that utilizes Autonomic Response Testing (ART) which includes Direct Resonance Testing to first detect the specific hidden stressors that are affecting the the body and it's biophoton field.

Stressors can be any of the following...........

1\. **Structural Stessors/Trauma** – muscle, bone, connective tissue, scars, etc (car accidents, blunt trauma, falls, sprains, breaks, crashes, impacts, surgery, burns, dental, etc)

2\. **Emotional Stressors/Trauma** - untimely deaths, abuse, neglect, accidents, events, negative programming, brainwashing, negative thoughts and/or emotion, unresolved psycho-emotional issues, etc.

3\. **Chemical Stessors /Trauma** – viral & bacterial infections, parasites, food allergies & sensitivities, genetic mutations, MTHFR, nutritional deficiencies, biochemical imbalances, vaccines, toxins, pesticides, herbicides, heavy metals, amalgam fillings, etc.

4\. **Electromagnetic Stressors/Trauma -** non-native EMF (electromagnetic fields), WiFi, cell phones, computers, TV, high power lines, x-ray’s, airplanes, florescent lights, LED lights, tanning beds, smart meters, geopathic, etc.

5\. **Spiritual Stressors/Trauma** - Unresolved spiritual conflict or trauma, attachments, entities, etc.

6\. **Other** - unclassified types of trauma

When the body is stressed by any of the above stressors, the cells start to emit incoherent light and this disrupts body's regulation of it's biophoton field which controls all communication between the cells throughout the body. There's 100,000 chemical reactions per second in the cells and these are all controlled by light/photons. In ART this disruption of communication throughout the biophoton field is called "Blocked Regulation".

When a person has "Blocked Regulation" from a chronic Stressor, the Autonomic Nervous System ANS often times is stuck in the Sympathetic Fight or Flight mode. A person will not get well in the Sympathetic Mode for a number of reasons. The initial goal is to get the patient out of Sympathetic Fight or Flight mode and in the Parasympathetic Rest & Healing Mode. The only way this can take place is by detecting the specific stressors affecting the body and biophoton field and addressing those stessors accordingly.

Anything that causes any level of stress on the Autonomic Nervous System for that patient should be addressed and/or removed from the patients environment.

Of course I have addressed my hidden stressors listed above, and here are a few key things that I focus on personally...........

1.  Pure RO water re-mineralized and structured with low surface tension and high zeta potential much like Hunza Water
2.  Sun Exposure
3.  Reduce EMF exposure, I wear a energy balancing pendant that not only blocks harmful EMF, but it also converts EMF into harmonious energy for the body

4.  Avoid all Hidden Food Allergies/Sensitivities/Intolerances , one of the biggest causes of hidden inflammation on a daily basis for most people
5.  Check for Sulfur Metabolism Issues,I would bet that you will have a problem with this, this is very common with chronically ill people, this leads to poor detox pathways and high ammonia levels, I have a severe case myself
6.  Ketogenic/Paleo type of diet for lower inflammation (avoid grains, increasing good fat intake, consume more veggies. moderate protein)
7.  Rectal Ozone Therapy at home, over the past 2 years this has been huge in my battle against mold and Lyme, I feel that it is a must for anyone battling chronic viral and/or bacteria infections and mold illness. Viruses such as EBV, Herpes, Ebola, Coxsackie are especially susceptible to destruction by Ozone Therapy. Ozone modulates the immune system and it also improves oxygen utilization is the cell, which leads to more energy production in the mitochondria. There is a very inexpensive way to do in home rectal ozone therapy.
8.  HBOT (Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy)/EWOT (exercise with oxygen therapy) Ive been doing HBOT in conjunction with Ozone Therapy for 2 years and this is massive. Because of increased oxygen utilization from Ozone, the hemoglobin has a greater ability to mobilize more Oxygen into the cells where it can be used for energy. It's not how much oxygen you breath, it's how much is utilized for energy production. My personal HBO chamber that I had made is a hard chamber that I use 4 days a week, but if you cant afford a chamber then EWOT has been shown to be as effective and its very simple set up and use, and is very cost effective.
9.  Eng3 makes the Nano Vi which is almost a miracle cure for oxidative damage on DNA & Proteins from chronic stressors including Lyme and Mold exposure. This machine emits "Exclusion Zone Water" that is Bio-Identical to the body's. This EZ water mist mimics the signaling of the Reactive Oxygen Species ROS that is responsible for refolding and repairing damaged proteins in the cells. Once the proteins are refolded and repaired they can be used for producing energy in the cells once again. [www.Eng3corp.com](http://www.Eng3corp.com)
10.  Magnetico Mattress
11.  Near infrared Sauna - detoxing, collagen production, helps form and charge EZ water, mine is Low EMF and home made for less than $120
12.  Supplements to help support mitochondria function, NADH, CoQ10, Folate, B vitamins, Zinc, etc.
13.  Lastly, I also feel that utilizing a technique based on quantum physics called "Destructive Interference" has been one huge key in defeating Lyme and mold. The law of destructive interference says that if you have the exact frequency of something (Freq, A) and then make an inverted copy of that frequency which would be its mirror image (Freq. B) and then emit Freq B into Freq. A, ...the result would be that Freq B will energetically neutralize Freq A. The new noise canceling headphones use this very same technique. So here's how its done with a person. First through ART/Direct Resonance Testing discover the specific strains of infection the patient has, next make an inverse copy of that infection imprinted into a homeopathic solution, next take a laser and laser the inverse frequency of the infection into the biophoton field of the patient. If lasered in at the right place into the biophoton feild, the inverse frequency of the infection will travel throughout the entire biophoton feild energetically neutralizing the infection where ever it may be.

So there is a breakdown off some of the tools that I used to beat Lyme and Mold. I think that the Surviving Mold Protocol was a must in my battle with mold.

Feel free to fire away with any questions!

Prior to 2008 I rarely went to the doctor except for gyn exams - didn't trust them and thought they were worthless since all they ever did was prescribe drugs or say "your labs are fine there's nothing wrong with you". I could have never imagined the depths of the chronic illness rabbit hole I've been living in since. I've tried many things, been to 50 doctors and practitioners, and spent MANY thousands of dollars trying to get well. I \*have\* made significant progress however light, water and magnetism are foundational pieces I've been missing. Through implementing these strategies it feels like my life is about to change in ways I can't imagine. Bring it on!
>
> I'm reading, listening and absorbing as fast as possible, which is probably slower than most since I have absolutely no science background. Doesn't matter! I am 100% committed to fully recovering my health, I love learning and especially love Dr. Kruse's no-nonsense approach and his direct and practical applications of science. I look forward to many fruitful interactions and celebrations of success.

Ditto!

# Thread: My Sarasota/Missoula Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sarasota-missoula-journal.19948/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DixieB — 2017-08-03T06:31:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sarasota-missoula-journal.19948/post-220795>

I'm a 65 year old white female and of mostly Scotch/Irish and Scandinavian descent (less than 1% black according to Ancestry if that's accurate).
I spend almost equal time in Florida (winter/spring) and Montana (summer/fall) and have the following health concerns: hypothyroidism, hypertension, sleep, (don't stay asleep all night but not really sure if I wake up from needing to go to bathroom or first wake up and then realize I need to urinate) intermittent joint pain, (varies but not severe-no meds needed-in one shoulder, both hips and knees) severe heat as well as cold sensitivity, brittle nails and need lenses for reading as well as now also for medium and long range to be 20/20. Also had a case of diverticulitis last year which required a short hospitalization.
I've been using an anti-aging Dr for about the past 3 years and was taking Rx's for the high BP, thyroid issues as well as Estradial patch, progesterone tablets, HGH injections, compounded cream of progesterone, extra, ester, testosterone and VSA cream. I was taking Vit D3 and some probiotics along with some Oreganol but wasn't very regular with the supplements. I did labs with him 2x a year but have been increasingly inclined to go off the Rx's again. Now I'm ready to re-evaluate it all, especially because when I did quit most all of them the first go round I did with him, I didn't see much difference really.
I've been off all of them but the BP Rx since seeing the Nourish Vermont video about 2-3 weeks ago and have been going out in the sun and on the ground almost every day for 30-45 minutes but not sure what times best since I can't seem anymore to access the Sun Azimuth table that Dr Mercola recommended last time I tried that protocol a few years back. I've ditched the sunglasses and never did much sunscreen, but then I rarely spent any time in the sun due to that heat sensitivity. I don't sweat much so I feel like it all just builds up inside me. Get too cold just as easily as I overheat.
I ordered some blue blocker glasses and have been wearing them a few hours before bed and trying to reduce my time on all screens.
I read Leptin Rx years ago and have more or less given up snacking and eating sooner than 4-5 hours after last meal. Recently read The Plant Paradox and have been following guidelines there about avoiding seed bearing plants as well as the nightshade family. Not sure anymore if thats relevant or not though.
Took a first cold bath today -no ice, just cold water-and have been blasting my face with AC in the car, all while opening the sun roof and cracking windows on my way to town a couple times a week which is about 90 min worth round-trip. I'd started doing some cryo therapy chamber sessions before I left Florida but gave them up after I suffered from a long bout of fatigue this winter and early spring and my acupuncturist recommended to hold off on that.
I do strength training only 1x a week and have begun yoga once a week but not regularly with that due to traveling back and forth on weekends a lot to a nearby glacial lake...going to start jumping in more often!
So far my sleep-and energy- have improved considerably. I lost about 5 pounds or so. Libido's also increased (a big plus since it was about nil previously). BP fluctuates between normal and around 140/80 some days, so I'm happy for the normal days and looking for more as I continue with all I'm now doing.
I had a total hysterectomy when in my early 30's and never found a hormone replacement routine I could tolerate back then so did not start hormone therapy until I was in my late 50's and I will say that I didn't feel any big problems in those first few decades after the surgery.
My weight is OK. I've gained about 10 pounds over the last few years due to entertaining/cooking for houseguests 1/2 the year (too far to go to town just for dinner) while in Montana and just generally doing too much eating and drinking while in Florida as well. Retirement has its good-and bad points...every day's a party day in other words! However, I'm glad to have lost a few pounds these last few weeks and at about 5'1" (now that my height has started shrinking) and 117 pounds I'm OK knowing that my weight will fall where it needs to as I continue to improve my lifestyle choices.
Thankful to have found Dr Kruse's video on Catherine Austin Fitt's website. I watched it because of my following more or less the Weston A Price recs on food choices. Have been cramming ever since and while I've had more than my share of trouble getting started with the Forums and basic website issues I look forward to continuing to learn and get better at my specific hacks.
Tomorrow I have a phone consult with my anti-aging Dr in Florida about re-defining our relationship and hopefully having him work with me (and learn as he goes) about ordering the recommended labs...though I haven't even quite figured out what those are yet still. There is so much info it's a bit overwhelming trying to find my way, but I'm so sure this is the right way.
I've had an awareness of how important the light is for at least 20 years. Somehow came across Dr Ott's work (the school he did his study at was near my home in Sarasota) back then and even had the private high school my kids were in back then let me pay for full-spectrum lights for the new library/gym they were building. There are many other similar things I've at least tried over the years so that when I heard that first video (I listened to it in the car on a 2hr drive) I too "connected the dots" about the importance of light, water and magnetism.
I've been sharing what I know with great enthusiasm and hope to influence a few others in the medical arena to pay attention...I'm a "high I" as they say in that Myers-Briggs world (I think that was it) I was tested for in a job position way back when...meaning I'm good at influencing others. Meanwhile, I'll at least be talking to a few people I know who are suffering from various issues and hope they'll have "ears to hear" as well. Not everyone will I already know. But, with time I believe this paradigm will change the understanding of how to have optimal health for many more people and that can only be a good thing.
Thank you to Dr Kruse and to you members as well...having this opportunity to share and learn together is not something I will ever take for granted but count it as a great blessing and joy!

### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-03T15:15:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sarasota-missoula-journal.19948/post-220817>

When you had the hysterectomy did they take ovaries or not?

### DixieB — 2017-08-07T23:27:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sarasota-missoula-journal.19948/post-221115>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=220817)
>
> When you had the hysterectomy did they take ovaries or not?

Yes, it was complete....btw, I just posted a thread asking about Cryo "chambers" as I had taken several sessions before I transitioned to MT last month. I've not heard anything mentioned but I just discovered theres a unit in MT at a new float tank place...wondering if that form of CT is recommended?

### DixieB — 2017-08-07T23:41:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sarasota-missoula-journal.19948/post-221118>

I realized after my initial post that I didn't mention my maternal history, which is one of rheumatoid arthritis from my grandmother who lived in a wheelchair because of it, as well as my mother had high BP and ended up w/vascular dementia and had several strokes of varying degrees before she died from the last one. My mom also had several 'pre-cancerous" breast issues and eventually had the tissue removed and implants put in to avoid ending up with breast cancer. She had also been treated for depression and anxiety as well and I had a Dr (who examined her for a guardianship for me) that even though he'd taken more time than he'd ever spent, he'd need more time to determine if she suffered from either Histrionic, Aggressive or Narcissistic personality disorder...I suggested she maybe had all 3!

Meanwhile, I met with my hormone/anti-agind Dr today and he is ordering the labs I've figured out so far that are recommended and says he's very interested to learn more about this as I dig deeper. He had pulled 1 or 2 papers off the internet on the light, water and magnetism factors and said those were all he could find but that it all made sense. Also that he had recently read some papers on the "evolutionary mismatch" theory and is willing to go along with me in my journey even though he said he "couldn't advocate" it as he has to maintain "standard of care" practices to ensure his ability to practice. I'm now ready to take the Hormone Bootcamp to learn how to read and understand the labs myself in the meantime.

Is the best time at sunrise, when the sun comes up over the horizon? If so, does it matter that as the seasons change, the time when this occurs will change? For instance, where I live (England, 51 degrees latitude), sunrise is currently about 5:30AM, but as the seasons change, and Winter comes along, it'll be hours later.

When sunrise occurs, how long does UV and IR-A last, before these beneficial wavelengths change to UV-B? (Is that the correct way of thinking about this?) Thanks a lot for any comments.

# Thread: Yo from North cyprus
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yo-from-north-cyprus.19952/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stevie D — 2017-08-04T12:11:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yo-from-north-cyprus.19952/post-220894>

Hi everyone I’m Steve I’m 24 and I live in north Cyprus.

First thing I ever heard of Jack was that his jacket made him a metrosexual and that he likes to blow people’s minds as a hobbie on primal edge.

And I’ve not looked back since.

I was originally born and raised in south London and after leaving school at 16 I was running on a pure sugar based metabolism and was heading from a school with 6 wifi’s into central London for work with god knows what RF flying about.

I was at time in ok physical shape since I had done MMA all my life but the stress of my job lead me to a slow downwards spiral of my body and mental health.

Fast forward to me being 21 and my dad retiring in North Cyprus. I came out for a holiday, sunbathed on the beach, and felt infinitely better. I then went back to London and got instantly worse.

So with the 21st century I can work from home pretty much so I moved out to Cyprus to live with my dad.

Then I started looking into my health and went through all the usual gurus until I found my favourite metrosexual who actually explained things I thought always made instinctive sense.

And now? Now I have a second hand ice cream freezer filled with water, a quantlet, an endless desire to consume seafood if the sun is up, and I watch as many sunrises as is humanly possible.

All in all I think I've done pretty good so far.

Hey [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) . I am from Cyprus, I was born here. Although there is a good sun here, the environment still sucks due to nnEmf and marine radars. ### JanSz — 2018-05-13T16:02:12+0300

I did the 23andme test and am waiting for results aswell. Why is it really helpful to know your haplotype regardless of the methylation? And why is it wise to know the latitude of your grandma 10000 years ago?

As humans travel from their origins in vicinity of Lake Victoria, their body changed.
It takes 5-10 thousand years to get accommodated to certain latitude (and altitude).
Not long ago most peoples have not travel significant distances. Now we have significant migrations.
Per Jack's teachings everybody would be better of on equator or real near to it, but definitely within tropics.
When trying to thin slice that advice, it should be observed by currently sick people (that are hoping for recovery).
Those that are currently healthy may still fare rather well if they are at or closer to equator relative to their grand grand mother 5-10 tousand years ago.

# Thread: My CT trick
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stevie D — 2017-08-04T16:42:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/post-220908>

So I've got a freezer filled with water I use for my CT and during the winter i just use my pool and the worst part when i first started was the "Holy fuck my balls are cold" moment when you first get in. The trick is one I learnt from my mma coach Steve Morris (I'm named after him lol) and it was one he taught me to do when body conditioning i.e when smashing various parts of the body on various wood, metal, and other objects to build toughness into the limb.

You find one of the softest cloths you can and rub it between your fingers constantly as you smash your shin against the target. As long as you rub the cloth it made the impact much easier to deal with. And I discovered it does the exact same thing in CT. I started using a bit of an old soft blanket I cut off and then realized I was an idiot and that I have an entire soft blanket stuck to my head by nature.

Now all I have to do is rub my hair for a few moments as I gently lower into my freezer and bam it totally distracts me from the chill. Hope it helps someone

### Sheddie — 2017-08-04T19:01:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/post-220909>

Very cool trick! I'm only at face-dunks, when I feel brave enough, but maybe it works then, too.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/post-220914>

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=220909)
>
> Very cool trick! I'm only at face-dunks, when I feel brave enough, but maybe it works then, too.

yeah it works on face dunking too. I used it when i first started by rubbing my towel between my finger and thumb. Good luck

### ScottishEmma — 2017-08-05T09:21:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/post-220960>

Seriously? Rubbing your hair helps get in the tub? I'll try it!!

### Lahelada — 2017-08-05T16:25:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-ct-trick.19954/post-220971>

Great post . I believe what you are doing is actually creating a paradox in your autonomous nervous system. So you are introducing an action from the feed and breed cycle (caress)to a time when your eye has already signaled "stress " at seeing the cold water in the tub.
We do it unconsciously more often than we think . We rub people who are upset between the shoulder blades, blow on a childs bruised knee, sing a lullaby to a child who cannot sleep , seemingly "stupid" things

# Thread: Hello from Myrtle Beach, SC

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5

### Brad Boyles — 2017-08-04T20:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-myrtle-beach-sc.19956/post-220921>

Hello Everyone,

I am excited to have stumbled onto Dr. Kruse. I was tickled to see that there was a forum. I am really looking forward to the opportunity to learn here. I am just really getting a good idea how far this rabbit hole is going to go. Quite deep! I am a slow learner, but I am eager and motivated.

Without going into too much detail about my history, it has been rather challenging. I have for almost all of my life been barely surviving and if it wasn't for the support of my parents, I highly doubt I would even be alive.

So when I read something to the notion of don't just survive, thrive, I am all for that.

So far the behavior modifications I have made: 1. I have dramatically reduced the amount of artificial light that enters my eyeball especially at night and 2. I have gotten my (angle side side) out of bed to see the sunrise with my feet grounded to the earth. I didn't do that today due to inclement weather ' Lighting'.

Thank you, Dr. Kruse, for allowing me to participate in this forum and I optimistically look forward to seeing where this will take me.

b

### JanSz — 2017-08-04T21:44:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-myrtle-beach-sc.19956/post-220935>

If not for my children residing nearby, Myrtle Beach is my favorite location for permanent residence.
I like to vacation in Breaker's Paradise Tower.

..

### caroline — 2017-08-05T03:02:54+0300

Have you had a 23&me done?

You have made a good start! Do you have blue blockers?

Do you watch Jack's podcasts? especially His Vermont talk a while back? The podcasts are a great and faster/easier way to get Jack's critical info.

Do you have John Ott's book? health and light. It is a quick read

Dr. Kruse is all about the 3 legged stool .....sun, light and magnetism

### Brad Boyles — 2017-08-05T15:12:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-myrtle-beach-sc.19956/post-220969>

Hi JanSz,
Yes, Myrtle Beach isn't a bad place to live. The Breakers is nice. I don't live to from there.

Hi, Caroline,

Thanks! Yes, I will compile a health history. It may take me a while.

No, I haven't done a 23 and me. I need to look into that.

Yes, I have blue blocking glasses that I got on Amazon. I have four different kinds so far for different times of the day. I am learning how to optimally use them. The ones I am wearing in my profile pic, are computer blue blockers which say they block 99 percent of blue light. I am not a savy consumer when it comes to this technology, so I am not sure if that is a true claim. I also got a uvex blue light filter for my phone, but I am not sure how effective it is.

It doesn't really show well, but the reflection on my glasses is Jack's Vermont 2017 talk at the point where Jack has the slide "the monk who sold his Ferrari". I was listening to the talk while I was signing up. Since I needed a human picture, I just took one using my computer. So yes, I love his talks. I have listened to many of them a couple of times.

I just received John Ott's book "Health and Light" yesterday and will begin to read it tomorrow on my day off. I skimmed through it last night before going to bed.

### Brad Boyles — 2017-08-14T23:48:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-myrtle-beach-sc.19956/post-221661>

years ago in around 1999 I was beginning to learn about yoga and I was set on getting a book on chakras. So at Barnes and noble, I was in the eastern philosophy section looking for such a book. I came across a subtle energy book, I thought, opened the book and saw the chapter titled "maxwell's silver hammer".

It just happened by coincidence the Beatles -maxwell's silver hammer on Abbey Road was playing in my car as I was parking my car at Barnes & noble and I still had the song in my head. I ended up getting the book without giving it too much of a review. Later, I wondered why in the french toast did I get that book. I didn't think it was relevant to me.

that book was, of course, Becker's "The Body Electric",

So now I am hearing Becker being mentioned a lot by Dr. Kruse and maybe me getting that book was not some random coincidence. I forgot I had it. I really need to read that thing.

But only after I get a good understanding of the recommended top 5 blogs for beginners. I am just getting my feet wet with the material. I have deferred the John Ott book for a later date.
I must say the webinars are making the process a lot easier.

1\. Leptin R(x)
2\. CT Protocol
3\. Brain gut 11
4\. Cold thermogenesis 7
5\. Energy and epigenetics 4

e=mc2
Energy and mass are exactly the same thing. The only thing that's different is the environment that each side of the equation is in. So what does this mean? This means that when you slow light down, you create things of mass. That means that everything in this room, this farm, this planet at one time used to be light and that is very counterintuitive to some people - they don't believe that's true. That was Einstein's innovation. And the problem is that in medicine, biology, research science - they don't get it. Last year when I came here I told you that was the basis of the food web - light through photosynthesis. So, if you don't know about light, you don't know about food. That's a really controversial thing to say to people who are big into food.
Now I'm going to show you how we work - the key innovation ocurred 600 million years ago in photosynthesis That's when we stole a bacteria and turned it into a chloroplast and we made food. What did the plants do? They made oxygen as a bi product and they also put DHA in the seas. Ok, we know that life started in the seas - what happened 600 million years ago is important to everyone in the room. That's where all complex life came from through the Cambrian explosion, but what was the key innovation? The mitochondria brought the sea inside the cell and why is that important? Because we had to bring water inside to make life work. Here is a key slide:
photosynthesis = CO2 + water = sugar
respiration = sugar = CO2 + water
both cycles make DC electricity from water... and basically what you have is a coupled system and what people don't realize is that system is reversed here and here's the key thing that you need to understand. Rubin talked a little about DC electricity and what people don't realize is that when a caterpillar eats a leaf it stimulates a DC current of injury that Becker talks about in his book. The researcher who did that was Harold Saxton Burr at Yale universtiy in 1929. You should know that - guess what, when I cut people in surgery every time I cut them what stimulates the healing repair? It generates a DC electric current and it tends to work with red blood cells too in a very elaborate way that Becker laid out in his books - if you don't know that, you need to and I'm going to show you why you need to.
Next slide: Does obesity begin in the eye? The monk who sold his Ferrari - what is special about the Himilayas? The equator bisects India and the Himilayas are way high up - 25000-30000 ft... What he realized immediately is that would change the light frequency of what that person would get and as it turns out, for every 1000 ft you go up, you get more UV light - UVA light tans. In medical school he remembers learning about mitochondrial haplotypes and how they vary by geography and also by lattitude. So, this is the key - this is where the cradle of life is, right here - why is this important? Because people who live in the east african rift zone - the original humans - they have an L0 haplotype - what does that mean? That means that they do not uncouple their mitochondria - why is that? They need all their energy to run away from lions. People who live up here (way north of the equator) which is most of us in the room - why? Because we come from Northern European haplotypes. We uncouple our mitochondria - what does that mean? It means we don't use food electrons to make energy to make ATP. We use it to make heat... why? Because there's no lions up here... instead of worrying about lions, you have to worry about freezing your arse off so you release heat from your mitochondria and that does something specific to water that's sitting around your mitochondria and what is that? It's something that's very counter intuitive: WARM SHRINKS WATER. COLD EXPANDS WATER. Why is that important to a mitochondriac? When you take a mitochondria and shrink it down, that brings the respiratory proteins closer together. What do we know in quantum physics? Anytime you have energy transfer - when electrons move across a redox couple, the closer they are together, the more energy efficient they are. Also, for every one Angstrom - the distance between respiratory proteins drops off the energy it makes by a factor of 10. So, what did I start to realize? Is obesity tied to energy excess or energy loss back to the environment? This came from Dr. Doug Wallace - he just won the Franklin award - usually what they give to someone right before they win a Nobel prize - he'll win one and he's the guy who discovered that mitochondria are inherited from your mother only and he's also the one who linked all diseases to energy depletion states in mitochondria (paraphrase) From the science of epigenetics (the mito make all the energy in the body): energy turns on and off the genome - that's the only thing that does it - so we come to a point - how to we make energy proximal to a mitochondria - where does the process first start? Well, this is when I first started looking at sunlight. What your eyes can see is 380-780.
Slide:
What is missing? 250nm-380nm(UVA) and 780nm-3000 (red)- the human eye is blind to them - why? We can't see UV or IR light -

### Penny — 2017-08-04T20:45:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nourishing-vermont-2017-the-first-hour.19957/post-220926>

Why is it there and why doesn't our eye look at it? I thought that was very peculiar. Then I started to learn about things in quantum mechanics. And the reason that occurrs is something called the Quantum Xeno effect (a system can't change while you're watching it) When you observe a frequency, it changes the reality you get. all the key signalling in your eye works from the frequencies you can't see - and it's done that way by design. So this question made me realize there's an issue in the eye - the eye is a camera but it is also a clock (SCN) - it's the main circadean oscillator.
"The suprachiasmatic nucleus or nuclei (SCN) is a tiny region of the brain in the hypothalamus, situated directly above the optic chiasm. It is responsible for controlling circadian rhythms. The neuronal and hormonal activities it generates regulate many different body functions in a 24-hour cycle, using around 20,000 neurons.\[1\]
The SCN interacts with many other regions of the brain. It contains several cell types and several different peptides (including vasopressin and vasoactive intestinal peptide) and neurotransmitters."
I started to wonder is there a difference between these two systems in the eye and how do they link? So I went furthere - in 1879 every thing changed - this is when we had the first incandescent bulb - chronic diseases began at the turn of the century - 80-85% of diseases are mitochondrial in origin - in 1900 colon cancer was the 37th leading cause of death in the U.S. and today it's number two. So, explain to me how in 5 generations we went from 37 to 2? It's impossible unless you understand the energetics of the mitochondria which is what Wallace has been teaching people but no one is listening - so the evolution of light doesn't equal the evolution built into your cells Let's take it further - picture form Rubin - red light balances blue light - don't forget that - in medical school every doctor is taught the the anterior chamber of the lens and cornea blocks UV light - they are still taught that today - so when I went on this journey, I found out that was B.S. too -and it turns out if you look at it 1? of the signals get through UVB and 3% of UVA gets through - someone else talked about collagen and piezoelectricity - the vitreous of the eye is a special collagen - it's not found anywhere else in the body - you know what it does? It slows. light. down. before it gets to the retina - that's kinda interesting - what did I say to you before about e=mc2? - you create things with structure/with mass/you create matter, don't you? So, I thought this was interesting but I also want to teach you another important thing about UV light - it's the only part of the spectrum that works in a non linear fashion - what does non linear mean? A small stimulus leads to a massive amplification - in other words, it can have massive effects that you don't anticipate - we're going to talk about some of those - the key thing is to question everything - you're looking at a guy who pretty much thinks nothing he learned in medical school is true...
The pupil is a perfect black box radiator - what does that mean? It means that light can go in and come back - how do I know immediately how UV light got through? I challenge anyone to go to Battries and Bulbs and get a UV flashlight, put it into anyone's retina - you know what you'll see? You'll see flourescence come right back - you don't need anything else - so every time the opthomologist I gave this talk to opthomologists to prove to them they were full of shit, that's how I did it... I brought a UVA flashlight, shined it in all their eyes and asked them what did you see? That shut them up pretty quick... because you don't need a lot of photons to see the effect.
All aromatic acids absorb UV light - the eye is loaded with these amino acids specifically so that raised the question: why is this?
Slides:
The Pupil is a perfect black box radiator
biogenic amines capture UV light /
aromatic amino acids absorb UV light - t
The eye is loaded with these amino acids: triptophan, phenylalanine, histidine, tyrosine
Well, I found out from physics that a benzene ring which is in every single aromatic amino acid is a photon trap and they absorb all frequencies of UV light so guess what that means?
About 250nm-380nm is absorbed by an aromatic amino acid benzene ring... that's the reason why we don't see it, because it's designed to do something different and here's the key - every single protein in the body - this is something that was missed earlier in the talks - every protein in your body is hydrated - zinc cannot bind to a protein the way you were told earlier today... and I'm going to show you the reason why and I'm going to tell you how it really works -

### Penny — 2017-08-04T20:45:30+0300

slide:
How do we capture light we can't see?
title: Benzene is a blueprint for chromaphore

left side: water EZ coherent domains 13%
