### Sue-UK — 2018-06-24T17:48:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-damaging-to-eyes.20004/post-244245>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=244168)
>
> My question though is, how do people with older kids REALISTICALLY expect to enforce this? Baby steps like everything else?

It was apparently St Francis of Assisi who said to start by doing what's necessary, then do what's possible; and suddenly you are doing the impossible. I didn't like the idea one bit either at first, but it came under the heading of necessary, because of my eye injury.

Its easier if children are old enough to not need supervision about bedtimes, homework etc. but I'd ask DH to take over that responsibility for 4 or 5 nights a week if I had to. I have two kids still in full time education, but for the last year both have been studying away from home. It would be much harder 52 weeks of the year. At Christmas DH went to pick DD up from the airport, coming back when I was in doing cold dark and boring in the bedroom. I wanted to see her before I went to sleep so she had to knock on the door, tell me the lights were all out, then she came in, we hugged and she sat chatting with me in the dark. Did she think I was nuts? Yup. Did it stop me? Nope. I just said I was doing a specific winter hack. If it worked I'd be carrying on with it, if it didn't do much, I'd ease off. I wasn't asking anyone else to do it, but I'd appreciate their support in being mindful of what I was trying to hack. Taking it up a notch next winter won't seem so crazy … but if it is, DD and her friends discussion about who had the craziest mother had me in top spot. If I drop from top spot I'm not trying hard enough. If I had them home all the time I'd rake out the old camping toilet we used to use when caravanning and have a mini en suite

# Thread: The people of the AM sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-people-of-the-am-sun.20005/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 73
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shiran — 2017-08-13T15:14:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-people-of-the-am-sun.20005/post-221587>

The AM sun fascinates me..
Yesterday's Sunrise was clear with no clouds , I saw all the stages it was amazing.
today's sunrise was not the same:
Here In the 31 latitude
started rising at 6:03 covered by clouds
Then the sun Popped through the clouds at 6:30 with a brighter light

I missed the red colors at the start...

Is that a problem for my hormones?
Did I get the right singles although I didn't see the IR?
Winter is coming... what then ?
As I understand The sunrise exposure is critical ...
What do you guys do and how are your experiences with the sunrises?

### Ted — 2017-08-13T17:08:06+0300

The AM sun we need is pre-uva, regardless of the season. That starts the signalling for our hormones. That time will vary at different times of the year and latitude. When uva shows up, then it does all the other magic to our skin and hormones. And as Sue said, the sun is always there, even beneath the clouds. You can't have perfect, clear sunrises every morning.

### John Nicholas — 2018-07-31T19:35:20+0300

When uva shows up, then it does all the other magic to our skin and hormones. And as Sue said, the sun is always there, even beneath the clouds. You can't have perfect, clear sunrises every morning.

I'm glad you posted this. Thank you. I recently dowloaded the D Minder app. I look towards the sun each morning as I wake up, which is around 5:45am to 6:30am. It shows there's no UV light. I didn't realize there was no UV light before a certain hour. I just assumed there was UV light when the sun rises. The prescription is to get am sunlight because it has UV and IR light. But how is that possible with no UV? Do we need to wait until UVA is present? Your post suggests not. So I'm confused. Also, am I supposed to look 15 degrees below the sun to the right of the sun or to the left of the sun? I'm also trying to get UVB light during solar noon. I've started out with 15 to 20 mins each day. UV is at 11 during this time. After watching Alexander Wunsch, that's seems to be the most critical time of the day as he mentions nothing about am sunlight. Your thoughts are appreciated.

### kris90 — 2018-07-31T20:34:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-people-of-the-am-sun.20005/post-247141>

At sunrise, you have a balance of blue and red. This is the stimulus to turn on the pituitary gland to release hormones. A couple hours later, UVA shows up, and that lowers the release of the pituitary hormones.

The idea is to spend as much time in AM sun as you can so you get the initial stimulus, and then the transition to UVA.

It all has to do with how the sun controls deuterium in you. Blue light favors deuterium in tissues to encourage adequate growth while UVA and IRA light keep deuterium in your blood so you can make UVC light to program the aromatic amino acids that control the timing mechanism (circadian rhythm).

Jack's Vermont 2017 talk is all about how we make things with mass and structure by slowing light down. He showed that the AM stimulus (blue/red) increased mass of the pituitary gland which demonstrates how light can be transformed into mass (chemicals, hormones). On the flip side, Jack's Vermont 2018 talk was about how we convert mass (deuterium) into light (UVC), and this light does some major programming in us for wellness.

I know I'm not supposed to look into the sun. Just trying to get a handle on it. It can be very difficult some days to wake up and look directly in the direction of the sun. My eyes (or at least one of them) wants to close.

### Ted — 2018-08-01T01:39:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-people-of-the-am-sun.20005/post-247165>

Nice explanation Chris.

He means you need to stay in the sun for both the pre-uvA and also the transition into the uvA. You need both. You can look directly at the sun if it is within 1 hour of the actually sunrise. I do this every day without a problem. If not, just look slightly away.

### kris90 — 2018-08-01T14:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-people-of-the-am-sun.20005/post-247188>

Yes what Ted says. AM is literally sunrise until noon. Sunrise is always blue/red, and UVA shows up within a couple hours of sunrise. You want to get as much of that sun as you can (both blue/red sunrise, and the UVA transition). We are designed to capture that stimulus every morning.

As for deuterium, read the old patreon blogs that came out late 2017, and then the QT series. Watch the Vermont 2018 talk and it will all come together. You will see how sunlight controls where deuterium goes, and why it matters. The light we get from the sun is not the same as the light we make inside our body. But we need the sun's electromagnetic force (i.e. UVA) as well as the IR light to help us generate this special light that becomes a laser.

### John Nicholas — 2018-08-03T23:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-people-of-the-am-sun.20005/post-247427>

Happy to answer.

Honestly, I thought Jack was a dick to people. Terse, unpleasant, arrogant and condescending. I liked his message instinctively, but I didn't totally understand it as it's way over my head. I didn't want to continue to ask stupid questions and feel like I'm being chastised. My 18 year old daughter hears me listening to pod casts he's on and she says, "Why do you listen to that jerk all the time. He's so mean."

His videos portray a different person. I believe his intentions are good and that's most important to me. He still has similar characteristics, but I've decided to put aside the messenger and listen to the message. After all, it's free and it also generous of him to spend his time helping people. Therefore, I decided it was me who had the problem and not Jack. After all, I'm on is site, he's not on mine. I don't have one.

Regardless, the more videos I see, the more I understand. The more it relates to me. The more I gravitate to his insight, regardless of how it's delivered.

I've also noticed, there are a lot of smart people on this site like yourself, Kris 90 and several others who really have a deep understanding of what's going on.

As for me, I'm obese. bad cholesterol is high and good is low. I have have muscle fatigue and it's been getting worse, a poorly working thyroid (managed well by Armour Thyroid), high blood pressure and fatigue. Cortisol levels are lower than they should be in the am, and in the evening they rise.

I've become allergic to eggs, which I love. I've been eating a ton of seafood (trying to find as much wild as possible, but much of it is farmed. However, like eggs, I have diarrhea when I eat scallops, oysters and lobster. I love lobster and scallops. I don't usually have a problem with shrimp. But eating Epi-Paleo (I have his book) is hard because I'm on the toilet all the time. I also have no sex drive whatsoever. And testosterone replacement increases my testosterone, but does nothing for my libido. Jack also has said that happens.

I just heard one of his videos where he says with proper light, magnetism and epi-paleo, those allergies should clear up when I'm not so toxic from blue light and nnemf. I've always on my laptop (on my lap most of the time or on my chest in bed) and iPhone. I've watched too much TV over the years and my sleep was not good. I tend to sleep during the day when I can because my energy levels crash during the mid-morning or afternoon.

I just went on the 5 day Fasting-Mimicking Diet to try something drastic. It was easy to do (which was surprising) and I didn't cheat once. My energy levels are great. I slept good. My edema in my legs have completely gone. I feel it has really put me on the right track of being totally focused and disciplined on my heath.

I'd already started the Epi-Paleo diet about 10 days before I started the Fasting-Mimicking Diet. Now that I'm off of that, I'm back to Epi.

I enjoy coconut oil, coconut manna, I've eliminated dairy, I do have grass fed butter and olive oil and doing many other things he suggests such as meal timing and trying eat when it's light out, four hours before I sleep and trying to get the sunrise. I've also been in the sun at solar noon for 15 minutes to get my D levels up.

I cut out Diet Coke (which I had 5 to 7 a day for 30 years). I know it made me eat more. I'm drinking spring water and really enjoy drinking San Pelligrino or Gerolsteiner. Those take away my cravings for Diet Coke.

I'm totally focused on the sun now and plan on rearranging my life to revolve around it, eliminating blue light (using Iris-mini instead of F.lux and find it much better), I do not let any lights in my path during the evening if I can avoid it. My family things I'm nuts. I will be happy when my food allergies go away. I love raw scallops, I've come to like oysters, and really love raw salmon sashimi. I also love eating Miso soup and it has seaweed in it. But I fear Miso having to do with soy is a problem.

I also ordered 23&me to get my haplotype.

So I hope that answers your questions.

Again, I keep seeing references to 8am - 11am as morning sunlight as being ideal. I don't understand what happens post 11am to make it different. Thank you.

Circadian rhythm is driven by changes in light frequencies from the sun. It all begins at sunrise when you have equal red and blue. Blue light creates the stress response because it puts deuterium in tissues, but the effect is always balanced by red. This is necessary to create the right stimulus to turn on the pituitary gland. The occurs in the very early hours of the morning when the sun is rising.

UVA shows up later and turns off the pituitary gland. UVA + IRA is the combo required to deplete deuterium in tissues. It favors deuterium in our blood, and the UVA creates the "pinch" that allows full spectrum UV light to be created in our blood plasma from the deuterium. That includes UVC light which is used to program the aromatic amino acids into the biogenic amines. It also includes the UVB light required to sulfate cholesterol to make Vitamin D.

Blue light creates the stress response because it puts deuterium in tissues, but the effect is always balanced by red. This is necessary to create the right stimulus to turn on the pituitary gland. The occurs in the very early hours of the morning when the sun is rising.
>
> UVA shows up later and turns off the pituitary gland. UVA + IRA is the combo required to deplete deuterium in tissues. It favors deuterium in our blood, and the UVA creates the "pinch" that allows full spectrum UV light to be created in our blood plasma from the deuterium. That includes UVC light which is used to program the aromatic amino acids into the biogenic amines. It also includes the UVB light required to sulfate cholesterol to make Vitamin D.

Right, I got all that and I understood that from all the posts and articles (I sitll question UVC and I'm not sold on that one, but that's not my interest). My question is what happens after 11:00am? Why is it only till 11am these things occur? Sorry, if I'm missing your point. Thank you again.

### kris90 — 2018-08-19T17:41:18+0300

Because all the important things I just mentioned occur before 11 AM. Circadian rhythm is driven by the changes. If you want to live outside our evolutionary biology, you cannot be promised wellness. It's as simple as that. You cannot replace a sunrise. You won't have another chance for this timing mechanism to be properly programmed. 6 AM and 11 AM are radically different, and our SCN and skin needs to detect this.

As for UVC light, we know it doesn't come from the sun, and we know the aromatic amino acids peak absorption is in the UVC range. It simply HAS to come from within. The fact that deuterium (google deuterium arc lamp) can make it, and our blood concentration has a high amount of deuterium, the pieces fit. In fact, low serum deuterium levels is a good indicator that deuterium has accumulated in tissues, which we already know is the mechanism for cancer.

# Thread: Balance and Co-ordination
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/balance-and-co-ordination.20008/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sajid Mahmood — 2017-08-14T12:36:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/balance-and-co-ordination.20008/post-221638>

My 5 years 8 months old son struggles with his balance and co-ordination. He has been having balance problems since he was about he has been 18 months old. When he was 2 years, he used to fall over quite easily when running. Other toddlers similar age never used to fall like him. Now that he is 5 years of age, he still struggles with his balance. As soon as I tell him to stand on one leg he struggles to hold it, and wobbles all over the place and falls over. When we went on holiday to Pakistan at the 33rd Latitude, his symptoms seemed to improve. I am developing him to become a soccer player, therefore have been teaching him to play football since he was 1 years of age. He has been also going to swimming, boxing, gymnastics. We also make him do plenty of bodyweight exercises at home. So I doubt very much he has a problem with his motor skills.

At the time, when my wife was pregnant, the mid-wife nurses stopped my wife from having fish and seafood due to the mercury toxicity. Little did I know at the time how important DHA was. Also when my wife was pregnant she was in a blue lit environment filled with nnEMF. I wish I had stumbled on Jack's work at that time. My wife is also Pakistani descent but she was pregnant in UK, 53rd Latitude. Moreover, when my son was born he was born in a blue light environment, filled with nnEMF.

Also not to mention his attention span is bad when he goes to classes, and is not very good at listening or following instructions. A lot of the time he's daydreaming or having difficulty following instructions and doesn't pay attention to details. Is there a chance he could have mild/moderate autism or ADHD? Also he tends to be clumsy and careless when running, thus falls over a lot and bumps into things. He's usually not looking where he is going? When we went to Pakistan he seemed to become a lot sharper mentally, with his listening, and with following instructions. In Pakistan at the 33rd Latitude there was obviously a much stronger UV environment and was much less nnEMF. In Pakistan we live in a village, surrounded by trees and greenery and the electricity turns off every hour or so.

Could the problem be because of nnEMF and blue light? What things could I do improve his balance and co-ordination? Will a blacklight help?

### Sheddie — 2017-08-14T15:31:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/balance-and-co-ordination.20008/post-221640>

**_"What things could I do improve his balance and co-ordination? "_**

Maybe his "motor skills" need 'reset exercises' before physical training classes IF those classes aren't incorporating something to strengthen both halves of the developing brain, like in the warm-ups -- before more rigorous and focused physical and/or mental practices?

There was an older book I liked but I can't find at the moment and forget the title/author (low dopa for decades!). It was material to do with readying/settling little kids before they sit down and 'learn.' I remember some moves like "zip-ups" and performing standing motions like doing a dry-land swim crawl. (Too bad I never thought of doing these with my ADD kid under the morning outdoor sky, neither see them in action except under schoolroom lighting.)

All I could find quickly was something like, [https://www.brainbalancecenters.com...-exercises-promote-hemispheric-brain-balance/](https://www.brainbalancecenters.com/blog/2014/08/brain-boot-camp-home-exercises-promote-hemispheric-brain-balance/) -- but you get the idea and could find something fun and doable.

What I'm correlating here with this, is The Revolutionary Idea that recently made instant sense -- if I'm not synched with the solar cycle nothing else I do to ameliorate my health is going to be corrective or reverse burdensome symptoms. Of course, there's more, but that's what blew me away.)

Addendum: I might have missed it, but I assume you've ruled out brain inflammation which, I think, has validity in some of these diagnoses that seem epidemic (epi-genetic?) in kids these days, and, the usual developmental assessments of hearing, vision and so on?

### Stijn — 2017-08-15T16:13:48+0300

Hi all,

Pleased to be here and meet you all.

First up, how do I get my profile to show Gold Member, as I've seen here for others. And how do I get in the Gold Member facebook group?

About me, here's a short list:

**Age:** 30 **Sex:** M **Joined JackKruse.com:** June 2017

**Location:** Ghent, Belgium (= around latitude 50N)
**Dx:** Myopia, astigmatism

**Surgeries:** None

**Current Rx:** None

**Current Therapies / Protocols:** Uvex blue blocking glasses, wearing long clothes when under artificial light, exposing as much skin as possible outside, cold showers, sweat lodge with IR & UV-A lamps, keto/epi-paleo/IF combo diet, movement, standing desk, music

**Health WINS:** blue blockers, leaving lights off or unscrewing them, in areas that need more light (like bathroom and kitchen) I have incandescent lights, Faraday cage for bed

**Health Goals:** Testing a Schumann generator, testing EMF at home, be consistent in AM sunlight, be consistent in cold thermogenesis (in either tub or lake), save up for a Magnetico, be consistent in diet and movement choices, live and work away from the dangers

**My Optimal Journal:** [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/stijns-optimal-journal.20013/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/stijns-optimal-journal.20013/)

I am quite fortunate in the health front. Haven't even broken a bone in my body. I have been geeking out on this for 6+ years. Curiousity, uncommon questions and answers, controversial beliefs have kept me in good shape. I work as a technical writer. This means: two computer screens, computers, wifi, a cell tower on the premises, commute on the train... 8.5h at work + 2h commute. For now it's 5/7 days. Should move to 4/7 days later in the year. Bad bad bad. I'm quite serious when it comes to health, abundance and relationships. But that's a story for another day. I'm not big fan of fora but will see how this goes.

Be well.

### Stijn — 2017-12-29T19:13:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stijns-optimal-journal.20013/post-231234>

What can we learn from the above image?
According to the standard model, the universe started with the Big Bang. The universe was as small as a single point and then rapidly expanded. It exploded. This is what we see to the left side of the image. In the initial nanoseconds of the universe's existance there were only photons with high energy, and it was very hot. As the universe expanded and cooled, other elements like hydrogen were created. Eventually making galaxies and stars. This is what we see to the right side of the image.
Matter is energy condensed.
I believe that it is determined (the objects in the universe have no other choice) that as the universe expands, there have to be galaxies, stars, planets, and eventually even cells. According to the First Law of Thermodynamics there is the principle that is the Law of Conservation of Energy. No energy can be created or destroyed but can change form. Combining this with the expansion of volume. The expansion necessitates a cooling. But, the total energy within the system remains the same. To keep it that way, the objects in space (starting out way in the beginning with photons) clump together to form stars. Stars are energy bundles in space. As the expansion continues, more and more clumping occurs, even to cells. Cells are small energy bundles in space.
The heavy elements that are used in our (human's) bodies were created in stars like the Sun. Elements like carbon and iron. These elements are released into the universe when the star dies. This is a supernova. This is what is meant with that we are all children of the stars.
This is the connection. This is what is meant with that everything is energy. The high energy photons go to matter with mass, and back again.
This was Einstein's contribution: E=mc2. This is the formula that descibes the condensation of energy to matter (to objects with mass), and vice versa.
This has always been obvious to me. This is what non-physics folk have to learn. Everything exists within the universe. The universe is made up of matter. Cells are made from matter. Humans are made from cells. If you want to understand anything in this world, you have to know something about physics.
Here's an analogy. You work with a computer. You've heard about bits and bytes, and zero's and one's. And these zero's and one's are written on your hard drive or your USB. And yet, this seems a satisfactory answer to the non-physics folk. As long as it works. But, the universe exists out of matter and energy. A hard drive is a metallic plate. How are those bits and bytes staying on that plate? A magnet orients the matter of that metallic plate in a specific way. To be read later, or altered. The quantum mechanical workings of energy and matter is what's keeping your files at your fingertips.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-08-16T14:45:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nightmares.20015/post-221760>

Has anyone any insights on nightmares?

Both my husband and I have been experiencing one nightly.

We removed mouldy mattresses a few weeks ago. I'm wondering if it's connected.

We've been following the protocols. Could this be some kind of detox?

It wakes me up and disturbs my sleep...

Any thoughts welcome!

### Allin — 2017-08-17T02:10:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nightmares.20015/post-221791>

Nightmares are the reflection of the stresses in your daily life. Different situations which are going on with your kids, job, news you hear or the TV you watch, the different discussion groups you participate in help create the nightmare. What is your mind set? Do you have nightmares concerning areas where you are winning, taking control or improving the situation? No you don't. No one does. Change your mind set where you use those nightmares to high-light or focus you attention. _"Nightmares - Another Great Tool for Self Awareness"_

It's possible of course. It's interesting that my husband is also experiencing them.

Hey drezy, my AF is severe. Getting out of bed is a chore. My sleep is terrible. I wake up at 4am daily. Not a good thing for my fatigue. Been trying to get sun daily, but it is a challenge with work. I am on a computer ten hours a day, so that isn't helping. Blessings.

Also, respectfully, how do you rate yourself on:

1.  Blue light exposure past noon (lights or entertainment at night)
2.  Morning 7AM-10AM IRA to UVA exposure
3.  UV solar noon exposure... We are both Texans and I'm a loco gringo(Polish & swiss descent) that worked my way up to 5 hours of sun exposure on a 13UV index day. No burn at all. Erratic erections though, but my wife helped me with that side effect. In fact she keep sending me back out in the sun... A guy can take a hint.
4.  Wifi exposure
5.  Grounding

Give me a rough honest idea where you stand on these and let's work together because us Texans have to have the biggest gains here on the forum. Everything in Texas is bigger including our gains.

### Eddie Garza — 2017-08-17T17:54:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-ct.20016/post-221826>

Hi drezy, I have posted my replies below after each item in bold text. Thank you.

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=221796)
>
> Would you like access to borrow a free Cornet meter to better see your environment? **Yes, please tell me more about the Cornet meter.**
>
> It should be somewhere between PA and GA at this point and it can easily be sent your direction next... **Sounds great**
>
> Also, respectfully, how do you rate yourself on:
>
> 1.  Blue light exposure past noon (lights or entertainment at night) **My work entails being on the computer. I would say at least 6 hours on the computer after 12 noon. I do have f.lux installed on my pc. I try to wear my blue blockers if watching TV on on my PC in the evening.**
>
> 2.  Morning 7AM-10AM IRA to UVA exposure. **Weekdays about 10-15 minutes, weekends 60 minutes.**
>
> 3.  UV solar noon exposure... We are both Texans and I'm a loco gringo(Polish & swiss descent) that worked my way up to 5 hours of sun exposure on a 13UV index day. No burn at all. Erratic erections though, but my wife helped me with that side effect. In fact she keep sending me back out in the sun... A guy can take a hint. **Weekdays 5 minutes, Weekends 15 minutes. I target the exposure between 11:30am and noon time. I wear only swimming trunks during the exposure. The past Saturday it was so hot, that I got a headache after 15 minutes of being in the sun.**
>
> 4.  Wifi exposure. **At work 8 hours daily. I have no choice, the building where I work is full WIFI. At home around an hour or two. My WIFI is turned off at bedtime.**
>
> 5.  Grounding. **I will take my shoes off when sitting outside for 30 -45 minutes. Mostly the grass or sidewalk. I recently started turning off the breaker to my bedroom. No electrical devices in the room.**
>
> Give me a rough honest idea where you stand on these and let's work together because us Texans have to have the biggest gains here on the forum. Everything in Texas is bigger including our gains.

**Please let me know if you need any additional information. Again, thank you for your assistance. My health is at the lowest point. I have only slept 8 hours once in the last 2 years, and that was a day I went to a funeral and was outside for about 90 minutes in the hot sun between 1 and 3 pm. The next day I felt the best I've felt in years. Only lasted that one day.

Note: I have mercury toxicity and possibly lead as well. Haven't been able to detox yet. My adrenals are totally wiped out. Still looking for the light at the end of the tunnel.**

### drezy — 2017-08-18T01:01:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-ct.20016/post-221841>

Don't go thanking me until we see the needle move some.

During your morning UVA light are you taking your glasses off? In a PM @[JoeT](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19594/) asked me to experiment with a UVA meter that I have. Part of what we learned:

Vehicle windows open:
UVA = 1.338 mw/cm2 UVA Irradiance
Vehicle windows closed (same time same light):
UVA = .026 mw/cm2 UVA Irradiance <--- basically zero

It's interesting that your funeral experience ended up being a temporary silver bullet for you. Solar noon + way more exposure clothed/covered. What month was it?

Not sure how much social pressure you've got the tolerance for, but if you could find a larger bucket to stand in or sit and put your feet in during weekend solar noon session then throw some ice in there and see if it buys you some more time in the sun without the headache. I'd look for metal buckets, but would pick knits just yet.

I'll PM you about the meter once I get an understanding where it happens to be. Also I'll gladly point you to some vids about that meter and I'm open to talking you through it over Skype or whatever. Keep a look for my PM.

Dude named Garza not being able to spend as much time in the sun as my white butt? We gotta fix that Eddie. Let's keep in touch.

### Eddie Garza — 2017-08-18T17:12:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-ct.20016/post-221882>

Hi drezy,

Yes, I remove my glasses when I am outdoors.

Not sure what the UVA data you posted signifies. I am sure I will pick it up eventually.

In regards to my sun exposure at the funeral, it was in January. It was the hottest day of the month. It was the only day in that month that the temperature was above 90 degrees. It was actually 95 degrees on the day of the funeral. I was wearing dark clothes so it felt much warmer. I was facing in the direction of the sun, and my eyes were exposed to more direct sunlight than is normal for me. My eyes felt irritated for days afterwards.

Yes, most of my life, I have worked indoors. I’ve never been much of an outdoors person. I’ve worked for over 40 years and my work has always been indoors. The heat just kills me when I am outside unless it’s early in the day or late in the evening.

I spent 15 minutes outside this morning right before 8am. Yesterday when I arrived at home, instead of watching TV, I went to sit outside side and read the article by Dr. Kruse on adrenal fatigue. I was sitting near my live oak tree for about 80 minutes with my feet on the grass.

On the article by Dr. Kruse, he obviously emphasizes Sun exposure, CT, Ketosis and Grounding. I am sure there are many more factors involved.

Yesterday evening I turned off my TV earlier than normal. I then switched off the power to my bedroom and went to bed at 10pm and was asleep in less than 2 minutes. I woke up around 1am to pee and went back to sleep and then woke up at 3:40am and tossed and turned until 4:30am and got up. I feel completely drained this morning.

Thanks

### drezy — 2017-08-23T22:03:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-ct.20016/post-222107>

> [Eddie Garza said:](/goto/post?id=221882)
>
> Not sure what the UVA data you posted signifies. I am sure I will pick it up eventually.

Only that glass (windows, glasses, windshields) kills the UVA that you actually want.

> In regards to my sun exposure at the funeral, it was in January. It was the hottest day of the month. It was the only day in that month that the temperature was above 90 degrees. It was actually 95 degrees on the day of the funeral. I was wearing dark clothes so it felt much warmer. I was facing in the direction of the sun, and my eyes were exposed to more direct sunlight than is normal for me. My eyes felt irritated for days afterwards.

This is what has me interested:

1.  You were not in your normal environments
2.  Last I checked cemeteries don't have wifi (give it time I'm sure they will...)
3.  January + clothes = lower UV index maybe a little like a long morning light session.

I think you may have an experiment there to pick through and possibly repeat. It would be kinda ironic if the key to your better living lies in a cemetery, wouldn't it?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-06T03:22:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-ct.20016/post-222945>

> [Eddie Garza said:](/goto/post?id=221795)
>
> Hey drezy, my AF is severe. Getting out of bed is a chore. My sleep is terrible. I wake up at 4am daily. Not a good thing for my fatigue. Been trying to get sun daily, but it is a challenge with work. I am on a computer ten hours a day, so that isn't helping. Blessings.

I laughed when I read this.......talk about missing the point of this entire site.

GET OFF THE DAMN COMPUTER BECAUSE THAT IS THE CAUSE OF YOUR PROBLEM.

When people hear you say “I can’t,” they hear “I won’t.” Not only does this suggest that you’re not willing to do what it takes to get the job done, but often asserting that you can’t do something is a self-fulfilling prophecy.........as it is in this very thread.

Often, we deny being able to do something because we are afraid — when we’re presented with a new opportunity that would introduce radical change, an exciting new role, or the chance to present in front of a room of senior executives. Next time you want to say “I can’t,” examine your motives carefully — is it coming from a place of inability, or fear?

# Thread: EMF VS LIGHTNING
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-vs-lightning.20024/>

### Billybats — 2017-08-18T01:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-vs-lightning.20024/post-221844>

Hey all,

Just wondering, is long term exposure to emf from wifi/microwaves etc a slow version of getting struck by lightning. I guess it would depend on your health status and strength of the lightning strike.

Thank you.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-04-02T20:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-vs-lightning.20024/post-238541>

BillyBats,

Yes, sort of I think.

Lightning discharges, from what I have learned, produce tremendous amounts of RF radiation. I don't know the frequencies involved but it probably includes the microwave part of electromagnetic spectrum.

I have noticed personally that I feel ill after particularly violent thunderstorms with lots of lightning. I believe that is due to overexposure to tremendous amounts of RF radiation as a consequence of these storms. I am not talking about positive ions that accompany storm fronts.

As an aside, we are currently entering another Grand Solar Minimum. Along with the sun dimming and reduction in total solar irradiance (our star is going quiet) there is an increase in cosmic ray flux. Cosmic ray particles cause cloud nucleation (more clouds in atmosphere) and so more atmospheric rain, snow, and hail events. Concurrently, the sun's magnetic field is weakening and providing less protection from galactic cosmic rays-- the earth's magnetosphere is weakening in sympathy with sun's weakening magnetic field. This also causes our jet stream winds to become more erratic (and more extreme weather results).

So, to sum up, be prepared for more violent weather and storms in years to come (mini-ice age expected within 30 years) with extreme lightning and hail events to be expected.

Does the Grand Solar Minimum make long sun exposure more dangerous because of the cosmic rays? On the lighter sider all this talk about the dangers of blue light made me think of James Thurber's book "My Life And Hard Times" where he talks about an aunt of his that when electricity was installed in their home was convinced it was harmful and was always taking out light bulbs and covering up sockets - a woman way ahead of her time

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-07-20T02:14:05+0300

Christina,

I have also noticed that sunlight feels hotter on my skin than I recall in earlier years. I have heard other people make similar remarks.

I have seen some statements elsewhere that UVC ultraviolet radiation is becoming intenser, and that UVC is more dangerous than UVA or UVB. I have not done research on this so I don't know if this is completely factual. Perhaps Dr Kruse knows the answer.

Yes, I believe increases in cosmic ray flux is more dangerous in general to life on earth, but it will be notably more hazardous to astronauts and airline crews and frequent flyers (because cosmic rays are more powerful at higher altitudes). Cosmic ray increases caused by our weakening magnetosphere are said to also increase incidence of earthquakes and volcanic eruptions because they can excite silica magma chambers underneath earth's surface.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-20T02:33:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-vs-lightning.20024/post-246243>

UVC sunlight does not exist on Earth. Clear you did not watch the Vermont 2018 talk. Large pieces of the puzzle are missing for you.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-07-20T18:13:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-vs-lightning.20024/post-246276>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=246243)
>
> UVC sunlight does not exist on Earth. Clear you did not watch the Vermont 2018 talk. Large pieces of the puzzle are missing for you.

Thanks Dr Kruse for setting record straight on UVC. A few minutes of checking would have revealed that UVC is filtered out by earth's atmosphere. There are still man-made sources of UVC like mercury lamps etc. but that is beside the point.

Still, many people are complaining that the sun's rays are uncomfortably hot compared to what was remembered in past. A quick web search turns up a lot of reports (including scholarly ones) about increasing UVB radiation being measured on earth, and that could account for this phenomenon.

A dated page (way back in 2010) on NASA's public website states that:

"UV Exposure Has Increased Over the Last 30 Years, but Stabilized Since the Mid-1990s" and goes on to report that "UV radiation reaching Earth's surface has increased markedly over the last three decades. Most of the increase has occurred in the mid-and-high latitudes, and there's been little or no increase in tropical regions." This increase is being attributed to erosion of the ozone layer.

There are reported bad effects of this increased UV exposure on earth's life, such as bark being fried off trees in some locations, backs of whales being sunburned, plankton being adversely affected, etc. But I would like to see more scholarly verification of these alarming reports.

One suggestion for why the mid-day sun appears more white today vs. yellow in past (I can verify this simply by looking at sun) is that depletion of ozone results in less scattering of sun's rays-- hence more direct exposure.

The hope is that the ozone layer will heal itself eventually and give us back more protection.

### Christina Gagnon — 2018-07-20T22:31:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-vs-lightning.20024/post-246304>

[@ElectricUniverse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21203/) @JackKruse - I've read that we're moving closer to a pole shift which is weakening the earth's magnetic field letting more galactic cosmic rays, and enhanced ultraviolet rays to hit theearth. This could account for the "hotter" feeling sun. It's supposed to happen gradually over a long period of time (gradually getting worse) - some scientists say parts of the earth will be unihabitable others say not so.

[http://www.sciencemag.org/news/2016...ld-may-have-led-one-earth-s-major-extinctions](http://www.sciencemag.org/news/2016/02/hyperactive-magnetic-field-may-have-led-one-earth-s-major-extinctions)

### Mike David — 2018-07-21T17:46:46+0300

Looks like the world of the internet of things will be passing me by as I become more wirelessly connected to nature. ### ElectricUniverse — 2018-07-23T18:35:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-vs-lightning.20024/post-246527>

Overexposure to UV light does have consequences, so I would not dismiss it lightly.

For those interested, there is a new product on market that will measure your UV exposure directly. It's called **UV Sense.** It is a tiny disc that can be worn anywhere on body, and is marketed by LaRoche-Posey corp. It will not become widely available until 2019.

I dislike the fact that it is powered by your mobile devices. Since I don't allow wireless devices anywhere near my body this probably would not be viable for me. Your mileage may vary.

> Algae = DHA major source in life. DHA is the major biophysical chemical that transfers energy , information, momentum from one biome to another. It can do the same from one form of life to another. 600 million years ago oxygen and DHA showed up at the same time in evolutionary history giving algae the ability to know how to interact with beneficial microbes while it was still in the water.
> **[Dr. Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/?hc_ref=ARRLy1QhlVmnK2BJlbbap9LwXNQCkQuGQc3Jv5wr0hpLEj8H0BhvHOeen-8f2Uz9ukw&fref=nf) FB**
> · [October 6, 2015](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1011683788895975)

There was a book by Thomas Gold about oil & gas being made by bacteria that live and migrate in rock, and thus, a self-renewing resource; his multi-disciplinary critical thinking countered global public relations media at the time about scarcity of oil on Earth (that oil reserves are finite). Googling him today, it seems he has been marginalized over time, but I do remember the book was a very interesting read. I'm not sure the article above (@Brent) is a directly connectable phenom but the oil-from-rock picture made Gold's thesis pop up. I do remember being interested that Gold thought there was Helium in the earth's crust -- another item of 'cognitive dissonance' I never resolved...

\[from Wikipedia\]
**The Deep Hot Biosphere\[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Thomas_Gold&action=edit&section=9)\]**
In a 1992 paper "The Deep Hot Biosphere" in the _[Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Proceedings_of_the_National_Academy_of_Sciences)_,[\[55\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thomas_Gold#cite_note-55) Gold first suggested that microbial life is widespread in the porosity of the crust of the Earth, down to depths of several kilometers, where rising temperatures finally set a limit. The subsurface life obtains its energy not from photosynthesis but from chemical sources in fluids migrating upwards through the crust. The mass of the deep biosphere may be comparable to that of the surface biosphere. Subsurface life may be widespread on other bodies in the solar system and throughout the universe, even on worlds unaccompanied by other stars.

Gold also published a book of the same title in 1999, which expanded on the arguments in his 1992 paper and included speculations on the [origin of life](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Origin_of_life).[\[56\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thomas_Gold#cite_note-56)

According to Gold, bacteria feeding on the oil accounts for the presence of biological debris in [hydrocarbon fuels](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fossil_fuel), obviating the need to resort to a [biogenic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biogenic) theory for the origin of the latter. The flows of underground [hydrocarbons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hydrocarbon) may also explain oddities in the concentration of other mineral deposits.

In short, Gold said about the origin of natural hydrocarbons (petroleum and natural gas): _Hydrocarbons are not biology reworked by geology (as the traditional view would hold), but rather geology reworked by biology_.[\[42\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thomas_Gold#cite_note-MetaResearch-42)

# Thread: Skin in the game - Help with PCOS, Hashis and creativity!  Journal

### KruseKadet — 2017-08-18T15:01:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-in-the-game-help-with-pcos-hashis-and-creativity-journal.20027/post-221878>

Hey Kruse-Folk,
Would love some help with my PCOS, Hashis, obesity and hormone mega-fail. I want to be a bright shiny peep, not a dreggy dopamine-vortex where creativity goes to die!! Would so appreciate help as am new to science!

Had a quick one-on-one with Dr Kruse. He thought my labs were alarming, and possibly incorrect, re my super high oestrogen. He told me to change my environment, and get retested, which I am in the process of doing. Currently investigating EMF proofing, and getting 3 hrs am sunlight, fish meal each morning, grounding, icewater face dipping, and trying to avoid carbohydrates. I am failing on the latter. Still drawn obsessively back to sugar just when I think I have broken it’s back, so it’s clear my dopamine / beta endorphin production is not yet fully cooking. Despite BAB breaky, mood falls low at 4pm, and craving. This seems crucial to me.

Is there anything else I can do to help support the changes to happen? I am on no thyroid meds, nor hormone assist at all. Australia is a wasteland for red-pilled doctors, zero help. Would progesterone or DHEA help me? Was put on it once before by a doc years ago and it seemed to help…

If any other women are working these angles, PCOS, HASHIS etc, would love to hear from you, and share the progress.

Much love to Dr Kruse for rocking our collective reality! X Kruse Kadet

HISTORY
vegetarian from age 6.
baby sat by TV from early age.
sugar binging from age 7
miopia age 8
zero sun ever.
yoyo dieting starving - sugar binging - obesity.
glasses and contact lenses constantly.
low DHEA, chronic anaemia, hypoglycaemia - diagnosed aged 22
musician, working in studio is my job, blue light toxic for decades.
depression - obsessive and negative thinking,
night owl. 3am often the sleep time.
No sweating and repulsion to cold.
fried ovaries with laptop for decades.
zero fish ever.
obsessive worker till adrenal fatigue blow out. Now lacking drive for passion projects.
low dopamine relationships (no more!)
fibroids in uterus seen in ultrasound four years ago.
periods stopped coming, huge build up of material but nothing coming out for a year and a half. Very uncomfortable, till I blasted them back to working with iodine painting.

Parents.
Obese but very lovely mother, athlete father, both older at conception.
Mother arthritis and macular degeneration. Father faulty heart valve, glaucoma, died of an aneurysm after triple bypass surgery.

CURRENT LABS

IODINE URINE corrected - 51.0 - (when range for deficiency is 51>100)
CREATININE Urine Spot - 4.9L - (when normal range between 5.0 >13.0 mmol)

25 OH VITAMIN D
Result 76 - (Normal Range in Australia is between 50>250m IU/l)
\- I got this up from 50 in just 4 months!

THYROID FUNCTION ASSESSMENT
TSH 2.15 - (Normal Range 0.50 - 5.00 mIU/L)
FREE T4 14.1 - (Normal Range - 11.0 - 21.0 pmol/L )
FREE T3 4.5 - (Normal Range 3.1 - 6.0 pmol/L )
Reverse T3 200.0 - (Normal Range \*L 230.0 - 540.0 pmol/L )
FT3 : Reverse T3 Ratio ( X 100) 2.235 - (Normal Range \*H 1.200 - 2.200)

THYROID AUTO-Abs
THYROID PEROXIDASE Ab. 122 - (Normal Range \*H 0.0 - 35.0 IU/mL )
ANTITHYROGLOBULIN Ab. 56.3 - (Normal Range 0.0 - 115 IU/mL )
TSH RECEPTOR AB <1.0 - (Normal Range 0.0 - 1.5)

HORMONES

Progesterone Metabolites
b-Pregnanediol - Below premenopausal range - 435.0 ng/mg - (Normal Range 450 - 2300)
a-Pregnanediol - Below premenopausal range - 90.0 ng/mg - (Normal Range 120 - 740)

Androgen Metabolites
DHEAS - Low end of range - 68.0 ng/mg - (Normal Range 23 - 350)
Androsterone - Within range - 1282.0 ng/mg - (Normal Range 400 - 1700)
Etiocholanolone - High end of range- 855.0 ng/mg - (Normal Range 260 - 950)
Testosterone - Within range - 6.1 ng/mg - (Normal Range 4 - 14)
5a-DHT - High end of range - 7.2 ng/mg - (Normal Range 0 - 8.8)
5a-Androstanediol - Within range -18.6 ng/mg - (Normal Range 12 - 30)
5b-Androstanediol - Above range - 145.4 ng/mg - (Normal Range 20 - 75)
Epi-Testosterone - Low end of range - 5.0 ng/mg - (Normal Range 4.5 - 22.3)

Estrogen Metabolites
Estrone(E1) - Above range - 60.4 ng/mg - (Normal Range 12 - 26\_
Estradiol(E2) - Above range - 15.6 ng/mg - (Normal Range 1.8 - 4.5)
Estriol(E3) - Above range - 30.8 ng/mg - (Normal Range 5 - 18)
2-OH-E1 - Above range - 19.0 ng/mg - (Normal Range 4.6 - 14.4)
4-OH-E1 - Above range - 2.7 ng/mg (Normal Range 0 - 1.8)

# Thread: Hello from Carmel, Indiana
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-carmel-indiana.20028/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sheryl quigley — 2017-08-18T18:41:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-carmel-indiana.20028/post-221887>

I am pretty new to this forum and website. I think I followed Dr. Kruse on FB for about a year but didn't really pay much attention to his posts and just scrolled right by. I got bored recently with the podcasts I was listening to and somehow found one of his. From the moment I started listening I was hooked and have voraciously listened to all of his podcasts- some of them 2-3 times. It is all slowly starting to sink in. Trying to read as much as I can on this website as well but I am in my car a lot (plus trying to minimize time at the computer) so the podcasts have been awesome for me. I love anything that goes against conventional "wisdom" or "medicine". I love the way Dr. Kruse connects so many dots and really gets to the root of why diseases are so rampant and getting worse.
I am currently a Bronze member but am going to upgrade to Gold membership. Just ordered my 23&me test and had blood work so will start an optimal journal with the new information when I get it.

**Age: 44
Sex:** F
**Joined JackKruse.com: July 2017
Location:** Carmel, IN USA
**Dx:** Hypothyroid for 10 years
**Surgeries:** 3 C-sections, 2 umbilical hernia surgeries and 1 partial tummy tuck
**Current Rx:** Cut out most supplements- am on compounded t3/t4, still take some iron and vit c, tryptophan, herbs for digestion and some homeopathic remedies
**Current Therapies/Protocols:** Keto since Feb 2016- lost 25 pounds in Feb 2016 and have kept it off**.** Changed all my LED lights to incandescent, Blu blocking glasses during day and orange ones at night, in AM sun for as long as I can every morning- now favorite part of day. Use Dminder app to get Vit D during the day. Have WIFI off at night while sleeping- would take it out completely but husband won't allow it (right now). Eating as much seafood as possible. Use the kiniki bathing suits. Have Iris software on computer and blu light blocking screen on phone.
**Health Wins**\- pretty much at ideal weight-have been able to cut out all melatonin at night , haven't used sunglasses at all for weeks- have gone from not being able to go to mailbox with out them to not needing them at all. Drive with windows open, work out with lights off and windows open. Getting outside so much more. Drinking tons of water and feel so much better- not needing afternoon naps!
**Health Goals- To** get off thyroid meds. CT therapy- hate the cold but am trying to change mindset to embrace- dunking my head in cold water- so far that is fine. Always have fasting blood glucose levels on the high end of normal -like 90-102 . My n=1 with turning WIFI off at night has brought it down 10 points in AM. Also will try to cut out BP coffee- can't do it just yet. Magnetico sleeping pad
**My Optimal Journal:** No link yet; not started

### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-18T23:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-carmel-indiana.20028/post-221900>

Welcome. I'm sitting on my balcony and thinking about humanity and the things I just saw in Cuba over the last week. We are really off track in the modern world. We are not hearty any longer. What separates the best of humanity from the rest is how they manage the gift of their time. If you settle for moderation, you're settling for a mediocre life. Too many people live the same year 80 times over, and call it a life. You can never let depolorable circumstances drive you to quit. Your attitude can over come any circumstance if your beliefs just get out of the way. If you accept mediocre, you have no room to complain when the consequences of the decision become apparent. Those 'who espouse' for things in moderation are often the beneficiaries of this action in some major way. If you are the consumer, patient, or a spouse, and accept something less than ideal, you're making a trade whether you know it or not. Mother Nature never accepts moderation in her response to life's challenges, so why should you?

# Thread: shameless cancer-free joy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shameless-cancer-free-joy.20030/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ValerieBee — 2017-08-19T00:00:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shameless-cancer-free-joy.20030/post-221901>

I know this is shameless, but we just got a NED (no evidence of disease) from our Little Man's latest CT. He was diagnosed with a very rare, unexplainable sarcoma in January 2017, chemotherapy along with alt therapies such as breast milk, cannabis, dietary changes and more helped shrink the tumor 75% (from 12x12x14 cm). 100% successful resection April 27 2017. NED since. This past scan is the trigger for me and his father, along with docs to officially stop chemotherapy (last treatment was in June). Supporting him through this journey lead me to this forum, and helped me re-awaken the inner mitochondriac and truly make me feel confident in trusting my instincts. We have had the best of a bad situation throughout his horrible diagnosis. He's 5. Generally super-healthy. His condition was a rare type of cancer-sarcoma. It a rare sarcoma-pleuropulmonary blastoma. on top of that, no genetic mutation in blood or tumor (detected in up to 70% of PPB cases). They're now testing genetics for a mutation they've never before linked to this rare sarcoma. Sheesh with the genetic determinism! His situation lead me to realize the only potential risk factor in his world was the EMF he's exposed to. He beat so many odds throughout his treatment. I think it is because of the healthy baseline he had--I reduced all the environmental toxins I was aware of & strengthened his immunity the best I could. He was born at home, not vaccinated, co-slept, breast-fed until after 3 years old, avoid GMOs & eat clean as much as possible, minimal TV etc. ........ Once my head cleared from the shock of his diagnosis I realized the smart meter was put on our house (we couldn't opt out), and we allowed him to use a tablet occasionally within the past year (a gift that we limited and quickly correlated with behavior issues so we stopped almost entirely). His docs think my concerns about EMF are BS. They disagree with me about sunscreen. The dietician told me she doesn't recommend organic produce because people tend to not wash it as well so it is actually prone to more illness and anyway glyphosate doesn't matter. (seriously). Regardless, I've been taking steps to reduce and eliminate what I can wrt EMF, and boost his immune system and circadian body as much as possible. At one point when I was researching how to help him not relapse, I had a moment of realization. I can't stop AllTheThings. I have to reduce what I can, but focus on strengthening his body. I'm so grateful for this community, and for what I've learned through the experience my son had to go through. He has been able to beat an aggressive cancer, and hopefully the lessons learned here will benefit him and the rest of his family (me, his father, his brother and beyond) for years to come.

### fitness@home — 2017-08-19T02:08:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shameless-cancer-free-joy.20030/post-221905>

Shameless joy is fantastic, especially the cancer-free kind. Great job Mom!

### caroline — 2017-08-19T05:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shameless-cancer-free-joy.20030/post-221911>

I am sitting here with tears running down my cheeks - You are one amazing woman .....keep going!

### ScottishEmma — 2017-08-19T08:38:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shameless-cancer-free-joy.20030/post-221923>

Absolutely wonderful! It's so lovely to read!!

### shiran — 2017-08-19T14:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shameless-cancer-free-joy.20030/post-221936>

Wow that's so exciting! Keep on the good work

Hello all,

I've been ill for almost 15 years and had trouble with vitamin D and sunlight exposure for the past handful of years where either one gives me a migraine that lasts three or four days. Recently discovered that I have Lyme disease and co-infections and believe I'm tolerating more sun now after working on improving my lymphatic health.

I first read Dr. Kruse a couple years ago and started getting more sunshine. Then I hit some setbacks and forgot about Dr. K's teachings. Now I'm going all out.

If you want to know more, I've written about my adventures with Vitamin D (I'm homozygous for VDR TAQ and BSM) on my blog at [http://howirecovered.com/vitamin-d-and-sunlight-master-theory-of-disease/](http://howirecovered.com/vitamin-d-and-sunlight-master-theory-of-disease/)

Eric

# Thread: Hello from London, Ontario Canada
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london-ontario-canada.20034/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Steph Roch — 2017-08-19T23:21:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london-ontario-canada.20034/post-221945>

Hello everyone. I am so grateful to be on this amazing journey of discovery and health. I have learned a lot of very valuable information and I am committing myself to learning and improving each and every day. I take this science very seriously and I am going to be treating this material as if I were studying for my PhD in mitochondrial biophysics. I do not have a university level science background (although I did earn very good marks in biology and chemistry in high school). I feel as though I can wrap my mind around all of this as long as I am diligent and ask many questions.

I am in this 100%. I have made many changes so far that have been very drastic (I pulled my three kids from school and am homeschooling them, I have eliminated the use of lights at night with the exception of candlelight and a UV flashlight, I have taken the wifi out of my home, I hired an EMF specialist to come in to measure nnEMF and I turn the breakers off at night). In addition to these changes, I only take cold showers, I use my Quantlet daily, I wear blue blockers whenever on a computer or looking at my cell, I get outside every day in the early morning sun grounded, as well as the afternoon sun and I quite often watch the sun set. I am working on other changes as well, including purchasing leather soled shoes, eventually getting a Magnetico and changing my eyeglasses to blue blocking lenses (I currently use Spektrum glasses over my regular eyeglasses if looking at blue light).

Diet: I eat quite a high fat diet just but just recently started incorporating oysters at least 3-4 times a week, salmon almost daily, sardines, scallops, lobster, and shrimp. I eat grass fed beef and some local chicken and produce from my garden only, with the exception of avocados. I try to limit fruit but occasionally have a bite of a local peach or some blueberries (in season).

A couple of areas that I am struggling with are where to purchase prescription glasses (in my area) that block out the appropriate spectrum of blue light (my local lab said that they could only provide lenses that block out up to 420nm, their version of a blue blocking lens). I am also looking at the BPI tint and what would be most appropriate.

I am also trying to figure out if I should be including more UV light at night, which may be helpful for seeing once the days get much shorter in the winter or whether I should be including more red light and exactly what bulbs to purchase).

Another area that I am very curious about is whether or not I should try a zero carb diet (meaning only seafood or other meat) and limiting all produce in the winter months.

If anyone else lives at around the 43rd latitude, are there any other hacks that I should be doing?

Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks,
Stephanie

# Thread: MCT oil and foods
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mct-oil-and-foods.20037/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Billybats — 2017-08-21T15:13:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mct-oil-and-foods.20037/post-221995>

Hello All,

Can one get MCT from just regular food. Is there an extra nutrient/mineral/fat in MCT oil that you can't get from food. Also Dr. Kruse says we are bacteria, (this may be a dumb thought) then why would one take coconut oil and/or MCT oil if they are antibacteria, antifungal, and antiviral. I could see for the antiviral and maybe the fungal, unless by taking them it balances it out and that may be why some people have reactions and some none. Maybe more helpful as antiviral since they say viruses are the cause of diseases, or not, unless I am understanding viruses wrong or got that all wrong. Thinking out loud.

Thank you

### WalterNL — 2017-08-21T20:53:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mct-oil-and-foods.20037/post-222006>

MCT oil is partly caprylic and partly capric acid, these are saturad fatty acids that can be found in many foods. Coconuts, palm oil, butter, other dairy products, etc. There is nothing special in MCT oil, it is merely a refined version of coconut oil (keep in mind that coconut oil is a refined product by itself). The antiviral/antibacterial properties are mostly related to lauric acid, which is not in most MCT oils, but it is in the natural package. It has been shown to kill several harmful germs, but it definitely does not kill ALL bacteria/funghi/viruses.

### Billybats — 2017-08-21T21:09:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mct-oil-and-foods.20037/post-222008>

Thank you WalterNL. I wasn't sure but that was pretty close to what I was thinking. Another reason I pretty much stopped all supplements except turmeric. I still have coconut oil but may just use it on my hair now. When I took multivitamins and other supplements they messed me up. This site has completed the puzzle of mine.

But I just had the thought: if I use a snorkel/mp3 player and just stay completely under water while I swim, will the faraday effect of the water protect me from the blue lights ??

Haha. Something along those lines.

What's everyone's opinion on this one - is raw food an environmental mismatch in a colder climate?

For example, imagine coming in on a cold winters evening to a dinner of slow cooked lamb with oven baked carrots and onion compared to a fruit salad and/or plate of sushi?

### ScottishEmma — 2017-08-26T10:24:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-222291>

> [Technosmith said:](/goto/post?id=222189)

>
> For example, imagine coming in on a cold winters evening to a dinner of slow cooked lamb with oven baked carrots and onion compared to a fruit salad and/or plate of sushi?

When I was a raw foodist I don't remember wishing that my food was hot in Winter. The point is that it is totally unnatural to eat salad in winter because aside from some wild greens, it just doesn't grow here.

### Sue-UK — 2017-08-26T12:38:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-222296>

> [Technosmith said:](/goto/post?id=222189)
>
> What's everyone's opinion on this one - is raw food an environmental mismatch in a colder climate?

I watched a food programme recently, not usually interested but it was about the Artic, partly about the drift towards imported food, but how there are people trying to maintain or revive the traditional diet. The show host was invited to a traditional meal, and there was one cooked dish offered, but the majority was raw seafood or sea mammal. I don't do well with just cold raw seafood or meat in the winter if I'm trying to live in the cold lane, and drinking ice cold water while doing CT in winter can take me over the edge ..... but I was thinking about their haplotype and lifestyle in general, and the traditional diet's effects on the microbiome, and the mitochondria. Their haplotype and the traditional wearing the skins of arctic mammals that are super efficient at conserving and recycling heat/light could make it possible to survive without needing to obtain heat from the cooking of food, in a area without trees and where oils from sea mammals would be a precious resource. All back to overall context ...?

### Valerie — 2017-08-28T00:38:27+0300

### Sue-UK — 2017-09-12T14:40:16+0300

> [Valerie said:](/goto/post?id=222383)

### Valerie — 2017-09-13T05:03:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-223514>

But should we eat high carb fruit (oranges) in December when the UV is at the minimum? I assume this is considered seasonal food

### Sue-UK — 2017-09-13T13:27:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-223534>

> [Valerie said:](/goto/post?id=223514)
>
> But should we eat high carb fruit (oranges) in December when the UV is at the minimum? I assume this is considered seasonal food

Your UV minimum is going to be higher than my UV minimum in the UK, so I think of it as if I can pick it off the plant and eat it, it is seasonal, it still represents the light signal of my latitude. I've been eating plums straight from the tree, but now that's it for another year, so for me the plums say "end August/early September". If I lived at a latitude where oranges were growing and edible in December, the oranges would say "December."

### ScottishEmma — 2017-09-15T08:59:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-223682>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=223534)
>
> Your UV minimum is going to be higher than my UV minimum in the UK, so I think of it as if I can pick it off the plant and eat it, it is seasonal, it still represents the light signal of my latitude. I've been eating plums straight from the tree, but now that's it for another year, so for me the plums say "end August/early September". If I lived at a latitude where oranges were growing and edible in December, the oranges would say "December."

I'm interested because I've been thinking of this...

I learned how to forage from some of the best foragers in the UK and a lot of what's talked about is the preservation of foraged foods. All of them preserve the foods in some way whether that's vinegars, condiments, fruit leathers etc.

Surely that's a mismatch, right? Even though they're accessing nutritional profiles that are off the charts and food that is so local it's growing in your back garden...

But yeah, preservation is a mismatch.

### Sue-UK — 2017-09-15T10:29:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-223687>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=223682)
>
> I'm interested because I've been thinking of this...
>
> I learned how to forage from some of the best foragers in the UK and a lot of what's talked about is the preservation of foraged foods. All of them preserve the foods in some way whether that's vinegars, condiments, fruit leathers etc.
>
> Surely that's a mismatch, right? Even though they're accessing nutritional profiles that are off the charts and food that is so local it's growing in your back garden...
>
> But yeah, preservation is a mismatch.

I don't think preservation is a mismatch necessarily. In the context of a truly connected human it could mean one thing, (survival during a harsh season) but to a disconnected human it could mean something else (mismatch). I was watching a programme on bears coming out of hibernation. One bear was grubbing through the remaining snow and found some frozen berries. He ate the bounty from mother natures own freezer .. In an otherwise truly connected animal it had survival value. There's lots of examples in nature where creatures store food, from nuts and seeds etc and some (can't remember which...) keep living insects or larvae in holes in the ground so cold they stay alive but immobile. So perhaps for the human, food preservation has the potential to be an innovation or a mismatch, depending on the context of the environment faced as a whole, and what you do with the food before eating it. So a few defrosted berries in the winter when I'm living in the cold lane would have a different overall context to living in a simulated summer with the berries made into a pie.

### Linz — 2017-09-15T10:50:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-223688>

Perhaps just a mismatch because redox is so damaged by the environment

### drezy — 2017-09-16T19:39:45+0300

The orange line of questioning has had me thinking. I have grapes that grow all over my property and like California oranges I suspect they are not truly native in the thousand year timeframe at least. That's a whole other bag of worms. Supposedly oranges originated in South Asia so that lattitude technically lines up with Mexico. That said orange trees themselves seem to be unburdened by this fact.

I try to use bears as my model. Why would a bear eat sweet fruits (berries) also available near the end of summer fall? Ok granted a bear wouldn't overthink it. It would eat all the berries because they taste really good. Perhaps unbeknownst to the bear it's to pack fat on for the winter. Compound that with the fact that hybridized fruit now has a way higher sugar content than wild fruits and eat at your own discretion.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-17T16:52:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-223857>

Not sure why this is a WOW. It is basic mitochondriac 101 stuff here:
**There are real quantum and evolutionary differences between fruits and vegetables. Fruits are reproductive organs of plants, whereas vegetables are structural body parts designed to hold the plant up, gather sunlight, and perform photosynthesis. Many fruits are "designed by nature “to be eaten by the animals that will best transport its seeds to good locations for reproductive purposes—some are intended for birds, some for mammals, based upon their specific biologic package built by the sun. Fruits we are naturally drawn to tend to be sweet because they are loaded with glucose or fructose—as opposed to cranberries, for example, which are transported by water, or hot peppers, which are intended for birds. Vegetables don't "want" to be eaten by the animal kingdom (vegan myth exposed) so they are often made bitter, tough, difficult to digest, and invested with defensive chemicals by nature. Fruits tend to contain much lower amounts of the most noxious toxins--there would be no advantage to the plant to ward off or even try to kill its seed. So the eating of fruits in strong light cycles makes far more quantum sense when food is thought about in this way.

**Food is just data about sunlight’s travels around our planet codified by photosynthesis that your mitochondria deciphers. Don't try to let food or exercise be the missing key in your life, or light will stop being your unmatched lock, that doesn't let you merge on the path to optimal. Evolution succeeds because of its continuous random violent execution of its imperfect plan by changing the geometry locally between the respiratory proteins using energy in sunlight to alter energy and matter from light, rather than relying on genetic perfection executed precisely.**
**

### drezy — 2017-09-17T17:30:46+0300

All the science I'm learning here is great, but as a foodie, connecting the dots between my life and that statement above was pivotal for me.

I was always amazed at some vegetables people eat like beans. Eating a food that will kill you unless you cook it always seemed very odd to me. I remember many a holiday gathering where the gastrointestinal distress(aka farting) of eating that former (slightly still?) toxic staple became a big potty humor joke.

Humans sure are odd creatures.

EDIT: So much of a change to our life that asking my wife what she wants for dinner is a running joke in my home anymore. Ever since she read jack's menopause(women gaining time) blog 2 months ago the woman hasten oysters for dinner 57 out of the last 60 days!

### Da-mo — 2017-09-17T22:29:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-223868>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=223687)
>
> I don't think preservation is a mismatch necessarily. In the context of a truly connected human it could mean one thing, (survival during a harsh season) but to a disconnected human it could mean something else (mismatch). I was watching a programme on bears coming out of hibernation. One bear was grubbing through the remaining snow and found some frozen berries. He ate the bounty from mother natures own freezer .. In an otherwise truly connected animal it had survival value. There's lots of examples in nature where creatures store food, from nuts and seeds etc and some (can't remember which...) keep living insects or larvae in holes in the ground so cold they stay alive but immobile. So perhaps for the human, food preservation has the potential to be an innovation or a mismatch, depending on the context of the environment faced as a whole, and what you do with the food before eating it. So a few defrosted berries in the winter when I'm living in the cold lane would have a different overall context to living in a simulated summer with the berries made into a pie.

A few things on the bear and the berries - just speculating . . . .

So the berries probably froze in a waning light cycle and the bear ate them in a waxing light cycle - maybe similar.

When the bear went into hibernation it was probably leptin resistant (fat) to store heat for the winter. Coming out of hibernation, CT has made the bear leptin sensitive . . . . shit on a shingle theory

If the bear was eating berries just before hibernation, it probably has the gut flora to suitably deal with the berries.

The bear's mission now is to put on condition over the warmer months for breeding and hibernation purposes. It will once again get leptin resistant to make it fat and lazy and want to den and store energy for breeding and raising cubs.

For the bear, hibernation and CT is the natural cure - cycling between leptin resistance and sensitivity.

I would surmise that the bear is naturally quite uncoupled.

Unfortunately for us, our hibernation has to be condensed down into a narrow few hours each night.

### Sue-UK — 2017-09-18T11:12:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-223887>

> [Technosmith said:](/goto/post?id=223865)
>
> The question once again, is vegetable eating beneficial or even needed if seafood and other animal foods are available?

I think even carnivorous animals get a dose of vegetation if they catch an animal in the wild. There's a report (Nourishing Traditions cookbook) of an account of someone watching a lion catch a zebra.. The lion ate through the intestine on the right flank of the zebra to get to the liver, and then it picked out the other internal organs, leaving the rest of the carcass for the scavengers. It would have got a dose of fermenting vegetation ...? Elsewhere it was also sometimes traditional to bury whole fish ... and other people who would eat the small intestine of animals. I also remember seeing a programme where in the artic they catch birds coming back to nest, and basically take off the feathers, bury them whole and let them ferment. So its possible that partially digested vegetation/enzymes generally is beneficial from the human enzymes and microbiome standpoint, and that fermenting vegetables as a condiment was a human innovation in some latitudes to offset cooking/scarcity/sharing of limited resources?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-20T17:00:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-224014>

The slant is on an equatorial story of how water and sunlight build the battery of life that runs our most critical programs. Wet season = more water and more light buried in fruits and vegetables via photosynthesis. In other words this is when more light energy is buried in foods and that light is the key to turning on or off the epigenetic programs in us. Deep lesson here about light. Life always results from a spark of light to make an electric plasma in a zygote. This is why food has a massive effect early in life and the effect diminishes with time when cells lose their ability to be multipotent. It also points out why young RBC's can slow aging compared to older RBC's (coming CPC blog). [https://www.youtube.com/watch?time\_continue=207&v=bzX78PDF1sg](https://www.youtube.com/watch?time_continue=207&v=bzX78PDF1sg)

>
> The question once again, is vegetable eating beneficial or even needed if seafood and other animal foods are available?

Good question, but just by observation, in almost all cultures on the earth vegetables or plants in some form are the main portion of food for people, and probably always has been. And often populations eating the most vegetables are found to be the most healthy, and live longest.

### drezy — 2018-02-21T00:49:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-235228>

FYI as a running experiment for the last 10 years my diet has been very significantly biased toward animal (and now seafood) protein. We're talking half a head of cabbage, a few garlic cloves, and half an onion a week tops.

I've been down for the count ill once in the last 10 years and mildly ill (headaches/tummy troubles) twice in the same ten years. My biggest problem in the workplace was having to always pick up for regularly ill coworkers.

I'm the cook in the house and my own wife's health/inllness frequency is very similar to mine.

I know you're trying to develop an understanding of diet in reference to the Okinawans. I just wanted to give you my own 2 cents.

### drezy — 2018-02-21T16:24:27+0200
