^^^ Inger does!

I'm glad that you've been experiencing improvements. It's exciting to start uncovering how vital you were supposed to be.

My wife does this and my son (a little looser since he's just a kid). It works as a good contrast for my boy when once or twice a month he has some pizza or burger with a bun and can feel the difference right away. It just doesn't burn as clean. Unfortunately for me this translates to fartiness so I keep a limit on it. I've been doing it the longest but my sons transition is the most telling. When he was 2 a large portion of his diet was up to my parents. We live near "the allergy capitol of the US" and my son had pretty severe allergies. Now that he's epi-paleo nothing really bothers him anymore. My dad warns us of the pollen count regularly, but is amazed that it just doesn't matter anymore for my boy. I think it's a deuterium story.

Also I had a coworker with a decades long issue with constipation. I kept sending him paleo food porn pictures until he finally gave in and tried dropping the carbs. Three weeks in he was right as rain.

### WalterNL — 2018-02-21T18:05:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-235292>

> [Jan Christer said:](/goto/post?id=235223)
>
> Good question, but just by observation, in almost all cultures on the earth vegetables or plants in some form are the main portion of food for people, and probably always has been. And often populations eating the most vegetables are found to be the most healthy, and live longest.

That's true. But are they healthy because of that, or regardless of that. Such research ignores pretty much 99% of their lifestyle and zeroes in on foods. When talking specifically about vegetables, they represent a form of culture. Vegetables and fruits are simply available to humans, and humans like to try a lot (food industry knows this all too well). So perhaps without really needing it, it just becomes an expression of culture, trying things with your group that are only available to your group. Even if in theory a human could survive on sun/grounding, water and fish, that would be a boring life. Vegetables are available and can make most meals better when prepared properly as cultures always find out through many generations.

Apart form that, I think the food perspective is not that important, and the western food perspective is plain nuts where we see food as a sum of nutrients that can be engineered however we like.

I thought I saw him say somewhere that his Daughter eats this way...

I was thinking similar. Wonder if anyone knows Jack's take on a carnivorous diet?

Are many on this forum eating minimal to zero plant foods then? When eating an Epi-Paleo ketogenic diet, is there really much benefit to adding in any plant foods that could easily take you out of ketosis.

There is also the deuterium content to consider.

### Jan Christer — 2018-02-21T23:43:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-235321>

> [WalterNL said:](/goto/post?id=235292)
>
> That's true. But are they healthy because of that, or regardless of that. Such research ignores pretty much 99% of their lifestyle and zeroes in on foods. When talking specifically about vegetables, they represent a form of culture. Vegetables and fruits are simply available to humans, and humans like to try a lot (food industry knows this all too well). So perhaps without really needing it, it just becomes an expression of culture, trying things with your group that are only available to your group. Even if in theory a human could survive on sun/grounding, water and fish, that would be a boring life. Vegetables are available and can make most meals better when prepared properly as cultures always find out through many generations.
>
> Apart form that, I think the food perspective is not that important, and the western food perspective is plain nuts where we see food as a sum of nutrients that can be engineered however we like.

Yes, it's possible that vegetables mainly represent a form of culture, and are eaten because they are available.

But also vegetables supply many essential vitamins, minerals and phytonutrients for the human body that are not available from animal-based foods.

So, nutrition-wise, it seems that they complement each other.

It would be very interesting to see how animal-only diets would affect longevity and health in populations, compared to mixed diets.

### Dean6789 — 2018-06-03T20:10:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-242621>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=235240)

> Fatty pork has been a large part of my diet the last 1o years. I'm also particular and cooked most of the foods I eat from scratch so maybe I was able to source higher quality meats. I'd say fatty pork 50%, beef 30%, seafood 20%mover the years.
>

>
> Do this exact google search and read a bunch:
>
> Code:
>
> ```
> cfs site:www.jackkruse.com
> ```

Here an excerpt from an email I received this week from a farmer:

"**Pork is the most widely eaten meat, making up 38% of meat production worldwide.**

Historically, pork has been a staple in traditional diets for thousands of years. Wild hogs were hunted in the wild and then eventually domesticated. Also historically, pork has been banned in cultures and religions for thousands of year. What’s the big deal? To eat pork or not to eat pork?

**Blood:** By reviewing live blood samples, the Weston A. Price Foundation determined that eating plain cooked pork has a negative effect on our blood. It causes clotting and biochemical inflammation and eventually chronic disease. These results brought shame to pigs.

On the other hand, Dr. Price observed many traditional people healthfully eating pork. It’s one of the oldest foods. What’s going on here?

The secret is that each traditional culture eats pork in a particular way – by marinating in vinegar or fermenting or curing or accompanying it with a fermented veggie. This counteracts the negative effects. Amazing!"

What's your take on this? Are these "negative effects" real"? Is there anything to the idea of needing to add some fermenting ingredients (or ACV, etc.)?

### drezy — 2018-06-03T20:59:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tropical-foods.20039/post-242623>

> [Dean6789 said:](/goto/post?id=242621)
>
> Here an excerpt from an email I received this week from a farmer:
>
> "**Pork is the most widely eaten meat, making up 38% of meat production worldwide.**
>
> Historically, pork has been a staple in traditional diets for thousands of years. Wild hogs were hunted in the wild and then eventually domesticated. Also historically, pork has been banned in cultures and religions for thousands of year. What’s the big deal? To eat pork or not to eat pork?
>
> **Blood:** By reviewing live blood samples, the Weston A. Price Foundation determined that eating plain cooked pork has a negative effect on our blood. It causes clotting and biochemical inflammation and eventually chronic disease. These results brought shame to pigs.
>
> On the other hand, Dr. Price observed many traditional people healthfully eating pork. It’s one of the oldest foods. What’s going on here?
>
> The secret is that each traditional culture eats pork in a particular way – by marinating in vinegar or fermenting or curing or accompanying it with a fermented veggie. This counteracts the negative effects. Amazing!"
>
> What's your take on this? Are these "negative effects" real"? Is there anything to the idea of needing to add some fermenting ingredients (or ACV, etc.)?

It is interesting that the very very pork eating Germans usually pair their pork with kraut. The Chinese usually pair pork with veggies but seem to be 50/50 or less with fermented veggie pairings.

I think the fattiness and amount of pork I eat would be a challenge for many people. That's my main guess.

Fat absorption (including absorption of dietary vitamin D,E,A,& K) is like a digestive relay race involving more of your digestive system than other nutrients. If anything falls out from a digestive system beating I think fat absorption is one of the first functions to fall out.

I will say that more traditional Goan Pork Vindaloo ( [https://www.saveur.com/article/recipes/pork-vindaloo-spicy-goanese-pork-stew#comment-link-to](https://www.saveur.com/article/recipes/pork-vindaloo-spicy-goanese-pork-stew#comment-link-to) ) disappears amazingly fast in my house. If you cook and aren't too sensitive to some heat you owe it to yourself to try that recipe out. Since you're on this forum the minuscule 1/2 tsp turmeric needs an obvious boost. Also FWIW I've skipped the tamarind paste and not really missed it.

> MB has a low toxicity and is used for staining cells in vivo \[1, 17\]. In aqueous solutions it is in monomer and dimmer forms and **has two peaks of absorption at 668 and 609 nm.** MB can be activated by irradiation with appropriate wavelength in the red spectral range to an excited state, which in turn activates oxygen to yield oxidising radicals. Such radicals have the phototoxic effect on pathological cells, bacteria and viruses \[17-23\].

[http://www.optics.edu.ru/\_media/optics/staff/bashkatov/genina\_spie\_04\_5486\_315.pdf](http://www.optics.edu.ru/_media/optics/staff/bashkatov/genina_spie_04_5486_315.pdf)

Did you know you're a prophet [https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/12/201204155410.htm](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/12/201204155410.htm)
_
In the new test, the Cas13 protein is combined with a reporter molecule that becomes fluorescent when cut, and then mixed with a patient sample from a nasal swab. The sample is placed in a device that attaches to a smartphone. If the sample contains RNA from SARS-CoV-2, Cas13 will be activated and will cut the reporter molecule, causing the emission of a fluorescent signal. Then, the smartphone camera, essentially converted into a microscope, can detect the fluorescence and report that a swab tested positive for the virus._

# Thread: Does the world of science properly protects us from Washington’s fate?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-the-world-of-science-properly-protects-us-from-washington%E2%80%99s-fate.20041/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-23T00:56:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-the-world-of-science-properly-protects-us-from-washington%E2%80%99s-fate.20041/post-222054>

Why do we rely on the RCT in medicine?

Anyone?

[https://medium.com/the-mission/the-placebo-effect-is-a-lie-61a26df35aae](https://medium.com/the-mission/the-placebo-effect-is-a-lie-61a26df35aae)

### Allin — 2017-08-23T04:20:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-the-world-of-science-properly-protects-us-from-washington%E2%80%99s-fate.20041/post-222068>

The focus is not on optimal heath. Focus is on money.... grant money, jobs working those trials, drugs sold from those trials, lawyers paid from suing for bad drugs, people staffing CDC, law offices, courts, insurance.....Ah "healthcare", now we are talking some real money....I could rant on....

### Rubicon — 2017-08-30T07:48:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-the-world-of-science-properly-protects-us-from-washington%E2%80%99s-fate.20041/post-222555>

In the market, competition between entrepreneurs regulates abuse, drives progress through innovation, and destroys bad ideas. Entrepreneurs in open competition with each other are always either acting as predator or falling victim as prey, and in doing so they maintain a balance much like in the coupled systems we observe in nature. Restricting competition in the area of health care has resulted in some particularly bizarre outcomes. In the case of the US, this sentence in the Constitution has perhaps been the cause of the whole bad series of events:

Article 1, Section8, Clause 8 of the US Constitution that granted Congress the power "To promote the progress of science and useful arts, by securing for limited times to authors and inventors the exclusive right to their respective writings and discoveries."

That clause led to the patent system
the patent system was eventually applied to chemicals/drugs, creating legal monopolies with the incentive to see medicine focussed on prescribing patented drugs instead of other treatments
having monopolies on the sale of chemicals has led to large corporations which have very narrow special interests
entities with special interests will naturally use their money/power to increase their position and are not necessarily motivated by overall social good
The money/power concentrated in drug companies has been used to influence laws, regulations, and academia
the regulatory system and academia has proven to be easily corruptible

And now we've ended up with a system filled with many examples of rules/regulation/dogma that are run counter to the social good. RCT's are another good example.

Crazy bad butterfly effect...

Cites:
[How democracies pass bad laws in 2 min](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mgJ644LPL6g)
[The Rockefellers, the FDA, and the Cancer Industry](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1593001754097506)

# Thread: Avoid bad meme's......to get to Optimal.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoid-bad-memes-to-get-to-optimal.20042/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-23T01:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoid-bad-memes-to-get-to-optimal.20042/post-222058>

The take away from the 2017 eclipse for me : Bust out an old meme. Create a meme of fear, get the low dopamine humans fears up they will go blind and use social media platforms to sell that idea and then sell the glasses when the lemmings come to you. How many stories have you heard about people are going blind today? I watched the entire eclipse and had no ee protection. My vision for the truth is even sharper today than it was yesterday. We have a duty to call out bullshit. People do not have to accept our version of the truth but we have a duty to call out bullshit for those who cannot and will not think for themselves. Selling the bullets between two opposing ideas that a selling paradigm made yielded handsome profits. That is the reailty check I got out of the eclipse. The fear mongers are always the profiteers. Lemmings always have a paucity of thought. The War for the truth is not just the shower of bullets and bombs we are subject to on both sides of a meme, it is also the shower of blood and bones on both sides that result. Profiteers always will seek to separate your money from your wallet even when you think you are fully aware of the situation..... You cannot intimidate people with real bullets in any game, but you can intimidate them with fake gun. The "fake memes' around looking at the sun yesterday made a lot of lemmings less wealthy. Survival of the fittest happens in many ways we fail to realize.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-23T04:12:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoid-bad-memes-to-get-to-optimal.20042/post-222066>

The power of the sun is immense and we always under estimate it as humans. This is why I do not consider us the smartest primate. I think wild primates are far smarter about their solar diets than we are. Consider that the creation of a thousand forests resides in one acorn; and that acorn came from the slowing of light from our star. Inside that acorn is the potential to create millions of years of oxygen for animals to live off of and for that forrest to create food for those animals to eat, survive, and thrive. That is the power of the sun we take for granted in this modern world..........it makes you realize that intelligence is a toxin at the wrong dose too.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-28T17:38:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoid-bad-memes-to-get-to-optimal.20042/post-222420>

LADIES: No sunlight = no sulfated proteins and lipids. Consider the effect alone on cholesterol and Vitamin D3 = no estradiol = lowered testosterone = no progesterone = dehydration due to poor mitochondrial flux = no sex drive = no sex = no oxytocin. You want to regain it? Get some sun and lower your heteroplasmy rate. So how does sunlight help blood vessels? Well many ways as I have discussed here before but one way you might not know is via the sex steroid hormone pathways of progesterone. In males, luteinizing hormone (LH) causes the production of testosterone, whereas in females it affects estrogen and progesterone levels........ In females, luteinizing hormone works on the ovaries--where it makes its effects by changing the levels of estradiol. The levels made are tied to the amount or lack of sun one gets. The more blue light one gets the more estrogen on makes and the less progesterone one makes. Sunlight increases both sulfated Vitamin D3 and progesterone in case you did not know this. People should be more aware that progesterone has many important functions: it looks after the smoothness of blood vessels; The link below shows you how these pathways affect are arterial health. Progesterone also affects the myelin sheaths of nerves in men, women and children and it links why MS is tied to a lack of solar exposure; and it prevents water retention in the brain of men, women and children and this affects the amount of CSF that is made and recycled in the brain.

Progesterone receptors are found, among other sites, in the endothelial lining of blood vessels. It’s those receptors that keep endothelial lining nice and smooth. With excess estrogen, the exact opposite happens: the endothelium becomes lumpy and bumpy, therefore excessive estrogen (unbalanced by progesterone) makes us prone to clotting diseases, strokes and angina. Also, the ability of the blood vessels to relax is related to the presence of progesterone receptors and sufficient progesterone level to stimulate them. Insufficient progesterone may affect one way or another the many systems of the body, including blood vessels. Men who do not have sufficient amount of progesterone tend to get cardiovascular diseases in their 40s, whereas women are protected until after their 50s. In other words, progesterone is necessary to maintain cardiovascular health.
Progesterone facilitates healing process after an injury. If you look at my pre operative considerations blog see what I say there about progesterone. Trauma, including that resulting from cerebral accidents, heals quicker in pre-menopausal women than in men because they have more progesterone. Also, they have far less water retention in the brain. For that reason some neurosurgeons (ME) use progesterone injections prior to surgery to prevent swelling due to water retention.
It is made in the brain and stimulates BDNF with appropriate levels of cortisol to get the proper physiologic effects. In women, it is made the corpus luteum of the ovary and in smaller amounts by the adrenal glands. In men, is produced in adrenal and testicular tissue as well. Progesterone is produced in the body from cholesterol and is a precursor to most of the other steroid hormones, including cortisol and testosterone. Look at Hormone 101 blog post or BG 12 as well to explain why prolonged stress further depletes progesterone because it increases the demand for cortisol. I think doctors should consider progesterone supplementation for men in their late forties and older, when they experience low energy or fatigue, decreased libido, have increased body fat, enlarged prostate or to want to help prevent prostate enlargement.Progesterone is needed to counter-balance the effects of excess estrogens. Also, by blocking the enzyme 5a-reductase, progesterone inhibits the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which is also a stimulant of prostate cell growth. This is why sunlight stimulates LH and testosterone as we talked about on this page yesterday.Benefits for men:-

Protects against prostate cancer

\- Helps normalize blood sugar levels

\- Helps use fat for energy

\- Prevents water accumulation (acts as mild diuretic)

\- Helps (normalizes) thyroid hormone function

\- Stimulates new bone formation (osteoporosis protection and even reversal)

\- Improves brain function, has antidepressant properties

\- Improves skin problems including acne, seborrhea, rosacea, psoriasis

\- Diminishes muscular aches and pains, has anti-inflammatory properties

\- Improves sleep pattern

\- Improves libido.

In women and men is also makes hair growth big time. When I hear a lady is losing her hair I know she is deficient in sunlight and thyroid hormone and progesterone due to her blue light toxic world. We see this after craniotomy when we use it after we open a head on the incision line.
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26469335](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26469335)

Hello again Dr. Kruse,

I have read all about progesterone before I found you and your site and as proof that taking progesterone verses making it with sun alone works. When I was using 500 mgs of progesterone cream mostly putting it on vaginally a day made me retain water, joint pain, poor sleep, moody if it went down below 500mgs even then I had a constant fluid retention (not sure if it was just recycling estrogen or what). Then I found you and your site that totally made sense I dropped my cream within 2 or 3 days while only doing morning sun and grounding (well I tried to get sun and grounding all day as well too) and felt better within a few days. I have been reading more and I am going to focus on my timing of night food, light. Not focus so much on food which I really haven't. I actually haven't changed much in my eating habits since I started, I tried but all I did was reduce my calories. I do find the more I am in the sun the less I am hungry and thirsty mainly while at the beach. I am still working on a new job without constant computer use. I will come here to update. FYI, most of my weight is in my gut (I look prego). Also every time I try to go keto I get very thirsty and slight cranial pressure not like I did 3 years ago when I felt like I was going to die, yeah my body felt like then I was being electrocuted on low volts. ### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-29T15:25:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avoid-bad-memes-to-get-to-optimal.20042/post-222507>

anything that PULSES (think PEMF) releases massive amounts of RF. What does this imply?

More bad news for living things: Internet of things sensors could connect via ambient radio waves. [https://bioengineer.org/internet-of-things-sensors-could-connect-via-ambient-radio-waves-77/](https://bioengineer.org/internet-of-things-sensors-could-connect-via-ambient-radio-waves-77/)

An expert cancer researcher and advisor to the World Health Organization International Agency for Research on Cancer (WHO/IARC) has issued his scientific opinion that radiofrequency (RF) radiation from any source – such as the signals emitted by cell phones, other wireless and cordless and sensor devices, and wireless networks – fully meets criteria to be classified as a "Group 1 carcinogenic to humans" agent, based on scientific evidence associating RF exposure to cancer development and cancer promotion.

"The evidence indicating wireless is carcinogenic has increased and can no longer be ignored," stated Dr. Anthony B. Miller at a July 31, 2017 lecture in Jackson Hole, Wyoming sponsored by the Environmental Health Trust where international experts presented the best available science on cell phone and wireless radiation. In 2011, WHO/IARC classified RF radiation from any source as a "Group 2B possibly carcinogenic to human" agent. Miller believes the evidence published since 2011 fulfills the requirements to re-classify RF radiation as a "Group 1 carcinogenic to humans" agent.

# Thread: Frederica's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Frederica — 2017-08-23T02:12:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-222060>

Where to start.
Female: 58 yo, 6', 220 lbs
Have struggled with sleep for 6 years. Restless leg, tinnitus, wired and tired, now just tired all the time. When this started I weighed 174 lbs. I did not sleep for more that 4-5 hrs per night. Sometimes I would not be able to get to sleep until 1-2 because of RL, other times I would fall asleep only to awaken at 2 -3 and not be able to resume sleep. I thought I would go crazy. You can have some pretty dark thoughts when its dark outside and you're so tired but you can't fall asleep. Gained weight and started having all kinds of pains and muscle trouble. Did hair analysis. Went to many naturopathic Drs. Hair analysis said I had high copper toxicity. My hormone labs basically said I had no hormones ( well, everything was might low). BIHR didn't seem to help anything. Many of the supplements they gave me just made me more wired. I had a 23 and me done and some of my snps show I have a problem with things building up in my system (CBS, MAO, COMT, MTRR). What do I do with all this information?
I lurked around the site 2 years ago and was starting the leptin reset when I was diagnosed with triple negative breast cancer (1/16). Kinda got sidetracked. Did a boatload of research on TNBC and decided to have a lumpectomy, no chemo, no radiation. Started Reams Biological Theory of Ionization (you test your urine and saliva ph, urine conductivity and ureas and supplement accordingly) - 18 months and no reoccurring cancer. Slipped on my stairs last summer and broke my ankle and tore off a rotator cuff muscle. Another surgery. Gained more weight ( I never had a weight problem till after my 4th child at 44 yo), sleep tanked further. Pain in just about every joint. Frozen shoulder after surgery. Funny skin tags in skin folds. Hearing, vision all going down hill. I'm a 58 year old geriatric patient!!
I got desperate and went to a DO and he gave me .025 mg. lorazepam to take nightly. It helps but I don't want to be on it for long. However, I have some brain and energy back and am ready to do any and everything to get back my health and beat this toxic world we have created for ourselves.

Things I've done:
Gotten rid of most of my wifi toys (from 20 gadgets to 3), turn off all wifi in house and power to bedroom
nightly.
Get sun in my eyes first thing in the morning,
Use D minder app to get minimum 4000 iu Vit D daily ( I live in Eastern Washington and it's hard to get a lot of D).
Ground outside while getting Vit D (30 - 45 mins).
I drink lots of distilled water with fresh lemon juice.
I'm planning on starting the leptin reset next week after finishing Epi Paleo Rx book and getting my 14 yo settled in school.
I had some labs done this week and should get the results back soon.

So, this is my start to discovering what my body is trying to tell me about my environment. I've been interested in the terrain of my cells since reading about germ theory vs soil theory while homeschooling my kids. One of those seeds that gets planted, just waiting for water and light to make it grow!!

### Frederica — 2017-08-23T22:04:23+0300

Fitness@home and Caroline - how exciting to have someone respond! Thank you.
Um, how to I give Dr. K this information about my mom and mom? Just post it here?

I do wear blue blocker glasses when I watch TV (I don't watch often but sometimes in the evening I sit with family and watch a show - I do strive to be off before 8pm). When I'm on my computer - I use the IRIS app set at the reading or health mode. I have led strip lights under my cabinets in the kitchen that
I turn to red in the evening. A 25 watt incandescent by my bed -not for reading but to see whats around me!!! Same in my bathroom. I'm working on the rest of the house. Most of them are LEDs but I don't turn them on much right now as where we live the sun doesn't set until 8. Winter will be a different story. I know that I need to change these lights soon. I have been sitting outside between 8 and 9 and listening to podcasts and other audios or just chat with anyone who will join me to avoid light in my eyes. Then at 9-9:30 I get ready for bed. It doesn't always happen but it is lovely when I can.

I haven't read John Otts book yet - It's on my list though. Would you say to put it at the top? I'm focusing on blog, forum, Epi Paleo now.

I have listened to a few podcasts - I think I have listened to the Vermont one - is there more than one of those though?

I live in a smallish town, 70,000 folks, but am near 2 other towns which ups the total population to about 200,000. We live on a half acre lot. Surrounded by other half acre to one acre lots so not too dense. But my phone registers many (4-6) wifi signals to choose from though they are mostly weak ones.

I am a stay at home mom.

I don't think it is a smart meter although it is digital, the guy still has to have access to the meter to read it.

I am going to order the meters to help me figure out what I am exposed to. On the list. So much stuff on the list.

In case this is where I post the information for Dr. K:

My mom had colon cancer at age 36 that metastasized into lung cancer - she died at 41. She didn't smoke, though my dad did. I believe she had a lot of stress in her life. My dad was a pilot in the Air Force working his way up the command path. We moved quite a bit, and there were other issues.
Her mother lived until she was in her 90's, she had dementia and strokes.

Thank you all for making this road a bit straighter for those of us following later.

### Frederica — 2017-08-27T02:56:59+0300

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/). exactly!! I download the video and then put my iPad on the ground full blast so I can hear but not see. It is a bit frustrating when he says to look at this slide to understand why such and such is true. It makes for good conversation with my neighbors though. I've already had to explain to a few why I'm out searching for early morning sunlight in my jammies - sometimes the edge of my front lawn by the street is the only place it exists. Now they see me sitting in my grass in the back yard grounding and listening to audios full blast. Got to get those fences up soon.

### Frederica — 2017-08-27T03:21:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-222323>

Caroline: thank you. I have ordered John Ott's book. Should get here soon.
I have dunked my face in ice water for 2 nights now. Wow, I can barely hold my breath for 10 seconds the first time - it really takes your breath away. But I keep doing it and coming up for air until my face is really cold and pink. I am not sure what is making a difference but last night I slept without taking any lorazepam ! I am excited. I decided to not take it because I started to fall asleep sitting on my porch after dinner and that never happens. I'm not even totally on the diet part - only the Big Breakfast and reducing carbs But I know Dr. K has started to say its all about the light. . .
I'll do a search for the easy start guide, I don't think I've run across it yet.
I thought about joining as Gold but I really haven't read much on the site and figured I'd spend some time assimilating because all the questions I have now have probably been answered somewhere. I haven't even gotten through the top 5 starting blogs. Leptin Rx and CT Protocol so far. Vermont Talks 2016 and halfway through 2017. Some of his other podcasts out on the web, and read most of the Epi Paleo book (skimmed some chapters). Wish I could do some CT on Father Time and slow it down, or would that just shrink it like fat, or would it expand the spaces between moments. Ha ha, I'm lost.

### caroline — 2017-08-27T05:46:11+0300

### Frederica — 2017-09-07T03:45:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-223039>

I keep telling myself that it took awhile to get here, it will take awhile to get somewhere else.
I've started the BAB. I have no problem eating that much protein, just getting it together in time is a challenge.
I do try to save some from dinner the night before. Most of the time I am not hungry till 2 pm. I can tell which proteins keep me feeling full longer.
Eggs, fish, steak - yes. Chicken not so much. And the more fat the better. I gotta find a good hollandaise recipe.
We have had the smoke from the Canadian fires all week. I still go outside and look toward the sun in the am but its pretty overcast.
I'm not getting much D in the afternoon because of it. I have even started going topless in my backyard. The sunsets are beautiful though - very orange.
I ordered some red light night lights to put around the house and use in the evening. I tried to read a book by candlelight
the other evening but all the flickering drove me nuts. Too much draft from the AC I guess.
My sleep has been off and on. Some nights I feel tired and get to sleep on my own, though not great sleep. I seem to have dreams where I think
someone is in the room standing over me and I holler like crazy. My poor husband. . . He doesn't know what to think. He is very supportive and
tries some stuff but he doesn't suffer from any chronic ailments so sees no need. He did watch me CT my face the other night and said that he
didn't know waterboarding was part of the protocol ;

I finally finished the Vermont talks. Dr. K. mentioned if you put your haplotype and some snps from 23andme he would let us know where our best environment
was.
Haplotype: I3
C677T: no mutation
A1298C: no mutation
VDR : both are mutated on both alleles
CBS: There are four on my report, 2 have no mutations and 2 have mutations on only 1 allele

times up for my computer today.

### Frederica — 2017-09-14T03:35:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-223588>

So, I've been busy reading. And not understanding much but seeing the importance of sunlight and that my life really needs to change. It's a bit discouraging that we are approaching the no UVB season here in Washington. I've been doing pretty well on the Leptin Reset diet, I had a glitch last weekend when I ended up in the Urgent Care with a kidney infection and put on antibiotics. I have added yogurt to my morning meal - no sugar added. And lots of sauerkraut to help my gut. I did have some constipation but am not sure if it was caused by the antibiotics or if I have a leaky gut issue that I need to resolve first. So, I've printed out the Leaky Gut blogs and will be reading them too. I get out every morning for 20 minutes as soon as the sun comes up. Lately I have been waking at 6:30 - the sun doesn't come up to 7:00 - so I make my BAB and sit outside and eat it. Then come in to get my daughter off for her day and come back outside and read in the sun for about an hour. UVB doesn't show up until 9ish here so I might get about 15 minutes in the morning. I have started wearing my glasses and not contacts until evening. I have amber glasses that don't fit over glasses so I have to change to my contacts in the evening. I go outside sometime around 1200 -1:00 for 20-30 minutes to get more UVB. I am trying not to use my iPad except in airplane mode. I have hardwired my laptop. We have everything to hardwire all the other stuff but haven't gotten there yet. My sleep is my main issue right now. I have some nights that are better than others but I still take a lorazepam nightly. Even then most nights are very restless. I am strongly considering a magnetico pad. However, our mattress is an innerspring and I have read somewhere you shouldn't mix the two. So a new mattress and magnetico is going to be a big bill! I am going to rent some meters to help me see what I have in my house and car and take them with me when we go buy a new car. So glad we hadn't done that yet. I'm still just face dunking. I haven't read any on CT yet so am just doing what I know now. Wish our gym had one of those 50 degree pools.

### Frederica — 2017-10-19T21:03:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-226232>

I need to update more but its a funny thing when you know others are reading - I probably need a daily journal just for me where I can put down all my hacks and then update to this journal weekly. I know Gretchen and Misty mentioned doing a journal in the recent video that was sent to my email. Am trying to come up with all the things I would list: Intakes (supps, food, water), Environment (sun, nnemfs, stress, CT), symptoms (sleep, hunger, mood, emotions). My husband, ever the engineer, said I should create a spreadsheet. Then I thought there is probably an app out there. But I am going the paper route because I want simple and less damaging tech.
I have been diligent on my morning sun. Eating my breakfast out on my back porch with my feet in the grass and reading print material or listening to audio for at lease an hour, topless, totally freaked that a neighbor might just drop by. I have friendly neighbors. I need a sign that warns them - beware of . . . . uncovered breasts? Seems a bit off putting.
Anyway, I have lost 16 lbs since the end of August. Pretty happy with that. My favorite find so far is bone broth (chicken or beef) heated with shrimp and scallops or mussels. I can get a whopping 40g of protein without making myself miserable. I then add some leftover dinner to round things up. My treat at lunch is a Tblsp of coconut butter with some macadamia nuts ground up and sprinkled on top. Makes my day and keeps me loose! I find I am less hungry for lunch and sometimes just have a protein shake. No fruit, maybe a handful of spinach. I like the vanilla Paleo Protein.
I do my lunch outside and spend another 30 mins - hour listening to my holosync. I am a big fail with meditation but I can listen to holosync and it settles my mind. I'm not sure that it does all that it says but it is a good relaxation and I had it.
We are losing UVB. I am not going to be moving soon so I need to figure out what will help me get through this winter. Eastern WA state at a latitude of 46 is NOT optimal, I know. I am not a tree, I know. But I am not going to leave my family and live alone in the tropics. This will be a slow and steady process. Well, I am trying for a trip South in January for a few weeks but with a 14 yo in school . . .
My sleep is such a hit and miss. For a few nights it is not too bad and then bam I have a night where I feel electrified all night, my legs won't stop moving and I feel buzzy and my feet burn. All power is off to our bedroom, Wifi off, no cordless phones. The Meters showed my bedroom pretty clean when we turn off power. This is where the daily journal needs to come in so I can see all the variables.
I am waiting to go to a doctor in town that I feel is very good at reading labs but she is so booked - until December. So I went to my standard Dr. and got a few labs. She wants to put me on T4. I need to do some research.
Labs:
D, 25 hydroxy: 44ng/ml. (highest its been since I started testing it in 2011)
ESR: 8mm/Hr (I have no idea what this is)
CRP: <.3 mg/dl (not hs crp)
CO2: 27 mmol/L
glucose: 92 mg/dl
BUN: 14 mg/dl
Creat: .8 mg/dl
Bun/CRE: 18
Free T4: .9 ng/dl
Free T3: 3.2 pg/ml
TSH: 5.09ulU/mL

there are more numbers but I need to get off and get outside.

### Frederica — 2017-10-20T01:48:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-226245>

I wanted to be sure and post my successes after that bit of a rant above.

I had my yearly eye appt last week and he was surprised to see the cloudiness in my lenses all cleared up!! I told him that I've been looking at the sun in the morning and he just cocked his head and said - "not right at it I hope". The thing is I can look right at it at my latitude and as soon as it rises. Anyway, woot woot. I also ordered new glasses (with no uv protection) so I can look over them toward the sun without having to keep taking out my contacts!! I have not worn glasses for 35 years, this is a biggy for me. But if I've gotten any message from this site its how important sun in your eyes in the am is.

I've lost 16 lbs from eating epi-paleo. Not hard and no extra workouts. I've actually taken a break from the gym because I think I'm dealing with Adrenal Fatigue and probably was making it worse with all my pushing. Not to mention all the blue lights and wifi.

Most nights, when I put my blue blocking glasses on at 7:00 pm, I am fighting to stay awake at 8:30. It doesn't mean my sleep is good but at least I'm tired and that is a change.

About half the time I face dunk at night I sleep better but not every time. Sometimes I can become anxious while holding my breath. I'm so afraid of suffocation - something in my childhood and having 5 older brothers. It didn't help when my husband compared it to waterboarding either. something to work through. I

I've started reading through the blogs by date. It keeps me from going down too many trails and not finishing what I set out to learn which creates procrastination. I need to learn something to the point where I can take "re"educated action.

Sometimes my family will let me share what I've read and not roll their eyes. Sometimes they'll come out in the morning and sit with me. But, it's getting cooler now so not so much. I am excited about the cold. It is a call to adapt, to change with the earth.

I have a nice tan. Everywhere.

I'm not as stressed because I have a plan. I can take little steps and a mistake isn't going to set me back too much because I'm not doing anything poisonous to my body. I get very confused at what I can do with where I live and can start to feel like my only hope is to move to the equator, but I see other folks in the group living above 35 degrees lat. and they are working it. You just have to be more diligent in the other areas. I have to keep learning how to listen to this body God gave me. Wish I had started sooner.

There is a ton of information out there on most of what Dr. Kruse talks about and you can usually find a way to understand it at your own level. It's just that you have to be on the internet a lot. So much of this is already known but no one applies it to people like Dr. Kruse does.

My timer is going off - that's my other success. I time my computer use and then make myself go outside for a walk, or pull some weeds, or chat with a neighbor. It's been great.

### Frederica — 2017-11-01T21:10:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fredericas-journal.20043/post-227124>

So much to read - i love it. I have always loved to research but am a flop at application. However, I believe I have leptin resistance and adrenal burnout. Now, this has been after reading jacks blogs sequentially so I haven't even gotten to the newer stuff. I do get the light, grounding and water stuff ( well "get" is probably to strong a term) . I understand it is of utmost importance. But, based on my flatlined low cortisol I am pretty sure of adrenal burnout. I went to my doctor yesterday and took 2 printouts of Jacks - the leptin part one, and the adrenal rx. She actually read them and agreed with a lot of it!!! Woot. She even used the M word for my condition!! You have a mitochondial issue. I left her office feeling so encouraged. She is still not a believer in nnemf (she just bought a car with all the techy rf, bluetooth stuff and loves it) but baby steps.
So now, along with the leptin reset, I am starting the adrenal rx. We have to start slowly because my detox pathways are poor due to snps but she put me on Progesterone at night and ordered some labs.
We were talking about all kinds of stuff, like maternal mtDNA research and Doug Wallace. Her poor nurse kept coming in saying the next patient was ready, but she said she needed a breather and this was doing her good. She used to be a biochemist before going into Natureopathy. Hoping for a long and profitable relationship with this gal. And, she's on my insurance (at least for now). She really worked the codes to try and get most of the labs paid for. I feel so sorry for doctors nowdays and all the hoops they have to jump through. She mentioned something about having to bury some of this "woo woo" stuff deep in my charts in case of a chart audit. What the heck is a chart audit? All the things to watch out for . . .

### JohnnyAZ — 2017-08-23T03:17:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increased-water-increased-indigestion.20044/post-222064>

Ok so I have spent every day since I joined this forum studying some aspect of all this material and I put together a template to begin working on. I started the epi-paleo diet with a renewed commitment level and also have removed all EMF from my room (as best I could without yet hiring the engineer) and also utilize blue blocking glasses. One thing that is advocated is increased water intake and I began this but whenever I drink a decent to large amount of water I get massive indigestion and in general don't feel well and it seems to run right through me and I have to use the restroom at an exorbitant rate. I found I can combat this with betaine hcl but I am not sure if this is suggested or if there is something else at work here that I should focus on.

### Allin — 2017-08-23T04:02:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increased-water-increased-indigestion.20044/post-222065>

Then start adding in the clean water, eating a BAB (big ass breakfast, with 60% fat, 25% protein and 15% carbs and those percentages don't have to be exact but tailored to your situation). My wife would say, "How can you get heartburn? You only drank water....)

I have read that indigestion is often a sign of low stomach acid and not too much. Figured maybe the water was diluting it and causing this.

# Thread: Am I getting AM sunlight too late or not enough?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-getting-am-sunlight-too-late-or-not-enough.20045/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eliza — 2017-08-23T08:16:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-getting-am-sunlight-too-late-or-not-enough.20045/post-222074>

Hi everyone

My hormones have been trashed for years and I've been trying to heal them ever since. As of 5 months ago, I have moved to a rural area where I can work from home, allowing me to spend at least 1-2 hrs in AM sunlight per day and away from all the Wi-Fi in a high-rise office building. I know I need to get out more now we're coming out of winter in Australia.

I see the sunrise every morning, and get 1-2 hrs sunlight between about 10am - 12pm (in Australia).

I recently got some labs done on day 4 of my cycle, and really expected to see some improvements in my hormones, since I'm developing a nice tan. However, I was really wrong!!

Is it nnemf, or am I not getting enough AM sunlight, or is the AM sunlight too late for hormone-making?

Things that have changed for me lately:

\- I have had a faecal transplant about 2 yrs ago via a gastroenterologist in Melbourne. This has helped my gut health massively!
\- - We've gotten rid of wifi in the house, though we may get one weak signal from the neighbours occasionally.
\- The gym where I go to has an ice bath set at 14c, allowing me to CT most days for about 25 mins (sadly in nasty water, but I felt the benefits outweighed the negatives).
\- I have a core magnetico coming on Monday!
\- I'm doing the leptin reset again for about the last 3-4 months (salmon sashimi BAB - admittedly farmed), with butter mostly as the winter fat.

hs-crp - 1.07 (ref: <4.91)
Homocysteine: 7.7 umol/L (4-14)
**BUN: Creatinine ratio (25:1) - terrible!!**
Sodium, potassium, chloride, bicarb, anion gap, urea, creatinine, eGFR, urate, bilirubin, AST, ALT, GGT, Alkaline phosphatase, protein, albumin, globulin, calcium, corrected calcium and phosphate all within reference ranges.
Serum iron: 15umol/L (10-30)
**Transferrin: 31 umol/L (32-48)**
Transferrin saturation - 25% (13-45)
**Serum ferritin - 163 ug/L (30-165)**
TSH: 2.7mIU/L (0.5-4.0)
FT4: 16 pmol/L (10-20)
FT3: 4.2 pmol/L (3.5-6) - unfortunately I didn't get RT3 tested - but I supplement 10mg T3 daily
Day 4 hormone tests:
FSH: 8 IU/L (2-12)
E2: 199 (100-530)
Progesterone: 1 (0.5-4.5) - despite 4% cream used on days 14-28 of my cycle
Total T: 0.7 nmol/L (0.5-2.2)
**SHBG: 94nmol/L (20-118)
DHEA-S: 1.9 umol/L (2.2-7.9) - yuck, despite daily supplementation of 20mg DHEA per day (didn't take it on morning tested).**
Calculated Free T: 5.8pmol/L (2.7-35)
25-OH Vit D: 75 nmol/L (51-200) - bit more pleased about this, as we're in winter here, and I've brought it up from in winter with just sunlight
**Total Cholesterol: 13 mmol/L (3.6-5.2)**
Triglycerides: 0.9 mmol/L (0.5-1.7)
**HDL: 2.1 mmol/L (1-2)
LDL: 10.2 mmol/L (1.5-3.4)
Non-HDL cholesterol: 10.6 mmol/L )
Cholesterol/HDL-C ratio: 6 (<4.5)**
Hb, platelets and WBC all within reference ranges
HBA1C - NGSP 4.9% (4.0-6.0)
HBA1C - IFCC - 30 mmol/mol (20-42)
Vitamin B12:421 pmol/L (301-740)
Serum folate: 46.4 nmol/L (>9)
The thing is, prior to starting the leptin reset again, I had been eating mostly epi-paleo but moderate carbs, and my labs (especially hormones) still weren't impressive at all, but weren't as bad as what's above.
For example, in August 2016, I had the following results: (Day 21 of cycle)
FSH : 8 IU/L (2-23)
Progesterone: 0.9 nmol/L (3.7-45)
E2: 317 pmol/L (80-1150)
Testosterone: 1.5 nmol/L (<4.6)
SHBG: 130 nmol/L (20-155)
calc. free T3: 10 pmol/L (3-37)
FAI: 1.2 (0.5-6)
DHEA-S: 9.1 umol/L (1.7-11.5)
Cholesterol: 6.1 mmol/L (3.5-5.5)
HDL: 1.7 mmol/L (>0.8)
LDL: 4 mmol/L ( <2)
Trigs: 0.83 mmol/L (0.5-2)
Tot/HDL 3.6 mmol/L (<5.1)
HS-CRP: 0.6 mg/L (0-5)
Free T4: 11 pmol/L (8.2-22)
TSH: 0.67 mU/L (0.5-4.4)
Free T3: 4.9 pmol/L (2-7)

My LDL cholesterol now seems to have gone crazy (which I get isn't necessarily a bad thing), but in this context, to me, it looks like my thyroid has stopped working (or is creating a lot of RT3). Is it just because it's winter? I have been CT'ing most days for around 20-30 mins. I have been trying to get as much AM sun as possible to help heal my hormones, but my DHEA has suddenly dropped in the last few months. Maybe due to inflammation? My ferritin levels and HS-CRP also point to inflammation.

But the cholesterol and hormone results always seem to be worse when I'm eating very low carb (a la leptin reset).

I have lost a little weight in the past few months doing the leptin reset (I weigh about 60kg, but still have a high body fat %), so perhaps some of that would increase the cholesterol?
Am I doing the wrong thing with CT and eating with the leptin reset? Or should I just be supplementing more DHEA, T3 and progesterone until the levels are better and then my leptin resistance will improve? I just don't seem to need quite the amount of supplementation when not eating as low carb.

I feel like in the first few weeks of the leptin reset + CT, I felt hot after most meals and was starting to feel really good. In the last few weeks, not so much, and my sleep has worsened a bit. I put it down to the time of the month, but given these last results I'm not surprised I'm not feeling great!

Any thoughts?? Thanks in advance!!

### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-24T03:12:40+0300

25:1 is a sure sign the redox is BAD. dehydration = mito make no water = no sun light can be added to your water to make a capacitor.

Getting up early with the sun gives you time to reflect on your goals because your eyes can use UV-A and IR-A to make dopamine and melatonin. If you build in time to embrace your existence, your reason for being (ikigai) and your purpose, your brain will become addicted to your new morning habit. Think about what you want to achieve in life, what your purpose is and how you are going to reach those goals. This active awareness helps you become more proactive and less reactive for your future.

### howirecovered — 2017-09-07T23:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-getting-am-sunlight-too-late-or-not-enough.20045/post-223113>

I have high ferretin like you and have been on a nutritional balancing program for about three years, mostly working on copper toxicity. At one point I consulted Morley Robbins who highlights the fact that high iron is very disruptive and highly correlated with disease. I wrote about it here: [http://howirecovered.com/its-the-iron-stupid](http://howirecovered.com/its-the-iron-stupid)

At first I thought that my iron was probably related to poor liver function and genetic flaws related to iron and copper transport. But I also have Lyme disease and co-infections and subsequently read that high ferretin can also be the bodies effort to sequester iron away from pathogens that feed on it. In other words, it can also be a sign of infection. Just something to think about, because you could search a long long time before you encountered an MD really good at detecting infections.

I never once came across an MD with a microscope and yet it turns out Lyme is easily visible under my $600 microscope.

Hi Emma

Sorry for my late reply, I haven't been on the forum for a little while. I purchased 1 straight from the supplier, and the one on the other side of the bed (they come in halves) is an old one I bought second hand in Australia. The killer is the postage unfortunately! The second hand one I bought privately needs replacing through as all the magnets are becoming unglued and starting to move in different directions. I feel like that's a bad thing.

It's really hard to say with the magnetico. I didn't experience anything strange when sleeping on it straight away like some others have. I don't think it's affected me negatively, but there are so many variables! I'm really glad to have bought it, as it's one piece to the puzzle, and it's not like EMF across the world is ever going to decrease.

My health is starting to improve, but my hormones are still far from optimal. I think the magnetico is helping, but again, that's very hard to quantify.

If you have the money, then I say go for it, it's an investment that should last a lifetime. But I think there are other more free, important things to be doing, like sunlight, reducing EMF exposure etc.

Let me know how you go if you get one (or you might already have got one given how late my post is!)

> At first I thought that my iron was probably related to poor liver function and genetic flaws related to iron and copper transport. But I also have Lyme disease and co-infections and subsequently read that high ferretin can also be the bodies effort to sequester iron away from pathogens that feed on it. In other words, it can also be a sign of infection. Just something to think about, because you could search a long long time before you encountered an MD really good at detecting infections.
>
> I never once came across an MD with a microscope and yet it turns out Lyme is easily visible under my $600 microscope.

### Eliza — 2019-02-21T00:21:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-getting-am-sunlight-too-late-or-not-enough.20045/post-263675>

Hi Christina, thanks for your thoughts. I haven't been tested specifically for haemochromatosis. When I had tests in the last few months my iron was at 20 umol/L (5-30) and my Ferritin was 131 ng/mL (130-200). I'd like my ferritin lower but is it high enough to be considered haemochromatosis within reference ranges- I think Jack would say my environment sucks more than anything. Also, I'm a female, but not losing an unusual amount of iron that way either

### Christina Gagnon — 2019-02-21T00:32:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-getting-am-sunlight-too-late-or-not-enough.20045/post-263676>

Sorry Eliza, Of course you're a girl lol. Doing a quick search "**Ferritin levels** greater than 300 ng/mL in men and 200 ng/mL in **women** support a diagnosis of **hemochromatosis**. " I have this condition and it is something you really want to junp on and treat because it can be so destructive to your internal organs,I also wouldn't eat any raw shellfish (especially oysters which are recommended in this forum and I love and wish I could eat them) until you confirm that you don't have it because raw oysters and other shellfish may be contaminated by a bacteria named Vibrio vulnificus that thrives on iron and will spread like wildfire through your system and can lead to amputation and death. When I was first diagnosed I learned a lot from a woman who wrote a book called The Iron Elephant and also sarted a web page [http://www.americanhs.org/ah00084.htm](http://www.americanhs.org/ah00084.htm) that can give you more facts.

> [Eliza said:](/goto/post?id=263675)
>
> Hi Christina, thanks for your thoughts. I haven't been tested specifically for haemochromatosis. When I had tests in the last few months my iron was at 20 umol/L (5-30) and my Ferritin was 131 ng/mL (130-200). I'd like my ferritin lower but is it high enough to be considered haemochromatosis within reference ranges- I think Jack would say my environment sucks more than anything. Also, I'm a female, but not losing an unusual amount of iron that way either

### Eliza — 2019-02-21T00:58:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-getting-am-sunlight-too-late-or-not-enough.20045/post-263678>

Thanks Christine, that's really interesting! I'll do some more reading about it

### Billybats — 2017-08-23T20:16:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-a-question.20046/post-222102>

To all the Geeks out there or whoever can let me know if I got this correct.

To most of you this may be a "well duh" reaction. I have been thinking of Vitamin A. I notice my skin gets better in the sun,,,lol well duh, but I decided to read more about it on here and this is what I think I missed, please correct me if I m wrong. I kept thinking I have to get my sun for vit D, well yes, and thinking how does vitamin A work in this process. This is what my brain got from Dr. Kruse explanations: that morning sun is what lets say" triggers" vit a which triggers vit d, and that vit d is processed through the skin not the eye so to speak, but when vit a is triggered in the eye it starts the engine so to speak. Now throw in the fake light "blue" it screws up the processes and lets say deactivates vit d, thats how we get high vit a that isn't being used to cycle in the system. I invision one of those turn style doors that you have to time yourself to jump in to enter a building without getting stuck..lol I also think of the inside of a clock . Now I have to figure out T3's mechanisms of helping vit a to make the vit d...more reading

P.S. I didn't know where to post this question.

# Thread: SNPs vs Epigenetics
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snps-vs-epigenetics.20047/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2017-08-23T21:07:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snps-vs-epigenetics.20047/post-222104>

I'm confused. When do we need to be concerned about SNPs?

We can test for SNPs, how do we test for epigenetics? Are we looking for the absence of something?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-24T02:53:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snps-vs-epigenetics.20047/post-222123>

SNP's modify how you handle light and how your genome is expressed.

### Huck — 2017-08-24T03:06:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snps-vs-epigenetics.20047/post-222127>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=222123)
>
> SNP's modify how you handle light and how your genome is expressed.

So does that mean some SNPs can be overridden by epigenetics and some can't?

For example, I have several SNPs that indicate a likely problem digesting and metabolizing fat. Is there a way to override them and eat a high fat diet, or do I need to stay on a low fat diet?

Something strange that happened is that when I went to Louisiana last winter for 3 weeks, I lost 7 lbs without trying to. I was even eating a pint of ice cream most days. Was that a combination of time of year and light?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-24T03:23:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snps-vs-epigenetics.20047/post-222133>

SNP'smodify how your genome can be expressed in a certain environment. SNP's are a light signal to RNA and DNA. SNP's play a huge role in miRNA. Last blog got into that.

Can you have a normal plasma sulfated D3 level and still have low vitamin D activity in vivo? Yes you can, and it is probably the biggest silent epidemic out there today I see in my Gulf South patients. In my new job I was just asked this question from a wise rheumatologist. I most commonly see this in obese African American folks with hypothyroidism. 90% of the cases of hypothyroidism in the USA are cause by Hashimoto’s disease now and this is a very new phenomena that began when technolgy showed up in our lives. This disease is an autoimmune disease and these patients universally have defective VDR receptors along with P450 enzymes. The genes that control the P450 enzymes are only properly made when the redox potential of the cytosol is optimized. That redox potential is linked to how much water your mitochondria can make in the environment youre forcing your body to withstand. This chain of facts implies some people need very high levels of blood Vitamin D levels and optimization of their thyroid function to get results. To get very high levels of D3 we need proper solar management of our skin. Quantum clinicians need to better advocate for this action in their treatment plans. On my website I made this prediction in 2013 because of what I was seeing my patients in TN and in MS. I thought in 2012 that it had to do with Vitamin D binding proteins in skin and the P450 enzymes in the kidneys......they have the massive effects on each other via positive and negative feedback control that make a coupled thermodynamic system. But I said we would have to wait til 2017 for the results of a study on supplementation. The Vitamin D Conundrum: To Supplement or Not to Supplement? Don't Expect Reliable Results Before 2017 on this topic. Why??? In a summary of the current state of vitamin D research by Kai Kupferschmidt, vitamin D supplementation enthusiasts and skeptics will have their day in the sun soon. This linked paper is the first in many to come the next few years on this topic. Moreover Kupferschmidt includes an overview of recently started large scale trials that could eventually tell us whether supplementation is advisable, necessary, superfluous or hazardous and idiotic. This paper below I have some issues with.....using mice to study a solar hormone is not wise. And when you consider that a mouse has a scotopic retina and skin many poor reviews of the findings are likely by researchers because mice cannot and will not every approximate the human experience. This paper has some interesting isights but I think it has some ideas in it based upon the design that will be very misleading to many biologists. [https://medicalxpress.com/news/2017-08-newly-deciphered-vitamin-d-regulatory.html](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2017-08-newly-deciphered-vitamin-d-regulatory.html)

Where was the thread from 2012:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/vitamin-d-and-autoimmunity.3425/page-4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/vitamin-d-and-autoimmunity.3425/page-4)

### Valsun12 — 2017-08-23T22:15:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-maternal-haplogroup.20048/post-222111>

So I wanted to dedicate a thread to Maternal Haplogroups and information on what they say about our mitochondrial health. I just did he 23and me and see that I am the below, V7.

....How do I translate this to what latitude my mitochondria might thrive in o any other information with my health? Any thoughts....what was your haplogroup and how did you use this information to improve your health?

**Maternal Haplogroup is V7:

**Origin and Migrations of Haplogroup V**
Recent evidence suggests that the members of haplogroup V descend from a woman who lived in Europe approximately 10,000 years ago. When her ancestors arrived in Europe is more of a mystery. They may have migrated to the west from the Middle East before the last great peak of the Ice Age, which occurred around 20,000 years ago. This wave of cold covered the continental interior in icy tundra and pushed Europe's human population south into a few temperate enclaves in the south along the Mediterranean. Haplogroup V likely arose in one of these refuges in the Iberian Peninsula, or perhaps in southeastern Europe.

**

### Gracelind — 2017-08-23T23:40:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-maternal-haplogroup.20048/post-222117>

23 and Me says my MtDNA haplogroup is H. FTDNA (uploaded raw 23 and Me file) and a professor who analyzes DNA have called it H85. My son also got this result through direct swab testing from FTDNA.

H is about as broad European as you can get. H85 is virtually unknown--little info about it.

So other than being European it's not helpful for me.

OTOH MtDNA has helped identify the birth mother of an adopted friend--but that's a another story.

MTHFR C677T = +/-

COMT P199P = -/-

Maximize on sunlight wherever you are. (naked eyes, naked skin, specially naked private area)
Do saliva cortisol test to check if you are doing enough.

For Snp's do this:

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

\===
For thyroid
drink excessive amounts of Lugols (but not Nascent)
normal amounts of selenium
test FT3 and RT3
Keep FT3 in upper laboratory range or slightly over that
if low use NTH, Natural Thyroid Hormone (from pigs), up to 3grains/day would be ok, if need more do frequent testing. Aim at 37C temperature under tongue in mid day.

Above will not work if 4x/saliva cortisol test is low and/or does not indicate proper Circadian rhythm.

### Sergio Valadez — 2019-02-02T04:29:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-maternal-haplogroup.20048/post-262104>

My maternal haplogroup is H10

(Both of my parents are Mexican.)

Here’s what Dr. Jack Kruse said (over in my Optimal journal) about my maternal haplotype:

“Your haplogroup is quite interesting.

The woman who founded this haplotype lived between 6,300 and 10,900 years ago in Europe.

This branch was born in Europe during the last glacial maximum, it sheltered in refugia there. As the ice melted, groups containing this line moved across Europe, resettling.

This means your surface SNPs might be the key to unlocking what is really going on here. You need to upload all your raw data into MTHFR tool and post it.

H10 is NOT a common Mexican clade. Your Mom's lineage and story then become critical in this puzzle. We need more data on her and your grandmother.

Today, the highest frequencies of this line are present in Belgium, where it makes up 6 percent of maternal lineages; in the Netherlands, it makes up approximately 3 percent of maternal lineages. In addition, it is also part of some Jewish Diaspora groups. Overall, this lineage is present at trace frequencies of 1 to 2 percent in Northwestern Europe and a frequency of less than 1 percent in Southern Europe.”

### Martha Ray — 2019-07-03T05:04:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-your-maternal-haplogroup.20048/post-273630>

**MRB Maternal haplogroup H1a3, 677, 1298, VDR, CBS,
These are tied to the COMT gene and Dopamine and Melatonin**

I am new here.
Also new to genetics. In the past worked a little with Michael McEvoy for metabolic healing.

99.9% European

Maternal haplogroup H1a3

MTHFR C677T +/-

MTHFR A1298C +/-

VDR Bsm +/- (Vitamin D Receptor)

VDR Taq +/-

CBS C699T +/-

CBS A360A +/-

These are tied to Dopamine and Melatonin levels. Right?

I had a margin notation that these two CBS were related to _Arsenic Toxicity_ and another notiation for CBS Mutations and possible _sulfur Intolerance_.

COMT V158M, COMT H62H AND COMT P199P were all **\-/-**

CBS Heart Health

CBS C699T +/-

CBS A 360A +/-

CBS A13637G +/-

CBS C19160T +/-

**“CBS A13637G, CBS A360A, CBS C19150T, CBS C699T (+/-)**

CBS (Cystathione beta synthase) — Involved in the conversion of homocysteine into

cystathionine via transsulfuration. CBS is involved in Glutathione synthesis. Requires

P5P (active form of Vitamin B-6) for synthesis. Mutations seen in abnormal detoxification.

Glutathione is a critical antioxidant, capable of preventing oxidative, free radical damage.”

RE: ‘Mutations seen in abnormal detoxification’
Not sure, it is related but years ago I tested high beta-glucuronidase and was prescribed Calcium-D Glucarate to try and help detoxification.

**I did not mention...**
I live in central Virginia at **38.0293° N, 78.4767° W** and we are at 594' average elevation however I live on the top of a hill in the county so not right in Charlottesville proper. **Sure would like to know from Dr. Jack Kruse if I have genetics to get enough light from here?**
... still looking to go south ideally to get back to my roots in Texas as I don't like Radon levels, tick borne disease rates, Volcanic possibility (A magnitude-5.8 earthquake, centered nine miles south of Mineral, Va., struck the **East Coast** on Aug. 23 2011-----Aug 23th is my birthday and that was my first earthquake and it was only approx 37 miles from our home!) and I do now like the altitude here. I feel better at higher altitudes but I know very little about the health of elevation or gravity or magnetism or how to research areas. I looked at magnetism maps talked about here on the forum but they were _greek_ to me. Our daughters live in Asheville, NC... great place to visit but radon levels are very high there as well... have looked at Greensboro, NC that looks better but I'd still prefer the hill county in Texas. I don't care for basements that pose more radon concerns and know you can have professional remediation done but who wants to have to do that. I have read that you can open your windows to help dissipate radon but I've had cancer and am Leary of any possible additional toxic load.

# Thread: London Calling
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/london-calling.20050/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jake Tassell — 2017-08-24T11:18:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/london-calling.20050/post-222156>

Hello

I've been dipping into the forum for ages. I'm not sure why I've signed up now, possibly hastened by the imminent arrival of 5G.

I came to this light/water/magnetism business through a strange and circuitous route going back to the 1980's, which would require a long and involved shaggy dog story to relate, so I'll probably just let bits of it out at a time where relevant.

Some stuff about me...

Off the top of my head... I have been at various points: a Northern Soul dancer, a cigarette smoker, a music journalist, a Greek sun-holiday freak, a night club host, a building site labourer, a call-centre worker, a model, a pirate radio op, a coconut oil enthusiast, an actor, a night-clubber, a window cleaner, a tratak practitioner, a vinyl junkie, a G.I. Gurdjieff fan, a crummy surf guitarist, an all around zeitgeist freak and plenty else besides. When I'm not working in my humble NNEMF heavy job, I generally have music running around the clock. When I'm at the computer I wear blue blockers, and am probably one of the last men in England that does not own a cellphone.

I've got quite a lot out of Jack's gear. Hopefully I can put a bit back, although at this stage my quantum physics-ese is probably paddling pool level. See how we go.

Cheers

Jake

### Jake Tassell — 2017-08-24T11:41:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/london-calling.20050/post-222158>

Thank you, Sue

Cheers

Jake

# Thread: Why people are so much concerned about weight loss?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-people-are-so-much-concerned-about-weight-loss.20053/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jay Hall — 2017-08-24T20:17:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-people-are-so-much-concerned-about-weight-loss.20053/post-222188>

People want to lase weight and they work on it for a good reason! Obesity has turning out as a major risk factor for heart strokes, high BP, heart disease, diabetes and cancer. All physical activity seems to be more difficult when you need to carry around a great amount of additional weight. moreover, Extra weight can also be the reason for different sleep related problems. When you are quite weight and lay down the weight pressure of the weight can make breathing difficult. Even turning over in bed is hard when you are very big.

Losing weight demands a considerable amount of consideration and motivation. Eating habits are mainly hard, as you need to make choices on a daily basis and many times a day. It’s quite different from quitting smoking or drinking. With nearly any other abusable material, you can just quit using entirely. This cannot be the case with food. You should manage it well.

Many people use _[natural appetite suppressants for weight loss](http://www.polestarnutraceuticals.com/product/brainikus-diet-drops-with-african-mango/),_ which also is a good option for the purpose yet the consumer should also be motivated to quit bad eating habits.

### Lahelada — 2017-08-24T20:21:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-people-are-so-much-concerned-about-weight-loss.20053/post-222191>

Spam has too much salt for my bp !!
