[www.fitflex.com/thyroid-bodybuilding.html](http://www.fitflex.com/thyroid-bodybuilding.html)
Especially this line caught my attention:
"The gland produces two iodine-containing compounds: liothyronine (T3), also known as triiodothyronine, and thyroxine (T")."
Connecting this with the the information from @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) Vermont talk and i got a much better understanding of this as thyroxine is an aromatic amino acid
[Thryroxine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aromatic_amino_acids#/media/File:Thyroxine-2D-skeletal.png)
[Triiodothyronine (T3)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Triiodothyronine)

# Thread: Why you need to be a skeptic of PEER review......

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-13T15:50:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-you-need-to-be-a-skeptic-of-peer-review.20146/post-223542>

Why you need to be a skeptic of PEER review......in medicine

I want you to think about how much PEER review has changed in 120 years.......it used to be open to get new ideas in the mainstream to be ripped apart.......or accepted by experiment. Today can any truth make it to doctors or patients based upon the guardians at the gate?

_The present peer review systems are so stifling that any manuscript so iconoclastic as Einstein’s initial papers would now have little chance of appearing in any ranking journal. This is particularly true in physics, and especially so in the United States._
_
Think about the implications for modern chronic diseases. IS it any wonder why we cannot solve them? PEER review is a paradigm protection._

# Thread: What is redox
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox.20150/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shiran — 2017-09-14T15:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox.20150/post-223614>

how can I explain to someone in the simplest way that is redox?
Simple easy words to understand
Thanks

### kris90 — 2017-09-15T16:12:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox.20150/post-223715>

Are you looking for what redox itself means, or redox potential?

Redox (as in redox reaction) = reduction/oxidation -> when something loses an electron = oxidation, when something gains an electron = reduction

Redox potential is the ability to receive/donate an electron. When we refer to a high redox potential on this site, it means you have a lot of electrons in your cells to donate to oxygen. That's why a low redox = dehydration, hypoxia, inflammation, etc.

>
> Are you looking for what redox itself means, or redox potential?
>
> Redox (as in redox reaction) = reduction/oxidation -> when something loses an electron = oxidation, when something gains an electron = reduction
>
> Redox potential is the ability to receive/donate an electron. When we refer to a high redox potential on this site, it means you have a lot of electrons in your cells to donate to oxygen. That's why a low redox = dehydration, hypoxia, inflammation, etc.

I was just about to look up redox.

Hi kris thanks for your great explanation I understand it .
So if Im explaining it to a person in Hebrew The redox reaction is clear
But the redox potential is a little difficult to explain ....
Can I explain it this way
A high redox means You have a lot of " electric power " in your cell that came from the exposure to the sun...
looking for very simple words..
Thanks

I'm going to read again the redox Blog and [https://www.jackkruse.com/guest-physician-blog-1-the-power-of-uv-light/](https://www.jackkruse.com/guest-physician-blog-1-the-power-of-uv-light/)

### kris90 — 2017-09-15T17:16:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox.20150/post-223724>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=223717)
>
> Can I explain it this way
> A high redox means You have a lot of " **electric power** " in your cell that came from the exposure to the sun...

I would word it as: "a lot of **electric potential**" rather than "electric power". Sunlight "excites" electrons. Electrons by themselves are in the ground state, until they become excited. Ground state electron = electric potential. Excited state electron = electric power, because it carries the power of the photon.

Sunlight DOES increase redox, because it splits water into negative/positive (i.e. the EZ). This "frees" electrons, and those electrons as discussed above carry electric potential, and are used in redox reactions once they become "excited".

Ground state electron = electric potential. Excited state electron = electric power, because it carries the power of the photon.
>
> Sunlight DOES increase redox, because it splits water into negative/positive (i.e. the EZ). This "frees" electrons, and those electrons as discussed above carry electric potential, and are used in redox reactions once they become "excited".

Perfect

Environment
Light exposure
Sleeping patterns
Understanding which systems fall apart first (and which did historically for your maternal line)

That's kinda 101 for beginning to identify the low hanging fruit.

### drezy — 2018-07-09T15:40:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox.20150/post-245422>

There is a benefit to understanding as much as possible, but I think learning in increments is very useful.

Rough idea:
Your body is made of cells, in your cells are mitochondria fueling them, in your mitochondria are atpase heads like little motors spinning away.

Don't drop stuff into the spinning motor and start jamming it. Decades will provide that unwanted feature anyway. Bad environments will jam up the works too.

You are claiming low redox so it sounds as though you know which system in your body breaks down first. You look young meaning that like a nice savings/compound interest scenario the chances are high to benefit greatly over your lifetime.

If you are looking to turn around a condition the folks here are a caring bunch and having Doc's comment and guidance is priceless.

Cute puppy. Is it part German Shepard?. I'd recommend a picture showing your eyes. I'm a pretty private guy and can understand the desire for privacy, but a more proper picture is helpful to the community here in ways you will likely come to understand over time.

### tallweeds — 2019-01-31T21:55:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox.20150/post-261950>

In the Redox Rx Jack says:

12\. **A bioimpedance assay tester (BIA), a dental pulp tester, a TENS unit, thermography, CAC, or intranasal red light exposure** can all be used to assess redox potential in biohacks for serial measurements.

Most functional medicine practitioners have BIA testers. They can go over the test with you. BIA is a good way to tell about total body water status as well. Dehydration is a recognized factor affecting BIA measurements, as it causes an increase in the body’s electrical resistance. For a low redox potential, we want a low electrical resistance in cells and tissues. Semiconductors are designed to have low resistance by their very nature. A high redox potential robs them of that ability.

Wouldn't a high redox mean low electrical resistance? High redox would mean more water produced? I'm confused.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-01-31T22:44:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox.20150/post-261961>

A high redox potential robs them of that ability.
>
> Wouldn't a high redox mean low electrical resistance? High redox would mean more water produced? I'm confused.

Describe dehydration and how you measure it.

..

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BPS — 2017-09-14T16:12:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interpret-my-fatty-acid-test-results.20152/post-223622>

Hi all

I got back my Fatty Acid test results, they look pretty good but would love your input on what could be tweaked?

I was particularly interested in this test as I'm prone to eczema/dry skin so getting this right is key.

Attached results!

Thanks

### BPS — 2017-09-14T16:15:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interpret-my-fatty-acid-test-results.20152/post-223623>

ps, I dont currently supplement with any EFAs but do eat fatty fish daily so quite happy with my levels considering

### JanSz — 2017-09-14T16:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interpret-my-fatty-acid-test-results.20152/post-223625>

This analysis does not give ranges.
You are at the mercy of their opinion of what is good or what is bad.
This analysis ALWAYS recommend that you get more omega 3.

That lab may be owned by fish oil company.

### JanSz — 2017-09-14T18:02:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interpret-my-fatty-acid-test-results.20152/post-223629>

Just to clarify.
I do not think of myself as any expert on fatty acids analysis.
But,
when I see someone insisting on this or that,
I would like that expert to also propose method of testing that is clearly defined, without bias and available to use by general public.
Hand waving
or
highly technical discussions on even very closely related topics
do not count.

....
Singling out one variable in highly multivariate situation is more political or economical than technical.

...

### BPS — 2017-09-14T18:50:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interpret-my-fatty-acid-test-results.20152/post-223635>

OK but many of the tests I looked at that were available to me did not show reference ranges for the profiles, as they all state that the % relative values (eg AA vs DHA) are what matters.

In which case, reference values ARE given on this test

So based upon what we have, are those good, and any suggestions?

**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sajid Mahmood — 2017-09-15T11:06:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wild-oysters-or-farmed-oysters.20155/post-223689>

Are wild oysters superior over farmed oysters in nutrient density?
Are wild oysters massively more nutrient dense or is it only a little difference?
What's the reason?

Thank You

### Hunger4More — 2017-09-16T21:01:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wild-oysters-or-farmed-oysters.20155/post-223816>

Don't think there is a difference.

Oysters sit it sea water and "eat" by filtering said water.

One appeared in the sea naturally, the other was placed there by a farmer.

Not a situation like farmed seafood where they are being fed corn and soy.

### Sajid Mahmood — 2017-09-16T21:10:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wild-oysters-or-farmed-oysters.20155/post-223818>

I'm not quite sure but if I can vaguely remember Jack say he preferred the wild ones to be far superior. Wanted to know the reason behind it and wanted to confirm if this was true

# Thread: Build Better Hormones Naturally!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/build-better-hormones-naturally.20158/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kathy1967 — 2017-09-15T19:14:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/build-better-hormones-naturally.20158/post-223737>

Hi All, I would love your opinion on getting great hormones without supplementing! I just had labs done through Life Extension & was advised by them that I could supplement with 15mg Progesterone & 50mg Pregnenolone to optimize my hormones. I'm not completely opposed to supplementing but first I want to give my body about 3 -4 months going the all natural route. My idea is to get back into CT consistently with cold water baths (around 65 Fahrenheit for about 30 minutes) and ice baths (50ish Fahrenheit for about 15-20 minutes) 3 - 5 times weekly. I get at least 10 minutes of am sun every morning. I'm adding 1 tsp of Maca to my morning hot cocoa, starting to take 1 tsp of celery seed powder 3x daily to help with my adrenals, and continuing with my ketogenic diet. I have grass fed dairy frequently (sour cream, heavy cream, butter). My waist has expanded about 2 -3 inches in the past 3 years. I'm almost 50, my cycle is perfectly 28 days still. Here are my most recent labs. Any and all advice will be so appreciated!!!!

Day 21 of my cycle
Testosterone, serum - 12 ng/dL
Testosterone, Free - .06 pg/mL
DHEA sulfate - 137.6 ug/dL
Estradiol - 101.7 pg/mL
Progesterone - 8.2 ng/mL

Day 3 of my cycle
DHEA (not sulfate) - 173 ng/dL
progesterone - .5 - ng/mL
Estrogens, Total - 81 pg/mL
Testosterone, serum - 11 ng/dL
Testosterone, Free - 1 pg/mL
Pregnenolone, Ms - <10 ng/dL
TSH-ICMA - 1.2 uU/mL
Free T4 by Dialysis/Mass Spec - 1.1 ng/dL

### nonchalant — 2017-09-15T20:04:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/build-better-hormones-naturally.20158/post-223741>

I would say a lot more sun. When do you catch the sunlight? When it is still red or orange? I would say you need at least an hour of sunlight before the UV light shows up, because that's when the pituitary makes those hormones.

### Kathy1967 — 2017-09-15T20:22:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/build-better-hormones-naturally.20158/post-223743>

Thanks so much Nonchalant! I live in NJ and have to be at work at 7:15am. I usually have about 15 minutes that I can stand outside at work before I need to go in, I work at a lake and I put my feet in there often! It is not still red or orange by time I get to work but actually with the sun rising later now it will be! I'll make sure though to get up earlier and catch the sun at home. SO I'll add in more sun and retest in about 3 months. I'm kind of worried about starting supplemental hormones and then NEEDING to keep supplementing!

Anyone have any pictures or plans for building a low nnEMF full spectrum sauna?

I remember Ruben Salinas talking about his but I never saw a picture. If anyone has a picture of his sauna or has an idea what his design is please post it.

Curious what others have come up with.

Otherwise I'm thinking just building a wooden enclosure out of whatever wood works best, adding in infrared heat lamps, maybe some UVA bulbs I could switch on independently, lining it with gold or silver foil in various areas. Am I missing anything?

# Thread: A Guy Named "Peter Smart" dismantled a Smart Meter FB group
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-guy-named-peter-smart-dismantled-a-smart-meter-fb-group.20163/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2017-09-16T22:04:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-guy-named-peter-smart-dismantled-a-smart-meter-fb-group.20163/post-223820>

TROLL WARNING

We received a warning regarding Smart Meters Health Problems group being taken over by trolls... a group that had more than 10,000 members. While I am not privy to the details and this is only speculation on my part it has been suggested that a member posed as an power industry insider who joined to help. An infiltrator, Peter Smart, gained the trust of the group admin and was eventually made an admin himself. Once inside he played the divide and conquer strategy.

This is in part what was reported: "Unfortunately, Smart Meters Health Problems group, which had amassed 10,500 members, has been taken over by a bunch of smart meter installers. The group has been made secret, and the members are being removed, and replaced with more smart meter and industry people.

It's now called "The Tin Foil Hat Brigade", or something like that. The person who seems to have been behind this is "Peter Smart"; you might want to see if you've accepted a friend request from him, and take action if so. You might also think about reporting him to Facebook; I did so on the basis that he represented an industry, as FB didn't give me a more appropriate option."

This is him:

[https://www.facebook.com/peter.smart.5477272?ref=br\_rs](https://www.facebook.com/peter.smart.5477272?ref=br_rs&fref=gc&dti=435524353449853&hc_location=ufi)

\=-=-=

So what's my opinion on this? What Smart did wasn't very Smart, was it. Now it's a story that can be told to raise even more awareness to the masses about the Smart Meters and nnEMF sources to the general public. Everyone likes the underdog. Someone should go to the media since it had an influence on a known 10,000+ people.

It also shows that many of our groups that are letting the public know about nnEMFs is very telling. If we attract Trolls, we know we are doing our job well. What trolls don't realize is how easy it can be to turn them to stone when sunlight strikes them.

# Thread: Jake Tassell is...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jake-tassell-is.20164/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jake Tassell — 2017-09-17T21:53:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jake-tassell-is.20164/post-223866>

Currently having a look at Methylene Blue after reading Jack's 'Biohacking "Time" with Methylene Blue' piece....
[https://www.jackkruse.com/biohacking-time-with-methylene-blue/](https://www.jackkruse.com/biohacking-time-with-methylene-blue/)

I got mine from a clean looking source in The UK...
[https://mitoblue.com/products/mitoblue-methylene-blue-solution-pharmaceutical-grade](https://mitoblue.com/products/mitoblue-methylene-blue-solution-pharmaceutical-grade).

A bit early for testimonials, although: Day one - felt a bit kind of mellow-ish. Halfway through the day I experienced a craving for liver pate, which I duly fulfilled. Don't know if that was incidental, or whether I was experiencing some kind of MB-related vitamin A/choline warp?

Making some early prep to go on holiday. Debating whether to ditch my tangerine swimming trunks. They're looking a bit pink-ish. LOL.

It's going to be a nice, cheap, 10-day blast-o del sol in Lanzarote. Notable features: They've got that restaurant 'El Diablo' that sits on top of a volcano where you can eat magma-grilled steaks, and the famous beach 'Papagayo' as used in the movie 'One Million Years B.C.', where, on a clear day you can see Raquel Welch being carried off by a giant pterodactyl....

### drezy — 2017-09-17T23:19:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jake-tassell-is.20164/post-223870>

Hmmmm. The more sun intense weeks seem to coincide with my pâté or liverwurst cravings. It seemed like such a small detail but it's interesting to see you note it as well. Enjoy your time on the island.

### Da-mo — 2017-09-17T23:56:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jake-tassell-is.20164/post-223872>

You wouldnt believe how hard it is to get beef liver in New Zealand. Lamb liver and Pig liver you can get from any supermarket, but beef liver (which is less inflamatory than the others) is nowhere to be seen.

I had to ask a small town butcher and even then had to wait until the next time he butchered a cow.

Someone told me the fast food burger chains buy it all up - so much so that they had to come to an agreement between them to prevent the prices of beef liver rising too much.

Anyway, since it is inflammatory I always eat it with onions.

### drezy — 2017-09-18T00:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jake-tassell-is.20164/post-223873>

@[Da-mo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9712/), Interesting. Growing up in the Northeast US, liver and onions is what your mom made for lunches when dad was at work. Liver was very cheap and, as a kid in my house, you had 2 choices eat it or eat nothing. That's why it seem odd to me now that when I get longer sun exposure weeks I get some liver craves.

I have made some changes by adding my favorite spice -- bacon!

Jake's soon to be digested food cooked by magma is so cool.

### Jake Tassell — 2017-09-18T00:58:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jake-tassell-is.20164/post-223874>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=223870)
>
> Hmmmm. The more sun intense weeks seem to coincide with my pâté or liverwurst cravings. It seemed like such a small detail but it's interesting to see you note it as well.

Now that _is_ interesting.

A) Because I didn't expect to get any feedback on what I thought was probably a trifle.

B) Because I don't think I've ever had a craving for liver pate before in my life.

C) The sun is pretty much gone in old London town, and I haven't been getting too much in the way of rays, apart from a bit of MM UV and IR in the flat.

### Sheddie — 2017-09-18T01:59:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jake-tassell-is.20164/post-223876>

I think liver & onions may be something passed down with mDNA? As a child, hated this meal, fixed by an immigrant (forgivable) grandmother and then a regular (unforgivable) mother who copied it, sometimes named "Liver & Lima beans" but the onions were there, too, on the dreaded plate. Several decades later, after the advent of The Food Processor for the family kitchen, liver, onions, butter, bacon -- the groundwork to liver pate -- brought the idea up many levels. Especially the recipes that used Cognac! (memo: cook off the alcohol for paleo) Great organ meat recipes are such treasures.

### Da-mo — 2017-09-18T09:35:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jake-tassell-is.20164/post-223881>

My Mum used to cook organ meats regularly - and she was a chef for 18 years.

My better half shudders at the thought though - only cooked liver once or twice when I asked . . . .

I suppose I should do more cooking around here - but she might not eat it

Edit: Oh just remembered, back in my teens when I was in Venturer Scouts (never was a cub or a Scout but in Venturers there was girls) we had a troop come over from New Caledonia (French speaking people). My friend put some tinned cat food (Chef Jellymeat) on a plate with some crackers and told them it was pate' - they loved it!!!!

>
> Can't talk my husband into it...

LOL. He should try them. I'm not used to tight shorts myself but they've become part of my routine now.

My wife gets really immature around the kinikis. Often during mutual sunning session she'll point and yell "I think it moved!" and then cackle and laugh. Almost counter-intuitively, and very much like a bored dog, any attention focused down there including laughing gets biologically construed as good attention. You know that effect when a dog is laying down and you make the mistake of looking at it and instantly it's all up in you space.

I'll guarantee your hubby when all is said and done that he'll like the looks and stares.

# Thread: Chris' Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris-journal.20166/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cjmorin — 2017-09-18T21:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris-journal.20166/post-223921>

Hello everybody!

I'm Chris, and have been lurking here for the past three months or so, absorbing much more information (and consequently full-spectrum sunshine) than I have in a long time. I value the communication that happens here and the true desire of other members to pursue their own personal growth while supporting the growth of one another! I am looking forward to adding to the discourse as time goes by.

**About Me**
I'm Chris, a 32 year old massage therapist, personal trainer and I have been a nutrition and health junkie for the better part of 14 years. I am tall, fair skinned with light blue eyes and medium brown hair, British and Italian heritage, as well as a bit of Native American in there. My 23&Me report says that I have more Neanderthal in me than most (ooga!).

I am generally quite energetic, I am physically fit and strong, very funny, quick-witted and engaging and have always enjoyed learning new things and keeping up on current science. I have also struggled for most of my adult life with social anxiety, depression, making and keeping friends and other fear/panic related challenges and low dopamine-type behaviors like alcohol and sex addiction and the never ending quest for "more" or "better".

This very well may have a lot to do with my birth trauma (more below), my job (picking up energetic baggage like some sort of Tantric trash man) and undoubtedly with my less-than-stellar environment (raised on video games, voluntarily surrounded myself with innovative lighting products that not only incorporate LEDs but also BT and WIFI!!, and I'm a recovering blue-light-at-night addict, all in the ironic pursuit of learning about health!)

Just last week I had a series of what I believe were my first real panic attacks, that is, the sudden and overwhelming feeling of intense anxiety and dread that started in my solar plexus and moved up into my chest, heart and head, leading to what felt like AFib and the convincing notion that I was surely going to keel over and croak at any second. This has happened only a handful of times so far, but my neurological system feels like it is now hyper vigilant and I have been feeling strong negative effects from stimuli that normally do not hit me so drastically (things like smoking weed, even strains with very low THC, staying up too late, eating crap foods, etc.)

Admittedly, I did not originally come here seeking answers to these problems, but found the site through a search on DHEA after reading much about it on the Ray Peat forums. I definitely feel as though my neuroses are directly linked to the fact that I have essentially become a creebling blue-lit cave dwarf that is relegated to a life of watching other people through electronic portals rather than actually meeting them in person. Isn't that what the kids are all about, nowadays?

My sleep is often quite terrible and I do not fall asleep quickly or get very tired until late at night. This has been a constant struggle for me.

Libido is high as it ever was, and perhaps even more so since experimenting with a few upstream topical hormones a la Peat. I will be tapering this off as I become more thoroughly steeped in the lessons here. I recognize (albeit not in an entirely graceful way, yet) that hormones are essentially a chemical translation of light and circadian rhythm signals. Thus, any exogenous hormones, aside from the fundamental building blocks provided by diet, should not be required as I become more sensitive to the natural cycles around me and produce what is needed inside.

**My Grandmother's History**
I know little about her or her life experience, but she did not seem to be a very resilient or energetic woman, and was prone towards narcissism. She liked to show us clothing and things that she bought for herself and pictures of where she went. I tend to think she probably suffered from social anxiety and some type of mood disorder.

She moved to Florida after having a double mastectomy and bypass surgery and lived for several more years, eventually passing in her late 70's or early 80's. I have to imagine that even the unintentional intense sunlight helped to prolong her life after her move.

**My Mother's History**
My mom is a lovely woman who has thankfully not struggled with many health-related challenges for her 70 years of life so far. She had a child at 18, another at 23 and finally me (with my dad) at 38. She has mentioned in passing that she had pretty severe postpartum depression after giving birth to me, and the process itself was not without it's challenges. I was breach, strangled by the umbilical cord and essentially my brain was dead, all before I even started. Not the best introduction to the world.

I believe that she has stayed healthy for so long because of her intense love of nature and animals, intentional time spent outside and lots of love and laughter at home. It was not without it's challenges, as helping to run a family business (as I was doing for the last decade or so) with my father is extremely stressful at times.

Lately, she has only really suffered from issues with balance and dizziness. I think this may be due to something in her inner ear, and perhaps more frequent usage of technology and nnEMF. She has become sort of a gadget fiend lately, too, finally matching my father's love of all things technological.

**My Initial Steps**
I just recently moved to Portland, ME from the Berkshires in NW MA. My girlfriend and I love it here, and I value the availability of fresh seafood and summertime sunshine. That said, the window for this season is quickly closing, and I am looking forward to getting more involved in CT this winter to compensate for it. I am currently seeking a very large animal drinking trough or watertight barrel to use as my CT lab this winter!

We live on the third floor of a three apartment house in a suburban neighborhood in a relatively dense school district. I do not know the light/nnEMF conditions for my downstairs neighbors, or if the house has a Smart Meter. I do know that I can pick up only 4 WIFI networks including my own from where we usually spend time. Those other signals are quite weak, as well, for which I am thankful.

I have replaced all but a few hardwired lamps with 2200K Edison-style incandescent bulbs, most on dimmers, for additional control.

I have begun turning off Wifi and Cable modem before bed.

We do not watch TV ever, but I work on my phone many hours per day and we occasionally watch movies on a 10' projector screen in ourliving room (lettin that Geek flag fly). I wear blue blocker sunglasses during any nighttime activity, especially those involving blue light like movie time. I downregulate blue as much as possible on all my devices, although I know this is only partially effective.

I use an app that is ironically called Appblocker to completely eliminate my usage of devices at night past 9pm. I even had my girlfriend set the password so that I cannot uninstall it! We set our phones on airplane at night or turn them completely off.

I keep my cell on airplane mode when in my pocket for extended periods of time.

I am in the process of reading John Ott's book and have already taken the time to watch several videos regarding his observations...fascinating stuff. I was browsing earlier and learning about potential cancer-promoting effects of "pink" light and thought a lot about the effects that colors and light frequencies have on us. I tend to wear very natural colors, drab or earthy tones with a bias towards green, blue or gray.

I have a couple NIR bulbs that I like to spend time under before bed. We own a fancy-schmancy Biomat, which I use in small doses, as I feel pretty crummy if I am on it too long while dehydrated.

I tried keto about a year ago during the winter and it did not serve me so well, and based on what I have read here so far I am starting to see why...poor sleep regulation, poor hydration from poor quality water, lack of frequent cold, terrible availability of natural light, little to no integration of DHA and mineral-rich foods from the sea, etc. I plan to try again this winter with the incorporation of loads of seafood, better hydration with RO water and a large reduction in nnEMF and out-of-cycle blue light.

I plan to install a countertop RO system so that I can better control my ingestion of fluoride. I have already been using a ZenWater system, which seems to be pretty fantastic for just about everything but Fluoride, so it's time to step up.

**My Concerns Moving Forward**
I am worried about this long Maine winter. Portland is at 43.6 degrees, which is higher than I have ever lived long-term, but not by much. That said, I believe that my fair complexion and pale eyes will make the most out of any light that is provided in this environment, and will do my best to expose my body when there are appropriate opportunities to do so.

I have considered getting a tanning membership, but lack the devices needed to check for nnEMF in the beds. I really would rather avoid tanning, but also cannot really lie out naked anywhere around my apartment to maximize wintertime light absorption. Guidance on this point is appreciated.

Thanks to Dr. Jack for his brilliant and nuanced insights into the true functioning of the world! I feel like I have been preparing to read these blogs for the last decade. Thanks again to all the highly active members here whose observations and n=1s have been so thoughtfully shared!

Chris

# Thread: Mother's Placenta: connections to Cortisol, Myelin and much more
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mothers-placenta-connections-to-cortisol-myelin-and-much-more.20170/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2017-09-19T07:10:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mothers-placenta-connections-to-cortisol-myelin-and-much-more.20170/post-223961>

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28864022](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28864022)

"Further, we will describe the placenta's role as both a barrier to maternally-derived bioactive substances critical for normal fetal brain development, such as **cortisol**, and a source of **neuroactive steroids** and **neurotrophins** known to have **critical functions in neuronal proliferation**, **axonal growth**, **myelination** and **the regulation of apoptosis**. Finally, emerging data supporting the potential utility of novel placental biomarkers in the early prediction of poor neurodevelopmental outcome in infants born both preterm and term will be discussed. These include the assessment of genetic variants (e.g. single nucleotide polymorphisms in placental tissue) and epigenetic biomarkers (e.g. **placental microRNAs** and placental **DNA methylation**). With the placenta the key tissue regulating the fetal environment, integration of observed changes in placental function **with genetic and epigenetic variations** may advance our ability to predict future infant health. Ultimately, this may facilitate targeted allocation of health resources with the aim of **improving lifelong neurodevelopmental capability**."

### shiran — 2017-09-23T20:25:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mothers-placenta-connections-to-cortisol-myelin-and-much-more.20170/post-224206>

Very interesting I'm thinking about fetal developments regarding the mothers environment it blows my mind

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-01T19:40:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mothers-placenta-connections-to-cortisol-myelin-and-much-more.20170/post-252088>

What is today Black Swan lesson about progesterone, melanopsin, and nnEMF? #melanopsinwisdom [https://www.facebook.com/drjackkrus...otif\_id=1538402603259356&notif\_t=feed\_comment](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/2338793329518341?comment_id=2338948736169467&notif_id=1538402603259356&notif_t=feed_comment)

# Thread: vestibular issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vestibular-issues.20171/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### twilightkid — 2017-09-20T02:28:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vestibular-issues.20171/post-223981>

Hey guys and gals, anyone here deal with vestibular disorders (vestibular migraine, vestibular neuritis, etc...)?
If so, do you have any tips or success stories that resolved the issue? Much THANKS in advance for your reply.
\-Paul

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-20T07:58:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vestibular-issues.20171/post-223991>

Very complicated group of disorders than need a work up by an ENT or functional neurologist in my opinion. There are so many causes that you cannot make recs on what to do without a diagnosis. I will say the number one thing I've noticed with 5 G is that people are complaining of these issues early with high BP and the only thing that helps is environment change and use of high dose Magnesium under controlled conditions similar to how toxemia is handled. It is baffling lots of docs now because we are seeing it more and more.

### twilightkid — 2017-09-20T16:47:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vestibular-issues.20171/post-224013>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=223991)
>
> Very complicated group of disorders than need a work up by an ENT or functional neurologist in my opinion. There are som many causes that you cannt make recs on what to do without a diagnosis. I will say the number one thing I've noticed with 5 G is that people are complaining of these issues early with high BP and the only thing that helps is environment change and use of high dose Magnesium under controlled conditions similar to how toxemia is handled. It is baffling lots of docs now because we are seeing it more and more.

Thanks Doc, I appreciate the reply.

Too add, not only am I dealing with it, but a lot of my own clients complain of these issues too. For me, prior to 2014 when my intial symptoms hit I was wearing blue tooth device in my ear ALL day. I have my suspicions but Im not sure I'll ever know what prompted it. For now, I'm a work in progress doing what I can to resolve the issue.

### JanSz — 2017-09-20T17:04:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vestibular-issues.20171/post-224016>

> [twilightkid said:](/goto/post?id=223981)
>
> Hey guys and gals, anyone here deal with vestibular disorders (vestibular migraine, vestibular neuritis, etc...)?
> If so, do you have any tips or success stories that resolved the issue? Much THANKS in advance for your reply.
> \-Paul

Just because I see word "migraine"
I would check progesterone.
If it is significantly less than 1.4 ng/mL (men) I would dig deeper.

Better yet:
preg
prog
cortisol(7:30AM)

.

### twilightkid — 2017-09-20T21:50:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vestibular-issues.20171/post-224044>

JanSz, long time no talk, buddy. Thanks for the heads up.

### dondioneal — 2023-07-10T20:39:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vestibular-issues.20171/post-323509>

Was curious, any updates?

[https://www.theguardian.com/lifeandstyle/2017/feb/14/sea-to-plate-plastic-got-into-fish](https://www.theguardian.com/lifeandstyle/2017/feb/14/sea-to-plate-plastic-got-into-fish)

Sadly, micro-plastics are in loads of products nowadays (including things like toothpastes and cosmetics) and end up in the environment one way or another. Shellfish filter water, which includes micro-plastics.

# Thread: High vitamin D
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-vitamin-d.20174/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shiran — 2017-09-20T18:41:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-vitamin-d.20174/post-224027>

should I be concerned about my vitamin D level- **188nmol/L
Thanks**

### Hunger4More — 2017-09-20T19:11:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-vitamin-d.20174/post-224030>

My last labs came back at 260nmol/L... not a single supplement, sunshine only. I think your question is good; but based on lab ranges your vitamin D level is still normal.

# Thread: Favorite articles for newbies?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-articles-for-newbies.20175/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2017-09-20T19:04:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-articles-for-newbies.20175/post-224029>

Hi all. I've been able to convince a few people to get in the cold tub and a couple more to take off their sunglasses during morning walks.

I've seen articles throughout the forum that would make for great starter material. My personal favorite, since I'm such a CT junkie, is:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106)

Favorite line:

> Cold water immersion (14 degrees C) lowered rectal temperature and increased metabolic rate (by 350%), heart rate and systolic and diastolic blood pressure (by 5%, 7%, and 8%, respectively).

I also have some basic info on light and melatonin levels and well as melatonin and repair mechanisms.

What is your personal favorite article(s) to show newbies a glimpse into our world?

@[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) once mentioned that she has papers that she takes with her and shows people. I think it's time for me to get in touch with my inner Sue-UK and do this more often too.

### caroline — 2017-09-21T03:25:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-articles-for-newbies.20175/post-224063>

I love the one - the importance of newbies

also - BG #6

### Sue-UK — 2017-09-21T13:46:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-articles-for-newbies.20175/post-224082>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=224029)
>
> Hi all. I've been able to convince a few people to get in the cold tub and a couple more to take off their sunglasses during morning walks.
>
> I've seen articles throughout the forum that would make for great starter material. My personal favorite, since I'm such a CT junkie, is:
> [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106)
>
> Favorite line:
>
>
> I also have some basic info on light and melatonin levels and well as melatonin and repair mechanisms.
>
> What is your personal favorite article(s) to show newbies a glimpse into our world?
>
> @[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) once mentioned that she has papers that she takes with her and shows people. I think it's time for me to get in touch with my inner Sue-UK and do this more often too.

I've found that people's attention is drawn to pictures more so I've been using a series of "slides" I've printed out. I'm going to the next level - giving a 2 hour workshop to 5 people next week so adding some more in and using them as handouts. I'll talk using the handouts and have a table with books, studies and blogs available for people who want to make a note of where they can go to get more info themselves and delve deeper, or if I need to grab a scientific source of what I'm covering.

 and  at the same time.

# Thread: PSA about Leptin Rx & Menstrual Cycle
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psa-about-leptin-rx-menstrual-cycle.20177/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ValerieBee — 2017-09-20T20:59:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psa-about-leptin-rx-menstrual-cycle.20177/post-224039>

Changing leptin sensitivity has an effect on your menstrual cycle. I've noticed change in length of cycle and in time of ovulation. Made me recall a story from a friend with a surprise pregnancy years ago. She was told she couldn't get pregnant, she was overweight. She joined a weight loss contest and lost a lot of weight rapidly. Surprise pregnancy. Just a PSA for those charting your own fertility--when you start messing with leptin, be extra vigilant with fertility tracking!

This makes sense given the relationship of leptin to fertility. (See [blog Central Leptin Dominance Pt 2](https://www.jackkruse.com/central-leptin-dominance-part-2/))

### ScottishEmma — 2017-09-21T08:42:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psa-about-leptin-rx-menstrual-cycle.20177/post-224071>

Interesting! My cycle has definitely shortened and my period is much lighter. I was putting it down to the DIM and Calcium Glucanate supplement but maybe it's the protocols. And yeah, my body is totally trying to get my pregnant. <---- BIG FAT NOPE

My husband says that he feels his erections are responding to my cycle too haha!

### ScottishEmma — 2017-09-21T08:42:38+0300

# Thread: So Many Questions :-) I'll Start with a Few
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-many-questions-ill-start-with-a-few.20180/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark Joyner — 2017-09-21T03:48:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-many-questions-ill-start-with-a-few.20180/post-224066>

Howdy folks,

First off, this is my first real post after my meet and greet, so if'n I'm fishin' in the wrong hole, please don't hesitate to correct my forum etiquette.

1\. Jack's Naked Bayou Sunrise Gaze Ritual

If I understood correctly, Jack mentioned (in one of the many videos and posts I've consumed so far, can't remember which) he wakes up to watch the sunrise. However, he also mentioned (if memory serves) that the optimal time for this ritual depends on latitude.

I travel quite a bit, so I'd like to have a way to determine the optimal timing / protocol for this ritual based on wherever I am.

\- Is there a chart that shows the optimal time based on latitude?
\- What is the optimal length of time?
\- Is the idea to gaze directly at the sun (a la the sun gazers out there) or to just be "in the sun" at that time?
\- If one is in an area where they'll be arrested for letting their naughty bits hang out during the ritual, will those tan-through shorts be an acceptable substitute? (Not sure if they selectively block bands that will make the whole thing unhealthy, for example ...)

2\. Blue-Blocking Shades

My buddy Joe Sugarman started that whole thing years ago and still sells BluBlockers at a pretty decent price. These days there are many more expensive brands and I'm not clear if there is any relevant difference in the brands beyond aesthetics. (Not that there's anything wrong with wanting to look cool

Are there any brands / tech specs I should look for?

3\. Computer Time

Wear blue-blocking glasses?

If one is in an enclosed space are there any supplementary lights that will improve the situation? (I'm going to endeavor to work outside as much as I can, but my work won't allow that all the time.)

OK ... I could go on. But probably best I stop there for now

Thanks in advance to anyone who can improve my understanding.

MJ

### Lahelada — 2017-09-21T16:56:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-many-questions-ill-start-with-a-few.20180/post-224087>

> [Mark Joyner said:](/goto/post?id=224066)
>
> Howdy folks,
>
> First off, this is my first real post after my meet and greet, so if'n I'm fishin' in the wrong hole, please don't hesitate to correct my forum etiquette.
>
> 1\. Jack's Naked Bayou Sunrise Gaze Ritual
>
> \- Is there a chart that shows the optimal time based on latitude? [www.timeanddate.com](http://www.timeanddate.com)
> \- What is the optimal length of time?(From the comments of Ubi24
> \- Is the idea to gaze directly at the sun (a la the sun gazers out there) or to just be "in the sun" at that time?
> Dr Kruse :"Vince take the glasses or contact off and look 15-25 degrees away from the sun but in its East wood direction…….how long depends upon your latitude and personal context. Those are things I tease out in details with my members in Q & A’s"
>
> \- If one is in an area where they'll be arrested for letting their naughty bits hang out during the ritual, will those tan-through shorts be an acceptable substitute? (Not sure if they selectively block bands that will make the whole thing unhealthy, for example ...) Good option
>
> 2\. Blue-Blocking Shades
>
> Are there any brands / tech specs I should look for? [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/) If they pass the tests mentioned in this thread you are good.
>
>
> 3\. Computer Time
>
> Wear blue-blocking glasses? Yes
>
> If one is in an enclosed space are there any supplementary lights that will improve the situation? (I'm going to endeavor to work outside as much as I can, but my work won't allow that all the time.) The answer to this is as long as you want it to be. My suggestion is not to start with this as you need to do a lot more reading to assess your situation. Basically you need to revisit your timing questions above,get lamp specs, etc . Best option is to plan NOT to be in that space you mention
>
> OK ... I could go on. But probably best I stop there for now
>
> Thanks in advance to anyone who can improve my understanding.
>
> MJ

BTW - welcome !! Edit: Look in the quote above for my comments.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-22T05:19:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-many-questions-ill-start-with-a-few.20180/post-224106>

1\. Just do it as soon as the sun rises where you are.
2\. Yes. None are based upon vanity but on spectrum Look up Matt Marruca on my Dr. Jack Kruse FB page. His solution is solid.
3\. Limit it. Technology is a fool's assassin. The problem is the fool never realizes he is his own target.

The marketer tells you "You could have the world’s most important message, or greatest story, but no one will see it if you don’t promote and package it intelligently. Nature reminds you marketing entrepreneurs are dopamine junkies that have found their calling. Just realize what is good for them may not be optimized for your biology. This is why nature uses the quantum zeno effect and marketers tell you the opposite. Ponder why. Nature must hide her secrets from our senses because quantum mechanics requires it due to the observer effect for life's reality to manifest.

### Mark Joyner — 2017-09-24T02:39:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-many-questions-ill-start-with-a-few.20180/post-224214>

@[Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/), thank you! I'm figuring out how to work outside more often ... (it's a bit tricky for me as I work standing up ... and finding the right spot is a little tricky as well for various reasons, but ... yeah

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5/), very interesting! Any advice on duration for the morning sun gazing? As for the nature's use of the Quantum Zeno effect and how marketers are telling us the opposite ... I had to ponder this a bit, indeed. Are you implying to that a marketer's obsession with constant measurement - combined with their packaging of ideas into a perennially static presentations - is so counter to the ever-changing nature of the universe that it freezes them into some kind of unhealthy eigenstate? Thus marketing itself (as it is practiced by most) cripples human evolution?

### Billybats — 2017-09-21T14:53:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-a-cold-and-hungry.20182/post-224084>

Hi all,

They say feed a cold, well it must be true because I got a mild cold with nasal drip but more hungry then normal unsatisfying feeling and of course a little tired. Why am I so hungry. My stomach feels hungry but my body doesn't feel satisfied ( if there is such thing as a tapeworm that's what it feels like). Does a cold take more energy so I am using more nutrients. So basically what I am asking why so hungry when we get a cold.

bone broth....

# Thread: Here Comes the Sun...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 100
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Freader — 2017-09-22T06:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-224108>

"Information is a two edged sword that creates a quandary regardless of your mother tongue. An educated person is one who has learned that information almost always turns out to be at best, incomplete, very often false, misleading, fictitious, mendacious—just dead wrong eventually. Why do we seek it so much if this is the case? Because what we believe about information is false. Data is not information, Information is not knowledge, Knowledge is not understanding, Understanding is not wisdom. Only nature is wisdom..........embrace it." ----Jack Kruse

I was on my own journey that started 6.5 years ago, 2011, when I lost 65 pounds with HCG. I had struggled with my weight most of my life. I had given up. I was at a meeting with Dr. John Grey and he was talking about his book – Venus on Fire, Mars on Ice. During that presentation he mentioned HCG, I knew right away that was my answer.

I had lost 30 pounds with each of my 2 pregnancies. I was never ill, always felt great. It was the most “normal” I ever felt. I told both my OB-GYN and my Functional Medicine doctor that “ whatever these hormones were, I wanted to keep taking them”, they both laughed, but I meant it!!

So, December 2010, I told my Functional Medicine doctor, that I wanted to do this HCG and he told me the Clinic had just started their trials on it and that he had followed it on himself first and showed me his data and results. I love data and knew how I wanted to track my results.

I started April 1, 2011 and finished my second round September 17, 2011

My assumption was that I would need to exercise to maintain my weight loss and so to the gym I went and enjoyed it until the workout routines started taking 90 minutes to complete. I stopped. While there, I watched others that were working out to lose weight and didn’t. They worked hard and I thought – they are just stressing out their bodies! Exercise was not the answer. Along the way, I worked out so I could run the Berlin Marathon in September 2012. It was on my sister’s bucket list so I agreed to do it with her. I made it half way and had to stop because I was too slow. I felt great that I had achieved that at age 52. I meet some really awesome people that run marathons on a regular basis. It was good for them, not me.

So back to the Internet and Facebook to figure out the food side of weight loss. I had changed my eating habits substantially by doing the HCG Protocol. Gone was any fast food, sugar, fruit, starches and grains. I loved the meat protein, but still was avoiding vegetables. I started down the No GMO path. What was best to eat and what to avoid. (I still consider this info today). I wanted the quality of my food to be the best. My father provided pastured grazed Bison. I struggled with my weight and did more rounds of HCG. I found D.A. and started to drink BPC on April 23, 2014. I drank it everyday for breakfast I could until January 27, 2017. It kept me from hunger most of the day, but I basically stopped eating until the evening. Not the answer either. I gained 50 pounds back from following the BP Diet and doing Intermittent Fasting.

I thought the answer must be my in my hormones. They were making me fat. It wasn’t exercise or food. But no one had a clear answer. I loved Dr. Sara, but as I followed, she like many others I was following on Facebook and emails were “selling stuff” and I knew that Protein Shakes and Bars were not the answer either. I needed something I didn’t have to buy. I needed real food.

It took 20 months for me to regain all the weight I had lost and then I maintained that weight for over a year. Why didn’t I continue to gain weight? So I started eating starches, grains and sugar. Over the 12 months, I was maintaining, I gained 5 pounds. It wasn’t the food or the quality of it. It was something else. I needed quality sleep and the ability to get up in the morning feeling ready to tackle my day. I needed to stop staying up late watching TV and surfing the Internet.

I first encountered Jack Kruse at the D.A. BPC in Pasedena in 2014. At first I was in a different seminar room, and then I got the feeling –go to the other talk. I was amazed at his story and how straight forward he was with his info. I loved it when he said “ those idiots in the other room had no idea that what they were selling was horseshit”. It made me feel like I found someone that was going to be honest with me and tell me what I needed to know. I followed Jack out to the hallway when it was over, I stood 4 ft. away from him when he said “Boom” to Ruben Salinas. I watched the crowd around get larger and larger until the people on the outer edge couldn’t hear. We were there though the next cycle of talks and when they let out, we were a road block and were encouraged to break up and move on. I went to my next talk. I now know that Jack took his group across the street and continued for hours longer. When the conference tapes were released a couple of months later and Jack’s talk wasn’t’ included. I was pissed. It told me that what he had said really ruffled feathers and I knew that meant Jack was right! Jack’s talk was released separately because of “technical difficulties” the last part wasn’t recorded so Jack had to add it.

December 1, 2014 D.A's book was finally released. I had been so excited to get it, but when it actually came, I didn’t read it. I figured out 2 years later it was because I lost my Dopinine about 10 days before (Thanksgiving) That was when my energy for all of this disappeared. I had gained 40 pounds by then and was just done with it all.

I knew my weight issues had to be about hormones and Jack had the answer – It was about hormones!! I started to listen to Jack’s podcasts along with many others, I tried to understand his forum. I was never a blogger and found the whole thing tedious. I can’t tell you how much I appreciated all of the free stuff that was out there about Jack’s information. I respected someone that wasn’t “selling stuff”. He was over my head and I always thought I was pretty intelligent. My ability to remember isn’t that great and the more information I “retained”, the more I learned by re-listening to Jack’s podcasts. It is simple, Light, Magnetism, and Water. But so deep and there was so much information that I have accumulated over the years that I had to throw out of my brain! At my age, that’s a bunch! It would be easier to be young and learn what Jack was teaching first.

December 2015, I bought my first pair of blue blocker glasses and have worn them ever since. A few months later I bought them for my 2 children and they know I expect to see them wear them if they want to be on their electronic devices after sunset. At first, I found it made it easier to fall asleep and then I started to go to bed earlier and then it was easier to wake up in the morning and get up going. In the fall of 2016 I started to go outside in the morning to get sun in my eyes. I would roll down my window to get sun on my way to work. I like how simple and natural it was to do what Jack was recommending. But…

I was doing this all by myself. I really wanted a “health buddy”. I had questions, I wanted the conversation and debate that goes with idea exchange. I read all the Facebook posts on the Quantum page, I wanted to answer or share my thoughts but felt like I didn’t understand it well enough to contribute. Once, Jack said to get more sun, I challenged that and said I was fair skinned and burned easy, I couldn’t do that. He responded with, “So am I”! Blew me away. I had to figure this out. For the past 35 years I didn’t go out in the sun, I couldn’t tan, I would always burn, the sun was not my friend!! But Jack was of Irish descent, I was German, if he could do it, so could I. But how, he said “full solar exposure”. That meant naked. I had to think about that for a very long time…

Jack posted about going to Mexico, at a place I had never heard of. I had read about a cruise from the year before and I wanted to go, but why? I asked my husband, who goes to Cozumel twice a year to scuba dive, if he wanted to go with me. This is a guy that says “No” first to anything I ask him. When I asked him, he said “Yes” with absolutely NO hesitation! I was amazed! OK, he would be perfect to take, I didn’t have to explain anything to him, he would hang out on the beach or at the pool and have a great time regardless of what ever I was doing. Done deal. I signed up and was really excited. Then he started to ask questions, what was this seminar, why was I going, did he have to go to any of the events….. I assured him, he was on his own and I was glad he was my roommate. I didn’t know the answer to those questions. Why was I going to Mexico if there wasn’t a planned agenda with meetings and events? What was really going to happen there? I signed up for a consultation with Jack. That was why I was going!

I was looking forward to talking to someone that could answer my questions. Why was I gaining weight, what did my lab reports say about me, what did I need to do to fix my metabolism. How did everything Jack spoke about pertain to me. Me, Me , Me!!

Without going into all the details about the January experience, let me say that I was afraid of the sun. I stayed in my room a great deal of the time. My consult with Jack wasn’t until my last full day there and he was very clear, I needed to get sunlight and start doing CT. Everything I was focused on was steps 5 and 6. I needed to start at steps 1 and 2. That made total sense to me. That is why what I had been doing wasn’t working for me. Everything I tried wasn’t successful over the long term. I had always known that solving the “Root” cause was the purpose, however, what was that? I knew that I had to figure out how to make peace with the sun and embrace it. Maybe it was the solution to my situation!!

I love information systems by nature; I have spent most of my life in front of a computer monitor. Since I graduated college a long time ago, I have been around computers and electronic devices and had given up on any sun exposure. I have burned myself so badly as a young person, I had reconciled that the sun and I were not friends and I would avoid being in the sun as much as possible. Now what was I going to do?

I knew that Vitamin D was important and had heard about an App called “Dminder”. I used it to help guide me to sun exposure. It was January 2017, and 45 degrees outside. I knew I had to be outside and grounded to get the max effects and luckily, I found the perfect place. Right out side our walk out basement, on the concrete, there was a windbreak and I could lay out with my fully exposed solar panel!! I started on the weekends because I needed to lay out for so long due to the low UVB available, but I did it. Every weekday that I could go home for lunch I did it. It was working; I could see the color in my very white skin start to change color. The areas that I had previously burned tanned first. The other parts were much slower. I would listen to Jack’s podcasts while I lay out and that only reinforced why I was doing this and to keep going!

I went back to Mexico to see Jack in March. This time, my husband was fully engaged. He had seen the changes I made and how dedicated I was to improving my health. He listened and retained what he heard. Now, he is getting up with me to see the morning sunrise, we watch the evening sunset together. It is for me an awesome experience. Even if he is only doing it for me, he is all in!! I love him for it and I love that Jack touched him in a way I have never seen anyone do! The possibility that we can share this for the rest of our lives is mind blowing.

I have continued to get sun as often as I can. The Dminder App is awesome!! Wherever I travel, it knows what I need to know to not burn.

I have only had 2 minor burns during this year. I get sun while I'm grounded. The biggest issue is the bugs and spiders. I get bitten, it hurts. I'm just hoping that I'm building up some immunity to them!

I have made peace with the Sun. I'm not afraid of it anymore. I understand that it is the source of life on earth and without it, we die.

Info:
Mom died at 60, for me that is in 3 years. She had by-pass surgery at 55. She was diabetic.

Dad died at 85, 7 months ago, he stayed in the hospital too long after by-pass surgery. I miss him everyday.
I read John Ott's book while he was in ICU.

I have been wearing Blue Blockers since Dec 2015. Best decision I ever made!! I buy them in quantity and pass them out regularly to people that say they have problems sleeping or are tired all the time.

I have a Smart meter outside my office wall with electrical going over my desk. (this will be removed in the next 4-6 weeks!!!)

I thank Jack and Sandra for their sacrifice to help so many people on their Journey to health!!

My Halotype info:
J1b1a2
C677T C/C Neg
A1298C C/A Pos

Where should I live?
What else do you want to know?
What Mito Hacks do you recommend?!!

### caroline — 2017-09-22T09:35:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-224112>

Hi Freader and welcome.

Where do you live? how is your sleep?

Do you have a tri meter to measure nnEMF in your environment?

What about your grandma's health?

where should you live???? that's a million dollar question for a lot of us!

How did you feel in Mexico?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-22T16:59:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-224124>

Awesome Freda.........I remember that day well.

### Freader — 2017-09-22T17:01:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-224125>

Hi Caroline,
I sleep great compared to 2+ years ago. I do have a tri meter that I carry in my purse. My environment has spots and I avoid them. The issue in my office is being fixed and I will put down ceramic tile so I can ground during the day while at my desk. My father's mother lived a healthy life until 86. My mom's mom died when she was 70. Mexico is in the same Time Zone as Kansas and I felt great being there. I will go as often as I can.

### Freader — 2017-10-23T05:42:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-226418>

Today was a good day. This is the first day the ground was cold and wet when the sun came up. The leaves are still on the trees and so I continue to “chase” the sun through the branches. Probably will be 2 more weeks until they are gone again until next April. I try to get 10 minutes and find a video on You Tube that I can use to keep me company. It will be more difficult as it gets colder, the shoes will stay on or I'll come in sooner.

After cleaning up and throwing away a bunch of clutter, I laid out for 80 minutes at midday. The UV is only 4 now. I really thought I was done with bugs and spiders but there is something that bites like a mosquito still out here.

Went to the Mall to get my feet massaged at the Oriental Massage place for an hour. That was the best! Stayed for a half hour back massage. My back is a mess and I really need to get the chiropractor. Everything is so tight.

Listened to Jack’s Q&A. Logged in late because I stayed for the additional massage. So I missed the part about the farm. Can't wait for the details.
I decided to take a hot bath with my red lights to relax after my massage. Then a cold shower to cool down. It is much easier to take a cold shower when you body is hot! Then I headed back to work to pick up my Quantlet I forgot to bring home. Still listening to the Q&A. While driving the Cooling Vest was being discussed and I wonder about my memory because when I got home to look it up, I couldn’t remember the name.

Will it be easier to wear the vest in the cooler months ahead? I think putting cold on my fat is best.

This weekend I decided to get a whole new bed frame setup. My top mattress is more than 10” thick plus the memory foam and that is too thick for the Magnetico. I bought a 10 gauss twin pad for my daughter in college, but when it came in I realized it wasn’t going to work in her dorm so I put it on my side of the our King bed. I have been sleeping alright since I started wearing blue blockers at night and getting morning sun, so I couldn’t tell a difference. I’m going to get a Purple Platform Base steel frame, put a 20 gauss King size Magnetico on that, a Samina bed system on top of that with out the grounding pad. I’m hoping that the Samina System that Luke Storey promotes will be better for my back.

What do you need to know whether morning coffee is ok for me? I have been drinking coffee every morning since June 2006. Also I enjoy my Malbec wine at night, but I'm getting indigestion while I'm sleeping. Is there a time frame from last drink until you go to bed?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-10-23T06:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-226419>

Le Mademoiselle brought me my first café de matin today. She'd made it with straight London tap water, and I almost spat it out, it was so disgusting compared to the shungited gear I'd been imbibing for the past week.

She asks:
Why did George Washington have dentures? What was going on in his time to cause that? Why is this not an issue today?
Wifi - why does this harm us, but George's teeth was ok? What is different? Are we less healthy than they were that they needed wooden dentures?
Is Chlorine safe? Why is it toxic if it is safe to clean our water?

TV is suppose to make us smarter. It gives us life experience with out having to go through it. There is so much out there, why do we keep making the same mistakes over and over?

Because we are Catholic......
Venial sin. I just recently had the most enlightened conversation.... Don't think about sinning!!!! Stop it!!

The Muslims won the Crusades!! (I didn't know this)!!
1099-1291 we lost the Holy Lands. Google says so... I asked.. What happened since then that Jews/Christians live there... Democracy was her answer.

i've always encouraged Kham Academy. so much resistance. but. Saliadin takes the Holy Land.
He (Saliadin) had his own soldiers the went up into the mountain to collect snow to bring down to the enemy(people left behind) for fresh water for them to live.
Yes DDW for the save!! ( I'm hoping).

Jesus...If there is a devil, there must be God. with good there is equilibrium.

### Freader — 2018-03-28T05:54:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-238089>

Spring Break

Had a great time with my family in PDC. We were up every morning to watch the Sun rise. Pelicono’s for breakfast 3 mornings. Layed out in the sun every morning until 11. UV was 7 at 11:00am, which is the same as it is in Kansas at Solar Noon now. UV got to a high of 12, which is as much as it gets in Kansas in July. I love the DMinder App. It has opened my trust to be in the Sun, I have never burned using it. My forehead is the exception. I have a golden tan that will probably never get any darker, if it does, that will be the first time in my life!

Ate seafood as much as possible, Lobster, oysters, crab, crab legs. Friday night went to the Brazilian Steak restaurant. We ate so much meat with awesome juicy fat. Never had such an awesome meat experience. (I’ve been to Brazilian restaurants in the States but they were nothing to compare to this one). The local Seafood restaurant we tried from Chip’s recommendation was great food, lots of food and would definitely go back.

Every morning I was the one up and getting every one else up. This would not be the case 2+ years ago. I wouldn’t be able to get up. Since I started wearing Blue Blockers and getting up to watch the sun rise in the mornings, I sleep well and I can get up and get going like I never have since college.

Reread the Epi Paeolo Rx and the sections on CT. My daughter suffers from Amplified Nerve Pain and I would show her sentences to reinforce that I wanted her to try it. The spa had a cold plunge pool – 60 degrees. She got in it and said, ‘this is cold”. I thought she might try to get out, but she didn’t. I got in with her and we stayed in there for 20 minutes, only getting out because she wanted to try the sauna before her massage. Blow me away! Before that I had maybe done 3 minutes. I found that after the first few minutes, it was pretty easy to stay in. My daughter sang with her beautiful voice as a distraction at first and then we just talked. I hope that she continues to try CT as a pain management tool.

It was windy and the waves pounded the beach. I waded in to my thighs each day. I wanted to swim, but the rocks left me with a bruised purple big toe back in January and it didn’t take very many steps to rip some skin off my toes this time. One day I went ahead and sat down after a wave took me out, and ended up with a pound of sand in my swim pants! Jellyfish let me with some bites, big red spots that blistered on my hand, arms and thighs. Hopefully next time I go the water will be calmer.

The disappointment was that we didn’t try the Cenotes. I think staying in Cancun will make that an easier event to try next time.

I appreciate Jack’s encouragement to spend more time in Mexico and I will probably go a few more times this year. Our 20th Anniversary is in October and I hope that’s on my hubby’s list of places to go.

### drezy — 2018-03-28T15:29:56+0300

Here is to a golden Freader. I was going to say Golden Girl, but that term is laded with other meanings...

> I sleep well and I can get up and get going like I never have since college.

This is what my wife experienced. Be careful because my wife can still break it for now and it takes her a couple weeks to gain back.

> I thought she might try to get out, but she didn’t. I hope that she continues to try CT as a pain management tool.

It's incredibly humbling to see a loved one even start to try some of these techniques. If your daughter does choose to continue with CT keep in mind that air cryotherapy is big these days but water does such a better job it's not even funny. I've collected notes from people on this forum for a year and built my own system on the cheap. I'd be glad to share notes with you if you or your daughter ever want to construct a system if you ever go down that road.

> ended up with a pound of sand in my swim pants!

It's almost not a day at the beach without that. I just consider it a free derrière microderm abrasion. That would cost you in a spa.

> Our 20th Anniversary is in October and I hope that’s on my hubby’s list of places to go.

Hey congratulations. My wife and I are 6 years behind you. I let her know that "tomorrow is looking good" and that "we'll definitely make tomorrow" in reference to our marriage on a daily basis.

### Freader — 2018-04-17T17:17:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-239508>

How is the Sun tied to dehydration? For the past 7 years, I've been using a scale that measures body weight, water, fat, bone, visceral fat and muscle, I can't get my body water % over 45% it is usually 38-41%. Isn't it suppose to be 55%+? My skin is closer to dry than normal, I do not use lotions. I do not take daily showers. I'm assuming your going to tell me that electronics are microwaving the water out of my body, along with the coffee and wine. If I drink more water, I just pee more. Currently I'm drinking Preventa (talk about expensive pee).
I'm also going through a phase of vertigo (dizzy light-headedness). It started about 10 days ago. It is worse in the morning. I know it will eventually go away, because it has before. (It started after a head injury at a Party 6 years ago) Is that tied to Cortisol?
The Sun has been covered up for the past few weeks, yesterday and today it is back. I go out at sun rise and stand in the grass barefoot, I'll go back out for a while until I leave for work. Lunch, I'll come home to lay out and then back to work and try to be home for sunset. I will work at going outside more during the day.
I really expected my Cortisol to be great. I get up in the morning easily, that is a huge improvement from the past 25 years when you couldn't get me up for a fire. I usually have what I used to refer to as hot flash some where between 4-6 and I have assumed that is my Cortisol waking me up. Please help me connect the dots!

### JMO — 2018-04-21T20:08:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-239656>

> [Freader said:](/goto/post?id=238089)
>
> Spring Break
>
> Had a great time with my family in PDC. We were up every morning to watch the Sun rise. Pelicono’s for breakfast 3 mornings. Layed out in the sun every morning until 11. UV was 7 at 11:00am, which is the same as it is in Kansas at Solar Noon now. UV got to a high of 12, which is as much as it gets in Kansas in July. I love the DMinder App. It has opened my trust to be in the Sun, I have never burned using it. My forehead is the exception. I have a golden tan that will probably never get any darker, if it does, that will be the first time in my life!
>
>
> Ate seafood as much as possible, Lobster, oysters, crab, crab legs. Friday night went to the Brazilian Steak restaurant. We ate so much meat with awesome juicy fat. Never had such an awesome meat experience. (I’ve been to Brazilian restaurants in the States but they were nothing to compare to this one). The local Seafood restaurant we tried from Chip’s recommendation was great food, lots of food and would definitely go back.
>
>
> Every morning I was the one up and getting every one else up. This would not be the case 2+ years ago. I wouldn’t be able to get up. Since I started wearing Blue Blockers and getting up to watch the sun rise in the mornings, I sleep well and I can get up and get going like I never have since college.
>
>
> Reread the Epi Paeolo Rx and the sections on CT. My daughter suffers from Amplified Nerve Pain and I would show her sentences to reinforce that I wanted her to try it. The spa had a cold plunge pool – 60 degrees. She got in it and said, ‘this is cold”. I thought she might try to get out, but she didn’t. I got in with her and we stayed in there for 20 minutes, only getting out because she wanted to try the sauna before her massage. Blow me away! Before that I had maybe done 3 minutes. I found that after the first few minutes, it was pretty easy to stay in. My daughter sang with her beautiful voice as a distraction at first and then we just talked. I hope that she continues to try CT as a pain management tool.
>
>
> It was windy and the waves pounded the beach. I waded in to my thighs each day. I wanted to swim, but the rocks left me with a bruised purple big toe back in January and it didn’t take very many steps to rip some skin off my toes this time. One day I went ahead and sat down after a wave took me out, and ended up with a pound of sand in my swim pants! Jellyfish let me with some bites, big red spots that blistered on my hand, arms and thighs. Hopefully next time I go the water will be calmer.
>
>
> The disappointment was that we didn’t try the Cenotes. I think staying in Cancun will make that an easier event to try next time.
>
>
> I appreciate Jack’s encouragement to spend more time in Mexico and I will probably go a few more times this year. Our 20th Anniversary is in October and I hope that’s on my hubby’s list of places to go.

Freda...loved your update. Made me think back to December and the great time we had. Love getting up early and having it warm outside. Still working on hubby for the end of May....35 years. The sun in Nebraska has been non-existent & it even snowed last weekend...so yes I'll take Mexico in May.

Yes. Non-Existent Sun, but no rain! I also believe that a year ago, the leaves on the trees were full, currently we only have buds. Crops are in dire need of moisture!

I had scheduled to take my oldest to PDC in May after her finals, but my Aunt turns 80 in Germany and so it is postponed. I hope you both get to go for your anniversary - Congrats!!
