### Freader — 2018-05-07T18:07:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-240715>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=239767)

Thanks Jack! I remember discussing this and started to discover them and didn't find a connection. The vides about the bands history was very interesting and makes the music have more meaning. I have added Pink Floyd to my iTunes.
I have to ask, is it the band, the music, the lyrics? or is it what changes your perspective while listening to music? "Another brick in the wall" was the only song I knew, however, many of their songs have been weaved in the fabric of our culture since their release and I recognize them. I have always enjoyed--Billy Joel, Elton John, Barry Manilow, Carpenters, ABBA, Eagles, early Beatles, and I'm stuck in the 70's. As much as music was a big part of my life growing up, it isn't today and I miss it. I find I'm listening to webinars and other forms of learning (Facebook) with the TV in the background. I'm guessing "comfortably numb" means letting go and being more present and spiritual about listening. That requires a commitment of time. Morning sun gazing is the perfect time slot for me to do this.

### Freader — 2018-05-07T18:32:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-240716>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=239767)

The best quote from the video for me was
"I think that most of the songs I've ever written, all pose similar questions. Can you free yourself enough to be able to experience the reality of life as it goes on before you and with you, and as you go on as part of it or not? Because if you can't, you stand on square one until you die. That is what the song is about.....
Encourage yourself not to accept a lead role in a cage. But to go on demanding of yourself that you keep auditioning for the walk-on part in the war. Because that is where I want to be. I want to be in the trenches. I don't want to be at headquarters sitting in some hotel room somewhere. I want to be engaged in a way my father would approve of."
That spoke to me like a brick wall!! Purpose in life, using the gifts that God has given us. Focusing on that and not what is expected of us by others. Managing that transition and staying the course. Living a life that you would want to live again.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-05-07T19:33:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-240725>

You're beginning to see why I turned you onto them. You and Randy have become comfortably numb in Kansas. Most things are OK to good.......but they are not yet Optimal.

To view things, you have to look for them. To view things as nature does, you have to know HOW to look for them. Most people like lack this critical ability. Any fool can know things. The point is to understand how it fits in the grand scheme of how nature operates. If she is hurting your head may be the way you think about Mother Nature is not as good as it could be for you?

**Almost any disease/problem is curable.…… Just not every patient is because they refuse to alter their environment to suit their mitochondrial genome.**

You cannot get well in the same environment you got ill within. **It’s a stark and unpleasant message that most people don't want to ponder. I understand environment as firstly the macrocosm, secondly the microcosm. Worlds within worlds, Wheels within wheels. Waves within particles, particles within waves.......very much Gilmour music.**

Most people really don’t want the truth, they just want constant reassurance that what they believe is the truth. My mouth is not a bakery. I don’t sugarcoat anything. If you ask me my opinion I’m going to tell you the truth, no matter what you want to hear. That is how you help people who remain unaware of what they don’t know is the most important part of the recipe.

With you Freda, I see a caterpillar.
Just when the caterpillar thought her world was ending, she turned into a butterfly.........I see the metamorphosis in people before they do because of how I think about them and about things they chose.

### Freader — 2018-06-10T16:38:43+0300

### Freader — 2018-07-23T01:20:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-246478>

So much to catch up on.
June I spent a week with my daughter in PDC. I’m so glad I decided to leave a couple of days earlier because the last half of our week, it rained most of the days and were cloudy, even at sunrise. We had great bonding time. I learned that the UVB wasn’t much higher than our local UVB, but the temperatures were cooler 10-15 degrees. So summer isn’t a bad time to go. It’s cheaper and not as busy. The cold tub at the spa still feels great! We spent a day with Senior Cruz and did 2 Centoes. I was able to tolerate the 68 degrees very well, but my daughter and another lady in our group got cold and numb. It reminded me of the caves in Branson, Mo that I had been in when I was 12.

July, my other daughter and husband and I went to Germany for a wedding. Wonderful temps, the light cycle is much longer and spent time walking and eating 3 meals a day. Didn’t gain any weight, actually usually don’t when I go there. Even with all the beer and food we eat. Amazes me how that works. Every morning spent great time not in the sun but in great light. The have a cold foot bath walk that was originally built back in the 1930’s, feed by a Spring. It was taken over by a Nature group and restored. My aunt, Frieda, took me and my sisters there when we went in May and I was able to get 4 other of my relatives to go there with us this time. My dad had shown this to me back in 1982 and I really wished he could have seen it restored. I always get in the sun the first morning I’m there and that takes care of jet lag, plus drinking beer and wine makes going to sleep easier!

The bugs are back. Even in the morning, just walking in the grass, everything itches. My arms are full of sores from bites. Interesting, I lay on a cotton towel directly on the concrete to sunbathe, the parts not on the towel get bitten the most and sometimes the bites swell up and get inflamed, but go away in a week. I can’t see what is biting me. I clean the area before I lay out. So yesterday, I spayed bug spray on the concrete around me, helped a lot, but there are mosquitoes and other flying things. I have Type O blood and I’ve read that is supposed to attract them. I have heard that taking high doses of Vit B makes your sweat unattractive to them, but that isn’t an option. My husband doesn’t get bitten and he is outside much more than I. Why? Why? This has plagued me all my life.

My only guess is that,(like a Type 2 diabetic), I am releasing more light than I should and that I need more IR light in the morning. Or can I use my Red Lights instead, because being out in the morning isn’t working at this time.

### drezy — 2018-07-23T01:49:36+0300

> [Freader said:](/goto/post?id=246478)

> I can’t see what is biting me.

If I had to guess it could also be these little invisible critters that live in Texas too:
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trombiculidae](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trombiculidae)

A non-deet way to fend them off is to either pre-dust the area with sulfur powder the night before OR sprinkle it on the parts of your body closest to the ground (which is most of you if you are laying).

LDL was 134, now 117. HDL Ration was 3, now 2.4. My doctor GYN, wanted to put me on blood pressure meds before these results came back and I told him NO. I wouldn't take them. ### Freader — 2018-10-14T20:01:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-253133>

We had planned to spend our 20 year Anniversary, next week, near Navarre Beach, FL. As I see the Hurricane Michael pictures, my heart hurts for all those affected. Our honeymoon was extended for 3 very stinky days because of Hurricane Mitch. Weather can change your life in a day!!

Just returning from Germany, the days are noticeably shorter, cloudy days make them even more so. It is colder and I'm just plain "out of sync' with my routine. I'm glad that the sun was out yesterday and I was able to layout. I haven't done that for 3 weeks. The longest time I've gone without full sun exposure since I started in January 2017. I did stand in the sun when possible in Germany, but it wasn't much.

I've found some thin stretch knit "face shields" that I am able to use to cover my neck and protect my Thyroid that I will wear to work and at night I can use 2 of them to cover my forehead, neck, and chin so I can watch TV and be on my computer after dark. My favorite find is an amber light that attaches to a 9-volt battery and has a high/low setting. It allows me to move around the house without having to turn any lights on. My goal of getting the light bulbs changed out wasn't met because of resistance from the other lives that live here. I've taken out one of the bulbs from the fridge and no one has complained!! Where possible, I've been adding a black light to any cluster of lights.

I started using a 10 gauss Magnetico Pad on my side of the bed in August. I placed it under 4" of mattress toppers. My sleep had been good, so I didn't really notice a difference. In September, when it started to be cooler at night, I added a "Solaris Health Blanket" to the top of my covers. (healthboss.org) Now, I sleep well, but not as long. I wake up earlier and I'm ready to go. The problem for me is that it is dark and everyone else is asleep. So I will cover up and watch TV with my red glasses, but I need to do something else. What do you do before the sun comes up? you can't eat or exercise or work. Should I try staying up later at night? In a few weeks when Standard Time comes back will help a little but not for long. I've spent my adult life in search of more morning sleep and now I'm not!! HUGE!!

Next week I go back to the doctor for the blood in the urine issue. Since July, each test has been positive. Not worried, just wants to rule out any cancer polyps in bladder or kidneys. CAT scan and scope.

Looking forward to Mexico in November. Today it is so cool, I just want to get in the hot tub. Thinking about laying on the beach feels so good!

> We had planned to spend our 20 year Anniversary, next week, near Navarre Beach, FL. As I see the Hurricane Michael pictures, my heart hurts for all those affected. Our honeymoon was extended for 3 very stinky days because of Hurricane Mitch. Weather can change your life in a day!!
>
> Just returning from Germany, the days are noticeably shorter, cloudy days make them even more so. It is colder and I'm just plain "out of sync' with my routine. I'm glad that the sun was out yesterday and I was able to layout. I haven't done that for 3 weeks. The longest time I've gone without full sun exposure since I started in January 2017. I did stand in the sun when possible in Germany, but it wasn't much.
>
> I've found some thin stretch knit "face shields" that I am able to use to cover my neck and protect my Thyroid that I will wear to work and at night I can use 2 of them to cover my forehead, neck, and chin so I can watch TV and be on my computer after dark. My favorite find is an amber light that attaches to a 9-volt battery and has a high/low setting. It allows me to move around the house without having to turn any lights on. My goal of getting the light bulbs changed out wasn't met because of resistance from the other lives that live here. I've taken out one of the bulbs from the fridge and no one has complained!! Where possible, I've been adding a black light to any cluster of lights.
>
> I started using a 10 gauss Magnetico Pad on my side of the bed in August. I placed it under 4" of mattress toppers. My sleep had been good, so I didn't really notice a difference. In September, when it started to be cooler at night, I added a "Rejuvenator Blanket" to the top of my covers. (bioenergyclininc.info) Now, I sleep well, but not as long. I wake up earlier and I'm ready to go. The problem for me is that it is dark and everyone else is asleep. So I will cover up and watch TV with my red glasses, but I need to do something else. What do you do before the sun comes up? you can't eat or exercise or work. Should I try staying up later at night? In a few weeks when Standard Time comes back will help a little but not for long. I've spent my adult life in search of more morning sleep and now I'm not!! HUGE!!
>
> Next week I go back to the doctor for the blood in the urine issue. Since July, each test has been positive. Not worried, just wants to rule out any cancer polyps in bladder or kidneys. CAT scan and scope.
>
> Looking forward to Mexico in November. Today it is so cool, I just want to get in the hot tub. Thinking about laying on the beach feels so good!

There is a return magnetic field that exists around all edge of the pad that is the opposite of the magnetic field that the Sleep Pad is designed to give you while you sleep. If the pad is too small, you could be exposed to this magnetic field, undoing all the benefits the Sleep Pad can provide.

From the Q&A on their website they say:
1\. While it’s required to have a minimum of 4 inches of mattress (or other material) between you and the Sleep pad, as you get farther away the magnetic field will get weaker. All gauss measurements we’ve provided on our website note that those magnetic strengths are based on an 8 inch mattress.
2\. Metal springs in a mattress will have no impact on the function of the Magnetico Sleep Pad. A metal bed frame is also OK.
3\. We always insist that you only use a Sleep pad that matches the same size as the bed in order to avoid exposing the body to the return magnetic field.
Hope this clears everything up for you!

But they also say that their pad work with an adjustable bed and I wouldn't choose to sleep on a bed that is plugged into an outlet!!
They have a 6-month guarantee and I should know how well it works by seeing what it does for my hubby. The bed frame I use with the Magnetico is all wood, no toxic glues or finishes. I decided it was better not to sleep on metal springs or frames long before I had a few credible reasons to explain it. I used a compass to look at the fields around the Magnetico. It reversed directly below, not above the sleeping side. The sides seemed to have little compass effect a couple inches distant, while the 'field' registered maybe 3 feet above. Couldn't see the fall off under the pad as the bed is not all that high off the floor.

Since my big old dog died recently, I took in a small rescue dog and decided to keep her. This dog has a very favorite spot under the Magnetico bed. I wondered if this was healthy for her. I try to coax her out. Later, when we're more melded in a relationship, I was planning to fill up the space under the bed so she won't use it. I'd like some certainty on health effects for a dog curling up in that reversed 'return field' under the Magnetico. Thanks for the discussion.

They said that the magnetic field goes through the metal springs and frame, they do not have any impact on how the magnetic fields of the Pad work. They were not familiar with the blanket I'm using on top of the bed and as long as it wasn't electric or a grounded pad it was probably going to be fine, they would look into it and get back with me if it was a problem.

The head of our mattress is about 3ft. from a brick wall that doesn't have electricity running through it. The smart meters are at the other end of the house. I haven't removed all of the electronics in our bedroom, but the wifi is on a timer that shuts it off at night. I will make sure my computer and chargers are more than 3 ft away from the Pad.

We have cats and we have no idea what they do all day!!

### Synchronicity — 2023-12-07T02:22:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun.20187/post-328939>

> [Brent Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=253765)
>
> There is a return magnetic field that exists around all edge of the pad that is the opposite of the magnetic field that the Sleep Pad is designed to give you while you sleep. If the pad is too small, you could be exposed to this magnetic field, undoing all the benefits the Sleep Pad can provide.

Hi Brent, thanks for sharing your experience using a Magnetico. I have a couple Qs about your experience with the return positive field. I noticed you have your bed and Magnetico on the 2nd floor and elevated ~3' above the floor; is the distance above the floor to increase the distance from the the first floor occupants? I also noted you spend most of your days outside, so perhaps you avoid the return positive field altogether. I'm wondering if you've ever noticed the return positive field on the 1st floor or on the 2nd floor and what it feels like? I work a couple days a week from home. I think I can position my bed so that I'm 8' feet away from my bed when I'm at my desk. I'm wondering if that might not be enough distance and a bad idea spending a work day in close proximity to a Magnetico. I see Magnetico mentions in their Q&A that 4-feet is a safe distance for electronic devices, and also mentions in their Q&A that at 6-feet there's 1.7g and that seemed odd since EMF guidance recommends less than 1 milligauss for safe exposure, and 1.7g would be 1700mg. I asked Magnetico about this and they sent me a spread sheet that shows readings for all the bed sizes, and depending on the mattress size, there's often a negative field extending upwards of 8' from the bed laterally. Curious to hear your thoughts. Thanks.

### Brent Patrick — 2023-12-07T04:32:37+0200

> Hi Brent, thanks for sharing your experience using a Magnetico. I have a couple Qs about your experience with the return positive field. I noticed you have your bed and Magnetico on the 2nd floor and elevated ~3' above the floor; is the distance above the floor to increase the distance from the the first floor occupants? I also noted you spend most of your days outside, so perhaps you avoid the return positive field altogether. I'm wondering if you've ever noticed the return positive field on the 1st floor or on the 2nd floor and what it feels like? I work a couple days a week from home. I think I can position my bed so that I'm 8' feet away from my bed when I'm at my desk. I'm wondering if that might not be enough distance and a bad idea spending a work day in close proximity to a Magnetico. I see Magnetico mentions in their Q&A that 4-feet is a safe distance for electronic devices, and also mentions in their Q&A that at 6-feet there's 1.7g and that seemed odd since EMF guidance recommends less than 1 milligauss for safe exposure, and 1.7g would be 1700mg. I asked Magnetico about this and they sent me a spread sheet that shows readings for all the bed sizes, and depending on the mattress size, there's often a negative field extending upwards of 8' from the bed laterally. Curious to hear your thoughts. Thanks.

> _**I noticed you have your bed and Magnetico on the 2nd floor and elevated**_

**"3' above the floor; is the distance above the floor to increase the distance from the the first floor occupants"**

Yes this is correct the distance between the magnetico on the second floor and the top of my head (standing)on the first floor is approximately 1.6 metres (5.24ft) and yes this is to create distance . Like you said , I also spend most of the day outside however the bedroom I sleep in is on top of the kitchen which I spend plenty of time in. I do not feel any effects from the return positive field when in the kitchen nor do I feel any real tangible effects when standing along side the bed (magnetico)

"**I see Magnetico mentions in their Q&A that 4-feet is a safe distance for electronic devices, and also mentions in their Q&A that at 6-feet there's 1.7g and that seemed odd since EMF guidance recommends less than 1 milligauss for safe exposure, and 1.7g would be 1700mg"**

Although I do not match these guidelines as stated by Magnetico regarding the 6ft I have not had any issues. Ultimately my own n=1 tells me to keep my set up the same as my sleep is very good and I wake up refreshed.

**"I'm wondering if that might not be enough distance and a bad idea spending a work day in close proximity to a Magnetico"**
Obviously keeping a distance from return positive field is important but this is highly individual based on time spent near the field and other factors such as time outside away from your desk.
Producing intercellular water is a natural faraday cage.
The Magnetico in my opinion works even better when you build your redox with Sunlight when grounded!

# Thread: mystery illness in neighborhood child...just wondering
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-illness-in-neighborhood-child-just-wondering.20188/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ValerieBee — 2017-09-22T16:13:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-illness-in-neighborhood-child-just-wondering.20188/post-224118>

A neighbor's child has a sudden medical condition that is baffling the docs right now. The initial dx was HSP-Henoch Schonlein Purpura, which is an inflammation of small blood vessels that occurs in children. He could barely walk. He was discharged then readmitted hours later because the left side of his face became swollen and he had abdominal pain. Now his fingers are peeling--it looks like he had been sunburned. All testing negative for whatever they're testing for so far. This info is vague because it's what my neighbor is posting on facebook.

I'm reaching out here because I'm wondering about the smart meters. Now I could be paranoid, or maybe I have a hammer so everything is a nail, but there have been a lot of illnesses over the last couple of years, since the smart meters were installed. Maybe I just wasn't privy to all this info before? After the smart meters my mother who had a good prognosis took a turn and passed away from colon cancer. 3 months later my son was diagnosed with cancer (now in remission). My dad who was always in stellar health now all of a sudden has a vascular disease and his legs are in constant pain and he has fatigue and he tells me he has to undergo a procedure so "docs can scrape out the arteries in my neck so I don't have stroke". Another family member has had constant colds and is now going to the doc because they found out her WBC is through the roof......

I'm still pondering the best way I can handle smart meter advocacy here in my state... honestly or if it's worth it to try to go to the legislators at all or just try to get out of Dodge. The meters are being installed with no opt-out. I've honestly been contemplating some sort of grassroots community health data collection (Erin Brockovich style).

So what say this community? Am I just now seeing all of this because it's my focus, or does this all sound like legit potential EMF exposure fall out? It does to me, and I feel like that dude in the Independence Day movie that was living in his van and even his children shunned him because he kept trying to tell the people about the aliens.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-22T16:50:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-illness-in-neighborhood-child-just-wondering.20188/post-224120>

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28883444](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28883444)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-22T16:51:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-illness-in-neighborhood-child-just-wondering.20188/post-224121>

[https://phys.org/news/2017-08-thought-impossible-scientists-liquid-thz.html](https://phys.org/news/2017-08-thought-impossible-scientists-liquid-thz.html)

Platelets RBC's and WBC's all swim in blood.......and blood is highly sensitive to nnEMF because it is magnetohydrodynamic fluid. THis is why I've been warning on Q & A 's to mitochondriacs to pay attention to weird things going on in zip codes when 5 G goes live.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-22T16:52:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mystery-illness-in-neighborhood-child-just-wondering.20188/post-224122>

### taiyang — 2017-11-19T14:09:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-chance-to-change-the-meme-on-breast-cancer.20195/post-228405>

i agree. i am doing this (minus exposing breasts) and am sleeping better. i will buy the special swim suits you mention that allow light through. literally every night i have a good sleep, i feel my developing cancer/brain neurodegeneration is further off (my mother died of breast cancer at 78 and i have very problematic brain fog with lack of sleep). i know that you are trying to simplify the message to make it more easily adaptable. but i feel your message has to been slightly more complex because if people just do the AM sun and don't get a result, they will stop. to make the message a little more accurate, i would tell people that you need seafood + hydration + being replete with certain vitamin/mineral substrates (iron, vitamin C? B6?) in order to sleep better. also, their sleep environment has to be absolutely dark and not too hot. have i listed the essentials here? i was going to get some sleep tracking device, but i decided not to have that nnemf on me at night. plus i can evaluate my sleep quality without it. the ultimate measure of a good sleep for me- 1) i did not need to remove sweat pants and socks in the middle of the night because of getting hot 2) i have no memory of tossing and turning in bed 3) i wake up in a good mood with no feeling of having had upsetting dreams 4) i wake up without an alarm clock. last night i met all of these criteria. yay!!!!!!!!

# Thread: Remembering Song Lyrics
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remembering-song-lyrics.20202/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2017-09-25T16:15:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remembering-song-lyrics.20202/post-224262>

Anyone else unable to remember the words to your favorite song? I used to marvel at people I would see singing along at concerts, then I realized everyone but me could.

As a child I had a fantastic memory and I remember on one test, in my mind's eye I could see the page in the book that had the answer and read it right off the page. Sometime between 13 and 18, I lost a large part of my ability to remember things but never realized it until years later. In college, I would walk out of lectures remembering almost nothing that was said, while my friends could quote it back almost word for word.

My favorite song is Moondance by Van Morrison and beyond "Well it's a marvelous night for a Moondance", I draw a blank even though I have listened to the song hundreds if not thousands of times. I don't even distinguish words as the song is playing, but hear the words as just part of the music.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-09-25T17:27:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remembering-song-lyrics.20202/post-224270>

I'm one of those weird people that remember song lyrics from songs I haven't heard in 30 years...

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-26T03:30:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remembering-song-lyrics.20202/post-224299>

It is easy to know the lyrics for the Rain Song or Stairway to heaven for me.

### Pogo — 2017-09-26T05:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remembering-song-lyrics.20202/post-224303>

Beats me, Huck. I struggle to remember the words to songs that I wrote myself, most of the time. Pretty much always had to have "cheat sheets" in front of me, on stage.

My partner, however, remembers EVERYTHING. Intimidating, but also very useful.

### Billybats — 2017-09-26T17:52:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remembering-song-lyrics.20202/post-224321>

Dang.. Me too can't remember words, some but my boyfriend he can quote movies and sing a song.. He is a BIG stones fan. My sisters are like him with music. Hey I am a better dancer.

# Thread: Hello from Switzerland and a mtDNA question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-switzerland-and-a-mtdna-question.20203/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bic — 2017-09-25T21:31:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-switzerland-and-a-mtdna-question.20203/post-224279>

Hi,
My name is Bic and I am a Texas boy living in Lucerne, Switzerland. I have been lurking the site since April of this year.I have tried to soak up much of the information here and have watched many of Dr Kruse's youtube videos. I have taken a lot of sun since then and dipped my toes into CT. I have MS (diagnosed 2011) and ITP (immune). I have been busy gathering up the information required to start a journal. I have a round of doctor appointments in the next few weeks so I hope to get some meaningful data to post here for members to utilize to help me conquer MS.
I was gifted an Ancestry DNA kit th { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

is Summer and I have the results. I have two variants of the MTHFR gene. It was not apparent to me as to what my haplotype is and after doing some Googling I uploaded my Ancestry data to WeGene and the result is L1'2'3'4'5'6. I am not sure what this means and I am asking for some help here. Here is the screenshot from the WeGene site.

I am 56 years old. 186 cm tall and weigh 120 kilos. My maternal grandmother died at age 77 from complications from a stroke and had dementia for several years prior to her death. My mother died a prolonged death, being bedridden for 7 years before her death. The diagnosis being
Shy-Drager syndrome.
My current treatment for MS is 900 mg Rituximab every 26 weeks. I am due for treatment next Tuesday, October 3 and will have some blood tests the day before and hope to post those results here on the forum. I also have an appointment with an Endocrinoligist later in October related to my tendency towards diabetes.

Thanks for taking the time to read this and feel free to chime in if you have any information to add.

Bic

### Da-mo — 2017-09-25T22:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-switzerland-and-a-mtdna-question.20203/post-224281>

Welcome Bic.

I will be following this thread as I have a workmate who has MS. He has a medical note so that he no longer has to do night shifts. Unfortunately his work still keeps him indoors under fluorescent lighting and surrounded by computers and wireless. He tries to get as much sunlight as possible but the work environment is taking its toll. Probably not a coincidence that he was diagnosed shortly after buying a 3 story building in the middle of the central business district and lives in it - more nnEMF to deal with.

All the best in your quest to get optimal.

### Bic — 2017-09-25T22:15:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-switzerland-and-a-mtdna-question.20203/post-224282>

Thanks for the reply. I forgot to mention I am the IT guy at a school with 70 wifi access points and a cell phone tower on the top pf the building. Florescent lights from the 1970s in my office.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-26T03:21:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-switzerland-and-a-mtdna-question.20203/post-224296>

With that story you must exit south to remedy your autoimmunity conundrum

### Bic — 2017-10-02T13:55:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-switzerland-and-a-mtdna-question.20203/post-224703>

Got some labs to post. Sorry, they are in German. Of note to me is that pretty much all of the labs are improved over the last several months. The labs show the values of 4 tests from March until now.
Vitamin D is up significantly with little to no supplementation, just glorious sunshine.

Any advice appreciated.

Labs from the Endocrinologist are still a few weeks out

# Thread: Reading a Bone Density Scan
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading-a-bone-density-scan.20204/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### CFAN — 2017-09-25T22:04:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading-a-bone-density-scan.20204/post-224280>

Hi!
So I have had a version of the bone density test done (not the one I wanted)... I had to push my doctors something fierce to even approve it. I am meeting with my rheumatologist in November to go over it and my symptoms. She thinks I have fibromyalgia, which does fit most of my symptoms...

but what does this pdf say about my bones... I don't understand it.

(in addition, I think the girl measured me wrong in height... she was about 5ft and couldn't touch the measurement bar well and I think she pulled it down more in the back for my height, but she was pushy and wouldn't redo it... so I think I am a 1/2in taller than she marked me as).

My main current symptoms:
1) A lot of muscle pain/tightness
2)hand weakness (no tremors, they just stop gripping things)
3) hair loss
4) height loss and twice breaking same ankle in 5yrs.
5) GI issues.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-26T03:04:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading-a-bone-density-scan.20204/post-224292>

Your bone is OK based upon that scan but I will tell you using the spine in people is bad idea because it is often falsely elevated by osteoarthritis so in my ladies I like wrist studies or full full body densitometry as I laid out in my book.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Amenorrhea advice 3 month hack Quintano Roo..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amenorrhea-advice-3-month-hack-quintano-roo.20205/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Innerfuego — 2017-09-26T01:22:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amenorrhea-advice-3-month-hack-quintano-roo.20205/post-224286>

**Quick facts:

Last period Jan ‘17

Initial problems with period started ‘10/’11 based on overtraining for sports “performance” (trained like a demon) coupled with eating more ‘paleo’ (I know, don’t shoot me down, I;m catching up on the blogs)...newbie apologies..

Lost a lot of weight during this time (not overweight to begin with)...and to point where my mum thinks I’ve eating disorder or something-not on my mind then, just too focused on training...this however probably raised my cortisol level association with food..

Went on the pill to start it up for a few months and then naturally it came back after but with irregularity.

Continued on this way since...period coming at irregular times.

General decline noticed in quality of skin (thin, dry, loose, wrinkly and more moles-have been attending dermatologist for mole mapping and she had one removed at Christmas last as a precaution due to change-but it was fine)...based on blogs I’m thinking pregnenolone steal (so dry brushing).

Receeding gums a little-put down to brushing, but really?! Dentist not asking questions

Hair is dry-falls out a lot but it’s still thick so I don’t know.

Normally struggle with cold (except when did Wim Hof course last Dec)

No proper relationships

Have moved location several times during this period...initially in US (2008-2011), then few months latin America, Spain 1.5 yr, Dubai 2 years, back in US 2017. Now here till mid-Dec.

Otherwise, grew up in Ireland till 18..white, pasty muppet...was outdoors a lot as a kid but sunscreen quite a bit. Definitely lived an outdoors life for the most part and not much tv & first phone when 13.

Mitochondrially speaking it gets rough around the edges:

Granny died of pancreatic cancer when old, but I never saw her in great shape, so I can’t tell how she was before that-raised 8 kids though. There is a lot of mental health/alcoholism etc in the siblings...which I didn’t really know about till recently (classic Irish fashion ha).

Mum was diagnosed with hypothyroidism several years ago and she’s skinny as a whip-apparently levels were so low they’re surprised she was still alive...so thyroxin meds for her, and a bit of osteopenia, so those tabs too. Then the whole “high cholesterol” debacle (she’s a cheese fiend), so has minimized that, and then drugs for cholesterol (both Mum and Dad). Then, about 2 years ago she has a mini stroke while abroad...neurologist didn’t do a great job as left her with a lot of doubt and STATINS of course (fadgery dadge)...and she experiences periods of breathlessness and generally no bueno symptoms.

Not a fan of how the doctors are back home and just seeing how they’re dealing with my parent’s cases makes me pissed off, added to not being at home very often and the associated expenses...hence, not having gotten labs done (which I know you won’t enjoy as a definitive marker of where I’m going), ….so you may give me the shunt but I figured 3 months investment here might help and then if still hitting the pervervial fan in December check out someone decent back home (thinking bioidentical hormone replacement and whatnot?)....

In a sense, it’s like prehab before surgery...if I strengthen my body now the results will be better.

APOLOGIES for not having the hard DATA right now, sometimes you just gotta FEEL it out.

SO, the goal is fix myself and then share nuggets that change her’s and others’ perceptions...but baby steps as I’m not even sure of myself but it’s worth the leap of faith!

So, in PDC as per right now…

Sunrise everyday=check

Normally in the water too up until 7.30/8am or walking the length of beach=check

Water-filtered rain water = check

Grounding like a mofo, more than any other time in my life = check

Dry skin brushing = check

Brief intervals during the day bikini exposure (2-20 mins) =check

Sardines in am(even though not a fan), with shrimp and veg = check

Some sweet potato carbs/plantains thrown in veg =check

Sweating well after breaky =check

Reading as much of blogs as my dopamine levels can handle right now = check

No sunglasses = check

Night time normally dark = check

Sleep is improving (had diarrhea first few days-but seems to be better)...haven’t gone to the loo last 2 nights (normally I urinate once per night in the middle)

OUESTIONS:

Sun dosages...prolong am sunlight most? Can be quite hungry at this time so come in earlier sometimes? Don’t want damage either with thin skin.

Leptin prescription worthwhile at this latitude for this first month (plus still scrawny)?

If urinating at night, any biohacks?

Was going to spend 1 more month here and then do some organic farming volunteering in the area (based partly on expense, plus it would be nice to learn from and hang out with people while connecting with nature-little bit isolated socially here so that aspect could improve, but less control of certain parts of my environment)...too soon?

When to implement more exercise=right now just a bit of foam rolling and posture work (functional patterns who introduced me first to your website)?

CT implementation worthwhile, or not till Ireland in December?

Will bathing in the sea increase my charge or just dry my skin out too much from the salt?

Peeing too much during the day, add sea salt?

Any other recommendations for these issues ladies?

Time to take charge of this n=1...THANKS for empowering the change...even if I fail, I will fail better next time...less of the half-truths...

Any advice is MUCH APPRECIATED and kudos for the amazing content!
**

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-26T02:55:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amenorrhea-advice-3-month-hack-quintano-roo.20205/post-224290>

Welcome. Let me digest this and come back sane. Just getting over some food poisoning.

### shah78 — 2017-09-26T19:38:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amenorrhea-advice-3-month-hack-quintano-roo.20205/post-224328>

Welcome to the Yucatan. you've lead the horse to the water, but the horse isn't staying in long enough!!!!.  Don't get out so early. I'll assume you're pasty white . Then buy the largest and cheesiest tourist sombrero you can find and a long sleeve shirt put zinc oxide on your nose and facial cheeks and stay in that aqua, baby. stay till ten, then add 15-30 minutes a day. then go make manana's meals and then get back in at around 4 pm and stay till sunset. first and last two hours of the day you can be out of the water and suck up the Yucatan IR "orgy". No human can burn in this time frame. there's no uv present . you'll be warm,but you won't burn. eat way more fish. CT as much as you can. and most important....... sleep in complete darkness. your sleep must get you ready for the intense sunlight exposure the next day. It's just like doing obsessive compulsive exercise. except you don't have to move, sweat, get injured and eat lot's of food. you just sit there and train your mitochondria to work better and allow every ailment to dissolve. let the "perfume of progreso" envelope you. good luck. p.s. eat in the water.eat all your meals in the water.

;3

### Hunger4More — 2017-09-27T15:21:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amenorrhea-advice-3-month-hack-quintano-roo.20205/post-224370>

> [Innerfuego said:](/goto/post?id=224286)

>

>
> CT implementation worthwhile, or not till Ireland in December?
>
> Will bathing in the sea increase my charge or just dry my skin out too much from the salt?
>
> Peeing too much during the day, add sea salt?
>
> Any other recommendations for these issues ladies?**

Based on my experience, to answer your questions:

\- AM sun most important. Afternoon sun (after 3pm) is the 2nd most important. Assess your tolerance to the UV around these times.
\- Leptin prescription is definitely not a bad idea. Helps entrain the body's circadian clocks.
\- Night time urination = poor melatonin levels. Need more sun + grounding, block blue light and EMF, especially after dark.
\- Organic farming: what's the environment like?
\- Foam rolling and posture work should be fine. Use your redox status to determine when it's a good idea to implement more intense exercise.
\- CT is always a good idea - though I found it difficult to execute in Mexico
\- Sea bathing: great idea
\- Peeing too much: add sea salt. Limit exposure to nnEMF
\- Other recommendations: get naked

Cheers!

# Thread: Big ass breakfast for an 8 til 5 worker...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sean Waters — 2017-09-26T13:06:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224316>

Hi everyone,

Looking for ideas on how I can reduce my Cooking time down so i can get to work earlier...

The new project I'm on is 1 hour 30-45 minute commute and I'm getting in about 9.30-45, and i'm meant to be there at 8. they don't mind 1 hour late as it is a far commute, but any more is considered a piss take.

At the moment, I make omelettes with frozen veg to reduce preparation, or fish curries, but still takes over an hour to prep and eat and let it go down. Then 45 minutes to do my daily essential CT shower and get ready and it's taking 2 hours to get ready.

I would get up earlier than 6, but my body seems to need to sleep from 9pm til 6am, to be fully optimal - i sleep like a rock its great unless its disrupted then I feel awful.

I've started just 24 hr fasting, skipping breakfast, to get to work on time, but this isn't Optimal. So I either find some quick BABs or I have a confrontation with manager's at work... I don't mind the latter, but i'd rather find a pragmatic solution myself before that happens.

I used to do protein shakes, but stopped for some reason, they aren't really optimal - but i'd consider some good recipes with lots of heavy cream etc. I love coffee too.

Anyone got any ideas or share their own quickly made BABs?...

Thanks

Sean

### Bic — 2017-09-26T18:19:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224322>

Hi Sean,
Cook more in the evening and eat it in the morning. Left over roast chicken and some veggies gently reheated for a few minutes, or leftover medium rare steak should get you where you need to go. If you are trying to up your fat from cream make a batch of say, Clam Chowder. Place it is a crock pot on high while you are CTing. Should be hot when you are finished.
Bic

### Sean Waters — 2017-09-26T18:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224323>

> [Bic said:](/goto/post?id=224322)
>
> Hi Sean,
> Cook more in the evening and eat it in the morning. Left over roast chicken and some veggies gently reheated for a few minutes, or leftover medium rare steak should get you where you need to go. If you are trying to up your fat from cream make a batch of say, Clam Chowder. Place it is a crock pot on high while you are CTing. Should be hot when you are finished.
> Bic

Why have I never heard of Clam Chowder before? That is absolutely perfect...

Yes agreed on chicken/ steak... I usually buy a Cooked Whole Chicken and fry up Onions, Broccoli and Mushrooms in butter to serve with it. This is one of my quickest meals! But it's not enough DHA for my liking... Plus, it's hard to store 5 whole chickens in the fridge lol...

The seafood meals like Prawn curry/ fish curry etc. take a long time to make and prep, reheating is ok but I don't want it to destroy the beneficial fatty acids by overcooking.

Clam chowder is agreat one though thank you!

Feel like a low carb sushi that I can make in a batch - like Smoked salmon, egg and avocado in a Nori sheet (1 g carb per sheet).

### JCDC — 2017-09-26T22:16:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224336>

I cook stuff in crock pot overnight all the time. Its perfect. Especially since BAB is supposed to be consumed within 30 min of waking, which means an hour of prep time in the morning defeats the purpose.

### Billybats — 2017-09-26T22:30:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224338>

This may be a dumb question. What do you all make in your crock pot. I know me personally I will be making beef stew soon when it gets a little colder, been craving it.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-27T00:06:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224342>

Whatever is in the fridge goes in and I make it work.

### shah78 — 2017-09-27T00:33:29+0300

### Billybats — 2017-09-27T01:23:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224347>

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=224343)
>
> roast chicken???????????? wtf!  speechless

Hi Shah,

Shah please don't take this the wrong way but some of us are slowly making changes (I guess you can say building up our dopamine). I personally tried doing jump right in approach but that made me worse, so I started with going out into the sun and grounding, some ct, and especially blue blockers along with controlling nnEMF help ALOT to were I gained more energy to get things done. The part was the least important on my list but eating in the right time fram helped too ( not even changing a whole lot food yet) Now I can focus more each change I make. Its baby steps for some of us starting out.

### kris90 — 2017-09-27T14:22:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224368>

I personally skip breakfast because I feel better fasted in the morning when I have to work, but here are some quick ideas for you:

\-Smoked salmon with cream cheese rolled up
\-Canned fish (salmon, sardines, mackerel, herring, oysters, mussels)
\-Pickled eggs and leftover cold bacon

Those are good cold food options. Nuts are great to have on the go too if they suit your needs.

### Penny — 2017-09-28T21:04:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224433>

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=224316)
>
> Hi everyone,
>
> Looking for ideas on how I can reduce my Cooking time down so i can get to work earlier...
>
> The new project I'm on is 1 hour 30-45 minute commute and I'm getting in about 9.30-45, and i'm meant to be there at 8. they don't mind 1 hour late as it is a far commute, but any more is considered a piss take.
>
> At the moment, I make omelettes with frozen veg to reduce preparation, or fish curries, but still takes over an hour to prep and eat and let it go down. Then 45 minutes to do my daily essential CT shower and get ready and it's taking 2 hours to get ready.
>
> I would get up earlier than 6, but my body seems to need to sleep from 9pm til 6am, to be fully optimal - i sleep like a rock its great unless its disrupted then I feel awful.
>
> I've started just 24 hr fasting, skipping breakfast, to get to work on time, but this isn't Optimal. So I either find some quick BABs or I have a confrontation with manager's at work... I don't mind the latter, but i'd rather find a pragmatic solution myself before that happens.
>
> I used to do protein shakes, but stopped for some reason, they aren't really optimal - but i'd consider some good recipes with lots of heavy cream etc. I love coffee too.
>
> Anyone got any ideas or share their own quickly made BABs?...
>
> Thanks
>
> Sean

Coffee shake with the following: 4 eggs / 1/2 can coconut cream / 4 brazil nuts / handful of almonds/ 1 gram potassium bicarbonate/ cinnamon / cocoa powder / cardamom / ginger powder (Morrocan shake) / turmeric / black pepper/ 2 tbl gelatin / 1/2 tsp glycine (adds sweetness) - mix up the spices the night before - load up your coffee pot the night before - should take less than 5 minutes to throw it all into a blender - that's over 40 grams of protein and enough fat to keep you going all day with zero hunger - for fast food, canned fish/oysters ( I eat a can of oysters every day) or smoked salmon in a cooler or make some hard boiled eggs up - get the DHA ones where the chicken eats flax - Trader Joes has dark yolk eggs that do the trick...

### Penny — 2017-09-28T21:05:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224434>

Sorry, chicken reduction is not part of the baby step cop out.  The"Baby steps" exemption  applies to difficult adaptions. Like sitting in 50 degree water for two hours. Switching from chicken- shit to seafood/ grass fed beef/lamb does not need "baby steps". You either never buy it again, or better yet throw out any you have in the fridge/freezer. I love chicken. If I'm wrong I don't want to be right

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-09-29T12:14:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224462>

If you were to come to my home and open my freezer, you would see a large bag of pastured organic chicken feet from my neighbor's farm that I use to make my bone broths. We drink our broth daily here.

### Linz — 2017-09-29T14:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224467>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=224451)
>
> We can get real chicken.
>
> I love chicken. If I'm wrong I don't want to be right

Are you sure? It is very difficult to find chicken with absolutely no imported ingredients in the feed, let alone fed on an appropriate local diet and kept in the right conditions if you don't keep your own hens.

### caroline — 2017-09-30T10:01:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224512>

what about pates and liverwurst and shrimp and cold steak and leftover salmon - all kinds of great options for a BAB - and a thermos full of BPC

### ScottishEmma — 2017-10-01T08:13:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224560>

> [Linz said:](/goto/post?id=224467)
>
> Are you sure? It is very difficult to find chicken with absolutely no imported ingredients in the feed, let alone fed on an appropriate local diet and kept in the right conditions if you don't keep your own hens.

Yes, I'm sure. And anyway, food isn't at all important, right? #shitonashingle

### caroline — 2017-10-01T09:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224572>

This morning I baked thick slices of pork belly. They are very yummy cold ....and I don't care much anymore anyway - too many things I want to do - don't have time to eat...

I had the pork belly 10 hours ago and still not hungry - so I will just have water and go to bed in awhile.

### Sue-UK — 2017-10-01T11:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-224587>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=224560)
>
> Yes, I'm sure. And anyway, food isn't at all important, right? #shitonashingle

My take is that food isn't important unless its important,  I can cope with the occasional shitonashingle if I'm leptin sensitive and my redox is good, (and its an occasional test ....) But in a low UV environment, the 1/3 electrons from food is more important. If my redox is crap then shitonashingle has a negative effect. I avoid chicken because I don't have easy access to a good source, and there's too many other better choices, and for my n=1 eating poor quality chicken crowds the better choices out. When I was leptin resistant, and now when I'm trying to maintain redox, giving it up was/is a low hanging fruit.

### Sean Waters — 2017-10-06T17:53:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-225158>

I made Chowder today which was great and didn't take long at all...

I've also noted down all the other suggestions, they were all perfect - thank you everyone.

I've literally never had chowder.

### caroline — 2017-10-07T10:59:21+0300

Clam chowder is delicious .....give it a go Emma!

### Jack Kruse — 2017-10-08T01:24:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-for-an-8-til-5-worker.20207/post-225236>

10/1/2017 Nobel for circadian biology......what does it mean for this post?

Nature doesn't sit down with you and start a discussion.........you need to remember how you were made to exist within her frame work and if you do not, disease is the result! Simple. Sunlight creates time for cells. That is part of nature's recipe. I am not interested in what any paradigm has to tell or sell as I am in how you choose to live and give first. That is the fundamental tenet I teach. It is the mitochondriac credo really.
We hate to be sold, but we sure love to buy in America and corporations use awareness campaigns to make $$$$. And this, my quantum friends, is how you can learn to "sell" natural sun light to your friends, family and clients, because everybody wants to buy more "time". The beliefs of modern man are the batteries that make them fuel for the matrix in which they live. You don’t need people’s expert opinions on a fact of nature, says Mother Nature. Light from the sun is tied to this idea. We’ve crafted an idea the sun is toxic for us and this is ludicrous....Life is designed to be as predictable as a pair of dice and this is why eukaryotes stole a bacteria and turned it into a mitochondria. It allowed cells to make better predictions using solar light to gain stability. You bury the sun you get modern chronic diseases. EVERYTHING that happens in ones life is because they tolerate it. Most humans today cannot face this reality. Ignorance to a solution is not exemption to reality because your conscious ability is tied to how a mitochondria responds. At the banquet table of nature, there are no reserved seats for any living thing. You get what you can take, and you keep what you can hold. If you can't take anything, you won't get anything, and if you can't hold anything, you won't keep anything. And you can't take anything without circadian organization. Simple. This is why I such a disdain for so many healhtcare experts on social media touting nonsense about food and exercise. If you do not get the light environment you live under correct nothing works in a cell. Inferior talent can only be graceful when it's carrying inferior ideas.

# Thread: Aleve and aspirin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aleve-and-aspirin.20208/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Billybats — 2017-09-26T15:20:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aleve-and-aspirin.20208/post-224318>

Hey All,

I read that aspirin uncouples and not sure if that is good or bad. Why is it when I take aspirin for a headache it makes it worse. I was a while back taking low dose 81 mgs everyday and had black and blue without pain when touched and on and off bloody nose and it did give me energy, but have stopped taking it. Now when I take aleve (for my occasional headache) it makes me sleepy and helps my headache pretty good (Ibpro stopped working for me). I do sleep really well when I take it. I wish I can use it for a little while just to have some good sleep. So why does this happen. What pathway does aleve take.

Thank you ahead.

# Thread: Mama Bear is out of the Den!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mama-bear-is-out-of-the-den.20210/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Leslie hamud — 2017-09-26T20:30:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mama-bear-is-out-of-the-den.20210/post-224332>

Hello Dr Jack Kruse and Friends,

I have been reading as much as I can since last January 2017. I have a blue light issue so time on the computer is limited. This is not new to me I remember my husband purchasing a large tv screen about 15 years ago and sitting in front of it I got a sunburn on my face. Not watched much tv since then. I did have a little back ground at the time with nutrition, so I started to put magnesium chloride on my face it did help but I generally do not watch tv.

A little background on me I have been a biohacking Mama for years and didn't know it! I have two boys who were on the spectrum, although my Mamma antenna was up years before the diagnosis's, I just kept my nose to the ground and did the hard work. My objective was, I don't care what we call it, I just need to learn how to fix it. They both had apgar scores of 9/9 and I just knew this was not an issue at the start. Either way they both had good eye contact, they new my voice, they did all the rooting responses to nursing. The oldest had difficulty latching on, the youngest did not. During these years mothers were not informed of vaccines at birth. My oldest came home content until about the 5th day then the neurologic scream set in for 24 hours. Little did I know. My youngest was fine the first 6 hours but then could not maintain his temperature. Little did I know. Both boys ended up with sensory integration issues and visual difficulties. Their auditory processing was very strong. I ended up homeschooling both of them and it was the best thing I ever did for them and our family.

We did the all the genetic testing back then ages 5 and 9. I got on the methylation approach although a lot of it didn't make a lot of sense to me. Diet up until this point was making big changes and I was ready to get back ice cream. I had just got back milk back into the diet, as in ice cream from grass fed cows. Their doctor wanted them of the milk but I had enough nutritional information to realize if the concern was the calcium then I could balance that out with enough magnesium. We did do a few protocols of the Dan approach etc but I honestly thought they were dangerous approaches because of the detox symptoms and the need to remineralize after. I experienced a very dangerous situation with the youngest after a session of detox and it sealed my opinion that it was not the best approach. My thoughts then and now are if the body wants to detox it will do it on its own, in the safest environment, with out our added help. One of my first Mama Bears experiences. I learned to trust my gut and go slow. Which we did. These are my first bio hacks early days with the boys. And boy many more ahead after this.

Some bio hacks, magnetic pads to sleep on, ground mats to sit on, color water therapy using colored glasses and lenses with water. (Jack your green bottle from Mountain Valley is what I would do but using colored drinking glasses) I found purple lens helped with math issues like multiplication, yellow helped with division etc. I also found certain microbes helped with math problems and reading problems as well. Just hanging out in the kitchen we were in the lab. Hexagonal water was always helpful magnesium baths were must in the evening. Grounding, out playing was a must and part of the curriculum, as we homeschooled and play was a big part of balancing out the frustrations. Now, knowing about light, it was the light influence of playing out side that was helpful. I had a rebounder in the hallways that was a must to jump on passing thru. Horse back riding, learning golf, climbing trees, piano lesson and art classes were a must to stay in balance. I also learned they couldn't handle toxins in stores very well. Shopping was out in any mall. The color white was to glaring to learn in and did I speak of the spectrum lighting that we soon learned was our better friend than most other bulbs out there. So the french manicures had to go and back to the natural nails was a must for Mama Bear. Changing wall colors helped out in learning as well. Avoiding difficult schooling times during the months of October November and December was best to have a lot of PE. Winter months were vey hard on learning and frustration.

So the kids are improving and learning at their own pace and Mama bear, me, starts to fall. Stress of doctors, school boards, coping, cooking everything from scratch, sprouting breads, making cheeses etc was taking a toll on me. At 38, I started to have thyroid issues got on some thyroid meds they didn't work. My legs inflamed quickly after about 3 months I dropped the medication and decided meds and me are not a match. Just like aspirin and other meds I had taken in the past, I react opposite to and sometimes I would just fall asleep for hours.

Back to the drawing board. I too was eating much better along with the kids. Organic, low to no sugar, grass fed beef and wild caught fish. I wasn't to concerned about mercury, I grew up on fish and felt the company I was buying fish from was doing a good job at their selection and delivery. Vital Choice is my choice... I later became a holistic practitioner in nutrition to understand why food was important. I must admit I wasn't crazy about suggesting vitamins, even as nutritionist. I wanted to get to the island of food. I still feel the same today. Vitamins are a bridge and should only be used if nec temporarily. Its better to understand why you might need them and then look for those nutrient in food. Today, I would add sunlight!!! I have studied in the field of brain health mainly to understand addictions and neurology was my focus because I could see the spectrum of children heading that way if under allopathic care. Even holistic care can head this way as well. This all improved my understanding of how the body/brain works.

As a young mother I didn't realize my instincts were something that I needed to listen to. Instincts are a warning sign, its not about power over someone or something. If someone in the room smells smoke and they mention it, listen, something might be burning!!! In my case my senses are strong. I was that person at age 11 saying to my mother and any adult who would listen. I smell something hot and burning. No one would stop and hep me investigate, 45 minutes later, my mother opened the cellar door and up came a storm of flames she is lucky she didn't get burned or seriously hurt. We are lucky the house didn't burn down. Not much has changed today.

The problems were smoldering but I couldn't get a direction in my own health. Then my problems tipped. After the installation of over 100 plus LED lights in my home, I fell and fell hard. My youngest son was getting very depressed and I was loosing eye contact with him that December. By January I started having aniexty issues, heart pounding symptoms. I knew I didn't like the lights so I bought some lamps. I was well aware of the cost to install them and well aware of my electric bill without them. But I did not put two and two together until later in February. I was having such aniexty in my heart I signed up for a cardiology class in my town. I went there thinking I could find help. Once I arrived my mind was so fried I couldn't even sit down and listen to any of the speakers. It was if the words all blended together in one sentence nothing seemed easy to listen to. All I could remember was that GABA puts the space in-between words. My kids often felt this. I decided to go down into the exhibit hall and see what was there. I came upon a machine called ENG3 it was a device that omitted water vapor thru colors. Its purpose was to settle down the nervous system and gain balance between the sympathetic and parasympathetic nervous systems. I sat on the device for over an hour. For the first time in months I could think clearly. Went home to rethink what I had learned at this conference, I knew that my nervous system were in flight!!! I knew I need another session on this device. I found one in Palm Springs so I made an appointment to go. For the first time in my life I was not able to drive in a new area. I mean, I drove across county when I was 18 with just a road map from MA to CA. I could sail at 28 foot sail boat at 19. I knew how to move in space. What was happening? I was getting confused on which was the left or right side. I couldn't make a turn or a decision in the middle of an intersection. My mind was failing me at the most basic level. I remember looking at my hands and saying to myself, well I know I write with my right hand, this must be the right side. Crazy stuff. After my season on the ENG3 I again felt better for a few days. I took more EPA and DHA fats, ate more butter, drank more raw milk. I knew the fats would help my nervous system calm down. But, I still found myself taking baths in the dark and turning off the lights. I new magnesium baths would help and I realized I needed the lights turned off to be able to think. At this point I could think clearly in the tub and I sat in that tub talking to myself. Reminding myself where my clothing was, what I would put on, where the keys were, my shoes, the car and how I would get dressed, get into the car, start the car up and go the store to get the old light bulbs we used to get. I live in California and they are hot about Led lights. I got out of the tub, no lights on, got dressed and went thru the rehearsal once more because I knew once I got under those lights I would loose my train of thoughts. I literally got into the car and just started to cry, my oldest son came out and asked what I was doing. I explained to him what I thought the problem was and my plan to get these lights. He offered to drive and I purchase the first 10 new light bulbs that were not LED. Now things could get back to normal, but not so quick. I had been under a great deal of stress over this and other family matters and although I was feeling better the next 12 months were going to be a doosey for me. The stress and wearing down of my cell membranes was just the beginning. In the next 12 months I experience stress like no other time in my life and it resulted in 6 ER visits by ambulance because I would just feel these emotions come over me and the heart would start racing up to 170 beats a minute and I would faint. This went on for another year. What healthy fats I had, had to protect me were gone and with all the electrical lights and devices I was taking another nose dive. Did the best I could with magnesium, meditation, time at the beach when I could, all awhile trying to feed my sensory system to relax. I did find taking little weekend trips to the ocean was vey helpful. My husband and I did this often for the next year. I did some neru fed back thru EMDR diagnosed with PTSD which Im not sure this is very helpful labels and all. The EMDR did help for a bit, but like most electrical devices I saturate at some point and have to stop. Even a heating pad can do this to me. Dirty electricity anyone? Being my nature, I research until I can find answers. Someone of Facebook suggested I look in Dr Jack Kruse's work and I did... Boy what a difference. All my intuition has been confirmed. So although it has taken me a bit of time to get thru your work and I also just finished up a class, that was in a Skype setting for 18 weeks two hours a week, again fried my brain. I was abel to get most of your points and headed to maui just a two weeks ago for 8 days. I can say I felt a tremendous improvement just doing this.

So this is where I'm at today

1) I wake up every morning for morning light, shoot for an hour
2) Breakfast of 4 eggs, some fish as in salmon and raw cheese
3) 2 months into this now I only need breakfast and If I do decide to eat later I will in the light out side as I do at breakfast
4) Consume seaweeds thru nutritional support, as eating seed weeds are not my favorite. Looking for those electrons to attract the light in my body.
5) I still do a magnesium baking soda baths every night
6) Eat fish and still take Fish oils
7) I find, I crave vitamin C right now but this will be ending as autumn approaches and fresh fruits move out
8) Blue blockers on the eyes and over the throat area to protect the thyroid while on the computer. I limit computer time to about an hour a day in 2 half hour intervals at the most. And I putting down the cell phone another issue.
9) I have started CT with ice packs at night while sleeping at my feet and I sleep like a bear!! I also use the ice packs in the sun during the day while sun bathing
10) While in Maui I slept out doors every night and wore my see thru bikini!! Of note I met another gal in the dress by the same company Kiniki, she gave me the confidence to wear my bathing suit.. Thank You!
(As for sleeping outside... Reminds me of my grandmother, while growing up in Cape Cod MA she slept out doors on a patio for over 30 years throughout the entire year. They would staple up plastic on the screen in walls to prevent the snow and rain coming thru. She lived on her own until 90 and had a great mind and spirit. I own a great deal to to my grandmother, she was very wise...)
11) I have my Bellicon rebounder outside and I rebound every day three to four times. Rebounding inside it wore me out but out side it strengthens me and give me more energy
12) Looking to make a sleeping area outside
13) Locating a rental in Maui, so that I can go back and fourth when I want
14) I have recently rediscovered the power of drinking water in colored glasses using the amethyst as it is purple and omits infer red light. A company called Vitajuwel makes water bottles with the crystals in inter changeable pods. The first night my husband had a sip of the amethyst jeweled water he sweat from chest up for about 3 hours to the point of having to change his shirts. He's not always up to my level of trying stuff, so his reactions can be stronger. Something we all need to heed when adding new environments, products even food or light into our environment. If it is new to you, take it slow and get to know your body and its reactions. Be very kind to yourself.

LAST BUT NOT LEAST I WOULD LOVE TO HAVE A CHAT WITH YOU JACK ON THE PHONE AS MY TIME ON THE COMPUTER IS SHORT AND TODAY I HAVE EXTENDED MY SELF A BIT TO LONG. PLEASE IF YOU OFFER SUCH APPOINTMENTS LET ME KNOW WHO I MAY CONTACT. I WILL STOP FOR NOW AS MY RIGHT SIDE OF MY HEAD IS JUST INFLAMED AND I MUST...

TAKE CARE AND THANK YOU SO VERY MUCH FOR WHAT YOU DO!!

Leslie AKA Mamma Bear

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-27T00:04:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mama-bear-is-out-of-the-den.20210/post-224341>

Welcome. I do offer educational consults when I have time for silver gold and platinum members.

### Sheddie — 2017-09-27T01:25:11+0300
