>
> As a young mother I didn't realize my instincts were something that I needed to listen to. Instincts are a warning sign, its not about power over someone or something. If someone in the room smells smoke and they mention it, listen, something might be burning!!! In my case my senses are strong. I was that person at age 11 saying to my mother and any adult who would listen. I smell something hot and burning. No one would stop and hep me investigate, 45 minutes later, my mother opened the cellar door and up came a storm of flames she is lucky she didn't get burned or seriously hurt. We are lucky the house didn't burn down. Not much has changed today.
>
> The problems were smoldering but I couldn't get a direction in my own health. Then my problems tipped. After the installation of over 100 plus LED lights in my home, I fell and fell hard. My youngest son was getting very depressed and I was loosing eye contact with him that December. By January I started having aniexty issues, heart pounding symptoms. I knew I didn't like the lights so I bought some lamps. I was well aware of the cost to install them and well aware of my electric bill without them. But I did not put two and two together until later in February. I was having such aniexty in my heart I signed up for a cardiology class in my town. I went there thinking I could find help. Once I arrived my mind was so fried I couldn't even sit down and listen to any of the speakers. It was if the words all blended together in one sentence nothing seemed easy to listen to. All I could remember was that GABA puts the space in-between words. My kids often felt this. I decided to go down into the exhibit hall and see what was there. I came upon a machine called ENG3 it was a device that omitted water vapor thru colors. Its purpose was to settle down the nervous system and gain balance between the sympathetic and parasympathetic nervous systems. I sat on the device for over an hour. For the first time in months I could think clearly. Went home to rethink what I had learned at this conference, I knew that my nervous system were in flight!!! I knew I need another session on this device. I found one in Palm Springs so I made an appointment to go. For the first time in my life I was not able to drive in a new area. I mean, I drove across county when I was 18 with just a road map from MA to CA. I could sail at 28 foot sail boat at 19. I knew how to move in space. What was happening? I was getting confused on which was the left or right side. I couldn't make a turn or a decision in the middle of an intersection. My mind was failing me at the most basic level. I remember looking at my hands and saying to myself, well I know I write with my right hand, this must be the right side. Crazy stuff. After my season on the ENG3 I again felt better for a few days. I took more EPA and DHA fats, ate more butter, drank more raw milk. I knew the fats would help my nervous system calm down. But, I still found myself taking baths in the dark and turning off the lights. I new magnesium baths would help and I realized I needed the lights turned off to be able to think. At this point I could think clearly in the tub and I sat in that tub talking to myself. Reminding myself where my clothing was, what I would put on, where the keys were, my shoes, the car and how I would get dressed, get into the car, start the car up and go the store to get the old light bulbs we used to get. I live in California and they are hot about Led lights. I got out of the tub, no lights on, got dressed and went thru the rehearsal once more because I knew once I got under those lights I would loose my train of thoughts. I literally got into the car and just started to cry, my oldest son came out and asked what I was doing. I explained to him what I thought the problem was and my plan to get these lights. He offered to drive and I purchase the first 10 new light bulbs that were not LED. Now things could get back to normal, but not so quick. I had been under a great deal of stress over this and other family matters and although I was feeling better the next 12 months were going to be a doosey for me. The stress and wearing down of my cell membranes was just the beginning. In the next 12 months I experience stress like no other time in my life and it resulted in 6 ER visits by ambulance because I would just feel these emotions come over me and the heart would start racing up to 170 beats a minute and I would faint. This went on for another year. What healthy fats I had, had to protect me were gone and with all the electrical lights and devices I was taking another nose dive. Did the best I could with magnesium, meditation, time at the beach when I could, all awhile trying to feed my sensory system to relax. I did find taking little weekend trips to the ocean was vey helpful. My husband and I did this often for the next year. I did some neru fed back thru EMDR diagnosed with PTSD which Im not sure this is very helpful labels and all. The EMDR did help for a bit, but like most electrical devices I saturate at some point and have to stop. Even a heating pad can do this to me. Dirty electricity anyone? Being my nature, I research until I can find answers. Someone of Facebook suggested I look in Dr Jack Kruse's work and I did... Boy what a difference. All my intuition has been confirmed. So although it has taken me a bit of time to get thru your work and I also just finished up a class, that was in a Skype setting for 18 weeks two hours a week, again fried my brain. I was abel to get most of your points and headed to maui just a two weeks ago for 8 days. I can say I felt a tremendous improvement just doing this.
>
> So this is where I'm at today
>
> 1) I wake up every morning for morning light, shoot for an hour
> 2) Breakfast of 4 eggs, some fish as in salmon and raw cheese
> 3) 2 months into this now I only need breakfast and If I do decide to eat later I will in the light out side as I do at breakfast
> 4) Consume seaweeds thru nutritional support, as eating seed weeds are not my favorite. Looking for those electrons to attract the light in my body.
> 5) I still do a magnesium baking soda baths every night
> 6) Eat fish and still take Fish oils
> 7) I find, I crave vitamin C right now but this will be ending as autumn approaches and fresh fruits move out
> 8) Blue blockers on the eyes and over the throat area to protect the thyroid while on the computer. I limit computer time to about an hour a day in 2 half hour intervals at the most. And I putting down the cell phone another issue.
> 9) I have started CT with ice packs at night while sleeping at my feet and I sleep like a bear!! I also use the ice packs in the sun during the day while sun bathing
> 10) While in Maui I slept out doors every night and wore my see thru bikini!! Of note I met another gal in the dress by the same company Kiniki, she gave me the confidence to wear my bathing suit.. Thank You!
> (As for sleeping outside... Reminds me of my grandmother, while growing up in Cape Cod MA she slept out doors on a patio for over 30 years throughout the entire year. They would staple up plastic on the screen in walls to prevent the snow and rain coming thru. She lived on her own until 90 and had a great mind and spirit. I own a great deal to to my grandmother, she was very wise...)
> 11) I have my Bellicon rebounder outside and I rebound every day three to four times. Rebounding inside it wore me out but out side it strengthens me and give me more energy
> 12) Looking to make a sleeping area outside
> 13) Locating a rental in Maui, so that I can go back and fourth when I want
> 14) I have recently rediscovered the power of drinking water in colored glasses using the amethyst as it is purple and omits infer red light. A company called Vitajuwel makes water bottles with the crystals in inter changeable pods. The first night my husband had a sip of the amethyst jeweled water he sweat from chest up for about 3 hours to the point of having to change his shirts. He's not always up to my level of trying stuff, so his reactions can be stronger. Something we all need to heed when adding new environments, products even food or light into our environment. If it is new to you, take it slow and get to know your body and its reactions. Be very kind to yourself.
>
> LAST BUT NOT LEAST I WOULD LOVE TO HAVE A CHAT WITH YOU JACK ON THE PHONE AS MY TIME ON THE COMPUTER IS SHORT AND TODAY I HAVE EXTENDED MY SELF A BIT TO LONG. PLEASE IF YOU OFFER SUCH APPOINTMENTS LET ME KNOW WHO I MAY CONTACT. I WILL STOP FOR NOW AS MY RIGHT SIDE OF MY HEAD IS JUST INFLAMED AND I MUST...
>
> TAKE CARE AND THANK YOU SO VERY MUCH FOR WHAT YOU DO!!
>
> Leslie AKA Mamma Bear

Hello Leslie,

I think you wrote a riveting description of chronic "Circadian Disease" with acute flare ups! What a journey and much wisdom along the way! (You are definitely now among Friends.) We generally go to the Signature page (hit your name and on the menu below, for Signature) and sketch in some details that attach to every post like, Age, Sex, Haplogroup, health notes...). I started with Leptin Reset and Light efforts and I see you're getting along nicely in that.

However, maybe your "6)... still take Fish Oils "consumption isn't best although seafood is a good strong food factor: Have you seen \[

\] ? I just learned a lot there about DHA and the Vit D & A cycles, and somewhere after 25min in the interview Fish Oils get explained; how the supplement form goes in the wrong place (SN1 & SN3 in the DHA loop, for a 'useless' effect, or increased inflammation... Needs to go in the SN2 position...) I'm sure this is explained in other places on this site, too... I especially like learning listening to Kruse podcasts and You Tube clips because I also limit my screen time... I'm planning CT once I feel leptin sensitivity is switched on, so you're way ahead of me on CT!

I'm intuiting, too, your nnEMF exposure environment, beyond the home interior, might be very troublesome, and maybe more focus on that. Hope you don't have a smart meter or live close to cell towers or RF masts...

Lucky lucky cubs, to have a Mama Bear like you!

### Leslie hamud — 2017-12-09T00:19:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mama-bear-is-out-of-the-den.20210/post-229816>

A litte mitochondria history...

My mother who is currently 79, has had recently had a few stints put in. I suspect her energy cycle (cellular respiratory) is not up to par. Difficulty with breathing these days, but much better since the stints were place in. She does not smoke now but did for many years until the age of 35. My mum is still very active for her age. She walks several times a week. My mother and her mother, my grandmother, were both horse gals and loved those beauties. They rode for years. My mother grew up on horses and riding was second nature to her. We had horse stables and horses on the property and they were very much a part of their life. My grandmother was very active all her life, not a smoker or drinker but she was on the go. If she wasn't out on the horse she was in the garden, gardening. She also had a not so usual sleeping arrangement on the east coast. She slept outside all year round! I remember stapling up plastic on the the screens as to not let the snow or rain come into the deck area. Sleeping outside, on Cape Cod, MA with those blister blustery seasons, she was there. She moved to Ca was she was in her late 60's got her life guards license when she was 72 and lived on her own until 92. She biked every day, stationery and on the actual bike, as well. When she moved to Ca, she decide she did not need a car. A bike was good, a great way to get sun and exercise. Her health was good she wasn't afraid of work or play. She did have a bought of colon cancer but we suspect it was do to the use of Dawn liquid detergent in her enema bucket!!. I think she may have been late 70's at that point. Of course we found out she had colon cancer as we started to miss her during the week. She lived on her own, but we saw a pattern and soon asked what was up. As she was taking the bus to get the chemo and radiation treatments. She would never ask for a ride. Again, thou it wasn't unusual as she would go missing for a few days now and then. Ending up in Nova Scotia looking for family history or just needed a little train ride out of town for a few days. We often would get a post card of her new location. Independent really is a term she used with great vigor. Her mother, my great grand mother was equally a go getter. Her husband was a Captain who sailed the east coast seas from Main to Cape, Africa and back to England. As a result she often was at home alone caring for and tending to the families needs. She lived into her middle 90's as well and lived on her own until the late 80's. Equally independent and ready for any challenge. I feel my mothers family line as given me great strength. As side from the smoking or cancer, these ladies have had great strength and determination. Generally of good spirit, good health and a great outlook in life. I'm very blessed to be a part of this heritage.

Ice packs, sitting in the sun with blue blockers on seemed to increase healing effects. I can say that my lower lungs did feel much healthier, far as deep breathing goes. I also broke a toe while there, threw the ice packs on, along with blue blockers and sat in the sun for about 4 hours and by evening time I was back into shoes. Swelling and black blue issues were resolved. Just amazing is all I can say.

I had another incident with blue light. Im sure we all have gone to the dentist lately Well they are using blue LED to help harden products such as cements. After my visit I had such awful nerve pain down my right side arm and hand, the same side as the Led light was used. I have over several months got the pain down to a point and noticed it is slowly moving down the body and now sits on the outer side of the right calf muscle. DDW
Started with 105 DDW 3 cups a day
First night I had slight cramps in upper stomach
Second Night slight cramps in lower stomach
2nd week felt like my lungs where draining as if excess fluid in the night I would wake to cough and clear it
Then by the 3rd week I felt similar feelings in the lungs excess fluid but in the heart and a felt like I needed to strech it. Soultion-Bellicon bounce, exhale all the breath in the lungs, don't breath in and bounce 10-15 times. This really helped.
I had to slow down consumption of the DDW as I felt as if I was using up or stripping the fat off my lips and mouth while drinking. I tried eating butter, coconut oil, first then drinkthe DDW but of no help. Then I notice a very dehydrated tissues in my nose could not moisten them up. Then I stopped drinking DDW evey other day. Did ithis for a while thenjust stopped. After reading Dancing with Water I ytird to soak the DDD in shungite. I added shungite sphere to the water for 15 mins. I did not feel the fat being stripped from the tissues this was better.
Each day I moved up in time for the soaking pf the shungite sphere in the DDW and after every raise in exposure I can feel a deeper clearing of tissues again as if I need to strech them and shake the deuterium off.
DDW 85 soaking with the shungite sphere for 1 hour 15 mins in a red glass thankyou Mitochrondria!!!

Week of March 19th, 2018
Took pregnenolone for two days 100 mg felt very flat flat. Decided against it. Went to using the Young living product called Progessence Plus Serum Contains seven essential oils including Copaiba, Frankincense, Cedarwood, Bergamot, Peppermint, Rosewood and Clove which all have natural harmonizing properties. A few drops in the am applied topically to vaginal area. I feel much better not as unattached or flat.

Week of March 26, 2018
Started on Premier DHEA of 25 mg am. Still working with DDW water and shungite. Feeling very overwhelmed these days week of no sunshine and rain is def taking a tole on me. Continue with Tumeric and Gaia product after each fish meal. Which is twice a day.

April 1, 2018
DHEA is a no go, Progessense Plus Serum is still working and I will add in the Eveing Primrose topically. Not feeling well no sunshine lately feeling overwhelmed by it all. My feet hurt so bad from the cracks in them olny two but enough to sit me down and not walk or put shoes on. Very painful... No sun did I mention this?

April 4, 2018
Progressense Plus Serum is done only using the Evening Primrose Oil topically I would say it is healing the bottomes of my feet. I can actually wear sandals and walk better now.

April 17, 2018
Stopped DHEA again just did not feel well on it. I did start using the Evening Primrose OIl topically One capsule a day doing much better on this. I also stopped the Young living OIl product Progessense Plus Serum. As the sun starts to reappear I do feel better. Still getting out in the AM thru the PM. I have dropped the eggs they were feeling very heavy in my body. I notice forst the cheese i use in my eggs was just not feeling well. All I can say is I can smell the cheese proteins in my urine so I was not digesting them at all. It was time to quit. The more sun exposure I have the more I can eat thewe fermented type foods. My sunlight battery just ran to low. Mostly fish now halibut in the am with purple sweet potatoes. Salmon by 3 with sweet potatoes once more. Craving lots of pepper fresh ground is the key. I have added in Tumeric/Heart Gaia is the brand and I started noticing weight loss for the first time. Cold Thermogensis every evening with ice packs and CT in the pool outside daily. I have dropped now 13 lbs and moving in the direction of better health. I had to step into a court room a few days ago and an old ear aches resurfaced in the court room with in the 45 mins that I was there. Came home used my tunning fork and the next day I decided to take a trip up 5,000 feet to the local mountains with my rebounder to see if this could change the ache. 24 hours up in the mountains with the tunning bar, rebounder, fresh fish and potatoes I brought with me to cook. Took a few hikes went out on the local lake took a swim. Headed back downthe hill ear ahce has resolved itself. Go figure!

DDW water I was up to 1 cup at 65 DDW soaked in shungite for 3-5 hours the longer the soak the deeper the DDW will get into my tissues. Each time I would get a damp feeling in a specific area. Now the dampness is in the thyroid area upper lungs. So I will stop for a few days get more sun exposure dry up this dampness and then decide if I want to go back. Instinct tells me season is changing and I will soon need to stop the DDW water and go to just lemon and water for the summer months. I still crave ther Gerolsteiner water with a full lemon squeezed with a splash of OJ and alittle sea salt in the am.

NOTE on DDW water. I did find when I consumed more than 2 cups of DDW saoked in sungite - that I created a sensation of magnetisim in the sense of when in the bath tub I could feel the pressure of the water against my skin and every breath seemed to increase this sensitivity. Strange feeling overall but I can relates it to how I felt in Maui last Septemebr 2017. It was this same sensation of water pressure up against my skin. A light pressue or a pull on the fluids in the body. Maybe Dr Kruse can makes sense of this for me....

Computer still makes me forgetfull and uncordinated to be in front of it for any length of time. Im still having diffuculty organizing my thoughts at time but as summer approaches I will get better. A trip is needed, heading down in the lower lattitude will help. My fear is forgetting to pay the bills, etc. Im normally much more organized and a big multi tasker this of latley is not my strength and I know it! I have visual memeory still but have diffucluty with language/memeory and math skills/memeory. The fainting epsiodes 4 years ago are still effecting me although I have recovered some physicals skills such as my gait is better, ability to feed myslef, there are still some skills left behind. To some extent I think I may have caused some form of a miny stroke.

Having a hard time organizing myself still, I know I need blood work and a talk with Mr, Dr. Jack Kruse would be helpful too.

**July 2018**
Got my Sh-- together headed to Mexico to stay at the Unico. They have tubs on the patio, a huge plus for me. Which I can fill up with ice if the cenotes does not work for me. Arrived for a stay of 14 days. Cenotoes was not cold enough for me and the ocean was worst. Glad the tub is here. I filled it up every morning as our room faces East I also got the bonus of a sunrise! I knew from my exp in Maui that I would need progesterone for healing and growth. I brought Evening primrose and found that using any where from 10-14 caps a day topically settle my desire for this hormone production. Im not able to take it internally dont know why but not a real concern at this point. The sun helps me digest and process it so Im good with that. For the most part I felt good down here. I missed my cold pacific ocean and halibut. I wished they had more fish and I'm not at oysters yet!! With that said I ate crab cakes and their local fish for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. I brought with me my hawaian butter which I relied on when food was not avalible or correct. I thought I would drink but decided best not to as all the effort Im making to achieve my optimal of life is more important. I did travel to a few cenotoes. I need the sunshine still low on endorphins. Perhaps one of my biggest problems. Stay was good I ate all my butter I had DDW with me but did not seem to want it. I noticed I did not burn which is odd for me. I would think I built my UV up so that I would not burn makes the most sense. I did not have a fire here and no late afternoon sunset. As we are in the jungle and I only have exposure to the sunrise, All good, but now I need the sunset!!!

When I arrived home I notice that my hunger was not as strong and my morning growel of the tummy is gone. I think I need to rebuild some red light receptors and get the thyroid up and running once more. 14 days without sunset may have been to much.

At home I get both sunrise and sunset. That is my optimal I need to have and COLD too.

**November December 2018**
Tough tough we went live September 28th for 5 G. I Iost my dog Max 2 weeks later. Tough tough time for me in the winter. I still swimming every day in the cold waters either the pool or the ocean. Hot fires at night - 2 hours. I sleep beside ice packs as well. All help me but I notice the more ice the more red light I need to balance. I crave both!

**January 2019**
I bought tencel sheets woven with eucalyptus, by Molecule. After sleeping on them for a few weeks, I have decided that possible its creating a vacum thru topology. Last night I removed my clothing in the middle of the night and I felt the respiratory proteins working at a higher level. Im not sure how to explain this but thats the best I can do. I did decide to place the ice packs on top of the sheets and have the sheet next to my skin surface which seem to amplify this vacuum, drag effect on my cells. A mini vortex if your will. Now with a little duck tape I will tape the ice packs together to creat a balnket size and place this above the tencel sheets while I have as much skin contact to the sheets as possible.

I have also wearing a silver neckless through out the day. As a way to combat the blue light toxicity and what it does to the bacteria in the system. What has me more perplexed than anything is how my son stays on the computer from 4am to 6pm every day. Yes, He's eating carbs. and low fat and protein that makes sense for his light exposure. Redox is high in the 23's and its prob a matter of time... But what is he doing? Looking at his life he is getting up at 4 with the sun, going to sleep at 6. Sleeps on bed linens infused with silver threading. Drinks raw milk and raw cocoa. I slip a little extra Hawaiian butter from the 20's, we live in the 30"s in with the scamble eggs. Which are a 1 egg - 2 yolk ratio to bring the much needed cholesterol and phospatidylcholine (PC) into his diet. I think the silver thread has a lot to do with it. I do notice that today Im on the computer myself it raining and normally I would be any where near this blue light. But I have the silver neckless on so maybe its keep bacteria and yeast down toa point I can think not soi much brain fog. For NOW I will travel with this thought for a while to see if it pans out.

**Nutrition/Food I**ts winter so no evening primrose topically unless its a bright day and Im in the sun. Fish for breakfast or oysters now. I have found if I eat more than 6 oysters I smell like charcoal but with the addition of a copper food like avocados, seem to be the best for my location now, I can eat more oysters. Oysters 12 is the best with a full avocado. Halibut or Mahi Mahi. No salmon I cannot break it down and I look very juandice from eating it .

I have a thought that Im willing to express here.... When we get down to brass tacs, it is BEHAVIOR that we are fighting. Mans behavior. Do we behave in a way that can benefit us? Well if, Dr Hilda Clark's research about parasites and their relationship to human behavior are correct. Maybe, we should look into what parasites are on board. We should check our protein status. For me personally if I suspect a parasite I look for the protein this fella is not happy with. As parasites are proteins so why not digest them instead of detoxing them? Its much easier and then I can ride myself of a few unhealthy habbits or behaviors in the process that are in the way of my OPTIMAL. Just say-in. Take Care...

# Thread: Vitamin D, 25 Hydroxy vs. VITAMIN D 1,25-DIHYDROXY

### curves2.0 — 2017-09-27T02:14:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-25-hydroxy-vs-vitamin-d-1-25-dihydroxy.20212/post-224356>

I live at the 45 latitude. Getting sufficient Vitamin D naturally from April through August has been problematic. If I use a UV tanning bed or at home reptile lights the Vitamin D goes up to the mid-40s or more.

I heard on a recent Dr. K podcast that obtaining a 1,25 value would be a good intel but I couldn't find more info on understanding the value when doing a search here. Also, I heard Dr. K say that a good hack would be to get more IR-A so as to lay the ground work for more UV absorption -- did I get that right? Any idea where I can find more info on either of these hacks? Thanks!

Vitamin D, 25 Hydroxy
33.2 ng/mL30.0 - 100.0 ng/mL

1,25 DIHYDROXY VITAMIN D
70.5 pg/mL19.9 - 79.3 pg/mL

# Thread: 2017 science now firmly calls T2D a mitochondrial illness.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2017-science-now-firmly-calls-t2d-a-mitochondrial-illness.20217/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-28T14:19:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2017-science-now-firmly-calls-t2d-a-mitochondrial-illness.20217/post-224427>

Looks like 2017 science is finally realizing the foundational relationship of mitochondria to disease generation via heteroplasmy. This paper will be the first of many and is critical for all diabetics to read. Diabetes is a mitochondrial disease. This vindicates Dr. Wallace and myself for pounding the table on looking to mitochondria dysfunction as the cause of insulin resistance and diabetes.

CITE: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28951827](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28951827)

How can we heal mitochondria that are destroyed by high heteroplasmy rates due to our poor environment? We need to optimize PGC-1 alpha signaling. How? Add electrons and lower protons to limit inflammation. Protons are another synonym for inflamation. protons = pH and pH that is low lowers the EZ in cell water. When the cell water EZ decreases light cannot make a battery in a cell. Paraoxonase serum concentration is influenced directly by pH changes and inflammatory changes and the levels of serum oxidized-LDL. Proliferator-activated receptor-γ (PPAR) has a co-activator called (PGC)-1 alpha. PGC-1 alpha is a member of a family of transcription co-activators that plays a central role in the regulation of all cellular energy metabolism. PGC-1 alpha protein may be also involved in controlling blood pressure, regulating cellular cholesterol homoeostasis, and the development of obesity and T2D. PGc-1 alpha also helps control mitochondrial number and density within a cell. Mitochondrial numbers are regulated by mitochondrial biogenesis to meet the energy demands of the cell and compensate for cell damage. This process is mediated by peroxisome proliferator–activated receptor γ coactivator 1α (PGC1α), which is relevant to mitochondrial dynamics since it is a transcriptional coactivator of the fusion mediator mitofusin-2. Mitofusin-2 is what connects the endoplasmic reticulum to mitochondria and control calcium homeostasis in the cell. Mitochondria– endoplasmic reticulum connectivity is regulated by mitofusin-2 and can create microdomains that facilitate fission.

PGC-1 alpha can interact with, and regulate the activities of, cAMP response element binding protein (CREB) and nuclear respiratory factors (NRF’s) that allows us to make new mitochondria to produce more energy. CREB interacts with UV light as well in the brain to stimulate neuron growth. This is likely how exercise links to neocortical growth and repair in neuro-degeneration cases like Alzheimer’s disease. PGC-1 alpha is strongly induced by cold exposure. This is why I use ice when I exercise and swim in freezing water; it also links the environmental stimulus to adaptive thermogenesis (think UPC-1 from the leptin series). If one can mix this cold exposure to AM sunlight you really are doing yourself a big help in reversing diseases. The link to cold exposure and UV sunlight on the skin and circulatory system also involves AMPk pathways. The reason for this is how the AMP-activated protein kinase pathway (AMPk) works to these environment stimuli. The AMPk pathway is best described as a fuel sensor for lipid and glucose metabolism. Activated AMPk inhibits PPAR-{alpha} and PPAR-gamma . Cold exposure on our skin surfaces and face increase fat release from adipocytes. AMPk rises with cold. Ultraviolet radiation and reactive oxygen species from cytochrome 1 (ROS) impair the AMPk signaling axis. This is how AMPk works within a circadian cycle with UV light. Both leptin (nutrition success) and adiponectin (nutrition deprivation) activate AMPk pathways. Cold and UV light are at opposite ends of the circadian cycles but both work in unison to help us absorb UV light and assimilate it and transform it to a DC electric current to regenerate mitochondria. Metformin mimics cold exposure but it does not work ideally in a artificial lit environment.

CITE: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3883043/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3883043/)

### Josh Rosenthal — 2017-09-29T00:01:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2017-science-now-firmly-calls-t2d-a-mitochondrial-illness.20217/post-224441>

### Jack Kruse — 2019-05-01T01:26:48+0300

What do you think motivates professional skeptics like this one? Note this is an actual MD. And how do you respond if at all? I noticed no one is sticking up for grounding in the comments on this page:

[https://www.skeptic.com/reading\_room/bogus-claims-grounding-bare-feet-to-earth](https://www.skeptic.com/reading_room/bogus-claims-grounding-bare-feet-to-earth)

I also notice the skeptics are always using hit-and-run tactics. They don't seem to have any interest in knowing the truth if it involves any effort.

In the comments, I was disappointed to see the CEO of XeroShoes making fun of grounding also, in spite of the fact that he believes expensive sneakers cause knee and back problems whereas running barefoot is running how man evolved to run.

How could he not see the possibility of an electrical benefit?

This makes me grumpy!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-04T16:16:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-motivates-professional-skeptics-like-this-one.20219/post-234116>

There is an easy way to silence your critics; just try to do what they say you can't do. The imagination of the genius of nature vastly surpasses human intellect; the intellect of the academic vastly surpasses his imagination of what nature is capable of. I'm never dismayed by the opinions of critics. They are only the traffic cops of the healing arts. They are there to protect the power in place. When you know this it makes your thinking about things easier. The louder the critic the more insecure the position. Sometimes I will speak up and act as a critic for others in the healthcare world to alert people to the Dunning Kruger effect that may be in operation. I wont call out the person.......I will call out how they are thinking about a topic to make the reader more curious what might be off the menu.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-04T16:31:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-motivates-professional-skeptics-like-this-one.20219/post-234117>

Consider this....What is critcal to a plant to grow? Sunlight and water. Being oblivious to both is like a scientist asking why a plant will not grow in the wrong kind of light............
Sometimes research is only difficult because they don’t know what we don’t know. This is the basis of the Dunning Kruger effect I often speak about. As a result researchers ask the WRONG questions and order the wrong tests and clinicians keep looking under the wrong stones to find answers. This is what functional medicine does with methylation and depression. It turns out methylation is altered by ambient light frequencies and nnEMF.

To answer this question precisely, mental health professionals and scientists must ask better questions by stepping outside of its normal line of questioning and think about what blue lit technolgy screens, depression and lack of sleep all have in common: the use of light. Moreover, how this light varies from sunlight that plants and brains need to develop properly. technology screens introduce a very alien spectrum of 5700 K (solar noon) 24/7 to the eyes and brains in very high doses. This light affects the central reitnal pathways which connect to the SCN, pituitary, and brain via POMC release just by shining the fake light directly into the eye which then has its effect on the brain and mood and mental status. Sleep is regulated by the light hormone melatonin. Everyone knows this now. Depression is influenced by proteins made from POMC and from the large neurotransmitters which are made from aromatic amino acids that are specifically regulated by certain frequencies in solar light. It should be blatantly obvious to anyone, but it is not. The technology companies know exactly what they are doing. This is why google, facebook, apple, and many others have patents on blue light emitting screens. With kids now addicted to technology is is no shock how children mental ability, moods, and diseases are manifesting today.

# Thread: Aphanizomenon flos-aquae hack with Sunlight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aphanizomenon-flos-aquae-hack-with-sunlight.20220/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Brent Patrick — 2017-09-29T11:42:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aphanizomenon-flos-aquae-hack-with-sunlight.20220/post-224459>

After reading Eat Light & Feel Bright by Jeffrey Bruno PhD I decided to do a hack over a period of seven days with AFA Blue Green Algae. I consumed each morning AFA in a glass of water ,each morning has been full of sun above 25 degrees (77) and have stayed several hours in the morning Sun grounded of cause. I have observed a Dopamine High that lasts all day and energy increases more than I have experienced with normal Sun basking I have still been consuming regular seafood.
Sleep has become deeper but the most obvious affect is my elevated mood and I can only describe as a shift in my sensory awareness ,colours are more vivid and sounds are more crisp.
I can only suspect that the Algae being a photosensitizer is helping me utilize the light spectrum more efficient and also with absorption. I have had similar effect with MB but never experienced depth of colour like I have with this AFA hack.

### Sue-UK — 2017-09-29T13:48:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aphanizomenon-flos-aquae-hack-with-sunlight.20220/post-224463>

My emergency dig myself out of a hole is green powder mix for the chlorophyll in well water with MB. There seems to be a better synergistic result than either the green powder or MB separately. Get some light and its battery recharge central ....

### Brent Patrick — 2017-09-30T00:58:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aphanizomenon-flos-aquae-hack-with-sunlight.20220/post-224492>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=224485)
>
> Wondering if that hack may have different affects when done in different seasons
> Are all microscopic algae capable of bringing this affect?

Good Question Shiran I have pondered on this thought for several days and most probably will only use this hack in Spring/Summer as in Australia our UV INDEX is starting to climb so plenty of photonic power is on the way

I have come to this conclusion due to reading on a post elsewhere by Dr kruse that Carotenoids found in vegetables and also Blue Green Algae offers protection against UV penetration

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12239422](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12239422)

so It would appear I will not be using in Winter/Autumn when UV is at a low and Morning would be best to hack with due to wider spectrum of light as opposed to afternoon which is mainly Red IR light.

So there would appear on one hand Blue Green Algae Cyanobacteria have the ability to produce Light-harvesting chlorophyll pigments and also have a balance of protector Carotenoids for survival Billions of years ago when UV light showed up Nick Lane discusses this in one of his books regarding evolutionary survival strategy

**So how do your eye and plants marry in quantized fashion?

Like other carotenoids, xanthophylls are found in highest quantity in the [leaves](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leaves) of most green [plants](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plants), where they act to modulate light energy and perhaps serve as a [non-photochemical quenching](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Non-photochemical_quenching) agent to deal with triplet [chlorophyll](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chlorophyll) (an excited form of chlorophyll), which is overproduced at high light levels in photosynthesis. High light levels means high photosynthetic capacity or summer time when blue light is massive up regulated in sunlight to drive ubiquitination.JK
**
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...re-hugely-important-in-quantum-biology.16441/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/carotenoids-they-are-hugely-important-in-quantum-biology.16441/)

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/carotenoids.18511/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/carotenoids.18511/)

### Chris Day — 2017-10-01T08:24:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-224561>

Hi there,

I was wondering if anyone had any advice for getting DHA unto the diet on a budget.

Currently, I can't afford regular purchases of wild caught seafood products.

Is there anything wrong with taking supplements until I can get my economic situation to the point where I can have wild caught seafood in the fridge at all times?

[https://www.bodybuilding.com/store/now/ultra3.html](https://www.bodybuilding.com/store/now/ultra3.html)

Was considering purchasing this.

Thanks in advance,
Chris

### digital — 2017-10-01T15:36:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-224595>

How about Oysters? Generally pretty cheap then supplements:

Just quick Google searching:
\--- Three ounces of Pacific oysters provide more than 1 g of omega-3 fatty acids. (Source: Linus Pauling Institute)
\--- According to federal Food and Drug Administration requirements, here's how the count should break down for a 10-ounce jar, the size most commonly sold in markets:
Large, 4 oysters or less.
Medium, 5-8 oysters.
Small, 9-11 oysters.
Extra small, 13-15.

\--- [http://www.seafoodhealthfacts.org/s...ega-3-epadha-levels-common-fish-and-shellfish](http://www.seafoodhealthfacts.org/seafood-nutrition/patients-and-consumers/omega-3-epadha-levels-common-fish-and-shellfish)

### WalterNL — 2017-10-01T16:57:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-224601>

I think canned sardines will be one of the cheapest ways.

### JanSz — 2017-10-01T16:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-224602>

Even cheaper.
You may not need any
or
you may have too much already.

Check your blood levels of all Omega3 and Omega6.
See if you are short on DHA or any other item in those two categories.

Just make sure that the testing is done by honest laboratory that is not owned by fish oil industry.
Money talks.
Make sure that each and every of items tested have clearly stated their laboratory lower and upper range.

You do not want laboratory to summarize and tell you what to do,
reading test results you should be able to make your own decision.

Look for my posts, somewhere on this thread, and find out what testing will work for you.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6)

/.
////

..

### kris90 — 2017-10-05T14:47:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-225066>

I usually go with canned sardines and canned smoked oysters. I'll eat that as my lunch maybe 3-5x a week. My sardines are in spring water, and my oysters unfortunately are in sunflower oil, but I just drain them really well, and use paper towel to try to get the oil off.

It cost me about $1.50 per can, so I load up on my weekly grocery trip. My family usually has fresh, wild-caught atlantic salmon 1-2x per week as well. Every now and then I'll treat myself to fresh, raw oysters, and I'll often fry up some frozen shrimp and mussels to throw in a salad or over the salmon. But I use the canned sardines/oysters as my staple because they are so affordable and easy to have for lunch at work.

Phewww. Good.

I was reminded of a French food writer that sadly got a cancer and lost his sense of taste. The dude took a step outside... from 20 floors up.

He left a note. It was just a reminder to his brother reminding him that he keeps forgetting which quail leg tastes better (since the big sleeps standing on one leg). That story haunted me. That person was dedicated to food till the end. I like food, but not that much.

### LieselK — 2019-03-29T17:53:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-266759>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=266750)
>
> Canned oysters with a hit of sriracha (homemade if you can) = yum in my book.

It's a win. I mixed my homemade duck mayo with sriracha for them. Do you mind sharing your recipe for homemade sriracha?

### drezy — 2019-03-29T19:06:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-266770>

> [LieselK said:](/goto/post?id=266759)
>
> It's a win. I mixed my homemade duck mayo with sriracha for them. Do you mind sharing your recipe for homemade sriracha?

First let me put my apron on!

I like this as a foundation:
[https://nomnompaleo.com/post/36060636540/paleo-sriracha-homemade-20-minute-sriracha](https://nomnompaleo.com/post/36060636540/paleo-sriracha-homemade-20-minute-sriracha)

Forget the specific brand names mentioned.

Be ready to adjust sugar source(maybe I'm just not into honey) and sugar quantity according to taste.

### Amber Ament — 2019-03-30T00:22:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-266797>

> [Katie Durham said:](/goto/post?id=266748)
>
> I won't buy products like this in oil because there is no control on the quality of the oil even if it appears to be one of the better ones like olive oil. Perhaps there is less DHA if packed in water, but in that case I'll just eat more. I don't have a sense of smell so hard for me to check for rancidness.

I have had the same thoughts about the oil too, which made the spingwater more appealing....there is actually MORE DHA in the springwater ones - well the label indicates there is - so again I went for those ones instead of oil.
I'm the opposite with smell - I smell everything x10, I can smell the rain coming 1/2 an hour before anyone else, I can smell if there has been cockroaches in the cupboard when no one else can, ewwwwww

### Christine_L — 2019-04-08T03:35:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-267594>

I usually drain the oil or water from the can just because I like my own oils better. Add salt or hot sauce, wham!

### Kiersten Jarvis Webert — 2019-04-09T03:22:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-267646>

For anyone that doesn't care for sardines out of the can, I use avocado or olive oil mayo and make them into what looks like tuna salad. My kids love it. Another trick - Siete has almond flour tortillas... I saute them until crisp and add any of the fish/seafood salads that I make on top and it's like a tostada version. Feeding kids with autism takes lots of creativity but it's worth it - I love eating fish now, too!!

### drezy — 2019-04-09T17:16:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-267685>

> [Kiersten Jarvis Webert said:](/goto/post?id=267646)
>
> For anyone that doesn't care for sardines out of the can, I use avocado or olive oil mayo and make them into what looks like tuna salad. My kids love it. Another trick - Siete has almond flour tortillas... I saute them until crisp and add any of the fish/seafood salads that I make on top and it's like a tostada version. Feeding kids with autism takes lots of creativity but it's worth it - I love eating fish now, too!!

You've got a lot on your plate, but you may eventually want to try to seasonally rotate the fat source.

### Kiersten Jarvis Webert — 2019-04-10T21:32:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-267794>

Thank you, Dezy! Do you have suggestions on which fats for which season? We are way off as we are living in MN right now but I have all of us on primarily fish and seafood. We use ghee, olive oil, coconut oil and avocado oil depending on the recipe. I'm so glad you noticed that and said something - would not have thought of it on my own at this point. You know you have to move if you can only eat one food for 8 months of the year!!

### Katie Durham — 2019-04-10T22:19:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-267798>

Doc has cautioned to use unsaturated fats with DHA meals. So I save coconut oil for when I'm cooking beef or chicken.

### drezy — 2019-04-10T22:33:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheapest-source-of-dha.20223/post-267799>

# Thread: The Journal of an Outlier
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-journal-of-an-outlier.20226/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Outlier — 2017-10-02T06:17:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-journal-of-an-outlier.20226/post-224662>

I've been trying to start my journal for literally weeks now and have finally sat down to put it together. I will try to break things up into small chunks so folks don't have to read a book for just one post. Hopefully this will give an idea of my history as well as where I'm at now and then I can continue to document the journey.

⦁ Basic Info

⦁ 53 yr old female
⦁ 5'8 245lbs
⦁ 2 girls, 35 and 34 yrs old​

⦁ Location

⦁ Colorado Springs, CO past 40 years​

⦁ From

⦁ Originally from Virginia
⦁ All family relatives, maternal and paternal from VA​

⦁ Family History

⦁ Maternal Grandmother (deceased)

⦁ Diabetes
⦁ Parkinson's​

⦁ Maternal Grandfather (deceased)

⦁ Emphysema​

⦁ Mother (deceased)

⦁ Lifelong smoker (even during chemo)
⦁ Breast cancer​

⦁ Father (deceased)

⦁ High blood pressure
⦁ COPD​

⦁ Sister

⦁ Lifelong smoker/drugs/alcohol
⦁ COPD​

⦁ Brother

⦁ Significant weight gain in recent years
⦁ Has been generally healthy all of his life​

### Outlier — 2017-10-02T06:20:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-journal-of-an-outlier.20226/post-224663>

Brief History
⦁ Always 'bigger' than peers in height and weight since a child

⦁ At 14 years old, was 5'8 and 180lbs​

⦁ Have always been sensitive to the sun and heat
⦁ After moving to Colorado, realized what it was like to breathe through my nose (I have mold allergies)
⦁ First pregnancy at 18 yrs old

⦁ Gained 10lbs during the pregnancy
⦁ Regularly walked 9 miles a day during the entire pregnancy
⦁ No complications
⦁ Birth took over 2 hours due to cord around the baby's neck (not a full loop)
⦁ Natural childbirth; zero drugs
⦁ Healthy baby girl

⦁ She developed buldging disks in early 20s
⦁ She developed fibromyalgia shortly after​

⦁ Nursed for about a month but had no milk production and had to bottle feed there after​

⦁ Second pregnancy 19 years old

⦁ Body did not have time to recover from first pregnancy
⦁ Did not have ability to eat well if at all
⦁ Was not able to walk as I did during the first pregnancy
⦁ Gained 40lbs during pregnancy
⦁ Did not lose any of it after
⦁ No complications
⦁ Birth was fast (10 minutes after arriving at the hospital)
⦁ Natural childbirth; zero drugs
⦁ Healthy baby girl

⦁ Born with hearing issues - completely deaf now ( father had/has degenerative nerve damage)
⦁ She has struggled with weight issues all her life
⦁ Nursed baby for about 4 months until her lower teeth came in. This girl was a biter!!​

⦁ This pregnancy completely bankrupted my health
⦁ Struggled with weight my entire adult life
⦁ Atkins (i.e. dietary ketosis) was the only thing that helped with weight loss

⦁ Lost 70 lbs twice with Atkins
⦁ Became ineffective after that​

⦁ Had gall bladder removed at 23 yrs old
⦁ Hysterectomy at 48 due to fibroids and exteremely heavy bleeding leading to anemia

⦁ Ovaries and cervix were not removed​

⦁ Outside of weight issues and the events leading to gall bladder and uterine surgeries, I've been otherwise 'healthy'

### Outlier — 2017-10-02T06:21:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-journal-of-an-outlier.20226/post-224664>

Current Health Concerns
⦁ Developed sleep apnea during the past 3 years
⦁ AHI of 98 (for those that do not have an understanding of sleep apnea, that means that I was having 98 events per hour. An event must last for at least 10 seconds and be accompanied by a decrease in the saturation of blood oxygen.) An event means that I either wasn't breathing or was breathing extremely shallow. These events are caused by obstruction (which most people know about) or because the person 'forgets' to breathe (central apeneas) and I have both (known as mixed apnea). Sometimes I try to breath and can't and other times I just don't even try (my brain 'forgets' to breathe).
⦁ Oxygen sats during the original sleep study were in the 60% range and I have measured them in the 50s at home. Again for those without a reference range, generally, oxygen sats in the 90s are good. There is concern when it drops below 88% and under 80% can cause tissue damage to any and all major organs. Therefore, oxygen sats in the 60% range for 8-10 hours a night are really not good.
⦁ Sleep apnea as severe as mine (untreated) prevents the ability to have any healing sleep. You cannot rest at all when your brain is awakened twice per minute every minute you are sleeping
⦁ Of course one of the WONDERFUL benefits of sleep apnea is weight gain. Like I didn't struggle enough with this before
⦁ Let's throw menapause in here because I'm on the cusp of it and it is influencing labs
⦁ Somewhere in the past few years I developed sciatic pain. This is what truly prevents me from being outside and enjoying Colorado

### Outlier — 2017-10-02T06:21:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-journal-of-an-outlier.20226/post-224665>

Previous Leptin RX/ CT Results circa 2013
⦁ As mentioned before, Atkins had previously worked for me twice then didn't really help
⦁ In July of 2013, I decided to 'get healthy'. I started to read about paleo and found Jack Kruse at that time. I did not understand how his teachings were different, but liked them, got his book and dove in
⦁ I knew I was leptin resistance so I started the Leptin RX
⦁ I was fascinated by CT and had to try it!!
⦁ Within 6-8 weeks, I lost 45lbs (without effort) and got my HS-CRP to 0.4
⦁ All other labs looked well
⦁ An important note is that I really didn't understand the key components that contributed to my well being or what I was really doing right
⦁ I heard one thing from all my readings from JK: inflammation is the enemy and eridiate it at all costs. That was my goal and intent and it worked!
⦁ I was not making like the Sphinx nor was I grounding
⦁ I did not have a Magnetico
⦁ I also did NOT HAVE A JOB (significant because I was not surrounded by a cube farm full of computers)
⦁ I walked at least 2 hours a day during the week and 4 hours a day on the weekends
⦁ I ate in the morning and evening when I was supposed to
⦁ I am not into seafood - back then or now. I couldn't bring myself to eat from JK's Tier 1 or 2. But I did do Tier 3. Salmon? I CAN eat that and lots of it without getting tired of it. It was my staple food.
⦁ I was happy, healthy and my battery worked and I had the energy to prove it
⦁ I was enthusiastic enough that I decided to take kung fu lessons - something I always wanted to do but never did because it's very challenging and out of my comfort zone - I dove right in
⦁ My avatar pic is from this timeframe
⦁ I went off track after about 6 or 7 months. Specifically, I was hanging out with a friend that exposed me to blue light and nnEMF 24x7 - literally. Not to mention that I was hardly getting any sleep at all . . . 3 or 4 hours a night if I was lucky.

### Outlier — 2017-10-02T06:22:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-journal-of-an-outlier.20226/post-224666>

Current Labs - Sept 2017

Supplements at time of Labs

⦁ Betaine HCL with Pepsin, 600 mg - approximately 8 pills per meal
⦁ D3 - 5000 IU, 2-4 per day
⦁ Iron - 65 mg, 1 per day
⦁ B50 - 2 per day
⦁ Multi vitamin - 1 per day
⦁ Vit A - 8000 IU - 1 per day
⦁ Fish Oil - 1000-1200 mg, 5 to 10 per day​

Lab Results - 12 hours fasting but with supplements per above

⦁ HS-CRP : 2.2 : mg/L
⦁ D3 : 52 : 30-100 ng/mL
⦁ DHEA : 38 : 102-1185 ng/dL
⦁ HDL : 59 : >= 46 mg/dL
⦁ LDL : 123 : <130 mg/dL
⦁ Total Cholesterol : 203 : 125-200 mg/dL
⦁ Triglycerides : 106 : <150 mg/dL
⦁ Glucose : 93 : 65-99 mg/dL
⦁ Insulin : 27.5 : 2.0-19.6 uUI/mL
⦁ Sex Hormone Binding Globuline : 30 : 17-124 nmol/L
⦁ Estradiol : 29 : pg/mL
⦁ IGF 1 : 119 : 50-317 ng/mL
⦁ CO2 : 21 : 18-29 mmol/L
⦁ Leptin : 45.5 : 8.0-38.9 ng/mL
⦁ Free T3 : 3.0 : 2.3-4.2 pg/mL
⦁ Free T4 : 1.9 : 1.4-3.8
⦁ Reverse T3 : 14 : 8-25 ng/dL
⦁ TSH : 1.54 : mIU/L
⦁ Testosterone (total) : 37 : 2-45ng/dL
⦁ BUN : 9 : 7-25 mg/dL
⦁ Creatinine : .85 : 0.50-1.05 mg/dL
⦁ BUN/Creatinine Ratio : 10.59
⦁ Hemoglobin : 14.2 : 12.0-16.3 g/dL
⦁ A1C : 5.2 : <5.7 % of Hgb
⦁ Hemotocrit : 41.9 : 36.8-49.5 %
⦁ Homocysteine : 6.3 : <10.4 umol/L​

### Outlier — 2017-10-02T06:27:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-journal-of-an-outlier.20226/post-224667>

What I'm doing now
⦁ Goals

⦁ The first order is to improve/heal the sleep apnea; I don't plan on sleeping with that damn machine all my life even though that's what the sleep experts tell me the plan is.
⦁ Second is the sciatic pain - this one is preventing me from enjoying the outside as I hurt badly if I stand or walk for more than just a few minutes

⦁ CT baths are not helping this (that I can reasonably tell anyway0; maybe they are and I just don't know it yet)
⦁ I cannot resume kung fu until this is resolved enough that I can practice without pain​

⦁ Once those 2 major issues are resolved, I should be much further on the journey to optimal and I think solving those issues means getting my battery working
⦁ My ultimate goal is to be optimal for me. The best me that I can be​

⦁ Activities/RXs

⦁ Reading everything JK wrote

⦁ I have downloaded all blog posts to my Kindle
⦁ Reading everything twice - once for general understanding and a second time to drive home ideas I find important
⦁ Working through all the webinars​

⦁ Leptin RX . . . still can't bring myself to eat the raw oysters but my diet is almost entirely salmon, eggs and butter
⦁ Making like the Sphinx, grounding and looking at the morning sun
⦁ Sleep on a Magnetico
⦁ I have been getting 20-30 minutes of UVB around 11am-12pm daily for the past month. Now that it's getting cold, I'm still out in there wearing next to nothing soaking up my sunlight in the cold!
⦁ About to switch to Krill Oil
⦁ With @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) and @[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/)'s help, got a trimeter and learned how to use it

⦁ Microwave is unplugged and completely unused
⦁ Sleep apena patients BEWARE!!! A major source of nnEMF was coming from the hose of my ASV. It had a heater coil to maintain the humdity and temp of the air in the hose to prevent rain-out. This hose attached to my mask ON THE TOP OF MY HEAD and provided toxic levels of EMF directly to my brain for 8-10 hours every night. I was most unhappy when I discovered this after 3 months of use. I use a plain plastic hose now.
⦁ I've found my other major sources of nnEMF and eliminated them (mostly unplugging things when not in use.)​

⦁ I have blue blocking glasses for my eyes but I will be ordering screen covers for every electronic device I have (because blue light penetrates skin . . . and your thyroid . . . thanks @drezy!)
⦁ I use red light and/or fire light after sunset
⦁ I am doing CT but the water isn't cold enough. You know you've cold adapted when the water comes out of the spigot at 64 degress and you're pissed because you didn't want a 'warm bath' tonight!! I have been adding ice to get it around 55 degrees but that's all the ice my ice maker puts out

⦁ When I did CT a few years back, my deep freeze in the garage had room for lots of 10lb bags of ice. I don't have that space right now so am making do until I do
⦁ Currently water is 55-60 degrees; however, within 15 min, my body raises the temp by 5 degress minimum (I think my tub holds about 100 gallons) and this is the same tub I used with success before​

⦁ I'm using NIR at about 850nm for 30-60 min a day aimed at my sciatic hip or directly on my chest and neck
⦁ Despite all the changes I've made over the past month, I have only lost 5 lbs and have been in a homostasis for the past 2 - 3 weeks
⦁ I am additionally having a mid-day crash around noon where I just can't stay awake and have to lay down
⦁ The most glaring issue in my labs was my DHEA where I didn't even register on the minimal reference range

⦁ I've been supplementing at 75mg/day for the past 3 weeks and the pharmacy is re-evaluating to see if that is appropriate given the latest results
⦁ I had asked my doctor about pregnenolone instead of DHEA and he thought given my values, DHEA was the right thing. I'm still not sure​

⦁ Obviously my battery is not working right

⦁ This is evidenced by my labs and my sleep apnea
⦁ I don't see improvements right now and wonder if I need time to heal or if I'm missing something altogether
⦁ Maybe everything is helping and I'm just not over the critical mass to put me on the positive side yet?​

### Outlier — 2017-10-02T07:28:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-journal-of-an-outlier.20226/post-224674>

One of the reasons that people become attached to those machines is that it IS better than the alternative. That alternative being a life with no healing sleep where your battery is about to die any minute. That is a horrible way to live and these machines can be literally life changing when you're coming from that perspective. However, the sleep industry does not do well on educating folks about alternatives or risks. I was never really asked or told about my future, yet the 'system' and the insurance company had it all planned out. When I asked what the plan was AFTER the machine, there was a big pause then I was told (like I was 5) that this IS the treatment and I'd have to do it the rest of my life. NO IT'S NOT!!! In my case, I had the advantage of knowing what Dr. K teaches and I knew that's where I needed to be and here I am!!

I don't know exactly how to heal sleep apnea, but I do know JK said do not concentrate on curing sickness, but instead focus on light, water and magnetism. So that's where my effort is and hopefully I can prove that by doing so, my issues will be reversed.

So what do your labs look like? You meantioned sleep apnea and now migraines. In my readings on sleep apnea, I came across a neurosurgeon named Stasha Gominak. She had a number of migraine sufferers and a college suggested she send them in for sleep studies. For everyone she sent, the had a finding of sleep disturbances. She evolved to the next level of by passing sleep studies and going straight for labs. Unsurprising, she found all these patients were Vit D deficient. Her experiences were that you needed at least a Vit D level of 60, but no more than 80 to sleep soundly.

Are you doing the Leptin RX and other protocols recommended by JK? While you will never hear me say CT is a bad thing (I'm personally infatuated with it!), CT cannot fix everything and Vit D levels are one of them. You have to get direct sunlight at the right times of the day. Are you doing any of that?

The sleep from bedtime until I wake feels deep and restful and I have no concept of time - meaning when I do wake, there's a slight disorientation and I can't tell if I've been sleeping for a 5 minutes or 5 hours. However the interruptions affect my wake time later and I can easily sleep through an hour of snooze buttons. On weekends, I sleep til my body is ready to rise which is usually between 9 and 10 am.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-10-26T01:37:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-journal-of-an-outlier.20226/post-226584>

AM sunlight is critical to get the charge on the cell membranes up and inside the cell.........I spoke about this in the October Q &A so have a listen.

When heteroplasmy rises we are losing mitochondrial charge and as a result mitochondrial swelling = cell swelling = increased ubiquitin marking = protoein turnover = very energy costly with bad mito..........so more sun not less is the key to fix it.

The reason is deeply tied to the two subatomic particles in the mitochondria we use. The electron and proton. An electric charge alters the space around it in nature. It also alters the mass of particles in mitochondria itself. This is why Wallace finds inclusion bodies in heteroplastic mito in his papers. There are two kinds of charge, positive (protons) and negative (electrons), like charges repel, unlike charges attract; positive charge comes from having more protons than electrons; negative charge comes from having more electrons than protons.
Charge is quantized, meaning that charge comes in integer multiples of the elementary charge “e”, that is used in physics equations. Not important here but for the sake of learning. What does this really imply? This means ALL charge is highly conserved in nature. Charge is a function of redox. In fact, mitochondriacs can use them as synonyms once they understand electrons and protons well.
What does it mean for charge to be quantized in nature? Charge comes in multiples of an indivisible unit of charge, represented by the letter “e.” In other words, charge comes in multiples of the charge on the electron or the proton. These things have the same size charge, but the sign is different. A proton has a charge of +1e, while an electron has a charge of -1e. People with mitochondrial diseases have more protons with +1/2 (e) charge caught in the mitochondria and that is their real problem. We have to get rid of them. When you learn how to get rid of them their mitochondrial illnesses get a lot better. That is what I teach my members and you're getting a large dose of this pn the blogs now and certainly in November 2017 webinar. Not pills, not supplements, not even food. Bio-physics 101. How a mitochondria really works to keep you going long and strong.

To make large collections of proteins coherent you need to link them together electrically. This idea implies that cells have some electric tuning ability built into their protein structure. This tuning appears to be linked to the timing of day and night because collagen needs incident sunlight to collect the energy of sunlight to store for lateral electro-mechanical wave generation. How does this occur? What are its implications?

My members have been learning a lot about the non linear effects of light over ten years. Today’s thread confirms more of my instincts. I like to discuss another unique non linear apsect of light that is used to create the basic functioning of all circadian oscillators in humans. Our cells use light in multitudes of unconventional ways that just continue to confound physics and stun biologic orthoxy at its core. For more than 100 years, scientists have debated the question: when light travels through a medium such as oil or water, does it pull or push on the medium as it travels by Fermat’s law? It creates immutable ancient rhythms we rely on.

### JanSz — 2017-10-14T16:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-rhythms-are-immutable-for-humans.20227/post-225815>

**Your schedule could be killing you**
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suicide\_among\_doctors](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suicide_among_doctors)
Studies report that physicians are more likely to complete suicide than the general population and even people with similar academic training who are not physicians.
\---------------------
In my time in Poland to attend college one had to pass a test.
I had to be better than the other 5 or 6 guys or girls.
I made it to a group of about 120.
Out of that group only 19 made it straight and got masters degree.
Some others took longer but many dropped out.
The worst was first year.
Whoever flunk first year had to go to the back of the line facing again his 5 or 6 opponents.
Some professors would bend over backwards, giving assignments purposely defined in obscure ways, announcing that with the hopes that this one will get us out the doors.
At first year diarrhea was my constant friend.

Second and higher years were allowed to be repeated, some in highly limited ways.
I think the suicide rate was the worst in the first year.
Couple of guys went bonkers.
There was a famous mathematical genius who was not able to hack anything else so eventually he dropped out.

......

.............

### Paleodocteur — 2017-10-08T16:43:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nobel-prize-in-medicine-2017-circadian-rhythm.20229/post-225293>

I keep a trace of patients' circadiome on a piece of paper. Six years ago I was drawing as they spoke ( Nonchalant style) It was helpfull to ask pertinent questions and keep a trace of it. Through the years some finding were recurrent and with new blogs insights I added more items ( hence the MS clutterish aspect) . So I found it handy to make a sheet ready with places to write hours,items to circle, cross or outline, and important verbatim. i have found it pretty informative ( stories often rime) and a good way to open a dialogue on some relevant topics. Sometimes they get it right away when they see what my pen is outlining.

Although there are some apps for it I like using paper.
It’s a soothing EMR reduction strategy..

Thank you Jack for opening my eyes to circadian medicine.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-10-08T18:42:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nobel-prize-in-medicine-2017-circadian-rhythm.20229/post-225300>

What is cool about bringing old science to new eyes by putting windex on the glass eyes of medicine?

Your certificates, degrees, status and wallet do not matter. How you treat your equals and people under neath your direction matter most. Doctors and patients have the same deficits these days = They are both chronically disconnected from circadian biology and nature.

If I can get one in hundred to see it I am doing OK.

We see the world as what we carry in our own hearts. They see a mistake, I observe a lesson. They sees a shadow, I observe a light that casts it.
They see the night, I observe the moon and its effect on the Earth's tilt. They see a corporation, I observe people who make it thrive. They see millennials as a problem, I observe our bright future. They see success, I observe the coming journey. They see me as a stranger. I observe them, as my future friend and cohort in mito-hacking for a better world.

Where others see nothing or impossible strings.........I observe answers.

The right thing isn't always real obvious to the rest of the world. Sometimes the right thing for one person is the wrong thing for someone else. And everything, in retrospect, is obvious to those who use forethought. How you think makes you great. 99.9% of humans use hindsight to dictate their actions. Some of us train to observe the unseen first. Ignore what the eye sees and focus on what the brain observes

Like most things in life, everything is great— until it isn’t. #bluelight and #nnEMF reality.

The irony of modern society is that we are now the best-informed society that has ever walked this planet, but yet we also carry the greatest risk of dying from our own ignorance.
**Technology allows for escapism from nature.

This is why good people can make tragic errors for people who follow them. Case in point below.

How does a half-truth about food lead to a full lie in reality?** The LCHF folks say this: Increase fructose load increases uric acid which inhibits the production of nitric oxide constricting blood vessels to the brain. [pic.twitter.com/SgYMDTvij4](http://pic.twitter.com/SgYMDTvij4)

**But these food zealots forget about the reality of the entire picture:** Fructose shows up in foods grown by nature that only grow in strong photosynthetic environments naturally which have full-spectrum sunlight So fructose is never designed to be present without strong solar UV/IR exposure. Nitric oxide is also made by short wavelength UV light, so who cares if fructose inhibits NO? The only people who do care are people eating an LCHF diet who live indoors way too much. They never get NO from their skin because they are rarely in it. This is also why they get fat. The SQ fat in humans responds metabolically and hormonally to sunlight or the lack thereof. It just so happens sunlight increases NO production to offset the loss in the food with fructose. So it is really a lack of sunlight and not the foodstuff that is the real problem. #LCHFdownunder fail. #Fettkefail

Take a look at the Nobel Prize for physics in 2016 and consider what we gave the Nobel Prize for medicine in 2017 on 10/1?

The same thing and nobody really recognizes the convergence of science.

[https://www.jackkruse.com/time-24-physics-finally-caught-nature/](https://www.jackkruse.com/time-24-physics-finally-caught-nature/)

There is no food we can't eat, but the other side of that coin is just because you can eat is at this particular time, doesn't mean you should do it. Humans might have survived from this flexibility, but optimal diet is not basic survival when you divorce circadian biology from photosynthetic process.

Sun Moon Earth = Solar Eclipse

Sun Earth Moon = Lunar Eclipse

Moon Sun Earth = Apocalypse

This simple verse shows you how important the location and tilt of the moon is for the Earth’s tilt and control of circadian cycles that control life on Earth.

# Thread: Coming to a new beginning
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-to-a-new-beginning.20230/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sufoto — 2017-10-03T06:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-to-a-new-beginning.20230/post-224737>

It has been about a month since I began to explore the ideas put forth by Jack Kruse. Read his book. Getting up early to greet the day. Finding the sun. Looking for the sun light. Wearing my blue blockers while on the computer.
My basic info

An older woman ( a grandmother) who still feels like she is in her forties or maybe fifty. I am not retired rather I am a working person who has an on-going career.

I have noticed inflammation lately and high blood pressure for the past few years
.
The high blood pressure comes from my mother and maternal family. Mother had congestive heart failure and died too young at 78. Mother had pneumonia and pleurisy as an infant leaving her with weak lungs and she had many bouts on pneumonia during her life. Smoking for most her life did nothing to help.

Her mother died of heart failure as well in her 70's.

Father died of a heart attack even younger - in his late 50s.

I have managed my blood pressure by diet and exercise. I was doing well without meds until very recently.
Then in June was at the gym and blood pressure spiked and had me seeing double so I went to the ER. I thought I might be having a stroke. I wasn't and didn't. My doctor (integrative medicine) tried several different BP meds all of which made me feel worse. I cut them in half and then could barely tolerate them.
The last time I took them was several weeks ago. I recently took my BP and it was 160/82. Now trying to stay away from caffeine. Eating a Big Ass Breakfast. Getting the light.

Only last week I found that I had had a smart meter put on the house while I was in California in June. I think this may account for the spikes in BP and am addressing the meter with my electric company. I will have an opt out. Soon I hope!!

There is much more to add but it's getting past my bedtime. (This used to be an early bedtime)
So I will continue this tomorrow.

### Sufoto — 2017-10-04T04:24:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-to-a-new-beginning.20230/post-224879>

More

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=224777)
>
> welcome.

Good to be here!

### Sufoto — 2017-10-04T04:43:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-to-a-new-beginning.20230/post-224882>

Where I reside: Columbus, North Carolina. 35° 15′ 2″ N, 82° 12′ 8″ W
We have lived here since October 2005. The town has just under a 1000 people. We live in a house on 8 acres of mostly hillside forest. The three bedroom house is two stories. The main story is upstairs and the basement level built out and if I want to be "grounded" I would need to be downstairs. My office is downstairs, inside the wall where the meters are. This is not something I gave thought to , at the time.
The news on the smart meter. I spoke with them today. The electric company will remove the smart meter. It will cost $50 to remove it and $11 a month to have a meter reader human come to read it each month. But I am happy to do that to save my body.
Trying to find a place where the light in the morning and the grounding can happen. we have a deck that gets early light through the trees. But nowhere on the ground and because of the trees very little light on the ground until the sun is high. This is a dilemma I am trying to resolve. Forgot to put in the elevation of the town which is 1100 feet.

### Sufoto — 2017-10-04T05:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-to-a-new-beginning.20230/post-224889>

Trying the hardest to get the most light early in my eyes. My eye doc in NYC who wrote the book "Total VIsion", Richard Kavner, recommends looking at the clear blue sky. I used to do that and I worked with him on my eyes' convergence, a problem I didn't know I had but it was significantly improved with his exercises.
We had a well put in when we had been here a year and the water seems fine when we have had it tested. No chemicals.
Took me a time to find out that there is radon naturally occuring in this area and we had a very minimal reading in the cellar so put in the device to vent it.
Also making an effort to eat paleo. Pastured meat. (Recently found Butcher Box). No gluten for quite a few years and had been grain free but fell off that now going back to grain free. Starting the CT with the iced face in the sink which I did years ago when I read it was something that Paul Newman did every day. Kept him looking pretty great. Ah, vanity!
And I am reading reading reading to get up to speed.

# Thread: France is banning Wifi.......around kids.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/france-is-banning-wifi-around-kids.20233/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-10-05T05:04:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/france-is-banning-wifi-around-kids.20233/post-225038>

The French government has moved to outlaw Wi-Fi in nurseries, schools and childcare centres that cater for children under six. You'd think this would wake up Americans and the FCC about why they are doing this. The French clearly get the risk of WiFi on a developing human but we just ignore it for convenience and profit. The move is offering "a chance" to the creators of Li-Fi, a system that uses light to provide internet access. This is not a good cycle of replacement for those developing infants either due to frequency or due to flicker effects in this system. I see many potential issues using light to deliver communications. [http://luxreview.com/article/2017/0...l&utm\_source=facebook.com&utm\_campaign=buffer](http://luxreview.com/article/2017/05/li-fi-eyes-opportunity-as-france-bans-wi-fi-in-childcare-centres?utm_content=buffer0a744&utm_medium=social&utm_source=facebook.com&utm_campaign=buffer)

### Da-mo — 2017-10-05T07:48:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/france-is-banning-wifi-around-kids.20233/post-225054>

ovaries of young girls.

### Erin Gaiser — 2018-07-18T18:01:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/france-is-banning-wifi-around-kids.20233/post-246132>

France also made a huge move to ban kids from bringing cell phone into schools! Just a month ago I think.
Paris used to have free wi-fi for the city, but it got taken out.
All municipal buildings (libraries, government buildings) have taken wi-fi out because people got headaches!
It just pisses me off because meanwhile Germany just keeps talking about building more towers, more wi-fi in school so that the kids aren't ,technologically' behind ect.
